title: "Kapłon" Mazurski (Mazovian "Capon")
body:
## Ingredients

- bread
- one clove of garlic, finely chopped, or onion
- salt and pepper to taste
- boiling water

## Directions

1. Chop bread into cubes
2. Add finely chopped garlic or onion and place in the oven to brown
3. Place on hot plates and pour boiling water over
4. Add salt and pepper to taste

title: Angielska Szarlotka z Jabłek (English Apple Charlotte)
body:
## Ingredients

- sour apples
- two-thirds cup sugar
- one quarter cup water
- butter
- salt
- grated lemon zest
- flour
- lard

## Directions

1. Cut sour apples into very thin slices and fill a deep baking pan or any vessel with straight sides to the brim
2. Sprinkle two-thirds cup sugar
3. Add one quarter cup water, a little butter, a pinch of salt and some grated lemon zest
4. Mix some flour with lard and water to make a stiff dough
5. Roll out on a table to the size of the pan with apples
6. Cover the pan and seal the edges
7. To help the dough hold better, wet the edges of the pan with cold water
8. Bake in the oven for half an hour
9. Serve hot with sugar and cream

title: Angielskie Muffins (English Muffins)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.5 cups warm water
- 1 teaspoon salt

## Directions

1. Pour 1.5 cups of warm water into a bowl, add 1 teaspoon of salt.

title: Angielskie Muffins (English Muffins)
body:
## Ingredients

- one and a half cups lukewarm water
- one teaspoon salt
- two-thirds tablet of pressed yeast
- three tablespoons lukewarm water
- four cups wheat flour

## Directions

1. Pour one and a half cups of lukewarm water into a bowl, add one teaspoon of salt
2. Dissolve two-thirds of a tablet of pressed yeast in three tablespoons of lukewarm water, then add to the bowl
3. Slowly add, while constantly stirring, four cups of wheat flour
4. Beat perfectly and place in a warm place so the dough rises, which should take five hours
5. When well risen, turn out onto a work surface and form round cakes, less than half an inch thick and two and a half inches wide
6. Arrange these on a warm buttered pan or baking sheet, place on a warm stove so they slowly rise, which will take about twenty minutes
7. Then move the pan to a hotter place and bake, turning frequently, which should take fifteen minutes
8. Brush with butter while hot and serve immediately

title: Anise Liqueur (Likier Anyżowy)
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart of spirit
- half a quart of boiled and cooled water
- one ounce of ground anise
- one and a half pounds of sugar
- sugar to taste

## Directions

1. Take one quart of spirit and add half a quart of boiled and cooled water
2. Add one ounce of ground anise and one and a half pounds of sugar and set in a cool place for two days
3. Then strain or filter and add sugar to taste

title: Babka Czekoladowa (Chocolate Babka)
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup brown sugar
- one cup grated chocolate
- one cup milk
- one cup sugar
- half cup butter
- two eggs
- half cup sweet milk
- one teaspoon soda
- two cups flour
- one teaspoon vanilla extract

## Directions

1. Boil together one cup of brown sugar, one cup of grated chocolate, and one cup of milk, then cool
2. Separately, beat one cup of sugar with half cup of butter, add two eggs, half cup of sweet milk, one teaspoon of soda, two cups of flour, and one teaspoon of vanilla extract
3. Mix, then add the cooled milk with sugar and chocolate, mix everything again
4. Fill the form and bake

title: Babka na Proszku (Baking Powder Babka)
body:
## Ingredients

- two eggs
- one cup sugar
- one cup flour
- one teaspoon vanilla extract
- one teaspoon baking powder
- half cup boiled milk

## Directions

1. Beat two eggs perfectly with one cup of sugar
2. Add one cup of flour, one teaspoon of vanilla extract, one teaspoon of baking powder, and finally half cup of boiled milk
3. Fill the form and bake in a moderately hot oven

title: Babka Oszczędna (Economy Babka)
body:
## Ingredients

- one egg
- one tablespoon butter
- four tablespoons cream
- water
- one and a half cups flour
- one cup sugar
- pinch of salt

## Directions

1. Break one egg into a cup, add one tablespoon of butter, four tablespoons of cream—which may be canned—and fill to the top with water
2. Sift together one and a half cups of flour, one cup of sugar, and a pinch of salt
3. Add the egg with butter, beat everything well, beating at least five minutes
4. Fill a form with this batter and bake

title: Babka Oszczędna (Economy Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- one egg
- one tablespoon butter
- four tablespoons sour cream (may be canned)
- water
- one and a half cups flour
- one cup sugar
- a pinch of salt

## Directions

1. Beat one egg in a cup, add one tablespoon butter, four tablespoons sour cream, and fill with water
2. Sift together one and a half cups flour, one cup sugar, and a pinch of salt
3. Add the egg mixture to the dry ingredients, beat well for at least five minutes
4. Fill a form with the batter and bake

title: Babka Słoneczna (Sunshine Babka)
body:
## Ingredients

- eleven egg whites
- three-quarters cup sugar
- six egg yolks
- one cup flour
- one teaspoon cream of tartar
- one teaspoon orange extract

## Directions

1. Beat eleven egg whites into foam, then slowly add three-quarters cup of sugar and add six egg yolks beaten until white
2. Add again three-quarters cup of sugar and mix
3. Sift one cup of flour with one teaspoon of cream of tartar and add, as well as one teaspoon of orange extract
4. Bake for about fifty-five minutes in a metal form

title: Babka z Kąpieli (Bath Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of sifted flour
- quarter of heated milk
- three beaten egg yolks
- one tablet of yeast
- two tablespoons of melted butter
- salt
- three egg whites
- fruit juices or clarified butter

## Directions

1. Mix one pound of sifted flour with a quarter of heated milk, add three beaten egg yolks, a tablet of yeast, two tablespoons of melted butter, a pinch of salt and meringue from three egg whites
2. Beat everything well for fifteen minutes, then place the dough in a buttered mold and set in a warm place to rise
3. When the dough has risen well, place the mold with it in a pot with boiling water and cook for one hour
4. After cooking, turn onto a platter or salad bowl and serve at table with fruit juices or clarified butter

title: Babka ze Śledzi (Herring Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- two large herring
- milk
- four medium potatoes
- one egg
- sour cream
- pepper to taste
- butter
- flour

## Directions

1. Soak two large herring in milk for eighteen hours
2. Remove from milk, clean, remove skin and bones and pound into a mass in a mortar
3. Bake four medium potatoes in their skins in the oven and then press through a sieve
4. Beat one egg strongly, add a few tablespoons of thick sour cream and pepper to taste
5. Mix all ingredients into a uniform mass
6. Butter and flour a small mold, fill with the herring and potato mass
7. Place in a moderately hot oven for twenty minutes, then serve immediately

title: Babka Zwyczajna (Plain Babka)
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup flour
- quarter teaspoon salt
- six egg yolks
- one cup sugar
- juice and zest of one lemon
- six egg whites

## Directions

1. Sift one cup of flour with quarter teaspoon of salt
2. Beat six egg yolks, add one cup of sugar, adding slowly, juice and grated zest of one lemon, and mix well
3. Beat the egg whites into very stiff foam and add as well
4. Add the flour, mix, place in a form and bake for about fifty minutes
5. Do not open the oven door for the first fifteen minutes

title: Babka Śnieżna (Snow Babka)
body:
## Ingredients

- eleven egg whites
- one teaspoon cream of tartar
- one and a half cups sugar
- one cup flour
- quarter teaspoon salt
- one teaspoon vanilla extract

## Directions

1. Beat eleven egg whites into foam, add one teaspoon of cream of tartar and beat further until the foam is stiff
2. Slowly add one and a half cups of sugar, one cup of flour sifted with quarter teaspoon of salt and mix
3. Add one teaspoon of vanilla extract, place in a form and put in the oven for forty-five to fifty minutes
4. After baking, turn the form onto a plate and let the babka fall out by itself

title: Banany z Pomarańczowym Sokiem (Bananas with Orange Juice)
body:
## Ingredients

- four bananas
- one tablespoon fine sugar
- two large oranges

## Directions

1. Peel four bananas and cut into pieces one inch long, arrange in a glass bowl, sprinkle with one tablespoon of fine sugar and then pour over with juice squeezed from two large oranges; set in a cold place for several hours.
2. It is best to prepare them in the evening for use the next day.

title: Baranina Duszona (Stewed Lamb)
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb roast
- vinegar
- roots and spices
- butter
- bacon slices
- celery
- parsley
- onion
- juniper berries
- flour
- sour cream

## Directions

1. Clean the lamb roast of excess fat and membranes and soak for several hours in vinegar with roots.
2. After removing from vinegar, place in a very hot oven for a few minutes to brown quickly on top.
3. Put some butter in a braising pan, several slices of bacon, chop celery, parsley, and onion, add a few juniper berries and place the browned meat on top.
4. Pour in about two tablespoons of the vinegar in which it was soaked and stew on low heat.
5. When almost tender, remove from the pan, dust with flour around it. Strain the sauce through a sieve, then place the meat back in the pan, pour the strained sauce over it and continue stewing until tender.
6. Shortly before serving, season with sour cream.

title: Baranina Faszerowana (Stuffed Lamb)
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb shoulder
- one cup of grated cooked potatoes
- one cup of grated roll
- one tablespoon of melted butter
- one tablespoon of vinegar
- one tablespoon of tomato sauce (catsup)
- salt
- pepper
- a pinch of powdered cloves
- bay leaf
- one tablespoon of orange juice
- one tablespoon of milk

## Directions

1. Butterfly the lamb shoulder on the inner side and remove the bones, which use for soup.
2. Make the following stuffing: Take one cup of grated cooked potatoes, one cup of grated roll, one tablespoon of melted butter, one tablespoon of vinegar, one tablespoon of tomato sauce (catsup), salt and pepper to taste, as well as a pinch of powdered cloves, a bay leaf, one tablespoon of orange juice and one tablespoon of milk; mix all of this well.
3. Fill the shoulder with this stuffing where the bone was removed, then roll the meat with the stuffing inside and roast on a roasting pan, basting frequently during roasting.
4. Roasting time is from fifteen to twenty minutes per pound of meat.

title: Baranina Odgrzewana z Makaronem (Reheated Mutton with Pasta)
body:
## Ingredients

- mutton drippings and skin
- lean mutton meat
- salt
- pepper
- butter
- bread crumbs
- cooked Italian pasta
- tomato sauce (catsup)

## Directions

1. Trim all fat and browned skin from leftover roasted mutton.
2. Cut the lean meat into thin slices and sprinkle with salt and pepper.
3. Butter a small roasting pan or cake form.
4. Place a layer of bread crumbs on the bottom.
5. Add a layer of meat and a layer of cooked Italian pasta cut into one-inch pieces.
6. Pour one tablespoon of tomato sauce (catsup) until the dish is filled.
7. Sprinkle everything with bread crumbs to a depth of at least one quarter-inch.
8. Pour two tablespoons of melted butter over top.
9. Place in a fairly hot oven for half an hour.

title: Baranina Smażona (Fried Lamb)
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb shoulder
- salt
- pepper
- butter

## Directions

1. Cut lamb meat from the shoulder into slices at least as thick as a finger, beat lightly, sprinkle with salt and pepper and fry in hot butter, being careful not to overfly.
2. When serving, pour hot butter over it and separately serve potatoes and salad, or some other vegetable.

title: Barszcz Białoruski (Belarusian Beet Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- a few pounds of beef bones
- water
- several peppercorns
- a few bay leaves
- a few beets
- sour cream
- flour
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Pour water over a few pounds of beef bones, add several peppercorns and a few bay leaves, and cook for an hour and a half
2. Chop a few beets very finely, add to the broth and cook further until the beets are completely soft
3. Season with a cup of sour cream mixed with a small amount of flour, add vinegar to taste, bring to a boil and serve
4. This soup can be made equally well with smoked meat or ham

title: Barszcz Czysty (Pure Beet Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef meat with bone, preferably with marrow
- small amount of water
- a few beets
- parsley
- carrot
- sugar
- a whole onion
- a few dried mushrooms
- a few small red beets
- beet kvass

## Directions

1. Take beef meat with bone, preferably with marrow, and pour a small amount of water over it, then cook like regular broth
2. Separately, lightly sauté a few beets, parsley, and carrot, sprinkle with sugar, and when they release juice, add to the simmering broth along with one whole onion and a few dried mushrooms
3. Cook a few small red beets separately, then peel them, chop finely, and pour beet kvass over them; set in a warm place and let stand for an hour or slightly longer
4. When the broth is cooked, pour in the kvass mixture, bring to a boil, and immediately remove from heat; do not cook longer as it becomes bitter and unpleasant
5. Before serving, strain the soup so all additions remain in the strainer, then pour into a dish and add a handful of previously cooked and chopped red beets
6. Serve with uszka, sliced cooked kielbasa, eggs in their shells carefully added to the plate, or even cooked potatoes

title: Barszcz Małorosyjski (Little Russian Beet Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef broth
- two red beets
- one carrot
- a few sprigs of parsley
- a few celery stems
- a small head of cabbage
- fat skimmed from broth
- flour
- a few chopped tomatoes
- bread kvass or beet kvass
- sour cream

## Directions

1. Cook ordinary beef broth
2. Chop finely two red beets, one carrot, a few sprigs of parsley, a few celery stems, and chop into small pieces a small head of cabbage; place vegetables in a pot, add fat skimmed from the broth which should already be nearly done, and brown them
3. Add a spoon of flour to these vegetables and mix well
4. Add the browned vegetables and a few chopped tomatoes to the broth, pour in bread or beet kvass, cook for another half hour and thicken with flour
5. If sour cream is available, lighten the soup with it
6. Cut the meat cooked in the broth into cubes and place in the dish, then pour the soup over it

title: Barszcz Podolski (Podolian Beet Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef meat, bones, or smoked meat
- a few onions
- bay leaf
- several peppercorns
- a few beets
- half a head of cabbage
- sour cream
- flour
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Set meat, bones or smoked meat as for broth, add a few onions, a bay leaf, and several peppercorns, and cook for a good hour
2. Chop a few beets and cut not very finely half a head of cabbage, add to the soup and cook as long as the cabbage and beets are completely soft
3. Season with a cup of sour cream mixed with a small amount of flour, add vinegar to taste and serve

title: Barszcz Polski Szary (Grey Polish Beet Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- pork bones
- a piece of smoked meat
- beet kvass
- a few onions
- a few dried mushrooms
- a clove of garlic
- ginger
- marjoram
- several peppercorns
- flour
- fresh dill

## Directions

1. Take pork bones and a piece of smoked meat, pour beet kvass over them and cook
2. After skimming, add a few onions, a few dried mushrooms, a clove of garlic, some ginger and marjoram, and several peppercorns, then continue cooking until the meat is tender
3. Strain the soup into another pot, thicken with flour, bring to a boil and serve in a dish or on plates with the meat from the soup cut into cubes
4. Sprinkle with chopped fresh dill on top

title: Barszcz Wołyński (Volyn Beet Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- good beef broth
- a small head of cabbage
- a few beets
- butter
- three or four small tomatoes (or a few larger ones)
- sour cream

## Directions

1. Cook good beef broth
2. Separately, prepare a small head of cabbage by first scalding it with boiling water and pouring off that water, then cook it along with a few beets
3. Cut the cabbage into quarters and the beets into thin slices and add to the broth
4. Braise three or four small tomatoes (or a few larger ones) in butter, press through a sieve to separate the seeds and skins, and add to the soup
5. Cook everything for half an hour
6. Before serving, pour a few spoons of good sour cream into the dish and pour the soup over it while stirring simultaneously so the cream blends with the soup; then take the meat from the broth, cut into small cubes and add to the dish

title: Barszcz Zabielany (Cream Beet Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- pure beet soup as prepared previously
- half a spoon of flour
- one large cup of good sour cream

## Directions

1. Prepare pure beet soup according to the previous recipe
2. Just before serving, carefully mix half a spoon of flour with one large cup of good sour cream
3. Add this mixture to the soup, pouring gradually and constantly stirring
4. Bring to a boil and serve
5. Serve with sliced kielbasa, or other cured meat, uszka, or hard-boiled eggs cut into quarters; grated potatoes may also be added

title: Barszcz ze Śmietaną (Beet Soup with Sour Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- several mushrooms
- a couple of onions
- various vegetables to taste
- several red beets
- beet kvass
- half a spoon of flour
- half a quarter litre of good sour cream
- cooked beets

## Directions

1. Cook several mushrooms, a couple of onions and various vegetables to taste
2. Separately cook or bake several red beets
3. Chop them finely and pour over with beet kvass, then set in a warm place for an hour or somewhat longer
4. Pour this kvass over the taste from the cooked vegetables
5. Mix half a spoon of flour with half a quarter litre of good sour cream and thicken the beet soup with it, pouring gradually and stirring constantly
6. Finally bring to a boil and pour into a bowl to which a couple of spoonfuls of cooked beets have been added

title: Barszcz ze Śmietaną (Beet Soup with Sour Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- a few mushrooms
- a few onions
- various vegetables to taste
- a few red beets
- acid

## Directions

1. Cook a few mushrooms, a few onions and various vegetables to taste separately; cook or bake a few red beets separately, then chop them finely and pour with acid

title: Barszcz Żytni (Rye Beet Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- a few spoons of rye flour
- warm water
- a few red beets
- a small head of cabbage
- hearty broth
- sour cream

## Directions

1. Pour a few spoons of rye flour with warm water and set in a warm place for fermentation, which should take forty-eight hours
2. Separately, cook a few red beets and a small head of cabbage; chop the beets finely and cut the cabbage into larger pieces
3. Have a hearty broth ready, mix it half and half with the rye ferment, bring to a boil together, season with sour cream if desired, and pour into a dish in which cabbage and beets were previously placed
4. Instead of rye flour, one can use ground barley with equally good results

title: Barszcz Żytni z Botwiną (Rye Beet Soup with Beet Greens)
body:
## Ingredients

- some rye flour
- warm water
- fresh beet greens from young beets
- dill
- chives
- salt to taste
- rye kvass
- sour cream and flour

## Directions

1. Take some rye flour, pour over with warm water and set in a warm place for forty-eight hours for fermentation
2. Cook fresh beet greens from young beets in fresh water with dill and chives, adding salt to taste
3. When everything is well cooked, take this cooked mass and dilute with rye kvass, thicken with sour cream and flour, and serve

title: Bażant Pieczony (Roasted Pheasant)
body:
## Ingredients

- pheasant with tail feathers and head with neck
- pheasant wings
- thin slices of lard
- butter
- grated bread

## Directions

1. Cut out the entire tail with feathers without damaging them, also remove the head with neck and wings without plucking them
2. Pluck the body
3. Cover the entire pheasant with thin slices of lard and let rest for twenty-four hours
4. After this time, stuff the entire pheasant very densely with very thinly sliced lard
5. Roast, continually basting with butter
6. Just before serving, sprinkle with grated bread and pour over browned butter
7. To serve: cut a slice of bread the length of a whole loaf, brown it, place in the center of a platter
8. Place the roasted pheasant on the bread, fasten the tail on a wire from one end and the head from the other
9. Place the wings on the sides, spread as if in flight
10. The body can be served whole, or cut into thin slices and then arranged again so the pheasant appears whole

title: Befsztyk (Beefsteak)
body:
## Ingredients

- meat cut one inch thick
- butter or fat
- salt

## Directions

1. The best beefsteak is fried on an iron grill over which fire burns and flame reaches almost the meat
2. If such a grill is not available, fry on a skillet on very high heat
3. Commonly used in America are the following cuts: tenderloin, porterhouse, short porterhouse, and sirloin; porterhouse is considered the best
4. A good beefsteak must be cut one inch thick, and generally the larger the slice, the better the beefsteak
5. Throw such a slice of meat onto a heavy, strongly heated skillet on high heat with a small amount of butter or fat
6. Since the quality of beefsteak depends largely on high heat during frying, when frying on a skillet it is probably best to use fat rather than butter, as the temperature of hot fat is higher than butter, but after frying on fat, smear the beefsteak with butter
7. It is important that the meat not be beaten or salted at all—salt it only after frying
8. Always fry the entire slice of meat, never cut it into pieces
9. Serve only with the meat juice remaining on the skillet
10. Fry briefly, at most ten to fifteen minutes, and after frying the meat should be red inside
11. Immediately after frying, smear the beefsteak with butter and serve
12. Serve with potatoes as a vegetable, preferably fried French style

title: Befsztyk po Niemiecku (German Beefsteak)
body:
## Ingredients

- a piece of tender beef for beefsteak
- olive oil
- salt
- butter
- onion, sliced

## Directions

1. Smear a piece of tender beef on both sides with olive oil and lightly salt
2. Immediately throw it onto a skillet with browned butter and sliced onion
3. The onion should be placed on the butter just before laying the meat on it

title: Befsztyk po Niemiecku (German-style Steak)
body:
## Ingredients

- a piece of tender meat for steak
- oil
- salt
- butter
- onion, sliced
- small potatoes, boiled in water
- mushrooms, sliced and stewed in butter
- eggs

## Directions

1. Coat the meat on both sides with oil and lightly salt it
2. Immediately place it on a pan with browned butter and sliced onion; place the onion on the butter just before laying the meat on it
3. Fry on strong heat until the meat is well browned on one side, then flip to the other side
4. Arrange small potatoes that were previously boiled in water on one side of the pan next to the meat
5. On the other side, place mushrooms that have been sliced and stewed in butter
6. On opposite sides of the pan, near the edges, crack one egg on each side
7. When the eggs are well set, serve on the table
8. Prepare this steak on a round flat pan without a handle, something like a large plate, and after frying, place the pan on a plate and serve it together with the pan

title: Befsztyk Tatarski (Tartar Steak)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef, preferably tenderloin
- mustard
- pepper
- salt
- capers
- chopped onion
- pickles
- lettuce leaves
- raw egg yolk

## Directions

1. Scrape a piece of beef, preferably tenderloin
2. Add mustard, pepper and salt to taste, and a few capers
3. Mix well and place on a platter, forming it into a neat shape
4. Around it, arrange chopped onion, pickles, lettuce leaves, etc.
5. On top of the meat, make a small hollow and place one raw egg yolk in it

title: Befsztyk z Bananami (Steak with Bananas)
body:
## Ingredients

- steak, cut thick
- four bananas
- butter
- salt
- red pepper

## Directions

1. Fry the thickly cut steak on strong heat
2. Peel four bananas and slice each lengthwise into three parts
3. Place them on a pan with hot butter and fry for five minutes
4. Place the steak on a platter and cover the top with the fried bananas
5. Pour melted butter over it, to which add a little salt and a pinch of red pepper
6. Serve on the table

title: Befsztyk Zapiekany (Baked Steak)
body:
## Ingredients

- steak, cut fairly thick
- potatoes, peeled and sliced into half-inch slices
- salt
- pepper
- butter

## Directions

1. Place the steak in the center of a baking dish
2. Arrange peeled and sliced potatoes around it
3. Salt the potatoes and sprinkle with pepper
4. Place a tablespoon of butter, broken into small pieces, on top
5. Do not salt the meat yet
6. Put in the oven, making sure it is not too hot, and bake for twenty minutes to one hour depending on the amount of meat and whether you want it well-cooked or not
7. Salt the steak only after baking, when serving on the table
8. Even lower quality meat prepared this way will taste good

title: Berkshire Muffins
body:
## Ingredients

- 0.5 cup cornmeal
- 0.67 cup boiled milk
- 0.5 cup cooked rice
- 0.5 cup wheat flour
- 2 tablespoons sugar
- 0.5 teaspoon salt
- 3 teaspoons baking powder
- 1 egg yolk
- 1 tablespoon melted butter
- 1 egg white

## Directions

1. Pour 0.67 cup of boiled milk over 0.5 cup cornmeal and let sit for five minutes, then add 0.5 cup cooked rice and mix.
2. Mix 0.5 cup wheat flour, 2 tablespoons sugar, 0.5 teaspoon salt, and 3 teaspoons baking powder, preferably sifting together several times to mix well, and add to the first mixture and mix.
3. Beat 1 egg yolk and add 1 tablespoon melted butter, then fold in 1 egg white beaten to stiff peaks and mix.
4. Fill forms and bake.

title: Biała Potrawka z Kury (White Chicken Stew)
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken, cut into pieces
- water
- quarter pound of salted pork fat, not smoked
- tablespoon of butter
- two tablespoons of flour
- milk
- salt and pepper to taste
- fresh parsley, chopped

## Directions

1. Cut the chicken into about fifteen pieces and place in a pot.
2. Cover with enough water to barely cover the meat.
3. Add a quarter pound of salted pork fat (not smoked), cut into strips.
4. Cover tightly and braise on low heat until tender; the water should completely evaporate.
5. In a pan, melt a tablespoon of butter, add two tablespoons of flour and fry briefly, stirring with a spoon, being careful not to brown the flour.
6. Add one and a half to two cups of milk to the flour mixture and bring to a boil.
7. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
8. Pour over the meat arranged on a serving dish.
9. Finish by sprinkling chopped fresh parsley on top.

title: Biała Zupa Postna (White Lenten Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart of good milk
- half a quarter litre of cold water
- one onion
- a couple of cooked and mashed potatoes
- one spoon of butter
- two spoons of tapioca previously soaked in a small amount of milk
- salt and pepper
- celery
- one beaten egg

## Directions

1. Add half a quarter litre of cold water to one quart of good milk, add one onion and a couple of cooked and mashed potatoes, as well as one spoon of butter and bring all this to a boil
2. When it boils, add two spoons of previously soaked tapioca in a small amount of milk, salt and pepper and boil for a few minutes
3. The quality of the soup can be improved by adding celery and one beaten egg

title: Biały Majonez z Ryb (White Fish Mayonnaise)
body:
## Ingredients

- pike or sandacz
- various vegetables
- onion
- salt
- gelatin (one ounce for a fish weighing six to eight pounds)

## Directions

1. Prepare a court-bouillon (smak) from various vegetables and onion, then cool it.
2. Clean a pike or sandacz and place it in a long pan.
3. Pour the cold court-bouillon over it.
4. Cook until tender.
5. Remove and arrange on a platter.
6. Strain the remaining court-bouillon through dense cloth and cool it.
7. For a pike weighing six to eight pounds, take one ounce of gelatin.
8. Pour one quarter of the cold court-bouillon over the gelatin.
9. When it softens completely, drain off this liquid and place it on the fire.
10. When it is well heated, add the gelatin which will dissolve.

title: Biały Majonez z Ryb (White Fish Mayonnaise)
body:
## Ingredients

- pike or sandacz fish, 6-8 pounds
- miscellaneous vegetables
- onion
- gelatin, 1 ounce
- cold broth, half a quarter
- oil, half a pound
- 1 whole lemon
- fine sugar, 1 teaspoon
- salt
- white gelatin leaves, a few
- red gelatin leaves, a few
- lemon juice
- carrot, sliced
- green peas
- capers
- cornichons
- olives
- mushrooms
- hard-boiled eggs
- lettuce leaves

## Directions

1. Cook broth with miscellaneous vegetables and onion, then cool.
2. Fillet pike or sandacz, place in an oblong pan, cover with cold broth and cook until tender.
3. Remove fish and arrange on a platter.
4. Strain remaining broth through dense cloth and cool.
5. For a 6-8 pound pike, take 1 ounce gelatin and cover with half a quarter of cold broth.
6. When completely softened, pour off the broth and heat on fire.
7. When well heated, add the gelatin, which will dissolve immediately, then cool and beat with a whisk until white.
8. Add half a pound of oil very slowly while continuously beating.
9. When all oil is incorporated, squeeze juice from 1 whole lemon, add 1 teaspoon fine sugar and a pinch of salt.
10. Beat everything into a uniform mass and cover the fish arranged on the platter with this mayonnaise so it is completely covered.
11. Decorate the top with sliced carrot, green peas, capers, cornichons, olives, mushrooms, hard-boiled eggs and lettuce leaves.
12. Separately dissolve a few white gelatin leaves and as many red, add lemon juice and a pinch of sugar and salt, and pour onto plates.
13. When set, cut this aspic into triangles and decorate the platter with the fish with them.

title: Bigos (Polish Hunter's Stew)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 head of cabbage, shredded
- salt
- 2 white onions, minced
- a few sour apples
- fat cooked beef, sliced
- cold roast
- raw pork
- various meats
- butter
- broth or bouillon
- 1 tablespoon butter mixed with 1 tablespoon flour
- cooked sausage, sliced

## Directions

1. Shred the cabbage, salt it well, and press out the liquid firmly.
2. Mince the onions and peel and chop the sour apples.
3. Slice the cooked beef and prepare various other meats.
4. Mix the cabbage with the onion.
5. In a pan, layer as follows: butter on the bottom, then cabbage, apples, pork, other meats, then butter again, repeating until all ingredients are used.
6. Pour broth or bouillon over everything.
7. Cover and braise for two hours or longer over low heat, shaking the pan frequently to prevent sticking.
8. When the bigos is nearly done, thicken with a tablespoon of butter mixed with a tablespoon of flour, stir well, bring to a boil a few more times, and serve.
9. Traditionally, cooked sausage sliced into rounds is placed on top when serving.
10. Bigos tastes even better when reheated the next day.
11. Fresh cabbage can be replaced with sauerkraut, but the sauerkraut should first be covered with cold water and brought to a boil, then the water drained, and about a quarter pound of bacon cut into cubes placed on the bottom of the pan instead of butter.

title: Bitki w Śmietanie (Beef Patties in Sour Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds lean beef, preferably first-cut cross-rib
- salt
- pepper
- one bun soaked in milk and squeezed out
- two egg yolks
- one tablespoon butter
- egg whites, beaten stiff
- flour
- butter for frying
- one cup sour cream
- half a tablespoon flour

## Directions

1. Take two pounds of lean beef, preferably first-cut cross-rib, and grind it in a meat grinder
2. Add salt and pepper to taste, and one bun soaked in milk and squeezed out
3. Grind again, beat in two egg yolks, one tablespoon of butter and egg whites beaten stiff
4. After mixing, form nine round flat patties from this mixture
5. Dredge them in flour and fry them in butter on low heat without browning them too much
6. Mix one cup of sour cream with half a tablespoon of flour, pour it over the patties in the pan when they are almost done and fry together with the sour cream for a few minutes
7. Serve these patties with potatoes fried in the French style, or with regular fried potatoes

title: Blanc Mange z Czekolady (Chocolate Blanc Mange)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a quart of milk
- half a cup of sugar
- three heaping tablespoons of chocolate
- one heaping tablespoon of cornstarch
- cold water
- cream flavored with vanilla or vanilla extract

## Directions

1. Heat half a quart of milk in a double boiler, then add half a cup of sugar.
2. Grate three heaping tablespoons of chocolate, mix with one heaping tablespoon of cornstarch, and dissolve in a little cold water into a smooth paste.
3. Pour this mixture into the boiling milk and cook together for five minutes.
4. Pour into a tin mold (or can be left in the pot) and place on ice.
5. When very cold, run a knife around the edges and place the mold in hot water for half a minute so the mixture pulls away from the bottom.
6. Turn out onto a glass serving dish.
7. Serve with cream flavored with vanilla or vanilla extract, which can be poured around the cream in the dish or served separately.

title: Bliny Francuskie (French Pancakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- two cups flour
- one tablespoon sugar
- quarter teaspoon salt
- one cup milk
- three beaten eggs
- butter for frying
- jam or preserves for filling
- sugar for topping

## Directions

1. Sift two cups of flour with one tablespoon of sugar and a quarter teaspoon of salt.
2. Slowly add one cup of milk and three beaten eggs, then beat vigorously for at least five minutes.
3. Fry on hot buttered pan.
4. Spread generously with jam or preserves, roll into a tube, sprinkle with sugar, and serve immediately.

title: Bliny Graham (Graham Pancakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- two cups boiled milk
- one teaspoon sugar
- one teaspoon salt
- half a cake of yeast, finely crumbled
- one cup graham flour
- one cup wheat flour
- quarter teaspoon soda dissolved in two tablespoons of warm milk

## Directions

1. Pour one teaspoon of sugar and one teaspoon of salt into two cups of boiled milk, stir, and set in a cool place.
2. When cooled to lukewarm temperature, add half a finely crumbled cake of yeast and stir until it dissolves.
3. Then add one cup of graham flour and one cup of wheat flour, beat well, and set overnight in a cool place.
4. The next morning, add a quarter teaspoon of soda dissolved in two tablespoons of warm milk, and fry on a hot pan as usual for pancakes.

title: Bliny Gryczane (Buckwheat Pancakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- half cup cornmeal
- half teaspoon salt
- half cup wheat flour
- one cup buckwheat flour
- half a cake of yeast dissolved in a small amount of warm water
- half cup milk
- quarter teaspoon soda

## Directions

1. Pour boiling water over half a cup of cornmeal, add half a teaspoon of salt, stir, and set in a cool place.
2. When cooled to lukewarm temperature, add half a cup of wheat flour and one cup of buckwheat flour, as well as half a cake of yeast dissolved in a small amount of warm water.
3. Make this mixture in the evening.
4. The next day, pour off the water that has collected on top, add half a cup of milk mixed with a quarter teaspoon of soda, and stir well.
5. Fry on a pan as usual for pancakes.

title: Bliny Kartoflane (Potato Pancakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- two cups grated potato mass
- two beaten eggs
- one tablespoon wheat flour
- half teaspoon salt
- butter for frying
- pinch of pepper

## Directions

1. Grate potatoes on a grater.
2. To two cups of potato mass add two beaten eggs, one tablespoon of wheat flour, half a teaspoon of salt, and a pinch of pepper.
3. Beat everything well and fry in butter.
4. It is recommended to peel potatoes in the evening and place them in cold water, where they should remain all night, and grate them only the next day, just before use.

title: Bliny Kukurydzane (Cornmeal Pancakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- two cups milk
- one teaspoon baking soda
- half teaspoon salt
- two eggs
- half cup wheat flour
- one cup cornmeal
- fat for the pan

## Directions

1. Pour two cups of milk into a clay bowl.
2. Dissolve one teaspoon of baking soda and half a teaspoon of salt in half a cup of hot water and pour into the milk.
3. Lightly beat two eggs and also pour into the milk.
4. Mix half a cup of wheat flour with one cup of cornmeal, pour into the milk, and stir into smooth batter.
5. Pour this batter with a spoon onto a hot, greased pan and fry on high heat.

title: Bliny na Proszku (Pancakes with Baking Powder)
body:
## Ingredients

- two cups flour
- three teaspoons baking powder
- one quarter teaspoon salt
- one and one half cups sweet milk
- one beaten egg
- one quarter cup melted butter

## Directions

1. Sift two cups of flour with three teaspoons of baking powder and one quarter teaspoon of salt several times through a sieve.
2. Pour in one and one half cups of sweet milk, one beaten egg, and one quarter cup of melted butter.
3. Beat well before adding the butter.
4. Pour by the spoonful onto a hot pan and fry.
5. When the bottom side is sufficiently browned, flip to the other side.
6. If the pancakes stick to the pan, do not grease the pan but instead put a teaspoon of butter into the batter so there will be no smoke.

title: Bliny na Sodzie (Pancakes with Soda)
body:
## Ingredients

- two cups flour
- half a teaspoon salt
- one teaspoon soda
- two cups sour milk
- two beaten eggs
- two tablespoons melted butter

## Directions

1. Sift the flour two or three times with half a teaspoon of salt and one teaspoon of soda.
2. Slowly add two cups of sour milk, two beaten eggs, and two tablespoons of melted butter.
3. Beat well before adding the butter, then mix with the butter and fry on a hot pan.

title: Bliny Polskie (Polish Pancakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart wheat flour
- half quart buckwheat flour
- one good quart sweet milk at lukewarm temperature
- one packet yeast dissolved in milk
- four egg yolks
- four egg whites

## Directions

1. Mix one quart of wheat flour and half quart of buckwheat flour with one good quart of sweet milk at lukewarm temperature.
2. Add one packet of yeast dissolved in milk and add more milk so the batter is thin.
3. Place in a warm location.
4. When it rises too much, stir it and repeat this mixing each time the batter rises too much.
5. It is best to prepare the batter in the morning so it is ready to use in a few hours.
6. Ten minutes before frying the pancakes, beat in four egg yolks.
7. Beat the whites into a foam and also add to the batter, mixing gently.
8. Fry on a small pan, pouring the batter with a spoon so it spreads over the whole pan.
9. When browned on one side, flip with a spatula or knife to the other side.
10. After frying, stack one on top of another on a platter which should stand on the stove or over steam.
11. Grease the pan with clarified butter.
12. Serve these pancakes with melted butter and sour cream.

title: Bliny z Chleba (Bread Pancakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- one and a half cups grated bread
- two cups sweet milk
- one tablespoon butter
- two beaten egg yolks
- one cup flour
- two teaspoons baking powder
- less than one teaspoon salt
- egg whites beaten to stiff peaks

## Directions

1. Pour two cups of sweet milk over one and a half cups of grated bread.
2. Add one tablespoon of butter and cook in a double boiler for fifteen minutes.
3. Pass through a sieve and set in a cool place.
4. When completely cooled, add two beaten egg yolks and one cup of flour sifted with two teaspoons of baking powder and less than one teaspoon of salt.
5. Mix well and add the egg whites beaten to stiff peaks.
6. Fry on a hot pan as usual for pancakes.

title: Bliny z Dyni (Squash Pancakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- squash, peeled and cut into pieces
- one cup cooked squash puree
- one cup hot milk
- one tablespoon butter
- one tablespoon sugar
- half teaspoon salt
- one well-beaten egg
- one cup flour
- one teaspoon baking powder

## Directions

1. Peel, cut into pieces, and cook squash until soft, then pass through a sieve.
2. Take one cup of this puree, place in a bowl, and pour one cup of hot milk over it.
3. Add one tablespoon of butter, one tablespoon of sugar, and half a teaspoon of salt.
4. Set in a cool place.
5. When completely cooled, add one well-beaten egg and one cup of flour sifted with one teaspoon of baking powder.
6. Mix well and fry on a pan until nicely browned on both sides.

title: Bliny z Ryżu (Rice Pancakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- two cups cooked rice
- two tablespoons melted butter
- half cup milk
- half cup wheat flour
- two beaten eggs
- baking powder or soda

## Directions

1. To two cups of cooked rice, add in order: two tablespoons of melted butter, half a cup of milk, half a cup of wheat flour, and two beaten eggs.
2. Beat everything well and fry on a hot pan.
3. You can also use baking powder or soda; if using soda, use sour milk instead of sweet.

title: Bliny z Zielonej Kukurydzy (Fresh Corn Pancakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- corn kernels from fresh corn cobs or canned corn
- two well-beaten eggs
- salt
- pepper
- fat or lard for the pan

## Directions

1. Scrape kernels from fresh corn cobs with a sharp knife, or use canned corn.
2. To a quarter of such a mass, add two well-beaten eggs, salt, and pepper.
3. Stir into smooth batter, place spoonfuls on a lard-greased pan, and fry until golden on low heat.

title: Brandy Cocktail
body:
## Ingredients

- two-thirds quart of brandy
- one-third quart of water
- one liqueur glass of Peyschaud or Angostura Bitters
- two liqueur glasses of syrup

## Directions

1. Mix well, pour into a bottle and cork tightly

title: Brukiew a la Piedmont (Rutabaga à la Piedmont)
body:
## Ingredients

- rutabaga
- butter
- milk or broth
- grated bread
- salt to taste
- pepper to taste

## Directions

1. Cook rutabaga in water until almost tender, then cut into thin slices.
2. Grease a metal plate or shallow cake pan with butter and arrange the rutabaga.
3. Pour a little milk or broth over it, sprinkle lightly with grated bread on top, add salt and pepper to taste and bake in a moderate oven until nicely browned.
4. Transfer to a hot serving platter and serve on the table.

title: Brukiew Duszona (Stewed Rutabaga)
body:
## Ingredients

- rutabaga
- water
- salt
- salt pork or pork
- butter
- flour
- sugar

## Directions

1. Carefully peel rutabaga and cover with cold water
2. Bring to a boil and drain the water
3. Pour in fresh warm water
4. Add a piece of salt pork or pork, salt to taste, and cook until tender
5. When completely tender, dice the rutabaga and the pork
6. Brown one tablespoon butter with one tablespoon flour
7. Dilute with the broth in which the rutabaga was cooked
8. Add the rutabaga and pork
9. Add a little sugar and stew on low heat for a while longer
10. Serve at table

title: Brukiew Duszona (Stewed Rutabaga)
body:
## Ingredients

- rutabaga
- cold water
- fresh warm water
- piece of young lard or pork
- salt to taste
- one tablespoon butter
- one tablespoon flour
- sugar

## Directions

1. Carefully peel the rutabaga and cover with cold water, bring to a boil once, drain the water, pour in fresh warm water, add a piece of young lard or pork, salt to taste and cook until tender.
2. When completely soft, cut into small cubes both the rutabaga and the lard.
3. Brown one tablespoon of butter with one tablespoon of flour on medium heat, dilute with the broth in which the rutabaga was cooked, add the rutabaga and lard, add a little sugar and braise on low heat for a while, then serve on the table.

title: Brukiew na Biało (Rutabaga in White Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- rutabaga
- salt
- one tablespoon butter
- one tablespoon flour
- one cup hot milk
- salt to taste
- pepper to taste

## Directions

1. Peel and cut rutabaga into half-inch cubes and cook until tender in salted water, then drain the water.
2. Blend raw one tablespoon of butter with one tablespoon of flour, dilute slowly by pouring one cup of hot milk, add salt and pepper to taste.
3. Pour this sauce over the rutabaga, mix well and serve on the table.

title: Brukselka na Mleku (Brussels Sprouts in Milk)
body:
## Ingredients

- Brussels sprouts
- water
- salt
- milk
- butter
- bread roll

## Directions

1. Brussels sprouts are small cabbages with heads barely the size of a walnut growing around a tall stalk.
2. Peel and blanch the Brussels sprout heads, throw into boiling water for five minutes, then turn out onto a sieve and drain.
3. When all the water has drained away, place Brussels sprouts in a pan, pour warm milk, salt and cook until tender.
4. Then drain the excess milk, pour the cabbage with butter mixed with a browned roll and serve.

title: Budyń z Włoskiej Kapusty (Italian Cabbage Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 medium head Italian cabbage
- 1/4 pound cooked ham, chopped fine
- 1 large roasted onion, chopped
- 1/4 pound butter
- 1 small cup grated bread
- 4 whole eggs
- salt and pepper to taste
- butter and grated bread for the mold

## Directions

1. Grate a medium head of Italian cabbage, pour water in a pot, bring to boil.
2. Pour off the water and squeeze the cabbage well of water.
3. Chop fine 1/4 pound cooked ham, chop one large roasted onion.
4. Add 1/4 pound butter, 1 small cup grated bread, 4 whole eggs, salt and pepper to taste.
5. Mix everything well with the cabbage.
6. Butter and sprinkle grated bread in a deep baking dish.
7. Fill with the mixture and place in a pot with boiling water, with 2 inches of water after placing the form.
8. Cover the form and cook for about an hour, adding hot water to the pot as it evaporates.
9. When completely tender, serve as a vegetable.

title: Bulion Cebulowy (Onion Broth)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 medium onions
- cold water
- 1 heaping tablespoon fat, preferably beef tallow
- 1 quart boiling water
- 1 teaspoon beef extract
- salt to taste
- pepper to taste
- 3 tablespoons sour cream

## Directions

1. Pour cold water over 3 medium onions and bring to a boil.
2. When they have boiled for five minutes, remove and chop finely.
3. Grate 1 heaping tablespoon of fat, preferably beef tallow, and fry for about ten minutes, constantly stirring.
4. Pour in 1 quart of boiling water, add 1 teaspoon of beef extract, salt and pepper to taste.
5. Cook on low heat for half an hour, then strain.
6. When serving, whiten with 3 tablespoons of sour cream.

title: Buraczki Duszone (Stewed Beets)
body:
## Ingredients

- beets
- butter or lard
- one tablespoon flour
- boiled vinegar to taste
- a few tablespoons of sour cream
- salt to taste

## Directions

1. Trim beets, scald with boiling water, cover with soft water and cook until tender.
2. When completely soft, peel them and cut into thin strips.
3. Place plenty of butter or lard in a pan, and when it heats up, add one tablespoon of flour and bring to a boil together, being careful not to brown it.
4. Salt, add boiled vinegar to taste, add the beets, pour in a few tablespoons of sour cream, cover tightly and braise on low heat for half an hour, then serve on the table.
5. Alternatively, instead of cutting into strips, beets can be grated, but then they should be braised for only about fifteen minutes.

title: Butersznyty Prasowane (Pressed Sandwiches)
body:
## Ingredients

- large loaf of bread
- cooked ham, chopped
- roasted chicken, chopped
- walnuts, chopped
- mayonnaise sauce

## Directions

1. Remove the crust from a large loaf of bread
2. Slice the bread into thin slices
3. Chop cooked ham, roasted chicken, and walnuts
4. Take one slice of bread and layer chopped ham on it
5. Pour mayonnaise sauce over the ham
6. Add a layer of chopped chicken on top
7. Sprinkle a layer of chopped walnuts
8. Place another slice of bread on top
9. Repeat the layers with ham, mayonnaise, chicken, and walnuts until all ingredients are used
10. Top with a final slice of bread
11. Wrap everything in damp cloth
12. Place a heavy weight on top
13. Let it sit for six to eight hours
14. Remove from press and cut crosswise into slices like bread
15. Serve as an appetizer

title: Butersznyty Tatarskie (Tatarian Sandwiches)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup finely chopped cooked ham
- 4 sardines, chopped without bones
- 3 tablespoons finely chopped pickled cucumbers
- 1 teaspoon mustard
- 1 teaspoon tomato sauce
- 1 teaspoon vinegar
- salt and red pepper to taste
- butter
- white bread

## Directions

1. Mix together the ham, sardines, pickled cucumbers, mustard, tomato sauce, vinegar, salt, and red pepper
2. Slice the bread thinly and butter each slice
3. Place the mixture on the butter
4. Cover with another slice of bread
5. Arrange on a platter or tray
6. For best appearance, use white bread in square loaves and cut each sandwich diagonally in half to form triangles

title: Butersznyty z Bananów (Banana Sandwiches)
body:
## Ingredients

- white bread, sliced very thinly, day-old
- mayonnaise sauce
- bananas, sliced lengthwise into thin slices

## Directions

1. Slice white bread very thinly (bread must be day-old)
2. Spread one slice thickly with mayonnaise sauce
3. Place a few thin lengthwise banana slices on top
4. Cover with another slice of day-old bread

title: Butersznyty z Daktyli (Date Sandwiches)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pound dates
- juice from 1/2 lemon
- butter
- bread
- chopped almonds (optional)

## Directions

1. Rub dates into a mass
2. Add lemon juice and mix
3. Cut bread into very thin slices and spread with butter
4. Place a layer of date mixture on the bread and cover with another slice
5. These sandwiches can be varied by sprinkling the date mixture with chopped almonds
6. Raisins may be used instead of dates

title: Butersznyty z Daktyli (Date Sandwiches)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound of dates
- juice of half a lemon
- butter
- bread, sliced thinly
- almonds, chopped (optional)
- raisins (optional)

## Directions

1. Mash the dates into a paste
2. Add the juice from half a lemon and mix well
3. Slice bread into very thin slices and spread with butter
4. Spread a layer of date paste on the bread
5. Cover with another slice of bread
6. Optionally, sprinkle chopped almonds on the date paste before covering
7. Alternatively, raisins may be used instead of dates

title: Butersznyty z Kury i Migdałów (Sandwiches with Chicken and Almonds)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup chicken meat, finely chopped or ground
- 1/4 cup blanched almonds
- sour cream
- butter
- bread

## Directions

1. Chop or grind very finely the chicken meat with the blanched almonds
2. Add sour cream and mix into a smooth mass
3. Spread on buttered bread and cover with another slice
4. Bread should be sliced very thinly

title: Butersznyty z Marynowanego Łososia (Sandwiches with Marinated Salmon)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup marinated salmon
- 1 cup milk
- 1 tablespoon butter
- 1 tablespoon flour
- salt and pepper to taste
- white bread

## Directions

1. Remove bones from the salmon
2. Cook with the milk
3. Add salt and pepper to taste
4. Rub together butter and flour into a smooth mass and thicken the simmering fish with this mixture
5. Toast several slices of white bread and spread with butter
6. Place the hot fish mixture on the toast

title: Butersznyty z Orzechów (Nut Sandwiches)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pound walnuts
- mayonnaise sauce
- butter
- bread
- lettuce leaves

## Directions

1. Rub walnuts into a mass
2. Mix with mayonnaise sauce, stirring until a thick mass is obtained
3. Spread on lettuce leaves and place on buttered bread

title: Butersznyty z Resztek (Leftover Meat Sandwiches)
body:
## Ingredients

- leftover meat, poultry, or fish, finely chopped
- salt
- pepper
- one tablespoon butter
- one tablespoon cream
- lemon juice
- mustard, a little
- bread, rye or white

## Directions

1. Chop any leftover meat, poultry, or fish very finely
2. Add salt and pepper to taste
3. Mix in one tablespoon butter and one tablespoon cream
4. Mix well
5. Add lemon juice
6. Add a little mustard
7. Spread butter on rye or white bread
8. Spread the mixture on the bread

title: Butersznyty z Sałaty (Lettuce Sandwiches)
body:
## Ingredients

- bread
- butter
- small lettuce leaves
- sauce made from egg yolk

## Directions

1. Cut bread into very thin slices and spread with butter
2. Rinse small lettuce leaves carefully
3. Place on bread, pour sauce over, and cover with another slice of bread

title: Butersznyty z Sałaty (Lettuce Sandwiches)
body:
## Ingredients

- bread, sliced thinly
- butter
- small lettuce leaves
- one egg yolk, raw or hard-boiled
- vinegar
- olive oil
- salt
- white pepper

## Directions

1. Slice bread thinly and spread with butter
2. Wash small lettuce leaves carefully
3. Place lettuce on the bread
4. Pour sauce over the lettuce
5. Cover with another slice of bread
6. For the sauce: mash one egg yolk (raw or hard-boiled) with vinegar and olive oil
7. Add salt and a pinch of white pepper to the sauce

title: Butersznyty z Selerów (Celery Sandwiches)
body:
## Ingredients

- young celery, finely chopped
- mayonnaise sauce
- butter
- bread, sliced very thinly

## Directions

1. Chop young celery finely
2. Mix with mayonnaise sauce
3. Spread butter on very thinly sliced bread
4. Spread the celery and mayonnaise mixture on the bread
5. Cover with another slice of bread

title: Butersznyty z Selerów i Sera (Celery and Cheese Sandwiches)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a cup heavy cream
- grated cheese (preferably Parmesan)
- celery stalks, white, finely chopped
- bread

## Directions

1. Whip half a cup of heavy cream
2. Add enough grated cheese (preferably Parmesan) to make a thick paste
3. Chop celery stalks very finely
4. Spread the cream and cheese mixture on bread
5. Sprinkle a generous amount of chopped celery on top
6. Prepare these sandwiches just before serving, as they lose flavor quickly

title: Butersznyty z Sera i Jaj (Sandwiches with Cheese and Eggs)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 hard-boiled egg yolks
- 1/4 teaspoon salt
- pepper
- 1 teaspoon mustard
- 1/2 pound grated cheese
- 3 tablespoons vinegar
- bread

## Directions

1. Rub the hard-boiled egg yolks into a mass in a small bowl
2. Add in order, mixing well after each addition: salt, pepper, mustard, grated cheese, and vinegar
3. Mix well and spread on bread

title: Butersznyty z Serem i Orzechami (Cheese and Walnut Sandwiches)
body:
## Ingredients

- Swiss cheese, grated
- walnuts, finely chopped, equal amount to cheese
- salt
- red pepper
- butter
- bread

## Directions

1. Grate Swiss cheese
2. Mix with an equal amount of finely chopped walnuts
3. Add salt and red pepper to taste
4. Mix well
5. Spread butter on bread
6. Spread the cheese and walnut mixture on the bread

title: Butersznyty z Siekanych Sardynek (Sandwiches with Chopped Sardines)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pound box of sardines
- 2 eggs
- mustard
- 1 teaspoon grated horseradish
- white bread

## Directions

1. Remove sardines from the box, skin and bone them, and chop the meat finely
2. Hard boil two eggs and chop finely
3. Add mustard and grated horseradish
4. Mix with the sardines
5. Spread on thin slices of white bread, cover with another slice, and arrange on a platter or tray

title: Butersznyty z Szynki i Jaj (Ham and Egg Sandwiches)
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup cooked ham, finely chopped
- three eggs
- butter
- grated mustard, a little
- red pepper
- bread, rye or white

## Directions

1. Chop cooked ham very finely into a cup measure
2. Hard-boil three eggs for fifteen minutes
3. Chop the hard-boiled eggs finely
4. Mix the chopped eggs with the chopped ham
5. Add a little grated mustard and red pepper
6. Spread butter on bread slices
7. Spread the ham and egg mixture on the bread
8. Cover with another slice of bread

title: Butersznyty z Twarogiem i Oliwkami (Curd Cheese and Olive Sandwiches)
body:
## Ingredients

- twaróg (curd cheese)
- cream, a little
- olives, finely chopped
- rye bread, sliced
- butter
- chives (optional)
- parsley (optional)
- dill (optional)

## Directions

1. Mash the twaróg with a little cream into a smooth paste
2. Add finely chopped olives and mix
3. Spread butter on rye bread slices
4. Spread the curd cheese and olive mixture on the bread
5. Alternatively, use chives, parsley, or dill instead of olives

title: Butersznyty ze Szpiku (Marrow Sandwiches)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef marrow from bones
- 1 teaspoon finely chopped green parsley
- 1 teaspoon lemon juice
- 1/2 teaspoon salt
- red pepper
- 1/4 teaspoon onion juice
- white bread

## Directions

1. Remove marrow from beef bones and cut into inch pieces
2. Cook in a quart of salted water for 1.5 to 2 minutes
3. Place finely chopped parsley, lemon juice, salt, red pepper, and onion juice in a hot small bowl
4. Remove cooked marrow with a slotted spoon and place in the bowl with the additions and mix well
5. Have toast made from white bread and spread the marrow mixture on the hot toast immediately
6. Serve immediately as these sandwiches are only good when hot

title: Bułeczki do Herbaty (Tea Rolls)
body:
## Ingredients

- 0.5 quart warm milk
- 1 cake of compressed yeast
- flour as needed
- 1 egg
- 0.5 cup butter
- sugar to taste
- salt to taste

## Directions

1. Mix 0.5 quart of warm milk, 1 cake of compressed yeast, and enough flour (previously warmed) to make fairly soft dough.
2. Set to rise, then add 1 egg, 0.5 cup butter, some sugar, and salt and mix well.
3. Turn out onto a board, roll out, and cut flat rolls.
4. Dip the rolls in melted butter, fold in half, and place in the oven for twenty minutes.

title: Bułeczki Graham (Graham Rolls)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 cups graham flour
- 3 teaspoons baking powder
- 1 tablespoon melted butter
- 1 well-beaten egg
- 0.5 cup sugar
- 0.5 teaspoon salt
- sweet milk as needed

## Directions

1. Mix 3 cups graham flour, 3 teaspoons baking powder, 1 tablespoon melted butter, 1 well-beaten egg, 0.5 cup sugar, and 0.5 teaspoon salt.
2. Add enough sweet milk to make fairly thin dough.
3. Pour into small round forms called 'gem pans' and bake in a hot oven.

title: Bułeczki Hiszpańskie (Spanish Rolls)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cups sugar
- 0.5 cup butter
- 1 cup sweet milk
- 2.5 cups wheat flour
- 2 teaspoons baking powder
- 1 teaspoon ground cloves
- 2 teaspoons ground cinnamon
- nutmeg to taste

## Directions

1. Mix 2 cups sugar with 0.5 cup butter, 1 cup sweet milk, 2.5 cups wheat flour, 2 teaspoons baking powder, 1 teaspoon ground cloves, 2 teaspoons ground cinnamon, and a little nutmeg.
2. Make into dough, shape into rolls, and bake in a hot oven.

title: Bułeczki Kolonialne (Colonial Rolls)
body:
## Ingredients

- 0.5 quart sweet milk
- 1 tablespoon butter
- 1 tablespoon sugar
- pinch of salt
- 1 quart flour
- 1 cake of compressed yeast

## Directions

1. Heat 0.5 quart of sweet milk enough to dissolve 1 tablespoon of butter in it.
2. After the butter dissolves, add 1 tablespoon of sugar and a pinch of salt and cool slightly.
3. When the milk reaches lukewarm temperature, add 1 quart of flour and 1 cake of compressed yeast.
4. Set in a warm place for the dough to rise.
5. When the dough becomes light, knead it a little and let it rise again.
6. Shape into small rolls, brush the top with egg white, and bake in a moderately hot oven.

title: Bułeczki Kukurydzane (Corn Rolls)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eggs
- 1 tablespoon melted butter
- 1 tablespoon cornmeal (com meal)
- 1 teaspoon sugar (preferably brown)
- 1 heaping tablespoon wheat flour
- 0.5 teaspoon baking powder
- 1 cup milk

## Directions

1. Beat 2 whole eggs well, add 1 tablespoon melted butter, 1 tablespoon cornmeal, 1 teaspoon sugar, and 1 heaping tablespoon wheat flour mixed with 0.5 teaspoon baking powder and 1 cup milk.
2. Mix everything well, place in small buttered metal forms, and bake in a hot oven.

title: Bułeczki Kukurydzane - Drugi Przepis (Corn Rolls - Second Recipe)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup cornmeal
- 0.5 cup wheat flour
- 1.5 teaspoons baking powder
- 1 egg
- 1.5 cups sweet milk

## Directions

1. Mix 1 cup cornmeal and 0.5 cup wheat flour and add 1.5 teaspoons baking powder and sift together to mix well.
2. Beat 1 egg, add 1.5 cups sweet milk and mix all into dough.
3. Place in forms and bake in a hot oven.

title: Bułeczki na Śniadanie (Breakfast Rolls)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup cornmeal (com meal)
- 1.5 cups wheat flour
- 2 teaspoons baking powder
- 2 tablespoons butter
- 3 tablespoons sugar
- 3 eggs
- 0.5 pint milk

## Directions

1. Mix together cornmeal, wheat flour, and baking powder and sift through a sieve several times to mix well.
2. Cream 2 tablespoons of butter with 3 tablespoons of sugar, then add 3 well-beaten eggs.
3. Add the flour mixture and then pour in 0.5 pint of milk, mixing into smooth dough.
4. Fill small metal forms with the dough and bake in a hot oven.
5. Small metal forms called 'muffin tins' are best for this purpose, as larger forms will not rise as well or bake evenly.

title: Bułeczki na Śniadanie (Breakfast Rolls)
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup of corn meal
- one and a half cups of wheat flour
- two teaspoons of baking powder
- two tablespoons of butter
- flour for kneading

## Directions

1. Mix together one cup of corn meal, one and a half cups of wheat flour and two teaspoons of baking powder and sift the whole thing several times through a sieve to mix thoroughly
2. Rub two tablespoons of butter together
3. continued on next page

title: Bułeczki Wiedeńskie (Viennese Rolls)
body:
## Ingredients

- two cups of milk
- one tablet of yeast
- half a cup of warm water
- three cups of sifted flour
- one well-beaten egg
- quarter cup of melted lard or butter
- two tablespoons of sugar
- one teaspoon of salt
- three more cups of sifted flour

## Directions

1. Heat two cups of milk and cool to lukewarm temperature, then add one tablet of yeast previously dissolved in half a cup of warm water and add three cups of sifted flour
2. Beat with a wooden spoon for about ten minutes until the mixture is uniform and smooth, then cover and set in a warm place
3. When well risen, add one well-beaten egg, quarter cup of melted lard or butter, two tablespoons of sugar and one teaspoon of salt
4. Gradually add three more cups of sifted flour while mixing, turn out onto a work surface and knead for at least fifteen minutes
5. Cover again and set to allow the dough to rise; when risen to double volume, shape into rolls or croissants of any desired form and set to rise again
6. Bake in a hot oven for about fifteen minutes; before removing from the oven, brush the top with egg white

title: Bułeczki z Bani (Squash Rolls)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup cooked and mashed squash
- 0.75 cup sugar
- 0.5 cup butter
- 1.5 cups sweet milk
- 0.5 cake of compressed yeast

## Directions

1. Take 1 cup of cooked and mashed squash and add 0.75 cup sugar, 0.5 cup butter, 1.5 cups sweet milk, and 0.5 cake of compressed yeast.
2. Set to rise, then bake as usual.
3. To have these rolls for dinner, the dough should be started in the morning.

title: Bułeczki z Kartofli (Potato Rolls)
body:
## Ingredients

- three medium potatoes
- one cup of boiled milk
- one tablespoon of lard
- one teaspoon of butter
- one tablespoon of sugar
- one teaspoon of salt
- one tablet of yeast
- warm water
- two well-beaten eggs
- flour

## Directions

1. Cook three medium potatoes and mash into a puree, then add one cup of boiled milk, one tablespoon of lard, one teaspoon of butter, one tablespoon of sugar and one teaspoon of salt and set to cool
2. When cooled to lukewarm temperature, add one tablet of yeast dissolved in warm water, two well-beaten eggs and enough flour to make a rather thin dough
3. Set in a warm place for one hour to rise, then add more flour and knead properly
4. Set again to allow the dough to rise; when well risen, spread on a work surface, roll out and cut small round rolls
5. Dip the rolls in melted butter and arrange on a baking sheet one inch apart from each other
6. Cover again and set in a warm place until well risen, then place in a hot oven for ten minutes

title: Bułeczki z Ryżu (Rice Rolls)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.5 cups wheat flour
- 2 teaspoons baking powder
- 1 teaspoon sugar
- pinch of salt
- 0.5 cup softly cooked cold rice
- 1 heaping tablespoon butter
- 1 beaten egg

## Directions

1. Mix 1.5 cups wheat flour, 2 teaspoons baking powder, 1 teaspoon sugar, and a pinch of salt and sift through a sieve to mix well.
2. Take 0.5 cup of softly cooked cold rice and mix with 1 heaping tablespoon of butter (previously melted on the fire).
3. Add 1 beaten egg and mix all with the sifted flour into smooth dough.
4. Shape into small rolls and bake in the oven, preferably in small metal forms, for about half an hour.

title: Catsup
body:
## Ingredients

- tomatoes
- half a quart of vinegar per gallon of tomato liquid
- two tablespoons of salt
- two tablespoons of sugar
- two tablespoons of ground mustard
- one tablespoon of Jamaican pepper (allspice)
- one tablespoon of cinnamon
- one teaspoon of black pepper
- one teaspoon of cloves
- three-quarters teaspoon of red pepper

## Directions

1. Scald tomatoes with boiling water, peel and then cook until soft, then press through a sieve
2. For one gallon of thus obtained liquid add half a quart of vinegar, two tablespoons of salt, two tablespoons of sugar, two tablespoons of ground mustard, one tablespoon of Jamaican pepper (allspice), one tablespoon of cinnamon, one teaspoon of black pepper, one teaspoon of cloves, three-quarters teaspoon of red pepper; all these spices must naturally be ground
3. Mix well and cook on very low heat for one hour, then pour into bottles and cork tightly

title: Catsup (Second Method)
body:
## Ingredients

- one bushel of tomatoes
- half quart of salt
- half tablespoon of cloves
- one tablespoon of Jamaican pepper (allspice)
- one-sixth teaspoon of red pepper (cayenne)

## Directions

1. Add half quart of salt to one bushel of tomatoes and cook until completely soft, then press through loose sieve
2. To the obtained mass add half tablespoon of cloves, one tablespoon of Jamaican pepper (allspice), one-sixth teaspoon of red pepper (cayenne), pour into a pot and cook until only ten quarts remain
3. Pour into bottles while hot, pour some melted paraffin on top and then cork

title: Cebula w Białym Sosie (Onions in White Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart small white onions
- water
- 1 teaspoon salt
- 1 tablespoon butter
- 1/2 tablespoon flour
- 1/2 quart hot milk
- salt and pepper to taste

## Directions

1. Peel 1 quart of small white onions, place in a pot and cover with boiling water.
2. Cook until almost tender, then add 1 teaspoon salt, cook a few minutes longer and drain on a colander.
3. Melt 1 tablespoon butter in a pot, thicken with 1/2 tablespoon flour and fry for a few minutes, being careful not to brown the flour.
4. Pour in 1/2 quart hot milk, add salt and pepper to taste and bring to boil for about two minutes.
5. Lay hot onions on a plate and pour this sauce over them.

title: Cherry Liqueur (Nalewka Wiśniowa)
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart of ripe cherries
- one quart of strong brandy
- half a pound of sugar or more to taste

## Directions

1. Crush one quart of ripe cherries without damaging the pits
2. Pour one quart of strong brandy over them and set in a cool place for three days
3. After this time, strain and add half a pound of sugar or more to taste
4. When the sugar dissolves, pour into bottles and cork well

title: Chleb Brunatny Bostoński Sposób 1 (Boston Brown Bread Method 1)
body:
## Ingredients

- two cups of rye flour
- one cup of corn meal
- one teaspoon of salt
- one teaspoon of soda
- two cups of sour milk
- one-third cup of molasses

## Directions

1. Mix together two cups of rye flour, one cup of corn meal, one teaspoon of salt and one teaspoon of soda
2. Add two cups of sour milk and one-third cup of molasses
3. Mix into smooth dough, then cook on steam in a butter-greased form for three hours
4. Place in a hot oven for twenty minutes to brown

title: Chleb Brunatny Bostoński Sposób 2 (Boston Brown Bread Method 2)
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup of wheat flour
- half a cup of corn meal
- half a cup of graham flour
- one teaspoon of salt
- one teaspoon of soda
- one tablespoon of lard or butter
- half a cup of raisins
- half a cup of syrup
- one cup of sour milk

## Directions

1. Mix one cup of wheat flour, half a cup of corn meal, half a cup of graham flour, one teaspoon of salt, one teaspoon of soda, one tablespoon of lard or butter, half a cup of raisins and half a cup of syrup with one cup of sour milk into dough
2. Place in a form and bake for one hour

title: Chleb Brunatny No. 1 (Brown Bread No. 1)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a cup of brown sugar
- one cup of wheat flour
- one heaping cup of graham flour
- one teaspoon of salt
- two heaping teaspoons of soda
- two cups of sour milk
- one tablespoon of molasses
- a handful of raisins

## Directions

1. Mix together the brown sugar, wheat flour, graham flour, salt and soda
2. Add two cups of sour milk, one tablespoon of molasses and a handful of raisins
3. Mix into dough and place in a moderately hot oven for one hour

title: Chleb Brunatny No. 2 (Brown Bread No. 2)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a cup of wheat flour
- one and a half cups of graham flour
- one cup of corn meal
- two teaspoons of salt
- two teaspoons of soda
- one cup of sour milk
- one cup of sweet milk
- one cup of molasses

## Directions

1. Mix together the wheat flour, graham flour, corn meal, salt and soda
2. Add one cup of sour milk, one cup of sweet milk and one cup of molasses
3. Mix into dough, cook on steam for two and a half hours
4. Place in a hot oven for fifteen to twenty minutes to brown

title: Chleb Francuski (French Bread)
body:
## Ingredients

- one quarter cup of boiled and cooled water
- one tablet of pressed yeast
- three quarters cup of sifted wheat flour
- half quart of boiled water cooled to lukewarm, about 82 to 84 degrees Fahrenheit
- one teaspoon of salt
- five cups of sifted flour

## Directions

1. Place one quarter cup of boiled and cooled water in a bowl and dissolve one tablet of pressed yeast, mixing well
2. Add about three quarters cup of sifted wheat flour to form a stiff dough
3. Knead well and form into a ball
4. Make cuts across the top of the ball with a knife on both sides
5. Drop into half quart of boiled water cooled to lukewarm, about 82 to 84 degrees Fahrenheit
6. Initially the dough will sink, but in fifteen or twenty minutes it will float to the surface
7. Pour the water with the dough into a bowl and add one teaspoon of salt and five cups of sifted flour
8. Mix thoroughly; the dough should be stiff enough for kneading; if too soft, add more flour
9. Turn onto a floured surface and knead for at least fifteen minutes
10. Cover in a bowl and place in a warm location for the dough to rise
11. When risen to double volume, carefully turn onto the surface without flipping, and cut in half
12. Form two long loaves and place in special long narrow forms (French bread pans)
13. Make a groove down the center of each loaf with a round stick or finger
14. Cover and let rise again
15. Bake in a moderately hot oven for half an hour
16. Just before baking is complete, remove the loaves and brush the top with egg white beaten with one tablespoon of cold water, then return to the oven for a smooth, hard crust; or brush with cornstarch mixed with cold water and then cooked, for a soft crust

title: Chleb na Rozczynie (Bread Made with Starter)
body:
## Ingredients

- half tablet of pressed yeast
- half quart of lukewarm water
- one quart of flour
- half quart of water
- two teaspoons of salt
- two tablespoons of sugar
- two tablespoons of lard
- two quarts of flour

## Directions

1. Dissolve half tablet of pressed yeast in half quart of lukewarm water
2. Add one quart of flour and mix to a uniform mass
3. Make this starter late in the evening and place in a warm location
4. The next morning, add half quart of water, two teaspoons of salt, two tablespoons of sugar, two tablespoons of lard and two quarts of flour
5. Mix everything thoroughly and place in a warm location for the dough to rise
6. After rising, knead the dough, then proceed as with white bread above, but the oven should have medium temperature
7. Both the starter and dough should have a temperature of 80 to 84 degrees Fahrenheit

title: Chleb Pszenny Razowy (Whole Wheat Bread)
body:
## Ingredients

- five cups of whole wheat flour
- two cups of warm water
- one tablet of pressed yeast
- two tablespoons of molasses
- half teaspoon of salt
- one tablespoon of butter or lard

## Directions

1. Mix five cups of whole wheat flour, two cups of warm water, one tablet of pressed yeast, two tablespoons of molasses, half teaspoon of salt, and one tablespoon of butter or lard into a dough stiff enough to knead
2. Turn onto a floured surface and knead for about fifteen minutes
3. Cover and place in a warm location to rise
4. Knead again, divide into loaves, let rise a second time
5. Bake in a medium oven for about forty-five minutes
6. This bread can be varied by adding raisins, chopped walnuts, a portion of sifted flour, or similar additions

title: Chleb z Rodzenkami (Bread with Raisins)
body:
## Ingredients

- half ounce or one tablet of yeast
- half quart of milk or water
- one teaspoon of salt
- two tablespoons of sugar
- two tablespoons of lard
- one and one half quarts of sifted flour
- one and one half cups of seedless raisins

## Directions

1. Dissolve half ounce or one tablet of yeast in half quart of milk or water
2. Add one teaspoon of salt, two tablespoons of sugar, two tablespoons of lard, and one and one half quarts of sifted flour mixed with one and one half cups of seedless raisins
3. The temperature of the prepared dough should be 82 to 84 degrees Fahrenheit
4. Cover the dough and let it rise well
5. Divide into three small loaves and let rise
6. Bake in a moderately hot oven like regular bread

title: Chleb Żytni (Rye Bread)
body:
## Ingredients

- one tablet of yeast
- half quart of lukewarm water
- one quart of rye flour
- half quart of warm water
- half quart of sifted rye flour
- one and one half quarts of wheat flour
- two teaspoons of salt

## Directions

1. Dissolve one tablet of yeast in half quart of lukewarm water
2. Add one quart of rye flour and place in a warm location
3. When the mixture has risen enough to begin falling, add half quart of warm water, half quart of sifted rye flour, one and one half quarts of wheat flour and two teaspoons of salt

title: Chleb Żytni (Rye Bread)
body:
## Ingredients

- one tablet of yeast
- half a quart of warm water
- one quart of rye flour
- half a quart of warm water
- half a quart of sifted rye flour
- one and a half quarts of wheat flour
- two teaspoons of salt

## Directions

1. Dissolve one tablet of yeast in half a quart of warm water, add one quart of rye flour and set in a warm place
2. When it has risen so that it begins to fall, add half a quart of warm water, half a quart of sifted rye flour, one and a half quarts of wheat flour and two teaspoons of salt, mix everything together and let it stand for about twenty minutes
3. Divide into loaves and let them rise in forms by one-third of their volume, which will take about 45 minutes
4. Bake in a moderately hot oven; when removing from the oven, brush the top with water

title: Chow Chow (Mixed Vegetable Pickle)
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart of small cucumbers
- one quart of large cucumbers
- one quart of small onions
- two large cauliflowers
- four large green pepper pods
- four quarts of water
- half a quart of salt
- one tablespoon of ground mustard
- half a cup of flour
- one teaspoon of yellow ginger (turmeric powder)
- cold vinegar
- sugar
- red pepper (optional)

## Directions

1. Slice large cucumbers without peeling
2. Break cauliflowers into small pieces and chop green pepper pods finely
3. Pour four quarts of water in which half a quart of salt has been dissolved over all vegetables
4. Let stand for twenty-four hours
5. After twenty-four hours, put on fire and when it reaches boiling temperature, pour everything onto a colander or sieve to drain the water
6. Mix one tablespoon of ground mustard with half a cup of flour
7. Add one teaspoon of yellow ginger (turmeric powder) and enough cold vinegar to obtain a thin, smooth paste
8. Add almost one cup of sugar and enough vinegar so that everything together makes two quarts
9. Cook until thick, stirring constantly
10. Remove from fire and add the cucumbers and mix well
11. Put on fire again and let everything heat well
12. If desired, add red pepper to make the pickle sharper

title: Christmas Plum Pudding (English Christmas Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- one and a half cups of grated bread
- two ounces of suet
- half a pound of flour
- two ounces of finely chopped almonds
- two pounds of finely chopped beef suet
- two grated nutmegs
- two pounds of common raisins
- two pounds of currants
- juice and grated zest of one lemon
- two pounds of sugar
- one teaspoon of salt
- two ounces of candied lemon peel fried in sugar
- sixteen eggs
- milk as needed to make a thick mass

## Directions

1. Mix the above ingredients in the order given and set in a cool place overnight
2. The next morning place in greased molds and steam for at least twelve hours
3. Before serving steam for another two hours
4. When serving pour two spoonfuls of brandy over it and ignite
5. Serve with the following sauce: cream half a cup of butter, add one cup of sugar, beat for fifteen minutes, then beat in two eggs and whip to a foam, just before serving add a quarter cup of hot water, beat strongly again, add two glasses of wine and half a teaspoon of vanilla extract
6. This pudding can be kept for a very long time without spoiling

title: Chłodnik Białoruski (Belarusian Cold Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- several red beets
- 3 eggs
- 1 four-inch fresh cucumber
- 1 tablespoon of chopped chives
- 1 tablespoon of chopped dill
- 1 cup of sour cream
- 2 tablespoons of vinegar
- 1 quart of cold water
- salt and pepper to taste
- crayfish tails (optional)
- ice

## Directions

1. Cook several red beets, peel, and chop finely
2. Hard-boil 3 eggs and cut each into 8 pieces
3. Chop 1 four-inch fresh cucumber into small cubes
4. Take 1 tablespoon of chopped chives and 1 tablespoon of chopped dill
5. Combine with 1 cup of sour cream and 2 tablespoons of vinegar
6. Add 1 quart of cold water and mix everything well
7. Add salt and pepper to taste
8. Place on ice where it should stay until serving
9. When serving, place a piece of ice the size of an egg on each plate and pour the soup over it
10. The soup becomes more delicious with the addition of crayfish tails
11. If chives are not available, use finely chopped onion

title: Chłodnik Polski (Polish Cold Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef or veal broth
- rye kvass to taste
- beet greens with dill
- 1/2 quart of sour cream
- 1/2 quart of broth
- 3 hard-boiled eggs
- cucumbers cut into cubes
- some crayfish tails
- ice

## Directions

1. Cook a strong beef or veal broth and strain it
2. Add rye kvass until the flavor is vinegary
3. Cook some beet greens with dill separately, chop finely, and place in a bowl
4. Pour 1/2 quart of sour cream over them
5. Mix together
6. Slowly pour in 1/2 quart of broth, constantly stirring
