7. Add 3 hard-boiled eggs cut into quarters, cucumbers cut into cubes, and some crayfish tails
8. Place the bowl on ice
9. When thoroughly chilled, serve on a plate with a piece of ice
10. Instead of rye kvass, you can use cucumber brine boiled briefly with broth, or sorrel juice

title: Chłodnik Rabarbarowy (Rhubarb Cold Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- a few rhubarb stalks
- 1 quart of water
- several cooked red beets
- 1 large pickled cucumber
- 3 hard-boiled eggs
- a few tablespoons of crayfish meat
- sour cream
- chopped dill
- salt and pepper to taste
- ice

## Directions

1. Chop a few rhubarb stalks and cook in 1 quart of water for about 1/2 hour
2. Strain the water into a bowl
3. Add several cooked red beets, finely chopped
4. Add 1 large pickled cucumber cut into cubes
5. Add 3 hard-boiled eggs cut into quarters
6. Add a few tablespoons of crayfish meat and sour cream
7. Add chopped dill, salt, and pepper to taste
8. Place on ice
9. Serve when very cold; you can also add a piece of ice to the bowl just before serving

title: Chłodnik Różowy (Pink Beet Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart of pickle juice from cucumbers
- water
- fresh dill, chopped
- a couple of red beets
- 2 pickled cucumbers
- 3 hard-boiled eggs
- several crayfish tails
- 1 cup sour cream
- ice

## Directions

1. Take 1 quart of pickle juice from cucumbers and add water if it is too sour. Add some chopped fresh dill and bring to a boil, then remove from heat.
2. Separately cook a couple of red beets, peel them, cut into thin strips and add to the warm pickle juice so it takes on color and sweetness.
3. Peel and slice 2 pickled cucumbers into slices; cut 3 hard-boiled eggs into quarters.
4. Add several crayfish tails. Whip 1 cup of sour cream well.
5. Mix the sour cream thoroughly with the soup first, then add the cucumbers, eggs and crayfish tails and place on ice. Serve on the table when completely cooled; you can place a piece of ice on each plate.
6. Note: This soup can also be served hot as borscht, in which case after adding the beets bring to a boil, remove from heat, then add sour cream and other additions and do not cook further, but serve immediately on the table.

title: Chłodnik Różowy (Pink Cold Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart of cucumber brine
- water (if too sour)
- chopped dill
- a few red beets
- 2 pickled cucumbers
- 3 hard-boiled eggs
- several crayfish tails
- sour cream

## Directions

1. Take 1 quart of cucumber brine and add water if it is too sour
2. Add chopped dill and boil, then remove from heat
3. Cook a few red beets separately, peel, and cut into thin strips
4. Add to the warm brine so it absorbs color and sweetness
5. Peel and slice 2 pickled cucumbers
6. Cut 3 hard-boiled eggs into quarters
7. Add several crayfish tails
8. Beat 1 cup of sour cream well
9. Combine all ingredients

title: Chłodnik z Botwinki (Cold Soup with Beet Greens)
body:
## Ingredients

- sour cream
- cucumber kvass
- beet greens from several beets
- 1 tablespoon of vinegar
- water
- several eggs
- 1 fresh cucumber
- 1 pickled cucumber
- cold roasted veal
- chives
- dill
- salt and pepper to taste
- ice

## Directions

1. Mix equal parts of sour cream and cucumber kvass
2. Cook beet greens from several beets in water with 1 tablespoon of vinegar and chop
3. Add to the sour cream mixture and blend
4. Add several hard-boiled eggs cut into quarters
5. Add 1 fresh and 1 pickled cucumber cut into cubes
6. Add some finely chopped cold roasted veal
7. Season with salt and pepper
8. Finally, chop some chives and dill and add to the soup
9. Place on ice where it should stay until serving
10. Serve when thoroughly chilled

title: Ciastka Cukrowe (Sugar Cookies)
body:
## Ingredients

- two eggs
- two cups of sugar
- one cup of butter mixed half with lard
- one cup of sour milk
- one teaspoon of soda
- two teaspoons of baking powder
- some extract for flavoring
- flour

## Directions

1. Combine all ingredients with enough flour so that the dough can be rolled
2. Roll out to half an inch thickness
3. Bake on a baking sheet

title: Ciastka Cynamonowe (Cinnamon Cakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- one-half cup butter
- one-half cup lard
- one heaping tablespoon sugar
- one egg white beaten to stiff peaks
- three-quarters cup sweet milk
- one heaping teaspoon baking powder
- flour as needed
- sugar and cinnamon for topping

## Directions

1. Mix one-half cup butter, one-half cup lard, one heaping tablespoon sugar, one egg white beaten to stiff peaks, three-quarters cup sweet milk, one heaping teaspoon baking powder, and enough flour to make a rollable dough
2. Mix thoroughly
3. Turn out onto a work surface and roll thin
4. Sprinkle thickly with sugar mixed with cinnamon
5. Cut into strips four inches long and two and one-half inches wide
6. Roll to form round cylinders
7. Arrange on a baking sheet and bake in a hot oven

title: Ciastka Czekoladowe (Chocolate Cookies)
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup of brown sugar
- half cup of melted butter
- one egg
- one and a half cups of flour
- half teaspoon of soda
- half cup of milk
- one cup of chopped currants and nuts
- three ounces of chocolate

## Directions

1. Cream together the sugar and melted butter
2. Add one egg
3. Sift together the flour with half a teaspoon of soda
4. Add the flour mixture and milk to the creamed mixture
5. Add one cup of chopped currants and nuts
6. Melt three ounces of chocolate and add it after mixing the other ingredients
7. Drop by spoonfuls of round cookies onto a greased baking sheet
8. Bake in a slow oven

title: Ciastka Imbierowe (Ginger Cookies)
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup of molasses
- one cup of brown sugar
- one cup of well-salted lard or lard mixed half with butter
- two well-beaten eggs
- half cup of cold tea
- one teaspoon of soda dissolved in the tea
- half teaspoon of ginger
- half teaspoon of cinnamon
- flour

## Directions

1. Combine molasses, brown sugar and lard or lard mixture
2. Add two well-beaten eggs
3. Add half cup of cold tea with one teaspoon of soda dissolved in it
4. Add half teaspoon of ginger and half teaspoon of cinnamon
5. Add enough flour to obtain soft dough
6. Make small round cookies
7. Bake in a moderately hot oven

title: Ciastka Kokosowe (Coconut Cookies)
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup of butter
- three cups of sugar
- eight beaten eggs
- one pound of shredded coconut
- a little salt
- two teaspoons of baking powder
- two and a half cups of flour

## Directions

1. Make dough from all ingredients
2. Drop by teaspoonful onto a greased baking sheet
3. Bake

title: Ciastka na Śmietanie (Sour Cream Cookies)
body:
## Ingredients

- two cups of sugar
- one cup of butter
- one cup of sour cream
- three eggs
- one teaspoon of soda
- wheat flour
- salt

## Directions

1. First cream butter with sugar
2. Then add egg yolks and sour cream, beaten together
3. At the end add the foam from the egg whites, a little salt and enough wheat flour to make a soft dough
4. Roll out to half an inch thickness
5. Cut out round cookies
6. Bake on a baking sheet

title: Ciastka Orzechowe (Nut Cookies)
body:
## Ingredients

- four cups of flour
- two cups of sugar
- one cup of butter
- one cup of chopped nuts
- one teaspoon of baking powder
- extract for flavoring

## Directions

1. Make dough from all ingredients, adding some extract for flavoring
2. Drop onto baking sheet by teaspoonful
3. Place in oven until they brown well

title: Ciastka Owsiane (Oatmeal Cookies)
body:
## Ingredients

- two and a half cups of oatmeal
- one cup of sugar
- two eggs
- two tablespoons of melted butter
- one teaspoon of baking powder
- a little salt
- vanilla extract

## Directions

1. Mix oatmeal, sugar, two eggs, two tablespoons of melted butter, one teaspoon of baking powder, a little salt and vanilla extract into dough
2. Drop small spoonfuls onto a greased baking sheet two inches apart
3. Shape into pyramids with fingers
4. Bake

title: Ciastka z Orzeszków Ziemnych (Peanut Cookies)
body:
## Ingredients

- three tablespoons of butter
- one cup of sugar
- two eggs
- four tablespoons of milk
- one cup of finely chopped peanuts
- one and a half cups of flour
- two teaspoons of baking powder
- one teaspoon of lemon juice
- split peanuts for topping

## Directions

1. Cream butter with sugar
2. Add two eggs
3. Add four tablespoons of milk
4. Add one cup of finely chopped peanuts
5. Sift together one and a half cups of flour with two teaspoons of baking powder and mix in
6. Add one teaspoon of lemon juice
7. Make dough and drop by teaspoonful onto a greased baking sheet in the form of cookies
8. Place one split peanut on top of each cookie
9. Bake in the oven for twelve to fifteen minutes

title: Ciasto Domowe (Home Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- two cups dried apples
- one cup molasses
- one cup sugar
- one cup butter
- one cup sweet milk
- one cup raisins
- one cup currants
- two eggs
- four cups flour
- one heaping teaspoon soda
- one teaspoon cinnamon
- one teaspoon mace
- one teaspoon nutmeg
- one teaspoon ginger

## Directions

1. Soak two cups dried apples in water overnight
2. The next morning, chop them finely and add one cup molasses
3. Cook for one hour, then add one cup sugar, one cup butter, one cup sweet milk, one cup raisins, one cup currants, and two eggs
4. Add four cups flour, one heaping teaspoon soda, one teaspoon cinnamon, one teaspoon mace, one teaspoon nutmeg, and one teaspoon ginger

title: Ciasto Domowe (Home Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- two cups dried apples
- one cup molasses
- one cup sugar
- one cup butter
- one cup sweet milk
- one cup regular raisins
- one cup currant raisins
- two eggs
- four cups flour
- one heaping teaspoon baking soda
- one teaspoon cinnamon
- one teaspoon mace
- one teaspoon nutmeg
- one teaspoon ginger
- one teaspoon cloves
- one teaspoon allspice

## Directions

1. Soak two cups dried apples in water overnight
2. The next morning, chop them finely
3. Add one cup molasses and cook for one hour
4. Add one cup sugar, one cup butter, one cup sweet milk, one cup regular raisins, one cup currant raisins, two eggs, four cups flour, one heaping teaspoon baking soda, one teaspoon cinnamon, one teaspoon mace, one teaspoon nutmeg, one teaspoon ginger, one teaspoon cloves, and one teaspoon allspice
5. Mix into dough and bake as usual
6. The cake can be made different by adding candied citron, orange peel, nuts, etc.

title: Ciasto Funtowe - Ciasto Białe (Pound Cake - White Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound flour
- one pound sugar
- three-quarters pound butter
- sixteen egg whites
- one teaspoon flavoring extract

## Directions

1. Prepare and bake the same as the Yellow Pound Cake

title: Ciasto Funtowe - Ciasto Żółte (Pound Cake - Yellow Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- half pound butter
- half pound sugar
- five eggs
- one glass wine or brandy
- half glass rose water
- half pound flour
- quarter teaspoon salt
- powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Wash half pound butter carefully and squeeze out all water
2. Cream the butter in a bowl, then add half pound sugar and beat until completely white and smooth
3. Beat in one egg and mix until uniform; repeat with remaining four eggs, beating four to five minutes after each addition
4. When the whole mixture resembles thick yellow cream, add one glass wine or brandy and half glass rose water
5. Mix well and add half pound dried, sifted, and slightly warmed flour mixed with quarter teaspoon salt
6. Line a form smoothly with buttered paper and pour in batter
7. Sprinkle powdered sugar on top and place in a very slow oven for one and a half hours
8. Cover the form with cardboard or thick paper at first and bake for one hour, then remove cover and let brown for the last half hour
9. If turning the form in the oven, do so very carefully so as not to jar the cake

title: Ciasto na Skórkę - Sposób 1 (Pie Crust - Method 1)
body:
## Ingredients

- three cups of flour
- one teaspoon of baking powder
- one teaspoon of salt
- one cup of lard
- ice water
- small pieces of butter
- flour

## Directions

1. Sift three cups of flour with one teaspoon of baking powder and one teaspoon of salt; add one cup of lard and mix with a knife, then add enough ice water so that the dough is thick
2. Turn onto a work surface and roll out to the thickness of an inch, then scatter small pieces of butter on top and sprinkle with a pinch of flour, fold the dough and roll out again; repeat this several times

title: Ciasto na Skórkę - Sposób 2 (Pie Crust - Method 2)
body:
## Ingredients

- one and one-half cups of flour
- half a teaspoon of salt
- half a cup of lard, or lard mixed half with butter
- ice water

## Directions

1. Sift one and one-half cups of flour, half a teaspoon of salt and add half a cup of lard, or lard mixed half with butter and mix thoroughly, then add enough ice water to obtain thick dough

title: Ciasto Owocowe - Ciasto Białe (Fruit Cake - White Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup butter
- two cups sugar
- one pound figs
- one pound dates
- one pound almonds
- one pound raisins
- half pound citron
- one pound walnuts
- two and a half cups flour
- two and a half teaspoons baking powder
- seven egg whites

## Directions

1. Cream one cup butter with two cups sugar
2. Chop figs, dates, almonds, raisins, citron, and walnuts separately, not too finely, then mix lightly with a spoon
3. Dust the chopped fruit and nuts with some of the flour
4. Sift the remaining flour with two and a half teaspoons baking powder
5. Add the flour mixture to the creamed butter and sugar, then add the fruit, and finally fold in seven egg whites beaten to stiff peaks
6. Line two square forms with buttered paper and bake in a slow oven for one and a half hours

title: Ciasto Owocowe - Ciasto Wykwintne (Fruit Cake - Exquisite Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound butter
- one pound and quarter dark brown sugar
- one pound flour
- one pound citron
- half pound orange peel candied in sugar
- half pound lemon peel candied in sugar
- six pounds raisins without seeds
- two pounds currants
- three tablespoons cinnamon
- two tablespoons mace
- two tablespoons nutmeg
- one tablespoon Jamaican ginger (allspice)
- one teaspoon cloves
- one cup molasses
- half quart brandy
- twelve eggs

## Directions

1. Prepare and bake according to the recipe for White Fruit Cake above
2. This proportion is for an enormous wedding cake weighing several pounds

title: Clam Chowder (Clam Chowder)
body:
## Ingredients

- fifteen clams with shells
- one quart of boiling water
- one quarter pound of salted bacon
- four large potatoes
- three large onions
- salt and pepper to taste
- one quart of milk

## Directions

1. Thoroughly wash fifteen clams with their shells using a brush, then place in a pot, pour one quart of boiling water and cook until the shells open
2. Remove from heat, take the clams out of the shells and chop into small pieces
3. Chop finely one quarter pound of salted bacon, cut four large potatoes into thin slices and three large onions
4. Place the bacon in a separate pot and brown it slightly on low heat without browning much, then add the strained water in which the clams were cooked, the chopped potatoes, onion, salt and pepper to taste and cook on very low heat for one hour
5. Then add one quart of milk and the chopped clams, cook for another fifteen minutes and serve on the table

title: Consomme a la Duchesse (Consommé à la Duchesse)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 whole eggs
- salt
- butter
- warm water

## Directions

1. Beat 2 whole eggs for five minutes, then add 1/4 teaspoon of salt and beat for three more minutes.
2. Pour the beaten eggs into a cup smeared with butter and place in a baking pan with warm water.
3. Cover with paper smeared with fat and place in a moderately hot oven for about half an hour.
4. When a knife inserted shows no mass remaining on it, this is a sign that the eggs have cooked, then remove from the oven.

title: Consommé Royal (Royal Consommé)
body:
## Ingredients

- Strong beef broth
- Caramel (burnt sugar)
- One whole egg
- One egg yolk
- Two tablespoons of cream
- Salt
- Nutmeg

## Directions

1. Cook strong broth on beef and strain
2. Color with caramel (burnt sugar)
3. Beat one whole egg and one yolk with two tablespoons of cream
4. Add salt and a pinch of nutmeg
5. Pour into a mold or cup greased with butter
6. Place the mold in a bain-marie with hot water
7. Cook in the bain-marie
8. After cooking and cooling, turn out of the mold
9. Cut into cubes or slices
10. Place in a bowl and pour broth over it
11. Meatballs can also be added to this soup

title: Consommé z Jajkiem (Consommé with Egg)
body:
## Ingredients

- One beef bone
- One carrot, sliced
- Two onions
- One turnip
- Several celery stalks
- Several red pepper pods
- Two cloves
- Two bay leaves
- Egg whites and shells from three eggs
- Four quarts of water
- Salt

## Directions

1. Mix all ingredients together
2. Pour in four quarts of water and place on fire
3. When it comes to a boil, add salt
4. Cook three hours on very low heat
5. Strain
6. Serve with one fried egg per person

title: Consommé à la Duchesse (Duchess Consommé)
body:
## Ingredients

- Two whole eggs
- Quarter teaspoon of salt
- Butter
- Fat for greasing

## Directions

1. Beat two whole eggs for five minutes
2. Add quarter teaspoon of salt and beat three minutes longer
3. Pour the beaten eggs into a cup greased with butter
4. Place the cup in a bain-marie with warm water
5. Cover with paper greased with fat
6. Place in a moderately hot oven for about half an hour
7. Test with a knife; when the mass no longer sticks to the knife, the eggs are cooked
8. Remove from the oven and place in a cold location
9. When properly cooled, turn out of the cup and cut into cubes
10. Place in a bowl and pour hot broth over it

title: Cynaderki Pieczone (Roasted Lamb Kidneys)
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb kidneys
- butter
- pepper
- parsley

## Directions

1. Clean the kidneys of fat.
2. Cut each kidney in half.
3. Scald with boiling water.
4. Salt and place on a roasting pan.
5. Roast in a very hot oven, basting with butter.
6. Just before serving, place a small piece of butter in each cut.
7. Sprinkle with pepper and chopped parsley.
8. Serve.

title: Cynadry Wołowe (Braised Beef Kidneys)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef kidneys
- water
- butter or fresh lard
- onion, chopped
- salt
- pepper
- grated bread

## Directions

1. Parboil kidneys in water, then remove and devein
2. Slice the kidneys
3. Melt some butter or fresh lard in a pan, add the sliced kidneys
4. Sprinkle with chopped onion and braise over good heat, pouring water from time to time
5. When almost cooked through, add salt and continue braising until completely tender
6. Sprinkle with some grated bread, bring to a boil, and serve

title: Czarnina (Duck in Blood Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- duck, goose, piglet, or hare front quarters
- vinegar
- salt
- blood from the animal
- vegetables
- mushrooms
- onion
- fresh pork fat with skin
- flour
- sugar
- dried pears and plums, or fresh pears and apples
- flour dumplings

## Directions

1. Boil some vinegar, salt it, cool it, and catch the blood in it during slaughter of the duck or piglet.
2. Clean the animal, scald it, singe the hair over fire, and place on broth.
3. For goose, typically use only the offal, as the goose itself is roasted as a roast stuffed with apples, since a stew from it is not good; instead cook a few pounds of beef with the offal.
4. After the broth comes to a boil and is salted, add vegetables, a few mushrooms, onion, and a piece of fresh pork fat with skin.
5. When everything is cooked tender, strain the broth, remove the duck or piglet to a platter and cover so it does not cool quickly, from which a stew with sauce is made, for which some clean broth should be reserved.
6. When the broth is cooked, strain it and season with the blood prepared with vinegar and flour.
7. Add vinegar to taste, a few tablespoons of sugar, cut the pork fat into cubes, add to the soup and boil everything together, being careful it does not curdle.
8. Separately cook dried pears and plums, or fresh pears and apples in a clay pot, and these together with the sauce in which they cooked, of which there should be very little, pour into the serving bowl.
9. Separately cook sliced dumplings in water and place in the bowl along with the offal.

title: Czekolada po Amerykańsku (American-Style Chocolate)
body:
## Ingredients

- half quart milk
- half quart water
- three or four heaping tablespoons chocolate
- sugar
- cream

## Directions

1. Mix half quart milk with the same amount of water and bring to a boil
2. Grate three or four heaping tablespoons of chocolate, add to the boiling milk and cook for fifteen minutes, skimming off the foam that rises to the surface
3. Serve with sugar and cream

title: Czekolada po Wiedeńska (Viennese-Style Chocolate)
body:
## Ingredients

- three heaping tablespoons grated chocolate
- small amount of water
- one quart hot milk
- half cream

## Directions

1. Beat three heaping tablespoons of grated chocolate with a small amount of water and mix to a smooth paste
2. Add one quart of hot milk mixed half with cream
3. Cook in a double boiler for five minutes
4. Pour into cups and top with some foam from beaten egg whites

title: Czepiec Barani (Lamb Caul)
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb caul (szlarl)
- poultry liver, or calf liver if unavailable
- bread crumbs
- milk
- egg yolks
- nutmeg
- orange flower
- egg whites
- salt
- bacon
- sweet almonds
- French wine
- sugar
- vegetables

## Directions

1. Clean the caul thoroughly.
2. Prepare the stuffing: use poultry livers, or if unavailable, calf liver with membranes removed and finely chopped, then pressed through a sieve to remove veins and large pieces.
3. Grate a couple of bread rolls and add two cups of milk, two egg yolks, a little nutmeg, a touch of orange flower, meringue made from two egg whites, salt to taste, and some bacon cut into small cubes.
4. Mix everything carefully.
5. Stuff the caul with this mixture, sew it, wrap in cheesecloth, place in a pot and cover with cold water.
6. Add whatever vegetables are on hand.
7. Cover and cook for two hours.
8. When serving, pour the following sauce over it: grate ten sweet almonds, add two egg yolks, one glass of French wine, and a little sugar; mix and bring to a boil.

title: Egg Nogg
body:
## Ingredients

- six whole eggs
- half a cup of sugar
- one quart of fresh milk
- one-quarter quart of good brandy
- grated nutmeg

## Directions

1. Beat six whole eggs with half a cup of sugar until thick like cream, then pour in one quart of fresh milk and one-quarter quart of good brandy, and some grated nutmeg
2. Beat everything thoroughly and mix, and at the end add three egg whites beaten until stiff peaks form
3. Serve in small cups or glasses with foam on top

title: Farsz Cytrynowy (Lemon Filling)
body:
## Ingredients

- one large citron
- one cup of sugar
- two eggs
- one tablespoon of butter

## Directions

1. Take the grated peel and juice from one large citron, add one cup of sugar, two eggs and one tablespoon of butter
2. Pour into a small pot, place the pot in a pot of boiling water and cook until the mass is thick, then cool

title: Farsz Czekoladowy (Chocolate Filling)
body:
## Ingredients

- quarter pound of chocolate
- one cup of sugar
- three-quarters cup of water
- two tablespoons of butter
- two tablespoons of cornstarch
- quarter cup of milk
- one teaspoon of vanilla extract

## Directions

1. Cook together for five minutes: quarter pound of chocolate, one cup of sugar, three-quarters cup of water and two tablespoons of butter
2. Mix two tablespoons of cornstarch in quarter cup of milk, pour into the chocolate mixture and cook another three minutes
3. Beat with a spoon until it cools, then add one teaspoon of vanilla extract

title: Farsz Migdałowy (Almond Filling)
body:
## Ingredients

- two egg whites
- two cups of sugar
- one teaspoon of vanilla extract
- two cups of finely chopped blanched almonds

## Directions

1. Beat strongly two egg whites with two cups of sugar
2. Add one teaspoon of vanilla extract and two cups of finely chopped previously blanched almonds
3. Mix everything into a smooth mass

title: Farsz Owocowy (Fruit Filling)
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup of currants without seeds
- half pound of blanched almonds
- half pound of figs
- half pound of citron
- royal frosting

## Directions

1. Chop finely one cup of currants without seeds, half pound of blanched almonds, half pound of figs and half pound of citron
2. Then add enough royal frosting to make a soft mass

title: Fasola po Bostońsku (Boston Baked Beans)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 1/2 quarts white beans
- cold water
- 1/2 pound salt pork
- 1/2 pound salt pork for top
- 1 teaspoon dry mustard
- 1/2 cup molasses
- boiling water

## Directions

1. Pour 1 1/2 quarts white beans in a bowl, cover with cold water and let soak overnight.
2. Next morning, drain the water, pour fresh water and place on heat.
3. When the beans are half cooked (just beginning to soften), drain the water.
4. Place the beans in a clay pot with a cover, having placed 1/2 pound salt pork on the bottom of the pot.
5. On top of the beans place another piece of salt pork of the same size, with skin on top.
6. Score the skin with a sharp knife in a crosshatch pattern.
7. Mix 1 teaspoon dry mustard powder with 1/2 cup molasses and pour on top.
8. Then pour boiling water until it covers the pork and place in a moderately hot oven, where it should bake slowly for eight to ten hours.
9. As water evaporates during baking, add fresh boiling water, watching carefully so the beans on top do not dry out.

title: Fasola Zielona (Green Beans)
body:
## Ingredients

- young green beans
- water
- salt to taste
- butter
- a little flour
- pepper to taste

## Directions

1. Remove the strings from young green beans and chop finely, throw into a pan with boiling water, of which there should be plenty; salt and cook on high heat for ten minutes, then throw into a colander and rinse with cold water to cool.
2. Brown a little butter with a pinch of flour on white, add the beans, sprinkle with pepper, heat and serve.

title: Fasola Zielona po Angielsku (Green Beans English Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- young green beans
- water
- butter
- water

## Directions

1. Carefully remove the strings from young beans, throw into a large pan with boiling water and cook until tender, then throw into a colander and rinse with cold water until completely cool, then spread on a napkin to dry.
2. Pour a few tablespoons of water into a flat pan and add one tablespoon of fresh butter, and when the butter melts, add the beans and cook quickly, shaking the pan, until the water evaporates.
3. Transfer the beans to a salad dish and place slices of good butter on top.

title: Flaki (Tripe)
body:
## Ingredients

- cleaned tripe
- water
- beef broth
- salt
- vegetables (celery and parsley)
- butter
- grated and sifted bread roll
- grated cheese or Parmesan
- ground white pepper
- sour cream

## Directions

1. Thoroughly clean the tripe and pour water over it, then bring to a boil. Pour out this water, add fresh water and cook the tripe until tender.
2. After cooking, slice thinly like noodles and pour over with warm beef broth prepared previously on beef meat. Add salt and vegetables and cook until it thickens well.
3. Separately, braise finely sliced celery and parsley in butter until soft, then add to the cooking tripe.
4. When the tripe has thickened, remove from heat and let cool.
5. After cooling, take a wide and low pot or stone jar or clay pot and arrange in layers in the following order: a layer of tripe, a layer of fresh unsalted butter in small pieces, a layer of grated and sifted bread roll, a layer of grated cheese or Parmesan, ground white pepper. Repeat these layers in the same order until all the tripe is used.
6. Mix everything well, add salt to taste, add good sour cream, mix gently and place in a slow oven for an hour.
7. Remove, wrap the pot in a napkin and serve at the table.

title: Flaki po Polsku (Polish-Style Tripe)
body:
## Ingredients

- tripe, cleaned
- water
- meat for broth
- celery, parsley, carrots, thinly sliced
- salt and pepper to taste
- marjoram, nutmeg or ginger
- one tablespoon butter

## Directions

1. Carefully clean the tripe and cover with soft water, cook while skimming like broth
2. Discard the water, rinse the tripe once more in cold water, add fresh water and continue cooking about four hours in whole pieces
3. Remove and slice into pasta-like strips
4. Separately cook a few pounds of meat for broth and use this broth to cover the sliced tripe
5. Chop some celery, parsley, and carrots into thin strips and cook until tender, then add to the tripe
6. Half an hour before serving, salt and pepper the tripe and add grated marjoram, nutmeg, or ginger to taste
7. Mix one tablespoon of butter and season the tripe, then bring to a boil, stir well, and pour into a dish
8. Meatballs can also be made with fat to add to the tripe, as described in the soup section
9. The vegetables can be braised in butter instead of boiling and added just before serving
10. The flour and butter can be browned in the pot first
11. Tripe is best made in an earthenware pot; if made in a metal pot it will be dark and less tasty
12. Tripe can also be served as a soup but should be made much thinner in that case

title: Flaki Smażone (Fried Tripe)
body:
## Ingredients

- cleaned and cooked tripe
- water
- smoked bacon
- salt
- pepper
- flour
- flour
- beef broth or water
- mushrooms

## Directions

1. Clean and cook the tripe in water. When cooled, cut into square pieces about one inch in size.
2. For every pound of such tripe, fry fat from a quarter pound of smoked bacon in a pan. Season the tripe with salt and pepper to taste, coat with flour and fry in this fat until well browned.
3. Remove from the fat and place on a hot platter.
4. To the remaining fat in the pan add one teaspoon of flour, a few tablespoons of broth or water, and a few finely chopped mushrooms if available. Place on heat and stir until it comes to a boil.
5. Add salt and pepper to taste and pour this sauce over the tripe arranged on the platter, which must be very hot.

title: Flaki w Cieście (Tripe in Batter)
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked tripe
- salt
- pepper
- two eggs
- two tablespoons flour
- one cup milk
- butter

## Directions

1. Cook the tripe until tender and cut into pieces about three inches long and wide. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
2. Beat two eggs in a bowl, add two tablespoons flour, one cup milk and a pinch of salt to create a thin batter.
3. Dip the cut tripe pieces in this batter and fry in butter in a pan until golden brown.
4. Serve immediately at the table.

title: Flaki z Ostrym Smakiem (Tripe with Spicy Flavor)
body:
## Ingredients

- tripe, cleaned
- water
- beef broth
- vegetables
- butter
- celery and parsley, thinly chopped

## Directions

1. Carefully clean the tripe, cover with water and bring to a boil, discard the water
2. Add fresh water and cook the tripe until tender
3. After cooking, slice thinly like pasta and cover with warm broth previously cooked on beef
4. Add salt and vegetables and cook until well set
5. Separately braise thinly sliced celery and parsley in butter, and when tender add to the cooking tripe
6. Once the tripe thickens, remove from heat and cool
7. After cooling, take a wide shallow pot or stone jar or earthenware vessel and layer in the following order: a layer of tripe, a layer of fresh unsalted

title: Flaki z Ryżem po Indyjsku (Tripe with Rice Indian Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- one and a half pounds cleaned tripe
- salted water
- small cup well-rinsed rice
- two sliced onions
- fat or lard
- hot water
- one teaspoon curry powder

## Directions

1. Pour salted water over the cleaned tripe and cook for ten minutes from the time it comes to a boil. Pour out the water, add fresh clean water, salt and cook for fifteen minutes.
2. Remove the tripe and to the remaining broth add a small cup of well-rinsed rice and cook until tender.
3. Fry two sliced onions in fat until golden brown, add the tripe, pour hot water just to cover it barely and cook briefly.
4. Add one teaspoon of curry powder, mix well, then arrange the tripe on a platter and surround it with the cooked rice.

title: Forszmak (Veal Hash)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound minced leftover veal roast
- one large herring, skinned and boned
- six medium potatoes
- two tablespoons butter
- one tablespoon wheat flour
- several tablespoons broth or strong beef broth
- a few tablespoons sour cream
- salt and pepper to taste
- one large roasted onion, grated
- two eggs
- grated roll

## Directions

1. Mince very finely the leftover veal roast, preferably using a meat grinder.
2. For one pound of such meat, take one large herring, skin and bone it, and crush in a mortar into a paste.
3. Cook six medium potatoes and grate them.
4. Boil two tablespoons butter with one tablespoon wheat flour, dilute with several tablespoons broth or strong beef broth and add a few tablespoons sour cream.
5. Season everything with salt and pepper to taste, add one large roasted onion grated, beat in two eggs and mix until a uniform mass forms, which however should not be thick.
6. Grease a baking dish or pan with butter, sprinkle with grated roll, place all the mass, sprinkle the top also with roll and place in the oven for one hour and ten minutes.
7. Then turn out onto a serving platter and place in a cool place to cool.
8. Serve cold with vinegar and oil as an appetizer.

title: Frinkando (Braised Veal Loin)
body:
## Ingredients

- one large piece of veal loin
- salt and pepper
- lard for larding
- one tablespoon butter
- various vegetables
- broth

## Directions

1. Take a large piece of veal loin, remove bones, remove veins, salt and pepper, then lard with lard and let it rest for about half an hour.
2. Place one tablespoon butter and various vegetables in a pan with the meat; braise on low heat, basting with broth, until tender.
3. Then remove the meat, add one tablespoon flour to the pan and brown with vegetables.
4. Finally strain and pour this sauce over the meat arranged on a serving platter.
5. The platter is usually surrounded with potato puree.

title: Galantina z Indyka (Turkey Galantine)
body:
## Ingredients

- turkey
- one pound young pork fat
- one pound veal
- veal liver
- turkey liver
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg
- two soaked and squeezed bread rolls
- three eggs
- young cooked pork fat, sliced
- turkey meat, sliced
- ham or tongue, in strips
- olives, optional
- salted water
- mixed vegetables
- four calf's feet
- two egg whites
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. After cleaning the turkey, discard the giblets and carefully butterfly it along the backbone, remove the bones, cut away meat remaining on the bones
2. Season with salt and spread a layer of the following filling on the flattened turkey: Take one pound of young pork fat, cut into pieces and sauté without browning; take one pound of veal, veal liver and turkey liver, braise all in the fat, then pass through a meat grinder, then strain through a sieve, add salt and pepper to taste, a pinch of nutmeg, two soaked and squeezed bread rolls and three eggs; mix everything properly and spread a one-inch thick layer on the flattened turkey
3. Over this spread a layer of slices of young cooked pork fat alternating with meat cut from the turkey and strips of ham or tongue, then again a layer of filling; if desired, add several olives to the filling
4. Roll the prepared turkey carefully, sew it shut, wrap in a napkin, shaping it into an oblong form, tie with twine and cook in salted water with mixed vegetables and four calf's feet for three hours
5. After cooking, remove from heat and let rest for one hour, then remove the turkey, unwrap the napkin and rinse in clean hot water, soak in cold water, wrap in the napkin again and press down with a weight so all sauce drains
6. From the remaining sauce, remove all fat, clarify with two egg whites beaten with water, remove from heat, add juice of half a lemon, then strain through a napkin, and place in a cold place to set as aspic
7. When the turkey has completely cooled, slice it, arrange on a platter and coat with aspic
8. Galantine can be made in the same way with chicken

title: Galantina z Ryb (Fish Galantine)
body:
## Ingredients

- pike, 2 fish (one large, one small)
- salt
- onion, parboiled
- butter
- bread roll, soaked
- pepper
- nutmeg
- hard-boiled eggs
- napkin, butter-smeared
- twine
- vegetables miscellaneous
- gelatin

## Directions

1. Pike is best for galantine, though sandacz may also be used.
2. Take two pike, one large and one small; clean the large one, fillet lengthwise, remove tail and head, and remove all bones, salt and leave for one hour.
3. Clean the small pike, remove skin and pick all the meat, which should be finely chopped, with the addition of parboiled onion, butter, soaked bread roll, salt and pepper to taste and a pinch of nutmeg.
4. With this forcemeat, after proper mixing, fill the filleted pike, interspersing with hard-boiled eggs cut into quarters.
5. Fold both halves together, wrap tightly in a butter-smeared napkin and tie around with twine.
6. Place the fish prepared in this way in an oblong pan, cover with broth made from the head, bones and miscellaneous vegetables and cook for at most one hour over low heat.
7. After cooking, remove from heat and let rest in a cool place until it cools.
8. After complete cooling, remove the fish, unwrap from the napkin and arrange on a platter.
9. Cook the remaining broth, clarify with egg whites, add gelatin and chill into aspic, which should then be cut into neat pieces with a fluted knife and used to decorate the fish arranged on the platter.
10. Separately cook miscellaneous vegetables, previously cut into nice cubes and stars, and use them to decorate the platter.

title: Galareta bez Ości (Aspic without Bones)
body:
## Ingredients

- pike or other fish
- broth
- burnt sugar for yellow color (optional)
- red gelatin for pink color (optional)
- pickles, miscellaneous
- mushrooms
- capers
- olives
- green cooked peas
- carrots, cooked and cut into cubes and stars

## Directions

1. Clean and cook pike or other fish as above, taking care not to overcook it.
2. Strain the broth through cloth and clarify.
3. For yellow colored aspic, add some burnt sugar; for pink, add some red gelatin.
4. Take one quarter of this broth and pour into a mold, distributing evenly throughout the mold.
5. Carefully pick the cooked fish of all bones and cut into small pieces.
6. Add miscellaneous pickles, mushrooms, capers, olives, green cooked peas, cooked carrots cut into nice cubes and stars.
7. Mix everything lightly with the fish.
8. When the aspic in the mold begins to thicken, arrange the fish with pickles and vegetables on the aspic and cover with the rest of the broth, trying to keep the fish in the center and the aspic around it.
9. Chill until set.
10. When firm, turn the mold onto a salad bowl and serve at table with vinegar and oil.

title: Galareta Ogórkowa (Cucumber Aspic)
body:
## Ingredients

- four not-too-ripe cucumbers
- one quart water
- one teaspoon salt
- small half teaspoon white pepper
- small package gelatin
- one cup cold water
- one cucumber for garnish
- lettuce leaves
- oil
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Peel four cucumbers and cut into pieces
2. Pour one quart of water over them and add one teaspoon of salt and small half teaspoon of white pepper
3. Cook until the cucumbers completely disintegrate
4. Soak a small package of gelatin in one cup of cold water
5. When the cucumbers are completely cooked, pour in the gelatin and stir until completely dissolved
6. Strain everything and place in a cold location
7. When almost completely cooled, peel and slice one cucumber thinly
8. Line a mold or bowl (which should first be moistened with cold water) with these thin slices
9. Pour the liquid into the mold and place in a cold location for several hours until completely set
10. Turn the aspic out onto a plate lined with lettuce leaves and pour French sauce made from oil and vinegar over it, or serve as a side dish to fish or cold meat

title: Galareta Pomarańczowa (Orange Jelly)
body:
## Ingredients

- one quarter box of gelatin
- one quarter cup of cold water
- half a cup of boiling water
- one cup of orange juice
- half a cup of lemon juice
- half a cup of sugar

## Directions

1. Soak a quarter box of gelatin in a quarter cup of cold water and let it soak for about half an hour
2. Pour half a cup of boiling water into the gelatin and stir until almost completely dissolved, then add one cup of orange juice, half a cup of lemon juice and half a cup of sugar
3. Strain through cloth, pour into a mold and place in a cool place for several hours

title: Galareta Porzeczkowa (Currant Jelly)
body:
## Ingredients

- currants not overly ripe
- sugar equal in weight to the juice

## Directions

1. Take currants not overly ripe, carefully sort and pour into an enameled pot
2. Place on the fire and crush the berries so as to extract all the juice from them, then strain through cloth
3. Repeat this straining several times until the juice is completely clear
4. For each pound of this juice, take one pound of sugar
5. First place the sugar in the oven and heat it well, not allowing it to burn
6. Pour the strained juice into the pot and let it boil for about twenty minutes, then add the hot sugar and stir until it dissolves completely
7. Pour into jars or glasses, seal tightly and place in the sun until the jelly sets

title: Galareta z Dzikich Winogron (Jelly from Wild Grapes)
body:
## Ingredients

- wild grapes
- sugar in the proportion of half a quart per quart of juice

## Directions

1. Gather grapes when they begin to ripen
2. Crush and cook them into a mass, place in a bag and hang it overnight over a bowl so all the juice drips out
3. The next day, press out the remaining juice, pour it into a pot and boil for half an hour, frequently skimming
4. Add half a quart of sugar for each quart of juice and boil until thick
5. Pour into glasses or jars

title: Galareta z Herbaty (Tea Jelly)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a box of gelatin
- water
- very strong tea
- one cup of sugar for every half quart of tea
- cream and sugar for serving

## Directions

1. Dissolve half a box of gelatin in just enough water to barely cover it
2. Make very strong tea, add one cup of sugar per half quart of tea, and when dissolved mix with the gelatin
3. Strain and pour into small molds, which should be placed on ice
4. Serve very cold with cream and sugar on the side

title: Galareta z Jabłek (Apple Jelly)
body:
## Ingredients

- ripe sour apples
- sugar in the proportion of three quarters of a quart per quart of juice
- geranium leaf (optional)

## Directions

1. Take ripe sour apples, chop them and pour a very small amount of water over them, then boil until they break down
2. Pour all the mass into a triangular bag made of thin cloth and hang it over a placed dish
3. When all the juice has dripped out, add three quarters of a quart of sugar for each quart of juice
4. Place on the fire and boil for fifteen minutes, counting from the time it boils
5. Pour into glasses and seal tightly
6. To make good jelly, never boil more than a few glasses at a time
7. A different and very pleasant aroma can be given to this jelly by placing a geranium leaf in a glass before pouring the jelly into it; after some time this leaf will turn yellow and float to the surface, then discard it

title: Galareta z Kury (Chicken Aspic)
body:
## Ingredients

- leftover roasted chicken from previous day
- chicken wings, neck, back, giblets and all remains
- water
- onion slices
- celery seeds
- salt
- pepper
- hard-boiled eggs

## Directions

1. Take the wings, neck, back, giblets and all remains from the roasted chicken and cover with a small amount of water.
2. Simmer covered with a few onion slices until the meat falls off the bones.
3. Remove all meat from the bones and chop fairly coarsely.
4. Add some celery seeds, salt and pepper to taste.
5. Take one-third of the chopped meat and spread on the bottom of a metal mold or bowl.
6. Lay a layer of hard-boiled eggs cut into thin slices on top.
7. Repeat with meat and eggs to make three layers total.
8. Place the remaining bones back in the pot and cook until only enough broth remains to well moisten the meat and egg mixture in the mold.
9. Pour the broth over the meat and eggs in the mold and place in a cold location.
10. Serve when well chilled.

title: Galareta z Pomarancz i Cytryn (Orange and Lemon Jelly)
body:
## Ingredients

- one tablespoon of gelatin
- one cup of warm water
- half a cup of sugar
- juice of two oranges
- juice of one lemon
- zest of orange
- egg whites

## Directions

1. Dissolve one tablespoon of gelatin in one cup of warm water and then add half a cup of sugar
2. Squeeze juice from two oranges and one lemon and add to the gelatin, then strain
3. Rub some of the yellow part of the orange peel and mix with the liquid, then pour onto a large platter
4. When the jelly begins to set, beat a couple of egg whites to stiff peaks and mix with the jelly and arrange on a serving platter
5. This is usually served with whipped cream

title: Galareta z Rajskich Jabłuszek (Crabapple Jelly)
body:
## Ingredients

- crabapples
- water
- sugar, measure for measure with the juice

## Directions

1. Chop crabapples, add a little water and cook until soft
2. Strain the juice and cook until clear
3. Add sugar in the same measure as the juice
4. Cook slowly for twenty minutes
5. Pour into jars or glasses and seal tightly

title: Galareta z Zielonych Winogron (Jelly from Green Grapes)
body:
## Ingredients

- green grapes, just beginning to ripen
- one cup of water per eight pounds of grapes
- sugar, measure for measure with the juice

## Directions

1. Select grapes that are just beginning to ripen, sort them, and wash carefully
2. Add one cup of water per eight pounds of grapes and cook until completely soft
3. Crush and strain all the juice through thin cloth, without pressing
4. Pour the obtained juice back into a pot, bring to a boil and skim
5. Add sugar in the same measure as the juice
6. Cook for twenty minutes
7. Pour into glasses or jars and seal tightly; best to seal with paraffin

title: Galareta z Zielonych Winogron (Jelly from Green Grapes)
body:
## Ingredients

- grapes just beginning to ripen
- sugar
- water

## Directions

1. Select grapes that are just beginning to ripen, sort carefully and wash well
2. To eight pounds of grapes, add one cup of water

title: Galareta ze Śliwek (Plum Jelly)
body:
## Ingredients

- plums not overly ripe
- sugar equal in weight to the juice, or less if the plums are sweet

## Directions

1. Take plums not overly ripe, sort them, pour into a pot, place on the fire and crush the plums so as to extract all the juice from them
2. Pour all the mass into a bag and press out the juice, then strain it several more times so that it is clear
3. For one pound of juice obtained this way, add one pound of sugar, or less if the plums are sweet
4. First place the sugar in the oven and heat it well, being careful not to burn it
5. Pour the pressed juice back into the pot and boil for about twenty minutes, then add the hot sugar and stir until it dissolves completely
6. Pour into jars or glasses and place in the sun until the jelly sets

title: Galareta Żurawinowa (Cranberry Jelly)
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart of cranberries
- one cup of water
- sugar measured equal to the amount of juice obtained

## Directions

1. Put one quart of cranberries in a pot, add one cup of water and cook until completely soft, then pour into a strainer made of fine cloth and hang over a bowl overnight
2. The next morning add as much sugar as there is juice, cook for fifteen minutes from the moment of boiling, skim, and when the liquid begins to thicken on a spoon, pour into a mold and place in a cool place so the jelly sets

title: Gołąbki (Stuffed Cabbage Rolls)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2/3 pound beef and 1/3 pound pork (or all beef)
- 1 pound minced meat total
- 1 cup grated bread or white bread
- salt and pepper to taste
- 2 medium onions, finely minced and browned in butter
- 1 whole egg
- fresh cabbage leaves
- butter for frying
- water

## Directions

1. Mix together 1 pound of finely minced meat, 1 cup of grated bread or white bread, salt and pepper to taste, 2 medium onions finely minced and browned in butter, and 1 whole egg.
2. Mix everything into a smooth mass.
3. Form into oblong cutlets and wrap each one in a fresh cabbage leaf and tie with string or pin with a toothpick.
4. Scald the cabbage leaves in boiling water before use so they are soft and do not break when wrapping.
5. Melt a piece of butter in a wide pan and arrange the cabbage rolls in it.
6. Fry them initially on high heat to brown them slightly.
7. Then add a little water and braise on low heat until the cabbage is completely soft.

title: Gołębie Pieczone (Roasted Pigeons)
body:
## Ingredients

- pigeons
- salt
- flour
- butter
- bacon
- flour
- butter
- sour cream

## Directions

1. Clean and dress the pigeons, then salt them and sprinkle with flour, then place in a roasting pan and roast, basting with butter, as you would with chicken
2. You can also cover them with thin slices of young bacon, tie them, and roast
3. Thicken the sauce with flour and butter, and you can also add sour cream

title: Graham Muffins
body:
## Ingredients

- 0.5 cup syrup
- 0.5 cup lard
- 1 egg
- 1 cup sour milk
- 1 teaspoon soda
- graham flour as needed

## Directions

1. Mix 0.5 cup syrup, 0.5 cup lard, 1 egg, 1 cup sour milk, and 1 teaspoon soda with enough graham flour to make fairly thin dough.
2. Mix well, pour into forms, and bake.

title: Graham No. 1 (Graham Bread No. 1)
body:
## Ingredients

- two cups of graham flour
- one cup of wheat flour
- three tablespoons of sugar
- one teaspoon of salt
- one egg
- two teaspoons of soda dissolved in two cups of sour milk

## Directions

1. Mix the graham flour, wheat flour, sugar, salt, egg and soda dissolved in sour milk into dough
2. Bake for one hour in a moderate oven

title: Graham No. 2 (Graham Bread No. 2)
body:
## Ingredients

- three cups of graham flour
- one teaspoon of salt
- one heaping teaspoon of soda
- two cups of sour milk
- one-third cup of molasses

## Directions

1. Mix together the graham flour, salt and soda
2. Add two cups of sour milk and one-third cup of molasses
3. Mix into dough, which should then be cooked on steam for three hours
4. Finally place in a hot oven for fifteen minutes to dry

title: Gramatka z Piwa (Beer Grits)
body:
## Ingredients

- quarter pound of rye bread without crust
- one tablespoon of butter
- beer from a quart bottle
- a couple of pieces of cinnamon
- four tablespoons of sugar
- two tablespoons of raisins without seeds
- croutons from bread cut into cubes and dried
- a few thin slices of lemon

## Directions

1. Break into small pieces a quarter pound of rye bread without crust, add one tablespoon of butter, pour such an amount of beer so that it barely covers the bread, place on fire and let it cook down
2. Then rub in a pot with a spoon, pour in the rest of the beer from a quart bottle, add a couple of pieces of cinnamon, four tablespoons of sugar, and two tablespoons of raisins without seeds
3. Bring to a boil together and pour into a bowl in which there should be croutons from bread cut into cubes and dried, as well as a few thin slices of lemon

title: Grochówka Postna (Lenten Pea Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- half quart of peas
- soft water
- pearl barley
- butter
- salt
- pepper
- bread sliced and fried in butter

## Directions

1. For four people, take half quart of peas and pour a quart of soft water over them
2. Bring to boil and then drain the water
3. Rinse the peas carefully and pour fresh hot water over them
4. Cook until the peas are completely soft
5. Strain through a sieve and add separately cooked pearl barley
6. Mix the barley with a spoonful of good butter, salt and pepper to taste
7. Line a bowl with bread slices fried in butter and pour the soup over them

title: Groszek Zielony po Francusku (Green Peas French Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart of shelled fresh green peas
- one tablespoon butter
- salt to taste
- water
- one tablespoon butter
- a little sugar

## Directions

1. Pour the shelled green peas, which must be very young and fresh, into a two-quart pan, add one tablespoon of butter, salt to taste, fill with water and braise covered on low heat for half an hour.
2. When soft, drain the water, add another tablespoon of butter, a little sugar and serve immediately on the table.

title: Groszek Zielony w Puszkach (Canned Green Peas)
body:
## Ingredients

- canned green peas
- butter
- a little cream or milk
- salt to taste
- pepper to taste

## Directions

1. Canned green peas should be rinsed several times with cold water after opening the can, poured into a pan, add butter, a little cream or milk, salt and pepper to taste, bring to a boil and serve on the table.
2. Some add a pinch of sugar.

title: Gruszki Konserwowane (Preserved Pears)
body:
## Ingredients

- ripe and completely healthy pears
- sugar equal in weight to the pears
- water
- lemon

## Directions

1. Select ripe and completely healthy pears
2. Peel, cut in half and remove the core and seeds
3. Pour all the peelings with water and boil for fifteen minutes, then strain
4. For every four pounds of pears, take one quart of this juice; if there is not enough, add water
5. For each pound of pears, take one pound of sugar and make a syrup from it
6. Boil the syrup and skim it
7. Add one lemon cut into small dice to each quart of syrup
8. Then place the pears in the syrup and boil until they are transparent
9. Store in tightly sealed jars

title: Gruszki Marynowane (Pickled Pears)
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart of yellow vinegar (cider vinegar)
- one and three-quarter pounds of white sugar
- six whole cloves
- twelve grains of Jamaican pepper (allspice)
- one teaspoon of ground cinnamon
- one teaspoon of mace
- a small piece of ginger
- large and healthy pears

## Directions

1. Pour one quart of yellow vinegar (cider vinegar) into a pot and add one and three-quarter pounds of white sugar
2. Tie six whole cloves, twelve grains of Jamaican pepper (allspice), one teaspoon of ground cinnamon, one teaspoon of mace, and a small piece of ginger in a cloth and place in the vinegar
3. Cook all of this for fifteen minutes from the time it boils
4. Select large and healthy pears, peel and remove the stems
5. Place enough pears in the vinegar so that all are covered with liquid
6. Cook on a very low fire until pears are almost transparent
7. Remove pears and arrange in jars, then pour boiling vinegar over them
8. To preserve for a longer time, seal the jars tightly or pour paraffin over them

title: Grzanki (Croutons)
body:
## Ingredients

- bread or bread roll
- butter, preferably unsalted

## Directions

1. Remove the crust from bread or a bread roll and cut the center into small cubes
2. Throw into melted butter in a pan, stir and fry until slightly browned
3. Alternatively, instead of frying on the stove, place in a hot oven for ten minutes until browned and dried
4. For fruit soups, dip the croutons first in cream, then fry in a pan, and before serving sprinkle with sugar and serve with the soup

title: Grzanki Czeskie (Czech Toast)
body:
## Ingredients

- two eggs
- half cup milk
- one teaspoon onion juice
- one quarter teaspoon salt
- pinch of pepper
- white bread, preferably day-old
- butter for frying

## Directions

1. Beat two eggs well.
2. Add half a cup of milk, one teaspoon of onion juice, one quarter teaspoon of salt, and a pinch of pepper.
3. Mix everything well.
4. Cut white bread, preferably day-old, into medium slices.
5. Dip both sides in the mixture.
6. Fry in butter on a pan until well browned on both sides.

title: Grzanki z Serem (Cheese Croutons)
body:
## Ingredients

- white bread or rolls without crust
- butter
- grated cheese - green, Swiss, or Parmesan

## Directions

1. Cut the bread or rolls without crust into small, very thin slices
2. Spread each slice with good butter and sprinkle with grated cheese - green, Swiss, or Parmesan
3. Place in a moderately hot oven for about ten minutes
4. When the croutons are well dried, remove and serve with soup

title: Grzanki z Serem (Cheese Croutons)
body:
## Ingredients

- bread or bread roll without crust
- butter
- grated cheese (green, Swiss or Parmesan)

## Directions

1. Cut bread or bread roll without crust into small, very thin slices
2. Spread each slice well with butter and sprinkle with grated cheese (green, Swiss or Parmesan)
3. Place in a moderately hot oven for about ten minutes
4. When the croutons are well dried, remove and serve with soup

title: Grzybek Biszkoptowy (Sponge Mushroom Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- four egg yolks
- one-eighth pound of powdered sugar
- two tablespoons of wheat flour
- grated zest of half a lemon
- four egg whites
- butter

## Directions

1. Take four egg yolks, one-eighth pound of powdered sugar, two tablespoons of wheat flour and grated zest of half a lemon; beat properly then add four egg whites, beaten into stiff meringue, and mix lightly, then pour into a buttered mold and place in a hot oven for fifteen minutes
2. Serve at table with fruit juice, preserves or marmalade

title: Grzyby Duszone (Braised Mushrooms)
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh mushrooms
- butter
- salt
- a few tablespoons sour cream

## Directions

1. Clean and blanch fresh mushrooms, then slice them thinly
2. Blanch once more in cold water, place in a saucepan, add butter and braise under a cover, stirring frequently to prevent burning
3. When soft, salt to taste, pour in a few tablespoons of sour cream, braise a bit longer and serve at the table

title: Grzyby Marynowane (Pickled Mushrooms)
body:
## Ingredients

- young fresh mushrooms
- a pinch of mace
- one teaspoon of ground pepper
- salt to taste
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Select the youngest and freshly gathered mushrooms
2. For one quart of mushrooms, take a pinch of mace, one teaspoon of ground pepper and salt to taste
3. Put mushrooms in a saucepan, sprinkle with salt, pepper and mace
4. Put on fire and hold on the fire, constantly shaking the saucepan, until all the juice that the mushrooms initially release evaporates
5. Pour in enough vinegar to barely cover them
6. Boil once, remove from fire and fill jars, which should then be sealed tightly
7. To preserve for a longer time, keep them in a dry and cool place

title: Gulasz Węgierski (Hungarian Goulash)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 pounds mixed meat (1 pound each of beef, pork, and lamb), cut into pieces
- 1/2 pound young bacon, cut into cubes
- 6 onions, sliced thinly
- salt and paprika to taste
- 6 medium potatoes, cut into cubes
- beef broth or consommé, 1 tablespoon at a time

## Directions

1. Cut 3 pounds of various tender meat into walnut-sized pieces.
2. Cut 1/2 pound of young bacon into cubes, place in a pot, and fry it without letting it brown.
3. When the bacon begins to fry, add about 6 onions sliced thinly and braise for 5 minutes covered.
4. Add the meat, sprinkle with salt and plenty of paprika.
5. Braise on low heat covered for 1 hour, without stirring, but add 1 tablespoon of broth or consommé from time to time.
6. After the meat has braised for 1 hour in this manner, peel and cut 6 medium potatoes into cubes and add them to the meat.
7. Mix everything and continue cooking for about 15 minutes until the potatoes are completely soft.

title: Gęś Pieczona (Roasted Goose)
body:
## Ingredients

- young and fat goose
- salt
- apples, quartered
- marjoram
- potatoes, optional

## Directions

1. For a good roasted goose, it must necessarily be young and fat
2. After preparing and cleaning the goose, rub it with salt outside and inside
3. Stuff with apples cut into quarters, place sprigs of marjoram between the apples, sew shut, place in a roasting pan and put in the oven
4. During roasting, frequently baste with the sauce that drips into the pan
5. Before serving, remove the apples, sprinkle with a little sugar, then pour the sauce over the goose
6. Instead of apples, the goose can be stuffed with potatoes cut into pieces

title: Główka Cielęca po Parysku (Calf's Head à la Parisienne)
body:
## Ingredients

- calf's head with skin
- beef loin, finely chopped
- wheat flour
- onions
- various vegetables, finely chopped
- clove
- salt and pepper to taste
- vinegar
- nutmeg flower
- parsley sprigs
- tartar sauce or vinegar with olive oil

## Directions

1. After cleaning the calf's head, which should have skin, split it into two halves
2. Remove the brain, tongue and bones
3. Cut each half into three parts so that the ear forms a square
4. Place in a large pot, pour full of water and cook for twenty minutes on high heat, constantly skimming
5. Drain the water and rinse with cold water several times
6. In the same pot melt half pound of finely chopped beef loin on low heat so it does not fry
7. Add half pound of wheat flour, add several onions and various finely chopped vegetables, one clove, salt and pepper to taste, one tablespoon of vinegar and a little nutmeg flower
8. Mix all this together and place on fire, and when it comes to a boil, throw in the cut calf's head
9. Cover with paper so it does not blacken and cook on low heat for two hours
10. When the meat is completely tender, remove and arrange on a serving platter in the following manner: Place the tongue cut lengthwise in half in the center; place the brains separately cooked and laced in a nicely cleaned skull; arrange the ears at the corners of the platter, and cover everything with sprigs of green parsley and pour with tartar sauce or vinegar with olive oil

title: Homary à la Newburg (Lobsters à la Newburg)
body:
## Ingredients

- one tablespoon of flour
- one tablespoon of butter
- half a quart of milk
- one small can of lobsters
- hot toast

## Directions

1. Brown one tablespoon of flour with one tablespoon of butter in a saucepan
2. Add one half quart of milk and cook until it thickens
3. Take one small can of lobsters, remove the meat and crumble it fairly finely
4. Add it to the milk and cook no longer than two minutes
5. Pour onto a platter lined with hot toast and serve
6. Note: if fresh lobsters are used instead of canned, the dish will be significantly better

title: Home Beer (Piwo Domowe)
body:
## Ingredients

- two ounces of good hops
- two quarts of water
- half an ounce of ground ginger
- one and a half pounds of sugar
- one small cup of liquid yeast

## Directions

1. Pour two ounces of good hops into two quarts of water and boil for three hours
2. Then add half an ounce of ground ginger and boil for another half hour
3. Strain and add one and a half pounds of sugar, stirring until all sugar dissolves
4. Remove from heat
5. When the liquid has cooled almost completely, add one small cup of liquid yeast and set at room temperature for fermentation, which should last about twenty-four hours
6. When fermentation is complete, carefully pour the clear liquid into bottles and cork tightly

title: Imbierówka (Ginger Liqueur)
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart of boiled and cooled water
- one quart of strong spirits
- one-quarter pound of sugar
- one tablespoon of dried and finely chopped orange zest
- one-quarter pound of ground ginger
- burnt sugar for color

## Directions

1. Pour one quart of strong spirits into one quart of boiled and cooled water
2. Add one-quarter pound of sugar, one tablespoon of dried and finely chopped orange zest, and one-quarter pound of ground ginger; mix well and place in a cool place for two days
3. Then strain or filter, add burnt sugar for color, pour into bottles and cork

title: Indyk Nadziewany po Polsku (Turkey Stuffed Polish Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- Turkey
- Lard, sliced thin
- Salt
- Butter (quarter pound)
- Turkey liver, boiled and finely chopped
- One bun, soaked and squeezed dry
- One roasted grated onion
- Two eggs
- Cooked diced lard
- Pepper
- Nutmeg
- Sugar (one teaspoon)
- Raisins
- Fat or butter for basting

## Directions

1. All poultry for roasting should be slaughtered several days before roasting in winter, and one day in summer
2. Immediately after slaughtering pluck the turkey on the breasts, back and thighs, leaving the feathers on the wings
3. Before use take all the giblets for broth, and the turkey, carefully cleaned, lard in thin strips of sliced lard on the breasts and thighs, rub with salt inside and outside, stuff, sew and place in the oven on a roasting pan
4. Roast at first in a very hot oven to brown, then complete roasting in a moderately hot oven, basting with fat or butter
5. The best stuffing is made in the following way: Cream one quarter pound of butter in a bowl, add the boiled, finely chopped and then sieved turkey liver, one bun soaked and squeezed dry, one roasted grated onion, two whole eggs, some cooked diced lard, salt and pepper to taste, a little nutmeg, one teaspoon of sugar, a handful of raisins; mix everything well and stuff the turkey with this stuffing and sew it up
6. During roasting cover the stuffing with paper so it does not cook too much, and prick the skin with a fork so it does not burst
7. To remove the fishy taste from the turkey, which is sometimes detected, rub it inside with powdered ginger

title: Indyk Pieczony z Kasztanami (Turkey Roasted with Chestnuts)
body:
## Ingredients

- Young fat turkey, weighing ten to twelve pounds
- Baking soda
- Salt
- Two dozen chestnuts
- Turkey liver
- Parsley, finely chopped
- Onion
- Pepper
- Two egg yolks

## Directions

1. Choose a young fat turkey weighing ten to twelve pounds
2. After plucking, singe the down by holding the turkey over a sheet of lit paper; carefully draw out so as not to tear the innards
3. Wash carefully in four waters; add one teaspoon of baking soda to the third water
4. Wipe dry both inside and outside, then rub inside with a little salt and stuff with the following stuffing: Roast two dozen chestnuts and peel them
5. Crush eight of these chestnuts and the turkey liver in a mortar into a paste, add some very finely chopped parsley, onion, salt and pepper, and two egg yolks

title: Jabłka Lukrowane (Frosted Apples)
body:
## Ingredients

- six tart apples
- sugar
- nutmeg
- butter
- six tablespoons fine sugar
- two tablespoons milk
- half a teaspoon vanilla extract

## Directions

1. Peel six tart apples and hollow them out to remove all the seeds.
2. Fill the hollow in each apple with one teaspoon of sugar with a pinch of nutmeg and a small amount of butter, then place in the oven.
3. When the apples are almost baked, remove from the oven and set in a cold place.
4. Mix six tablespoons of fine sugar with two tablespoons of milk, add half a teaspoon of vanilla extract and beat well.
5. When the apples are completely cooled, coat them around with this frosting and place in a hot oven for one minute to lightly brown the frosting.
6. These apples can be served with sour cream or without.

title: Jabłka Marynowane (Pickled Apples)
body:
## Ingredients

- dwie filiżanki cukru
- filiżanka octu
- kilka kawałków cynamonu
- kilka całych gwoździków
- jabłka słodkie

## Directions

1. Zrobić syrop z dwóch filiżanek cukru i filiżanki octu
2. Dodać kilka kawałków cynamonu i kilka całych gwoździków
3. Obrać jabłka
4. Wydrążyć w środku aby usunąć rdzeń i ziarnka
5. Wrzucić w syrop i gotować aż do miękkości
6. Ponakładać w słoiki, zalać syropem i przechować

title: Jabłka Parowane w Cieście (Apples Steamed in Batter)
body:
## Ingredients

- cooking apples
- flour
- baking powder
- lard or butter
- salt
- cold water

## Directions

1. Peel and cut into hazelnut-sized pieces enough cooking apples to make a quart
2. Sift three cups flour with two teaspoons baking powder and then mix with two tablespoons lard or butter, add a bit of salt and enough cold water

title: Jabłka Parowane w Cieście (Steamed Apples in Pastry)
body:
## Ingredients

- tart apples, one quart when cut into hazelnut-sized pieces
- three cups flour
- two teaspoons baking powder
- two tablespoons lard or butter
- salt
- cold milk or water
- nutmeg or cloves, ground
- sour cream or sauce

## Directions

1. Peel and cut tart apples into pieces the size of a hazelnut until you have one quart.
2. Sift three cups of flour with two teaspoons of baking powder and mix with two tablespoons of lard or butter, add a pinch of salt and enough cold milk or water to make a soft dough.
3. Roll out on a work surface into a long, fairly thick cake, arrange apples on one end, sprinkle with a pinch of ground nutmeg or cloves, and wrap the apples in the pastry.
4. Wrap in a napkin or piece of cloth wrung out in very hot water and steam for at least one and a half hours.
5. Serve with sour cream or with sauce made by pouring one tablespoon of hot water into half a cup of butter to soften it, then adding slightly more than half a cup of brown sugar and beating strongly for several minutes, then adding for flavor a pinch of nutmeg and a few drops of vanilla extract.

title: Jabłka Pieczone z Galaretą (Baked Apples with Jelly)
body:
## Ingredients

- a pair of large apples
- jelly, preferably currant

## Directions

1. Wash a pair of large apples and cut them in half lengthwise, but do not peel; hollow out the center of each half with a teaspoon so as to remove all the seeds and create a round hollow in the center.
2. Fill this hollow with some jelly, preferably currant, and place in a moderately hot oven.
3. Serve with sugar and sour cream.

title: Jabłka Pieczone ze Śliwkami (Baked Apples with Prunes)
body:
## Ingredients

- sour apples of medium size
- cooked prunes
- sugar
- cinnamon
- cold sour cream

## Directions

1. Take a sufficient number of sour apples of medium size, hollow out the core to remove all the seeds and fill these hollows with cooked prunes, and add in each one teaspoon of sugar and some cinnamon and place in the oven where they should bake slowly.
2. Serve at table with cold sour cream.

title: Jabłka Smażone w Cieście (Apples Fried in Batter)
body:
## Ingredients

- four cooking apples
- sugar
- one egg
- cup milk
- cup flour
- one teaspoon baking powder
- salt
- fat or lard for frying

## Directions

1. Peel and core four cooking apples to remove all seeds, then slice them crosswise into round slices and sprinkle lightly with sugar
2. Beat one egg, add cup of milk and cup of flour sifted with one teaspoon baking powder to mix well, and a pinch of salt; mix everything together and beat hard for about three minutes, then put the sliced apples into this batter
3. Heat plenty of lard or fat in a baking dish, and when the lard is very hot, take batter by large spoon with an apple slice and drop into the lard
4. Fry for about ten minutes until browned and serve hot

title: Jabłka z Tapioką (Apples with Tapioca)
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup pearl tapioca
- cold water
- six medium apples
- sugar
- cinnamon
- butter

## Directions

1. Soak one cup of pearl tapioca in cold water overnight.
2. The next morning, peel and cut into quarters six medium apples and place in a butter-greased baking dish, sprinkle with sugar and cinnamon and then pour the tapioca on top, sprinkle again with sugar and scatter a few small pieces of butter on top, pour a little water and place in a moderately hot oven for one hour.
3. Serve with sugar, and you can also serve it with sour cream.

title: Jaja a la Milanese (Eggs Milanese-Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a cup rice
- salted water
- six eggs
- butter
- half a cup broth
- salt
- red pepper
- white bread for toast, buttered
- egg whites

## Directions

1. Rinse half a cup of rice and cook until tender in a large amount of salted water and drain
2. Hard-boil six eggs; remove the yolks and chop them
3. Put two tablespoons of butter in a pot, add half a cup of broth, bring to a boil and then add the rice and yolks, salt to taste and a little red pepper
4. Place on heat to warm the entire mixture, then arrange on a serving platter lined with buttered white bread toast, or spread the mixture on the toast and then arrange on the platter
5. Garnish on top with egg whites cut into strips or slices

title: Jaja Faszerowane (Stuffed Eggs)
body:
## Ingredients

- hard-boiled eggs
- butter
- salt and pepper to taste
- chopped fresh dill or chives
- grated bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Cool hard-boiled eggs and cut them in half lengthwise along with the shell
2. Carefully remove the yolk and white so as not to damage the shells
3. Chop the removed whites and yolks together
4. Add a tablespoon of butter, salt and pepper to taste, and some chopped dill or chives
5. Mix everything well and fill the shells with this mixture
6. Place butter on a pan and when well heated, place the stuffed shells on it sprinkled with grated bread crumbs, filling side down
7. Fry until well browned, then flip and serve in soup

title: Jaja po Chińsku (Eggs Chinese Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- four eggs
- four large slices of bread
- fresh parsley, chopped
- salt and pepper
- thick tomato sauce

## Directions

1. Hard boil four eggs for at least fifteen minutes.
2. Place in cold water and shell.
3. Trim the whites around with a knife so as not to disturb the yolks.
4. Cut the whites into thin strips using scissors.
5. Make toast from four large slices of bread.
6. Place the toast on a hot platter.
7. On each piece of toast, arrange the cut whites in the form of a nest.
8. Place a whole yolk in the center of each nest.
9. Sprinkle chopped fresh parsley around it to form a green circle.
10. Salt and pepper to taste.
11. Pour plenty of thick tomato sauce around each nest over the toast.
12. Serve. This dish looks very attractive due to the contrast of four colors.

title: Jaja z Sosem Szynkowym (Eggs with Ham Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- one-eighth pound cooked ham, finely chopped
- one onion
- two sprigs green parsley
- water
- pepper
- four eggs

## Directions

1. Chop very finely one-eighth pound of cooked ham with one onion and two sprigs of green parsley, place in a small pot
2. Add half a cup of water, a pinch of pepper and braise covered for fifteen minutes
3. Hard-boil four eggs in their shells (it is best to use special metal molds without a bottom so the whites do not spread)
4. Place on a serving platter, sprinkle lightly with lemon juice, then pour over the sauce described above, which should first be strained through a sieve, and bring to a boil again

title: Jaja Zapiekane po Hiszpańsku (Eggs Baked Spanish-Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- onion, finely chopped
- pepper
- green parsley, chopped
- green bell pepper, chopped
- six eggs

## Directions

1. This dish is best prepared in a small earthenware dish, but a pot can also be used
2. Melt one tablespoon of butter, add finely chopped onion, some pepper, one teaspoon chopped green parsley and one tablespoon chopped green bell pepper
3. Fry this mixture on low heat for a few minutes
4. Then carefully crack six eggs so as not to damage the yolks
5. As soon as the eggs are fried on one side, flip them to the other side
6. Serve on the table in the same dish in which the eggs were fried

title: Jajecznica Gotowana (Boiled Scrambled Eggs)
body:
## Ingredients

- five or six eggs
- salt
- water
- fried bacon

## Directions

1. Beat five or six eggs until the mixture is uniform, then pour into a saucepan with boiling salted water and cook for two minutes, stirring with a spoon
2. Pour everything onto a colander, drain the water, and arrange the scrambled eggs on a serving platter
3. Decorate with thin slices of fried bacon
4. The saucepan should contain plenty of water, at least two quarts per six eggs

title: Jajecznica Gotowana (Boiled Scrambled Eggs)
body:
## Ingredients

- five or six eggs
- salted water
- smoked bacon, thinly sliced

## Directions

1. Beat five or six eggs until uniform
2. Pour them into a pot of boiling salted water and cook for two minutes, stirring with a spoon
3. Pour everything onto a colander to drain the water
4. Arrange the scrambled eggs on a serving platter
5. Garnish with thin slices of fried smoked bacon
6. Use plenty of water in the pot, at least two quarts for six eggs

title: Jajecznica Kubańska (Cuban Scrambled Eggs)
body:
## Ingredients

- a couple tablespoons chopped meat
- pepper
- salt
- one teaspoon finely chopped onion
- a bit of fat or butter
- six eggs
- toast from white bread

## Directions

1. Take a couple tablespoons of chopped meat seasoned with pepper and salt as for sausage, add one teaspoon of finely chopped onion and fry in a pan with a bit of fat or butter
2. Beat six eggs and add to the meat, place the pan on low heat and fry, constantly stirring, until the eggs are set
3. Place on a hot serving dish and serve on toast made from white bread

title: Jajecznica Pieczona (Baked Scrambled Eggs)
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- eight eggs
- salt
- pepper
- three tablespoons cream
- one tablespoon butter

## Directions

1. Grease a baking dish with butter and break eight eggs into it
2. Sprinkle with salt and pepper, pour in three tablespoons of cream and one tablespoon of butter, scattering it in small pieces over the entire surface
3. Place in a moderately hot oven for twenty minutes and serve immediately at the table

title: Jajecznica po Hiszpańsku (Spanish Scrambled Eggs)
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup rice
- two quarts boiling water
- one rounded teaspoon salt
- one tablespoon butter
- boiled eggs or fried eggs

## Directions

1. Rinse one cup of rice in cold water, drain, add to a saucepan and cover with two quarts of boiling water
2. Add one rounded teaspoon of salt and cook for half an hour
3. After cooking, pour onto a colander, and when all the water drains, return the rice to the saucepan and mix with one tablespoon of butter
4. Spread on a hot serving platter and arrange boiled or fried eggs on top

title: Jajecznica Szwajcarska (Swiss Scrambled Eggs)
body:
## Ingredients

- one tablespoon butter
- two tablespoons grated cheese
- four to six eggs
- salt
- pepper
- one or two tablespoons cream
- grated cheese

## Directions

1. Spread one tablespoon of butter on the bottom of a baking dish or cake form and on top sprinkle two tablespoons of grated cheese, spreading it evenly over the entire dish
2. Carefully break four to six eggs on top, taking care not to break the yolks
3. Sprinkle with salt and pepper and pour one or two tablespoons of cream, then sprinkle a fairly thick layer of grated cheese on top
4. Place in a slow oven for fifteen to twenty minutes and serve

title: Jajecznica z Pomidorami (Scrambled Eggs with Tomatoes)
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup pickled tomatoes
- a pinch of baking soda
- one teaspoon butter
- three eggs
- salt
- pepper
- toast from white bread

## Directions

1. Take one cup of pickled tomatoes, add a pinch of baking soda and one teaspoon of butter, pour into a saucepan and bring to a boil
2. Then break three whole eggs into the tomatoes and cook briefly, stirring gently, until the eggs are set
3. Salt to taste, add a bit of pepper and serve at the table with toast, or arrange on toast made from large slices of white bread spread with butter

title: Jajecznica z Sardynkami (Scrambled Eggs with Sardines)
body:
## Ingredients

- one tablespoon butter
- two tablespoons cream or milk
- four eggs
- four sardines, cleaned of bones and skin, crumbled
- one half tablespoon Worcestershire sauce

## Directions
