1. Heat one tablespoon of butter with two tablespoons of cream or milk in a pan, and when it boils, break in four eggs and stir with a spoon over heat until the eggs just begin to set
2. Then add four cleaned and crumbled sardines and fry for a few more minutes
3. Pour in one half tablespoon of Worcestershire sauce and serve immediately at the table

title: Jajecznica z Serem (Scrambled Eggs with Cheese)
body:
## Ingredients

- cheese
- one teaspoon strong mustard
- one cup milk
- one teaspoon salt
- a bit of red pepper
- five eggs

## Directions

1. Slice cheese thinly and line the bottom and sides of a baking dish with it
2. Mix in a cup of milk one teaspoon of strong mustard with one teaspoon salt and a bit of red pepper, and pour half of this mixture over the arranged cheese
3. Carefully break five eggs so as not to disturb the yolks and pour over the cheese, then pour the rest of the milk mixture on top
4. Place in the oven for about ten minutes; when the eggs are set, remove and serve immediately at the table

title: Jajka Faszerowane (Stuffed Eggs)
body:
## Ingredients

- eight eggs
- two tablespoons cooked ham, chopped very finely
- two tablespoons vinegar
- salt and pepper to taste
- one teaspoon mustard

## Directions

1. Hard boil eight eggs for fifteen minutes.
2. Shell the eggs and cut each one in half crosswise.
3. Remove the yolks without damaging the whites and mash them into a paste.
4. Mix the yolks with two tablespoons of finely chopped cooked ham, two tablespoons of vinegar, salt, pepper to taste, and one teaspoon of mustard.
5. Mix everything well and fill the cavities in the whites left by the removed yolks.
6. Cut one end of the egg flat so it can stand.
7. Arrange on a platter and pour over a sauce made from the remaining filling thinned with vinegar.

title: Jajka Gotowane (Boiled Eggs)
body:
## Ingredients

- eggs
- water

## Directions

1. To cook eggs soft-boiled, drop them into boiling water and cook for two and a half minutes in summer, or three minutes in winter since eggs are usually colder then
2. Hard-boiled eggs are usually cooked for five minutes, though in recent times in America the custom of cooking for fifteen minutes has spread; it is claimed that eggs cooked this way are both more tasty and easier to digest

title: Jajka na Maśle (Eggs in Butter)
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- eggs

## Directions

1. Melt some butter in a pan, and when it is well heated, drop eggs into it, holding just above the pan so they don't break, and fry until the whites are set; the yolks should remain soft
2. To fry eggs better, it is recommended to place them in warm water for a few minutes before frying

title: Jajka na Occie (Eggs in Vinegar Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- one half tablespoon butter
- a bit of flour
- two tablespoons sour cream
- one tablespoon vinegar
- two tablespoons cold water
- salt to taste

## Directions

1. Put one half tablespoon of butter in a pan, and when it melts, sprinkle with a bit of flour
2. Pour in two tablespoons of sour cream, one tablespoon of vinegar and two tablespoons of cold water, add salt to taste and mix well over heat
3. When it comes to a boil, break in four to six eggs one by one, holding just above the pan
4. When the whites begin to set, cover and place the pan on low heat to cook gently
5. If the sauce is too sour, add a little sugar and serve at the table immediately

title: Jajka w Koszulkach (Eggs in Jackets)
body:
## Ingredients

- eggs
- water
- a tablespoon of vinegar

## Directions

1. If using special tin forms without a bottom, insert them in a pan with boiling water and slip one egg into each form, cook until the white sets, then remove with a slotted spoon
2. If forms are not available, pour at least an inch of water into a deep pan, add a tablespoon of vinegar and bring to boil
3. Slip eggs in very carefully so they do not spread across the pan
4. Before slipping in, hold the egg just above the water and lower it in slowly
5. When the white sets, remove with a slotted spoon and place on a plate in sorrel soup, borscht or broth

title: Jajka w Koszulkach po Amerykańsku (Poached Eggs American-Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- water
- one and a half tablespoons vinegar
- half a tablespoon salt
- eggs
- white bread
- butter

## Directions

1. Fill a deep frying pan halfway with water, add one and a half tablespoons of vinegar and half a tablespoon of salt
2. Place on heat, and when water boils, carefully pour cracked eggs onto the pan and cook until the whites set
3. Cut thin slices of white bread and dry them in a hot oven or with a toast-making machine until golden brown
4. While the toast is hot, spread with butter and place one egg on each slice and immediately serve on the table
5. For cooking eggs it is best to use special metal molds without a bottom, which are placed in the water; eggs cooked in them will not spread and will have a nice form

title: Jajka w Majonezie (Eggs in Mayonnaise)
body:
## Ingredients

- eggs
- mayonnaise sauce (thicker and more salted than that used for fish)
- one green plum, cut in half

## Directions

1. Hard boil eggs for at least five minutes.
2. Plunge into cold water.
3. Once completely cooled, shell and cut each egg in half with a sharp knife.
4. Prepare a mayonnaise sauce, thicker and more salted than that used for fish.
5. Dip each egg half in the mayonnaise sauce.
6. Arrange neatly on a plate with a halved green plum in the center.
7. Serve for breakfast or as a snack.

title: Jambalaya
body:
## Ingredients

- half pound of rice
- cold water
- half pound of sausage
- half pound of ham
- tablespoon of lard
- onion
- small red pepper pod
- several medium tomatoes
- several sprigs of parsley
- water

## Directions

1. Rinse half pound of rice and soak in cold water for one hour.
2. Cut half pound of sausage and half pound of ham into one-inch pieces.
3. Add to a pot with a tablespoon of lard, onion sliced into thin pieces, a small red pepper pod, several medium tomatoes, and several sprigs of parsley.
4. Fry for several minutes on low heat.
5. Add the rice, pour in half a quart of water, and braise on low heat under a cover for half an hour.

title: Jarzyny z Serem (Vegetables with Cheese)
body:
## Ingredients

- Italian cabbage in equal quantity
- young kohlrabi in equal quantity
- green peas in equal quantity
- butter
- grated parmesan cheese
- sugar
- water or milk for cooking

## Directions

1. Take equal quantities of Italian cabbage, young kohlrabi and green peas
2. First scald the cabbage and kohlrabi with boiling water, then cook all three vegetables separately in separate water or milk
3. Cut the cabbage and kohlrabi finely
4. When all three vegetables are tender, drain them and sprinkle with a small amount of sugar
5. Put some butter in a pot, place a layer of cabbage on it, then again some butter and sprinkle with grated parmesan
6. Then arrange a layer of kohlrabi, butter and parmesan
7. Finally peas, butter and parmesan
8. There should be six layers total, meaning two of each vegetable
9. Pour everything with butter, sprinkle with parmesan and put in a moderate oven for one hour
10. Then arrange on a serving dish, pour with butter browned with bread roll and serve

title: Jarzyny à la Macedoine (Vegetables à la Macedoine)
body:
## Ingredients

- kohlrabi cut into equal cubes
- carrots cut into equal cubes
- water
- green peas fresh or canned
- green beans in pods cut into half-inch pieces
- one tablespoon butter
- one teaspoon flour
- triangular bread slices
- sour cream with egg yolk
- butter for frying
- sugar

## Directions

1. Cut equal quantities of kohlrabi and carrots into cubes
2. Cover with water, boil, drain the water and pour in fresh hot water
3. Cook until tender
4. Separately cook green peas, fresh or canned
5. When tender, drain and add to the carrots and kohlrabi
6. Similarly cook separately green beans in pods cut into half-inch pieces and also add to the vegetables
7. Season everything with one tablespoon butter browned with one teaspoon flour
8. Mix, boil and arrange on a serving dish
9. Around it arrange triangular bread slices dipped in sour cream with egg yolk, fried in butter and sprinkled with a small amount of sugar

title: Jelly Roll
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup flour
- one cup sugar
- one and one-half teaspoons baking powder
- three eggs
- six tablespoons hot water
- powdered sugar for dusting
- marmalade or fruit jelly

## Directions

1. Mix thoroughly one cup flour, one cup sugar, and one and one-half teaspoons baking powder
2. Beat three eggs and mix with the flour mixture
3. Finally, pour in six tablespoons hot water and beat everything thoroughly into a smooth batter
4. Pour into a flat pan to a depth of only one-quarter inch
5. Bake in a slow oven
6. After baking, turn out onto a sheet of paper sprinkled with powdered sugar
7. Take marmalade or fruit jelly, thin it with a fork, and spread thickly on the baked cake
8. Roll up into a log, wrap in cloth, and place in a cold spot
9. Must be rolled while hot, otherwise the cake will crack

title: Kaczka Dzika Pieczona (Roasted Wild Duck)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 wild duck
- vinegar
- roots
- butter

## Directions

1. To make the wild duck tender and soft, soak it for a couple of hours in vinegar that has been boiled with roots and then cooled
2. Roast, basting with butter, like any other game
3. You can also lard it with bacon

title: Kaczka Pieczona (Roasted Duck)
body:
## Ingredients

- duck, older and well-fattened
- apples
- marjoram

## Directions

1. Very young and lean ducks are not suitable for roasting, as they have very little meat, so it is best to take older and well-fattened ducks
2. Prepare and clean, then stuff with apples and marjoram and roast exactly the same as goose

title: Kaczka Pieczona po Amerykańsku (American-Style Roasted Duck)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 young duck
- salt
- 1/2 quart grated white bread, preferably stale
- 1/2 teaspoon salt
- 1/4 teaspoon pepper
- 1/2 tablespoon chopped fresh parsley
- 1 tablespoon chopped onion
- 1/2 teaspoon powdered sage
- 1/3 cup butter
- flour
- water
- lemon juice
- red currant jelly
- butter
- flour
- water
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Pluck, clean, and wipe the duck inside with salt
2. Prepare the stuffing: take 1/2 quart of grated white bread, preferably stale; add 1/2 teaspoon salt, 1/4 teaspoon pepper, 1/2 tablespoon chopped fresh parsley, 1 tablespoon chopped onion, 1/2 teaspoon powdered sage, and 1/3 cup butter; mix everything together well
3. Stuff the duck with this mixture
4. Wipe the top with salt and then with butter, and finally sprinkle with flour so that a coating forms from the butter and flour
5. Place in a very hot oven for a few minutes so the flour browns slightly
6. Then pour some water into the roasting pan and roast for one hour, frequently basting and adding fresh water as it evaporates, but add it gradually
7. When one side is well browned, turn to the other side
8. Before serving, sprinkle with lemon juice, place on a platter, and pour the sauce from the roasting pan over it
9. Separately serve red currant jelly and giblet sauce prepared as follows: chop the giblets finely and cook them covered on very low heat with 1 tablespoon of butter mixed with flour and a very small amount of water, salt, and pepper

title: Kaczka Pieczona po Amerykańsku (Roasted Duck American Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- young duck
- half a quart grated white bread, preferably stale
- half a teaspoon salt
- quarter teaspoon pepper
- half a spoon chopped fresh parsley
- one teaspoon chopped onion
- half a teaspoon powdered sage
- one-third cup butter
- flour

## Directions

1. Clean the young duck, clean it and wipe the inside with salt, then make the following filling: Take half a quart of grated white bread, preferably stale; add half a teaspoon of salt, quarter teaspoon of pepper, half a spoon of chopped fresh parsley, one teaspoon of chopped onion, half a teaspoon of powdered sage and one-third cup of butter; mix all this properly and stuff the duck with this filling
2. Then wipe the top with salt and then with butter, and finally sprinkle with flour to form a coating from the butter and flour; place in the oven

title: Kakao (Cocoa)
body:
## Ingredients

- three tablespoons cocoa
- two tablespoons sugar
- one cup boiling water
- three cups hot milk
- half cup cream
- vanilla extract (optional)
- one egg (optional)
- half cup hot water (optional)

## Directions

1. Mix three tablespoons cocoa with two tablespoons sugar, pour into a saucepan and add one cup of boiling water, pouring slowly
2. Cook for five minutes from the moment it boils
3. Then add three cups of hot milk and half cup cream, mix and serve
4. If desired, add some vanilla extract; some also add one egg, beaten with half cup of hot water

title: Kakao po Holendersku (Dutch-Style Cocoa)
body:
## Ingredients

- equal measure cocoa and sugar
- two teaspoons of the mixture per cup
- boiling water
- cream or milk (to taste)

## Directions

1. Mix equal amounts of cocoa and sugar well
2. For each cup, take two teaspoons of this mixture and pour boiling water over it
3. Add some cream or milk to each cup
4. Can be prepared separately in cups or in one large vessel
5. Water must be boiling

title: Kalafiory (Cauliflower)
body:
## Ingredients

- cauliflower heads
- salt
- water
- small amount of baking soda
- 1 teaspoon sugar
- brown butter sauce

## Directions

1. In a large pot, bring water to boil.
2. Add peeled and cleaned cauliflower and cook for a few minutes.
3. Pour off the water, add fresh boiling salted water and cook until tender, which should take about twenty minutes.
4. To ease cooking, add a small amount of baking soda and 1 teaspoon sugar to the water.
5. Lay on a serving platter and pour brown butter sauce over it.

title: Kalafiory au Gratin (Cauliflower au Gratin)
body:
## Ingredients

- cauliflower
- salt
- water
- butter
- grated cheese, preferably Parmesan
- béchamel sauce

## Directions

1. Cook cauliflower in salted water as described above.
2. Remove from water and place in another pot in which melt a little butter and braise slightly on low heat.
3. Sprinkle the entire cauliflower from all sides with grated cheese, preferably Parmesan.
4. Pour béchamel sauce over it, sprinkle again with cheese, pour good butter over everything.
5. Place in a hot oven to brown.
6. Serve with the pot.
7. Best baked in a deep serving dish or nice porcelain bowl.

title: Kalafiory z Białym Sosem (Cauliflower with White Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 large cauliflower
- salt
- water
- 1 tablespoon butter
- 1 tablespoon flour
- 1 1/2 cups cold water
- 1/4 teaspoon salt
- pepper
- 1 egg yolk
- 1 tablespoon cold water

## Directions

1. Place a large cauliflower stem side up in a pot, cover with cold salted water and cook for an hour.
2. After cooking, drain and place on a hot serving platter.
3. In a pan, make a roux with 1 tablespoon butter and 1 tablespoon flour.
4. Gradually add 1 1/2 cups cold water and cook on low heat, constantly stirring, until a smooth sauce forms.
5. Add 1/4 teaspoon salt and some pepper.
6. Remove from heat and add 1 egg yolk well beaten, mixed with 1 tablespoon cold water.
7. Mix and pour over the cauliflower.
8. Serve immediately.

title: Kalarepa (Kohlrabi)
body:
## Ingredients

- young kohlrabi
- water
- butter
- butter
- flour
- salt to taste
- a little sugar
- fresh chopped parsley

## Directions

1. Cut young kohlrabi into slices, cover with water and bring to a boil, then drain the water, add a little butter and braise until tender, then bring to a boil a little butter with flour on white, add salt to taste, a little sugar and fresh chopped parsley and serve on the table.

title: Kalarepa Faszerowana (Stuffed Kohlrabi)
body:
## Ingredients

- kohlrabi
- fat mutton
- bread soaked in milk
- salt
- pepper
- butter
- flour
- burnt sugar

## Directions

1. Peel the kohlrabi, cut off the top, and hollow out the middle.
2. Mince raw fatty mutton, add bread soaked in milk and then squeezed out, salt and pepper to taste.
3. Stuff the hollowed kohlrabi with this filling and cover with the cut-off tops.
4. Place butter in a broad pan and arrange the kohlrabi one next to another.
5. Braise covered until tender, pouring water on it from time to time.
6. Just before serving, season with butter mixed with flour and add a little burnt sugar for color.
7. For six persons use twelve kohlrabi and one pound of mutton.

title: Kalarepa Faszerowana (Stuffed Kohlrabi)
body:
## Ingredients

- kohlrabi
- fat lamb
- bread soaked in milk
- salt to taste
- pepper to taste

## Directions

1. In late summer and in autumn, when kohlrabi is too hard to cook in the usual way, it is served stuffed in the following manner: Peel the kohlrabi, cut off the top, and hollow out the middle.
2. Finely chop raw fatty lamb, add bread soaked in milk and then squeezed out, salt and pepper to taste and fill the hollowed kohlrabi with this stuffing.

title: Kalteszal (Kalteszal)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart beer
- 1/4 pound sugar
- small glass beer
- juice of 1 lemon
- handful of raisins without seeds
- 1/4 teaspoon powdered cinnamon
- a few whole pieces of cinnamon
- handful of grated rye bread
- 1 small glass red wine or 1 glass arak
- small handful bread cut into small cubes
- ice (optional)

## Directions

1. For four people, take 1 quart of beer. Put 1/4 pound of sugar in a bowl, pour a small glass of beer, squeeze the juice of 1 lemon.
2. Scald a handful of raisins without seeds with water, rinse, pour off the water and throw the raisins into the bowl. Also add 1/4 teaspoon of powdered cinnamon and a few pieces of whole cinnamon, a handful of grated rye bread and 1 small glass of red wine, or instead of wine 1 glass of arak.
3. Mix all this well in the bowl and add the rest of the beer, then place on ice.
4. Dry in an oven a handful of bread cut into small cubes and just before serving, throw into the bowl.
5. Note: To make the kalteszal colder, you can place a piece of ice in the bowl.

title: Kanapki z Oliwek (Olive Sandwiches)
body:
## Ingredients

- rye bread, sliced thinly
- butter
- salt
- pepper
- olives, pitted and finely chopped
- celery, finely chopped, equal amount to olives
- small pickled cucumber, finely chopped
- salt
- pepper
- one quarter teaspoon mustard
- optional: meat or sausage slices

## Directions

1. Slice rye bread thinly
2. Spread with butter
3. Sprinkle with salt and pepper
4. Remove pits from olives
5. Chop olives finely
6. Mix with an equal amount of finely chopped celery
7. Add one finely chopped small pickled cucumber
8. Add salt, pepper, and one quarter teaspoon mustard
9. Mix well
10. Spread on buttered bread
11. Cover with another slice of bread
12. Optionally, place a slice of meat or sausage on the bread before adding the olive mixture

title: Kanapki z Pomidorów (Tomato Sandwiches)
body:
## Ingredients

- two medium tomatoes
- one small onion, finely chopped
- quarter cup cooked ham, finely chopped
- one tablespoon butter
- two eggs, well beaten
- salt
- pepper
- mustard
- white bread, sliced
- butter for spreading
- optional: hot oven or pan-frying

## Directions

1. Scald tomatoes with boiling water, peel, and chop finely
2. Chop one small onion very finely
3. Chop one quarter cup of cooked ham finely
4. Put one tablespoon of butter in a saucepan
5. When melted, mix in tomatoes, ham, and onion
6. Stew covered for ten minutes
7. Remove from heat
8. Add two well-beaten eggs
9. Add salt and pepper to taste
10. Return to low heat and cook until the mixture sets
11. Remove from heat
12. Spread on slices of white bread that have been spread with a bit of butter and lightly browned in a hot oven or fried in a pan

title: Kanapki z Sera (Cheese Canapes)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup cheese, grated
- 1/4 teaspoon salt
- red pepper to taste
- white bread

## Directions

1. Grate the cheese and add salt and red pepper
2. Cut white bread into round slices
3. Sprinkle each slice with cheese, salt, and pepper
4. Place on a baking sheet and put in a moderately hot oven
5. When the cheese melts, remove from the oven and serve immediately

title: Kanapki z Szynki (Ham Canapes)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 cup finely chopped cooked ham
- 2 small tablespoons butter
- 1 teaspoon chopped green parsley
- white bread

## Directions

1. Mix the ham, butter, and chopped parsley into a smooth mass
2. Cut white bread into thin slices of any shape
3. Fry lightly in butter until golden
4. Spread the ham mixture on the fried bread

title: Kapelusiki Kolonialne (Colonial Hat Pastries)
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup of currants without seeds
- one citron
- one cup of sugar
- one beaten egg
- a pinch of salt
- flour
- lard
- cold water
- sweetened milk

## Directions

1. Chop finely one cup of currants without seeds; squeeze juice from one citron and grate the peel on a grater; add one cup of sugar, one beaten egg, a pinch of salt; mix everything well
2. Make a medium dough from flour, lard and cold water, roll out into thin circles like pasta, about six inches in diameter
3. Place a spoonful or more of the prepared currant mixture on each circle and fold the dough from three sides, giving it the shape of a triangular hat; seal the edges tightly
4. Brush on top with sweetened milk and place in the oven for fifteen to twenty minutes

title: Kapelusiki Kolonialne (Colonial Hats)
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup seedless raisins
- one lemon
- one cup sugar
- one egg, beaten
- a pinch of salt
- flour, lard, and cold water for dough

## Directions

1. Chop finely one cup seedless raisins
2. Squeeze juice from one lemon and grate the zest
3. Add one cup sugar, one beaten egg, and a pinch of salt and mix well
4. Make a medium dough with flour, lard, and cold water
5. Roll into thin circles about six inches in diameter
6. Place a spoon or more of the raisin filling on each circle
7. Fold the dough from three sides to form a triangular hat shape
8. Seal the edges tightly
9. Brush the top with sweetened milk and place in the oven

title: Kapusta Kwaszona Postna (Sauerkraut for Fasting)
body:
## Ingredients

- sauerkraut
- butter
- dried mushrooms
- water
- onion

## Directions

1. Squeeze the sauerkraut out of its liquid and place in a pan in which you have previously dissolved a tablespoon of butter.
2. Cook a few dried mushrooms in a small amount of water, pour the liquid into the cabbage, and finely chop the mushrooms and add them as well.
3. Also add some finely chopped onion.
4. Mix everything and braise until completely tender.
5. On serving season with butter browned with flour, bring to a boil and serve.
6. In the same way cabbage can be prepared with lard or smoked meat.

title: Kapusta na Biało (Cabbage in White Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- cabbage
- water
- salt
- cream or milk
- butter
- pepper
- flour

## Directions

1. Chop cabbage fairly finely, place in a pan, salt and pour water.
2. Cook for about fifteen minutes, then drain the water and finish cooking in fresh water.
3. When cabbage is tender, pour in a cup of cream or milk, add half a tablespoon of butter, salt and pepper to taste.
4. Season with a teaspoon of flour mixed first in a small amount of cold water.
5. Bring to a boil once more and serve.

title: Kapusta na Kwaśno (Sour Cabbage)
body:
## Ingredients

- cabbage head
- water
- broth
- vinegar
- salt
- pepper
- fat or butter or lard
- sugar
- flour
- sour apple

## Directions

1. Shred a cabbage head, throw into boiling water and let stand about ten minutes, then drain the cabbage and press the water out of it.
2. For a medium head of cabbage pour into a pan half a quart of hot broth mixed half with water, a teaspoon of strong vinegar, two tablespoons of fat, butter or lard.
3. Add the cabbage, salt and pepper to taste and braise on low heat covered for one and a half or two hours.
4. Just before serving, when completely tender, sprinkle in a teaspoon of sugar and season with a tablespoon of flour browned with a tablespoon of butter.
5. Some use finely chopped sour apples instead of vinegar.

title: Kapusta Panieńska (Maiden's Cabbage)
body:
## Ingredients

- white cabbage
- water
- eggs
- butter
- salt
- pepper
- cream

## Directions

1. Quarter a head of white cabbage, pour water in a pan and cook for fifteen minutes.
2. Drain all the water and pour fresh boiling water in which to continue cooking the cabbage until tender.
3. Turn out onto a sieve and let cool.
4. When completely cold, chop finely, add two beaten eggs, a tablespoon of butter, salt and pepper to taste and three tablespoons of cream.
5. Mix well and place in a buttered baking mold and set in the oven.
6. When well browned, remove from the oven and serve immediately, best of all in the mold.

title: Kapusta Włoska (Italian Cabbage)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 head Italian cabbage
- butter or lard
- broth
- 1/2 tablespoon butter
- a small amount of flour
- 1/4 clove garlic, grated
- 2 pinches fine sugar

## Directions

1. Peel the head of Italian cabbage from outer leaves, wash and cut into quarters.
2. Pour cold water in a large pot and bring to boil several times.
3. Pour off the water a few minutes after boiling, remove the cabbage and place in a small pot with 1 tablespoon butter.
4. Pour a little broth over and braise on low heat for about three quarters of an hour, being careful not to let it burn.
5. When tender, make a roux with 1/2 tablespoon butter and a small amount of flour.
6. Add with 1/4 clove grated garlic, pour over the cabbage, sprinkle with 2 pinches fine sugar, mix well.
7. Bring to boil once and serve.

title: Kapusta z Pomidorami (Cabbage with Tomatoes)
body:
## Ingredients

- cabbage head
- onion
- water
- salt
- pepper
- lard or butter
- tomatoes
- flour
- sour apple

## Directions

1. Shred a medium cabbage head and chop one large onion finely.
2. Pour boiling water and let stand for fifteen minutes, then press the cabbage out of the water.
3. Add to it a quarter pound of finely chopped and lightly fried lard, or a large tablespoon of butter.
4. Cover and braise on very low heat with a small amount of water.
5. When the cabbage has cooked this way for ten or fifteen minutes, add four medium tomatoes cut into pieces.
6. Cover and braise further until tender, frequently shaking the pan and stirring so it does not burn.
7. Just before serving season with a tablespoon of flour browned with a tablespoon of butter.
8. If the cabbage is not sufficiently sour, add a thinly sliced sour apple a quarter hour before serving.

title: Kapuśniak Angielski (English Cabbage Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 head of cabbage
- 1 onion
- celery
- parsley
- dill
- ham bone or veal bone
- butter
- 1/2 quart tomatoes, cut into cubes
- beef broth
- flour or lard

## Directions

1. Cut not too finely 1/2 head of cabbage
2. Finely chop onion, celery, parsley and some dill
3. Place everything in a pot with a piece of ham or veal bone
4. Add some butter and braise on low heat for twenty minutes
5. Add 1/2 quart of tomatoes cut into cubes
6. Cover with previously cooked beef broth and cook for one hour
7. Just before serving, fry some flour with butter or lard and thicken the soup with it

title: Kapuśniak Hygieniczny (Hygienic Cabbage Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef broth with vegetables
- sauerkraut
- butter
- buckwheat groats

## Directions

1. Cook ordinary broth with vegetables
2. Squeeze sauerkraut firmly of salt and juice, chop finely
3. Place in a pot with butter
4. Add some broth and cook until tender
5. Place the cooked cabbage in a dish and dilute with broth
6. Serve with fried buckwheat groats prepared as follows: Cook coarse buckwheat groats to tenderness, pour onto a board, roll it flat and let it cool
7. Once the groats have cooled well, cut it with a knife into long pieces and fry in a pan in butter or young lard

title: Kapuśniak Polski (Polish Cabbage Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- sauerkraut
- smoked meat (wędzonka)
- water
- butter
- flour
- salt and pepper to taste

## Directions

1. Finely chop a sufficient amount of sauerkraut
2. Place in a pot with a piece of smoked meat
3. Cover with water and cook until the meat is tender
4. Remove the meat, cut into cubes and return to the soup
5. Fry some butter with flour and add it
6. Add salt and pepper to taste and serve

title: Kapuśniak Wiejski (Village Cabbage Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh cabbage
- 1/2 pound lard (słonina)
- 3 onions
- broth made from bones and one pig's ear
- salt, pepper and vinegar to taste

## Directions

1. Finely chop fresh cabbage and let it lie on a board for half an hour or longer
2. Cut 1/2 pound of lard into small cubes, place in a pot and render
3. Then add three chopped onions to the lard and fry until they turn glassy, being careful not to brown them
4. Place the cabbage in the same pot, cover and braise on low heat until the cabbage begins to shine, stirring frequently to prevent it from burning
5. Have broth prepared on bones and one pig's ear
6. Dilute the cabbage with this broth and bring to a boil together
7. Cut the cooked ear into cubes, add salt, pepper and vinegar to taste and serve

title: Kapuśniak z Rybami (Cabbage Soup with Fish)
body:
## Ingredients

- sour cabbage
- salt
- pepper
- water
- fish
- butter
- flour
- sour cream (if available)

## Directions

1. Chop finely a sufficient amount of sour cabbage, add salt and pepper to taste, pour water over it and cook until the cabbage is soft
2. Then add fish and cook further until the fish is cooked through
3. Remove the fish, chop into small pieces and put back into the cabbage soup
4. Brown a spoonful of butter with flour and thicken the soup with it
5. If there is sour cream, use it to whiten the cabbage soup and serve

title: Kapuśniak z Ryżem (Cabbage Soup with Rice)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef or pork broth made from bones
- 1 small head of cabbage
- 1 onion
- 1 tablespoon butter
- salt to taste
- rice
- 1 clove garlic (optional)
- grated Parmesan or Swiss cheese (optional)

## Directions

1. Cook ordinary broth on beef or pork bones
2. Finely chop a small head of cabbage and cut one onion into thin slices
3. Place both with 1 tablespoon of butter in a pot, cover and braise for about twenty minutes
4. Then dilute with broth and add salt to taste
5. Cook some rice separately and add it to the soup
6. Those who like it can add one clove of garlic and grated Parmesan or Swiss cheese and serve

title: Kapuśniak z Ryżem (Cabbage Soup with Rice)
body:
## Ingredients

- cabbage head
- butter
- onion
- mixed vegetables (włoszczyzna)
- salt
- pepper
- rice
- garlic clove
- grated cheese

## Directions

1. Chop finely a cabbage head, put into a pot with a spoonful of butter and thinly sliced onion, and stew covered for about twenty minutes
2. Separately cook a broth from mixed vegetables, use it to thin the cabbage
3. Add salt and pepper to taste and place on fire
4. Then add separately cooked rice, also a clove of garlic and a bit of grated cheese, bring to boil and serve

title: Kapuśniak z Ryżem (Cabbage Soup with Rice)
body:
## Ingredients

- one head of cabbage
- one tablespoon of butter
- one onion, sliced thin
- vegetable broth made from mixed vegetables
- salt and pepper to taste
- separately cooked rice
- one clove of garlic
- some grated cheese

## Directions

1. Chop the cabbage finely and place in a pot with one tablespoon of butter and thinly sliced onion
2. Braise covered for about twenty minutes
3. Separately prepare a broth from mixed vegetables and use it to deglaze the cabbage
4. Add salt and pepper to taste and place on the fire
5. Add separately cooked rice, a clove of garlic, and some grated cheese
6. Bring to a boil and serve

title: Karasie Smażone (Fried Crucian Carp)
body:
## Ingredients

- crucian carp
- salt
- egg
- breadcrumbs
- butter
- sour cream

## Directions

1. Clean and salt the fish an hour before; after removing from salt, dry it carefully with paper, dip in egg beaten with water and then coat all around with grated breadcrumbs.
2. Put a piece of butter on a frying pan, and when it browns, place the fish on it and fry until golden on both sides.
3. Then pour a couple of spoonfuls of sour cream onto the pan, fry a moment longer, transfer to a serving dish and pour the sauce over it.

title: Karasie ze Śmietaną (Crucian Carp with Sour Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- crucian carp
- salt
- mixed vegetables
- butter
- flour
- sour cream
- lemon
- sugar
- dill
- potatoes
- noodles

## Directions

1. Clean and salt the crucian carp an hour before cooking.
2. Prepare a broth from various vegetables, strain and cool it; there should be just enough broth to barely cover the fish.
3. After removing the fish from salt, place it in a pot, pour the broth with vegetables over it and cook on high heat until tender, about fifteen minutes.
4. In a separate pot, mix a spoonful of butter with a spoonful of flour, add about three spoonfuls of sour cream, squeeze in about a quarter of a lemon, add a little sugar and some chopped dill.
5. Dilute with the broth from the fish, bring to a boil together and then pour this sauce over the fish arranged on a serving dish.
6. Serve with boiled potatoes and small noodles cooked in salted water and dressed with butter.

title: Karczochy (Artichokes)
body:
## Ingredients

- water
- butter
- vinegar
- oil

## Directions

1. Pour water into a saucepan and bring to a boil, then drain this water
2. Pour in fresh hot water and cook until tender
3. Serve hot with melted butter, or serve cold with vinegar and oil

title: Karp na Szaro (Carp in Gray Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- carp
- salt
- vinegar
- vegetables (miscellaneous)
- pepper
- English herbs

## Directions

1. If the carp is alive, kill it and let the blood drain into boiled and salted vinegar, as blood is very good for sauce.
2. Cut the cleaned carp into rings and salt it; do not discard the milt, but salt it together.
3. Cook stock from miscellaneous vegetables available, with the addition of pepper and English herbs, then strain and cool.
4. Arrange the cut fish in a pan and pour cold vegetable stock over it.

title: Karp na Szaro (Gray Carp)
body:
## Ingredients

- carp
- vinegar
- salt
- mixed vegetables (whatever is available)
- pepper
- English herbs
- rye bread crust
- lemon peel
- butter
- flour
- white wine
- sugar
- burnt sugar for color
- currants without seeds

## Directions

1. If the carp is alive, stun it and drain the blood into boiled and salted vinegar, as the blood is very good for the sauce.
2. Cut the cleaned carp into pieces and salt it; do not discard the soft roe, but salt it together.
3. Prepare a broth from various vegetables with the addition of pepper and English herbs, then strain and cool it.
4. Arrange the cut fish in a pot, pour cold broth from the vegetables over it, add a little rye bread crust and lemon peel, and cook until the fish is tender.
5. In a separate pot, brown a spoonful of butter with a spoonful of flour until golden, dilute with the broth in which the fish was cooked, add a glass of white wine, a few pieces of sugar and a teaspoon of burnt sugar for color.
6. Carefully wash a handful of currants without seeds and cook until the sauce thickens and becomes sufficiently thick.
7. Pour this sauce over the fish arranged on a serving dish and garnish with lemon slices.

title: Karp z Winem (Carp with Wine)
body:
## Ingredients

- carp
- salt
- vinegar
- parsley root
- celery stalks
- pickled cucumbers
- bay leaf
- peppercorns
- English herbs
- red wine
- cucumber pickle

## Directions

1. Clean the carp, salt it and pour strong vinegar over it.
2. For a three-pound fish, take two large parsley roots, a couple of celery stalks, two pickled cucumbers, chop everything into small pieces and place together with the carp in a pot.
3. Add a bay leaf, several peppercorns and English herbs.
4. Mix red wine half and half with the brine from the cucumbers, pour over the carp and place on low heat to cook slowly covered.
5. When the fish is tender, remove it, arrange on a serving dish and pour the sauce remaining in the pot over it.
6. The carp should be cooked whole; some cook it with the scales, claiming that cooked this way it is more delicious.

title: Kartoflanka Amerykańska (American Potato Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- milk
- celery stalks
- one onion
- potatoes
- water
- butter
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Cook together in a quart of milk a couple of celery stalks and one onion
2. Separately cook in water a few potatoes until completely soft
3. Drain the water and mash the potatoes properly
4. Add to the soup along with a spoonful of fresh butter, salt and pepper to taste
5. Strain everything through a sieve and serve

title: Kartoflanka Mleczna (Milk Potato Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- two large cooked potatoes
- one onion
- two spoonfuls tapioca
- a few grains allspice
- cold water
- hot milk
- butter
- salt
- white pepper

## Directions

1. Pass two large cooked potatoes through a sieve
2. Chop very finely or mince one onion
3. Put the above in a pot, add two spoonfuls of tapioca and a few grains of allspice, pour three half-quarts of cold water over everything and cook until the onion is soft
4. Then add half quart of hot milk, one spoonful of butter, salt and white pepper to taste
5. Bring to boil once more and serve
6. This proportion is for five people

title: Kartoflanka Szkocka (Scottish Potato Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb broth made from scraps and bones
- various finely chopped vegetables
- few grains of pepper
- 2 bay leaves
- 1 quart of potatoes, cut up
- lard (słonina)
- 1 onion
- 1 tablespoon flour
- fresh dill
- salt

## Directions

1. Cook broth on lamb meat—can be scraps and bones
2. After proper skimming, add plenty of various finely chopped vegetables, a few grains of pepper, two bay leaves and 1 quart of cut potatoes
3. Cook until potatoes are tender
4. Fry some lard cut into small cubes in a pan, fry one onion in it, thicken with 1 tablespoon of flour
5. Add chopped dill, add this to the soup, bring to a boil and serve

title: Kartoflanka z Porami (Potato Soup with Leeks)
body:
## Ingredients

- half dozen young leeks
- butter
- potatoes
- bay leaf
- peppercorns
- salt
- flour
- parsley or dill

## Directions

1. Chop half dozen young leeks into cubes and stew in butter
2. Separately cook several peeled and sliced potatoes, adding to them a bay leaf, a few peppercorns and salt to taste
3. When the potatoes are soft, add the stewed leeks
4. On the remaining butter brown half spoonful of flour, pour also into the soup, bring to boil and pour into a bowl, into which sprinkle chopped parsley or dill

title: Kartoflanka z Zacierką (Potato Soup with Paste)
body:
## Ingredients

- mixed vegetables (włoszczyzna)
- potatoes
- wheat flour
- one egg
- cold water
- butter
- onion
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Cook a broth from mixed vegetables, strain it and use it to pour over potatoes sliced into thin pieces and cook until soft
2. Make a mixture of wheat flour with one egg and cold water into a thin batter and use it to thicken the soup
3. Just before serving, fry in butter and nicely brown a spoonful of finely chopped onion and use it to season the soup

title: Kartofle a la Chateaubriand (Potatoes Chateaubriand Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- young potatoes
- three ounces of butter

## Directions

1. scrape a quart of young potatoes, which should not be larger than walnuts
2. put into a pan, add three ounces of butter and braise covered on low heat until tender, which takes about twenty minutes

title: Kartofle a la Chateaubriand (Potatoes Chateaubriand Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart young potatoes, no larger than a walnut
- 3 ounces butter
- salt

## Directions

1. Scrub a quart of young potatoes, which should not be larger than a walnut.
2. Pour into a pan, add 3 ounces of butter and braise covered on low heat until soft, which will take about twenty minutes.
3. Shake the pan from time to time so they do not burn.
4. Arrange on a serving dish, sprinkle with salt and chopped fresh dill and serve.

title: Kartofle a la Delmonico (Potatoes Delmonico Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- raw potatoes
- butter
- salt
- pepper
- chopped onion
- chopped fresh parsley
- milk

## Directions

1. peel raw potatoes and cut into very small cubes
2. butter a baking dish and spread a layer of potatoes on the bottom, sprinkle with salt, pepper, chopped onion and chopped fresh parsley, and scatter some small pieces of butter
3. add a second similar layer and continue until the dish is full
4. pour milk over everything, though it should not completely cover the potatoes
5. place in a moderately hot oven for forty to fifty minutes
6. if the potatoes dry out too much during baking, add a little more milk
7. for a quart of cut potatoes use two tablespoons of butter, one teaspoon of salt, one tablespoon of chopped onion and half a tablespoon of parsley

title: Kartofle a la Dixie (Potatoes Dixie Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 large potatoes
- salted water
- butter
- 1/2 cup heavy cream
- salt
- white pepper

## Directions

1. Boil six large potatoes in salted water until soft.
2. Drain the water and leave the pan on the fire for a moment so the potatoes dry.
3. Mash with a masher until smooth.
4. Add butter, half a cup of cream, some salt and white pepper.
5. Beat with a spoon until the potatoes are white.
6. Arrange them on a small metal plate in the form of a pyramid.
7. Place a piece of butter on top and put in a hot oven.
8. When the top forms a crust and browns well, remove from the oven and serve. Can be served on a serving dish or with the plate in which they were baked.

title: Kartofle a la Duchesse (Potatoes Duchess Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- floury potatoes
- one tablespoon of butter
- salt and pepper to taste
- one egg
- three tablespoons of sour cream or milk

## Directions

1. peel and cook the floury potatoes until tender
2. mash them thoroughly with the addition of butter, salt and pepper for half a quart of such potatoes
3. beat one egg well, add three tablespoons of sour cream or milk and also add to the potatoes
4. after proper mixing, form small cutlets half an inch thick from this mass
5. brush with egg white or milk and place on a baking sheet in the oven
6. keep them there until they are well browned

title: Kartofle a la Kentucky (Potatoes Kentucky Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- cold water
- salt
- pepper
- milk
- butter

## Directions

1. Peel potatoes and cut raw into thin slices and place in cold water for half an hour.
2. Drain the water, place the potatoes in a pan, salt and pepper them.
3. Pour enough milk to not completely cover the potatoes and place in a moderately hot oven for half an hour.
4. Before serving, add half a spoon of good butter.

title: Kartofle a la Maitre d'Hotel (Potatoes Maitre d'Hotel)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 tablespoon good butter
- 1 teaspoon chopped parsley
- pepper
- lemon juice
- cooked potatoes
- flour
- bouillon

## Directions

1. Mix a tablespoon of good butter raw with a teaspoon of chopped parsley, add some pepper and lemon juice, then place in a pan.
2. When the butter dissolves, add cooked potatoes sliced into pieces, sprinkle some flour, add a couple of tablespoons of bouillon and braise briefly on low heat.
3. These potatoes cannot be made for more than two or three people at once; serve with beef steak.

title: Kartofle a la Maryland (Potatoes Maryland Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 medium potatoes
- cold salted water
- milk
- butter
- salt
- chopped fresh parsley
- flour (if needed)

## Directions

1. Peel and cut into thin slices six medium potatoes.
2. Cover with cold salted water and let sit for fifteen minutes.
3. Drain the potatoes well through a colander.
4. Place them in a small pan and add enough milk to cover the potatoes.
5. Place on low heat and cook covered until completely soft.
6. Add some butter, salt to taste and finely chopped fresh parsley and serve.
7. If the potatoes look watery after cooking, they can be thickened slightly with a bit of flour.

title: Kartofle a la Waldorf (Potatoes Waldorf Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked cold potatoes
- melted butter
- flour

## Directions

1. Cut cooked cold potatoes into cubes.
2. Dip them in melted butter and then lightly coat in flour.
3. Arrange them on a sheet or baking dish and place in a hot oven for fifteen minutes.
4. Serve immediately.

title: Kartofle au Gratin (Potatoes au Gratin)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a quarter of cold boiled potatoes
- half a cup of grated cheese
- one cup white sauce
- half a cup grated breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Cut into half-inch cubes half a quarter of cold potatoes boiled in salted water
2. Pour into a flat dish, preferably a clay or earthenware bowl
3. Sprinkle half a cup of grated cheese on top
4. Cover with one cup of white sauce
5. Sprinkle half a cup of grated breadcrumbs on top
6. Place in a moderately hot oven and bake until the breadcrumbs are well browned
7. For the white sauce: boil one cup of milk in a double boiler; in another pan melt one tablespoon of butter until it begins to foam, then add one tablespoon of cornstarch and mix well, then pour one third cup of the boiled milk into the butter and mix to a smooth paste; add the remaining milk, stirring constantly until a smooth mass without lumps is obtained; season the sauce as desired with onion juice, parsley, mushrooms, curry powder and any other seasonings desired

title: Kartofle Książęce (Princely Potatoes)
body:
## Ingredients

- leftover potato puree from the previous day
- egg
- melted butter

## Directions

1. Use leftover potato puree from the previous day - cooked mashed potatoes which have hardened so they can be cut into strips.
2. Cut the potatoes with a knife into strips two inches long, one inch wide and half an inch thick.
3. Beat one egg on one small plate and pour some melted butter on another.
4. Carefully take the cut potatoes with a knife, dip first in butter, then in egg.
5. Take the prepared potato strips with a knife and place on a butter-smeared sheet.
6. Place in a hot oven and after several minutes remove and serve immediately.

title: Kartofle na Sposób Kolonialny (Potatoes Colonial Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- six medium raw potatoes
- butter
- salt
- pepper
- four tablespoons hot sour cream

## Directions

1. Peel and cut into quarter-inch thick slices six medium raw potatoes
2. Place in a baking pan or tin
3. Place several pieces of butter, salt and pepper to taste on top
4. Place in a hot oven; when they brown well
5. Transfer to a serving dish and pour over with four tablespoons of hot sour cream

title: Kartofle Nadziewane Grzybami (Potatoes Stuffed with Mushrooms)
body:
## Ingredients

- dried mushrooms
- couple of onions
- butter
- 2 egg yolks
- breadcrumbs
- salt
- pepper
- several large potatoes
- flour

## Directions

1. Cook dried mushrooms until soft and chop very finely.
2. Chop a couple of onions and sauté in butter, mix with the mushrooms.
3. Add two egg yolks, breadcrumbs, salt and pepper to taste and mix everything into a smooth mass.
4. Peel several large potatoes and place in the oven for a few or a dozen minutes, just long enough for them to lose their rawness.
5. Cut off the top slice from each potato, hollow out with a spoon and fill the hollow with the mushroom mass.
6. Cover with the cut slice, arrange on a sheet, pour butter over and bake in a moderate oven.
7. Just before serving, make a roux with flour and butter, thin it with the liquid from the cooked mushrooms and the sauce, and pour over the potatoes.

title: Kartofle Pieczone Nadziewane (Baked Stuffed Potatoes)
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 medium potatoes of uniform size
- butter
- salt
- pepper
- 1/2 cup sweet milk

## Directions

1. Choose six medium potatoes, trying to make them all the same size, of good shape and with smooth skin.
2. Wash them carefully, cut off both ends with a knife and bake in a moderately hot oven.
3. When the potatoes are completely soft, hollow them out with a spoon so that only the skin remains, being careful not to damage it.
4. To the scooped out mass add a teaspoon of butter, salt and pepper to taste and half a cup of sweet milk.
5. Beat everything with a spoon until it becomes a uniform mass, then fill the hollowed-out skins with it.
6. Arrange on a butter-smeared baking dish and bake in a hot oven until the ends brown.

title: Kartofle Pieczone z Szałwią (Roasted Potatoes with Sage)
body:
## Ingredients

- small potatoes
- sage, sprinkled
- salt
- pepper
- one large onion
- butter
- milk or broth, half a cup

## Directions

1. Peel only small potatoes to cover the bottom of a baking dish
2. Arrange them in this manner, then sprinkle with grated sage using about half a teaspoon
3. Add half a teaspoon of salt and some pepper
4. Slice one large onion into thin slices and cover the prepared potatoes with them
5. Place a tablespoon of butter in small pieces on top
6. Pour over everything half a cup of milk or broth
7. Bake in a moderately hot oven, which will take about forty minutes

title: Kartofle po Londyńsku (Potatoes London Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- several boiled and cooled potatoes
- butter
- hard boiled eggs
- one tablespoon butter
- one and a half tablespoons flour
- salt
- pepper
- a few tablespoons boiling water
- vinegar, same amount as water
- green parsley, chopped

## Directions

1. Peel and cut into fairly thick slices several boiled and cooled potatoes
2. Fry them in butter in a pan until they brown well on both sides
3. Arrange them on a hot serving dish
4. Place a thin slice of hard-boiled egg on each potato slice (two eggs are sufficient for four people)
5. For the sauce: melt one tablespoon of butter in a pan and add one and a half tablespoons of flour, pouring very slowly and stirring constantly
6. Add salt and pepper to taste
7. Then pour in a few tablespoons of boiling water and the same amount of vinegar
8. Boil for a few minutes
9. Sprinkle with chopped green parsley and pour over the hot potatoes

title: Kartofle po Wiejsku (Country Style Potatoes)
body:
## Ingredients

- boiled and cooled potatoes
- butter
- salt
- pepper
- milk

## Directions

1. Cut boiled and cooled potatoes into thin slices
2. Pour into a shallow pot
3. Add some butter, salt and pepper to taste
4. Cover with enough milk to barely cover them
5. Cook on low heat until the potatoes have absorbed all the milk, then serve

title: Kartofle Rumiane (Browned Potatoes)
body:
## Ingredients

- medium potatoes
- cold water
- salted water
- hot fat
- salt

## Directions

1. Peel a few medium potatoes and leave in cold water for one hour.
2. Drain this water, pour new water, salt it and cook the potatoes whole until almost cooked.
3. Drain the water and when the potatoes dry a little, throw them into hot fat and fry like doughnuts until they brown well on top.
4. Arrange on a serving dish and sprinkle with salt.

title: Kartofle Smażone (Fried Potatoes)
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- good butter

## Directions

1. peel the potatoes, cut into slices and scald with boiling water
2. melt some good butter in a pan, add the potatoes and braise covered for about twenty minutes, shaking the pan frequently
3. to make them brown, finish cooking without the cover
4. alternatively, fry whole potatoes in a pan filled with hot fat, or use a small spoon to cut little balls from potatoes, shape them like small potatoes and fry in hot fat until browned

title: Kartofle Zapiekane (Baked Potatoes)
body:
## Ingredients

- several large potatoes
- water
- two tablespoons butter
- one and a half tablespoons finely chopped parsley
- salt
- pepper
- one cup hot milk
- two beaten eggs
- grated breadcrumbs or white bread

## Directions

1. Peel several large potatoes, cover with water in a pot and boil for half an hour
2. Drain the water and mash the potatoes to a smooth paste
3. For each quart of potatoes take two tablespoons of butter, one and a half tablespoons of finely chopped green parsley, salt and pepper to taste and mix well with the potatoes
4. Add one cup of hot milk, pouring slowly and stirring constantly
5. At the end add two well-beaten eggs
6. Grease a baking form with butter and sprinkle fairly thickly with grated breadcrumbs or white bread
7. Fill with the potato mixture and place in a moderately hot oven for half an hour
8. After removing from the oven let stand a few minutes, then loosen around the edges with a knife and turn out onto a hot serving dish or plate
9. These potatoes look best baked in an oval form shaped like a serving dish

title: Kasza Gryczana (Buckwheat Groats)
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart of water
- one large tablespoon of butter or one quarter pound of bacon cut into cubes
- half quart of buckwheat groats
- fried bacon

## Directions

1. Bring one quart of water to a boil and add one large tablespoon of butter or one quarter pound of bacon cut into cubes and fried
2. Pour in half quart of buckwheat groats, mix well, cover and place in the oven for two hours
3. When serving, pour fried bacon over it

title: Kasza Jaglana Zapiekana (Baked Millet Groats)
body:
## Ingredients

- half quart of millet groats
- one quart of whole milk
- one tablespoon of butter
- fresh bacon slices
- fine sugar

## Directions

1. Scald half quart of millet groats
2. Then cook until tender in one quart of whole milk to which one tablespoon of butter has been added before boiling
3. When the groats are completely tender, line a baking dish with thin slices of fresh bacon
4. Place the cooked groats in the middle
5. Place in the oven for two hours
6. Then turn out onto a serving platter or bowl and serve, offering fine sugar separately

title: Kasza Jęczmienna (Barley Groats)
body:
## Ingredients

- barley groats
- water
- butter or bacon
- fried bacon

## Directions

1. Prepare barley groats exactly the same way as buckwheat groats, with the only difference being that you need to use somewhat more groats and keep it in the oven longer

title: Kawon (Watermelon)
body:
## Ingredients

- one watermelon
- sugar

## Directions

1. Place the watermelon on ice and when very cold, cut into slices about an inch thick
2. Remove all the seeds and then from these slices cut out small diagonal squares, which are then arranged on a glass serving platter and sprinkled with sugar, or placed directly into small bowls

title: Kedgeree (Kedgeree)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup plus a bit cold cooked or fried fish, crumbled
- 1 cup hot softly cooked rice
- 2 tablespoons butter
- salt and pepper to taste
- a pinch of red pepper
- 1 cup milk
- 1 teaspoon flour
- equal amount of butter
- 2 well-beaten eggs

## Directions

1. Crumble cold cooked or fried fish to make 1 cup with a bit extra
2. Add 2 tablespoons of butter to 1 cup of hot softly cooked rice and mix well
3. Mix with the fish, add salt, pepper and a pinch of red pepper to taste
4. Bring 1 cup of milk to a boil and thicken with 1 teaspoon of flour mixed with the same amount of butter
5. Pour into the fish and rice mixture and place on heat
6. As soon as it boils, pour in 2 well-beaten eggs and immediately remove from heat and serve at table
7. The eggs may be omitted and it will be good without them

title: Kiełbasa Higieniczna (Hygienic Sausage)
body:
## Ingredients

- one and a half pounds of pork tenderloin or loin
- fat
- half pound of soft veal without bones and sinews
- water
- salt and pepper to taste
- one clove of garlic
- thick intestine

## Directions

1. Cut the pork tenderloin into fine pieces along with fat, first in strips, then into cubes.
2. Pass half pound of soft veal through a meat grinder.
3. Mix together with the chopped pork.
4. Add several tablespoons of water, salt and pepper to taste, and one clove of garlic, crushed in a mortar.
5. After thoroughly mixing, stuff the thick intestine with this mixture.
6. Let it rest for an hour.
7. Arrange the prepared sausage at the bottom of a pot and cover with enough water to barely cover it.
8. Cook on high heat, frequently turning, until all water evaporates, which should not take longer than twenty minutes.
9. After the water has evaporated, add a small amount of butter and place in a hot oven to brown.

title: Kiełbasa Smażona z Sosem (Fried Sausage with Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh sausages
- water
- butter
- vinegar
- flour
- fat
- caramel for brown color
- pickled gherkins, finely chopped (optional)

## Directions

1. Arrange fresh sausages in a pot and cover with enough water to just cover them.
2. Cook for twenty minutes, being careful not to let them burn.
3. Add a piece of butter and fry until browned.
4. Then pour in several tablespoons of water and vinegar.
5. Sprinkle in some flour mixed with fat.
6. Bring together to a boil and serve.
7. For a brown sauce, add a tablespoon of caramel.
8. If desired, finely chopped pickled gherkins can be added to the sauce; in that case, remove the sausage while making the sauce.

title: Kilki w Marynacie (Smelt in Marinade)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound small fish (smelts)
- best quality olive oil
- 1 onion
- bay leaf pieces
- salt and pepper to taste
- 1 cup cold vinegar
- 2 cloves
- a pinch of sugar
- a piece of red pepper

## Directions

1. Clean 1 pound of small fish (smelts) and fry in a pan using best quality olive oil instead of butter until browned
2. Cool completely
3. Arrange in a salad bowl and sprinkle with finely chopped onion
4. Add broken bay leaf pieces, salt and pepper to taste
5. Pour 1 cup of cold vinegar that has been previously boiled with 2 cloves, a pinch of sugar and a piece of red pepper
6. Place in a cool location for 24 hours
7. Then pour a little olive oil into the vinegar and serve

title: Kleik Jęczmienny (Barley Gruel)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a quart pearl or regular barley
- soft water, one quart
- fresh butter, one tablespoon
- salt

## Directions

1. One of the most important conditions when making gruel is to cook it in a stone or clay pot, because when cooked in iron, even if glazed, the barley will always be dark or at least dark.
2. Rinse half a quart of barley on a sieve with water and rinse lightly once with the husks, which are still found there.
3. Pour into a pot, cover with one quart of soft water and cook on low heat without salt, having put in one tablespoon of fresh butter, so that the barley separates better; stir frequently so as not to burn it.
4. White glue will constantly settle on top, similar to scum, and it should be constantly collected with a spoon, as this is the best gruel for the sick.
5. One must be careful so that this glue does not cook into the barley, or it will not be as good.
6. When the glue stops appearing, pass the barley through warm water or broth, then add the glue collected from the top.
7. Before serving at table, only then salt it, add some butter and mix it well with the gruel.
8. Barley passed through broth makes an excellent white soup, very nourishing and healthy.

title: Klopsy z Manną (Meatballs with Semolina)
body:
## Ingredients

- one and a half pounds finely chopped beef
- salt and pepper to taste
- one chopped onion
- one beaten egg
- half a cup of semolina
- salted water
- half a cup of marinated tomatoes
- a little red pepper
- one tablespoon flour mixed in cold milk

## Directions

1. Salt and pepper the finely chopped beef to taste, add one chopped onion, one beaten egg, and half a cup of semolina
2. Mix everything into a mass and form into balls the size of a hen's egg
3. Braise in a pot with a small amount of salted water
4. Once cooked, remove the meatballs and arrange on a hot platter
5. To the remaining sauce in the pot add half a cup of marinated tomatoes, a little red pepper, and one tablespoon of flour mixed in cold milk
6. Bring to a boil and pour over the meatballs on the platter

title: Klopsy z Sosem (Meatballs with Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- ground meat
- butter
- broth
- juice from half a lemon
- one teaspoon sugar
- onion, sliced or chopped
- grated bread or flour mixed with butter

## Directions

1. Prepare meat the same way as for ground roast and mix well
2. Form into round meatballs and flatten slightly
3. Brown in a pan in hot butter over high heat
4. Place in a pot, add enough broth to not quite cover the meatballs
5. Add one tablespoon of butter, juice from half a lemon, one teaspoon sugar, some sliced or chopped onion, and a few tablespoons of grated bread
6. Braise covered for fifteen to twenty minutes
7. The quality of the meatballs can be improved by adding capers

title: Kluseczki Królewskie (Royal Dumplings)
body:
## Ingredients

- three whole eggs
- one cup whole milk
- salt to taste
- two spoons grated cheese, preferably Parmesan
- butter for greasing

## Directions

1. Beat three whole eggs in a bowl, add one cup whole milk, salt to taste and two spoons grated cheese, preferably Parmesan, and mix everything properly.
2. Pour into a buttered baking tin, place the tin in a pot with hot water, which should reach higher than half the height of the tin, place on heat and cook about twenty minutes.
3. When cooked, which can be known by placing a toothpick in the center of the tin—if the dough does not smear on the toothpick it is done—remove the tin and turn it over on the work surface and leave to cool.
4. Then cut with a knife into long dumplings, place in a bowl and cover with broth.

title: Kluseczki z Sosem (Pasta with Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- half pound flour
- one egg
- cold water
- butter
- six egg yolks
- quarter pound sugar
- vanilla
- half quart sweet cream
- burnt sugar for color

## Directions

1. Mix half pound flour and one egg with a little cold water to make fairly stiff dough
2. Turn out onto table, roll thin and cut first into strips, then into narrow noodles, then cook in plenty of salted water
3. When cooked, drain the water, add a tablespoon of good butter and two well-beaten egg yolks to the hot noodles, mix and spread on a buttered metal plate or pan and place in a hot oven for fifteen minutes
4. Flavor half quart sweet cream with vanilla, bring to boil and pour over six egg yolks that have been well beaten with quarter pound sugar, add a tablespoon of burnt sugar for color, shake briefly in the hot oven until thickened, then pour this sauce over the browned noodles, using no more than half the sauce, serving the rest separately at table

title: Kluski Francuskie (French Dumplings)
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- sour cream

## Directions

1. Rub together butter and sour cream in a bowl.

title: Kluski Francuskie (French Dumplings)
body:
## Ingredients

- half tablespoon fat
- one tablespoon good butter
- two egg yolks
- one whole egg
- one heaping tablespoon sifted flour
- two tablespoons good jam
- sugar
- two tablespoons sour cream
- ground cinnamon

## Directions

1. Mix fat and butter in a bowl until it turns white
2. Add two egg yolks one at a time and beat until it thickens while stirring in one direction
3. Add one whole egg and beat a bit more
4. Very slowly add one heaping tablespoon of sifted flour while simultaneously stirring until a thick uniform mass forms
5. Scoop the prepared batter with a spoon and place in a large pot of boiling water
6. When they float to the surface, carefully remove with a spoon onto a hot serving plate
7. Press two tablespoons of good jam through a sieve
8. Mix with sugar and two tablespoons sour cream
9. Add some ground cinnamon and mix thoroughly
10. Pour in three more tablespoons of sour cream and mix lightly
11. Pour this sauce over the dumplings on the serving plate

title: Kluski Francuskie (French Dumplings)
body:
## Ingredients

- one spoon fresh butter
- four egg yolks
- half spoon fat or broth fat
- four egg whites
- half quart flour

## Directions

1. Beat one spoon of fresh butter in a bowl, then add four egg yolks one by one, stirring constantly in one direction.
2. Take half a spoon of fat or broth fat, cool it well and pour into the yolks.
3. Beat the remaining four egg whites into stiff peaks and mix with the yolks, taking one spoon of foam at a time.
4. Sift half a quart of flour and add it very slowly to the beaten eggs, mixing together.
5. Take the dough with a spoon and place on simmering broth, cover, and when they rise, remove with a spoon and place in a bowl.
6. Pour some broth first into the bowl, then after placing the dumplings, pour the rest of the broth through a sieve.
7. Handle the dumplings very carefully with the spoon so they do not fall apart.

title: Kluski Francuskie bez Piany (French Dumplings without Foam)
body:
## Ingredients

- one spoon butter
- one spoon fat
- two egg yolks
- one whole egg
- one and a half spoon flour

## Directions

1. Beat one spoon butter and one spoon fat in a bowl until almost white.
2. Pour in two egg yolks one by one and continue beating until thick.
3. Then add one whole egg and stir constantly in one direction until thick.
4. Take one and a half spoon flour and add very slowly while beating.
5. When flour is well mixed, place dumplings with a spoon on broth, cover with a lid so they rise, then serve.

title: Kluski Gryczane (Buckwheat Dumplings)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound buckwheat flour
- salt
- warm water

## Directions

1. Take one pound buckwheat flour and add salt and enough warm water so the batter is quite thin
2. Mix well and drop large dumplings with a spoon onto boiling water
3. When they float to the surface, place on a serving plate and drizzle with rendered bacon

title: Kluski Krajane (Cut Dumplings)
body:
## Ingredients

- half quart flour
- one egg
- water

## Directions

1. Mix half quart flour with one egg and enough water so the dough is not too stiff
2. Roll out on a table
3. Cut into one and a half inch strips
4. Lay the strips one on top of another, sprinkling flour between them so they don't stick together
5. Cut into dumplings of any desired width
6. Drop the cut dumplings into boiling salted water and cook until they float to the surface
7. Then drain them, place on a serving plate and drizzle with hot melted butter or rendered young bacon

title: Kluski Parzone (Poached Dumplings)
body:
## Ingredients

- one whole egg
- three spoons milk
- three spoons flour
- quarter pound butter

## Directions

1. Beat one whole egg, add three spoons milk and three spoons flour and mix everything well.
2. Melt a quarter pound butter in a pot, pour in the above dough, place on very low heat and stir with a spoon.
3. When the dough pulls away from the pot, remove from heat and cool.
4. Then add one egg, salt, mix well with a spoon soaked in hot water, take the dough with a spoon and place on simmering soup and cook until it floats to the surface, then serve.

title: Kluski Parzone z Serem (Scalded Dumplings with Cheese)
body:
## Ingredients

- half quart water
- one cup butter
- salt to taste
- half quart wheat flour
- four eggs
- grated cheese

## Directions

1. In half quart of water, add one cup butter and salt to taste and bring to a boil
2. Into the boiling water, add half quart wheat flour and mix well until it separates from the pot
3. Set aside in a cool place
4. When completely cooled, add four eggs one at a time, beating strongly after each addition until a uniform mass forms
5. In a large pot, bring a lot of water to a boil
6. Drop medium-sized dumplings into the boiling water with a spoon and cover with a lid
7. When they rise and grow well, remove with a slotted spoon onto a buttered platter
8. Pour melted butter over the top and sprinkle with grated cheese and drizzle with butter again
9. Place in a hot oven for twenty minutes until the top is well browned

title: Kluski Pulchne (Fluffy Dumplings)
body:
## Ingredients

- half cup butter
- one cup water
- one cup flour
- two eggs

## Directions

1. Bring half a cup of butter and one cup of water to a boil in a pot and slowly add one cup of flour to the boiling liquid, stirring at the same time so a uniform mass forms.
2. Remove from heat and cool well, then add two eggs and mix properly.
3. From this dough dumplings can be made in three ways: 1) Take with a spoon and place dumplings on simmering soup, then cover so they rise; 2) make small round balls with fingers and fry them in hot fat, of which there must be much in the pot; 3) fill a buttered baking tin with dough to half full, place in a pot with hot water and cook in steam, cool, cut with a knife into cubes and place in a bowl before filling it with soup.

title: Kluski Saskie (Saxon Dumplings)
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart grated roll
- half quart hot milk
- three egg yolks
- half tablespoon butter
- one tablespoon chopped ham or cracklings
- salt to taste
- one roll, diced and fried in butter
- egg whites
- clarified butter
- eight ripe sweet pears
- sugar
- cinnamon
- anise
- one cup sour cream
- one tablespoon flour
- hot smoked meat

## Directions

1. Pour half quart of hot milk over one quart of grated roll and let sit until the roll softens
2. Add three egg yolks, half tablespoon butter, one tablespoon chopped ham or cracklings, salt to taste, one diced roll fried in butter and beaten egg whites
3. Mix everything and fry in clarified butter
4. Dust the table with flour, moisten hands in flour and shape the mixture into round dumplings slightly larger than a walnut
5. Cook in salted water
6. Take eight ripe sweet pears, peel and cut each in half, remove the seeds
7. Place in a pot and add just enough water to barely cover them
8. Add sugar, cinnamon and a pinch of anise and cook until soft
9. Thicken with one cup sour cream mixed with one tablespoon flour, bring to a boil
10. Pour this sauce over the dumplings arranged on a serving plate
11. Arrange slices of hot boiled smoked meat around them and serve

title: Kluski z Bułek (Dumplings with Rolls)
body:
## Ingredients

- several spoons butter
- finely grated roll

## Directions

1. Beat several spoons butter in a bowl into foam, then add finely grated roll until a fairly thick dough forms when mixed.
2. Make small dumplings from this dough and cook them in broth.
3. When they float to the surface, cover and simmer a moment, they will be fluffier and more crisp, then serve with broth on the table.

title: Kluski z Kartofli (Potato Dumplings)
body:
## Ingredients

- one and a half quarts of floury potatoes
- salt to taste
- one grated roll
- four egg yolks
- one cup flour
- marjoram
- four egg whites

## Directions

1. Peel and cook one and a half quarts of floury potatoes in salted water
2. Drain and mash in the same pot or bowl
3. Transfer to a dough bowl and add one grated roll, four egg yolks, one cup flour, salt to taste and a pinch of marjoram
4. Mix well with a rolling pin
5. Add the foam from four beaten egg whites and mix slowly
6. Turn the prepared dough onto a flour-dusted table
7. Roll out to finger thickness and dust well with flour
8. Cut large dumplings into the shapes of lazy dumplings
9. Cook in a large amount of water in a large pot
10. When they float to the surface, remove with a slotted spoon and place directly on a platter
11. Pour rendered young bacon over and serve

title: Kluski z Kaszki Mannowej (Dumplings with Semolina)
body:
## Ingredients

- one spoon melted butter
- four egg yolks
- four egg whites
- half quart wheat semolina

## Directions

1. Beat one spoon melted butter in a bowl to a cream, adding four egg yolks one by one.
2. Then pour in four egg whites beaten to stiff peaks, mix well and add, pouring slowly and constantly stirring, half a quart of wheat semolina.
3. Mix until the semolina swells and the dough becomes thick and set aside for about half an hour.
4. When the semolina swells well, place dumplings from this dough with a spoon on simmering broth, each time dipping the spoon in clean water before taking dough.
5. Cover the broth so they rise, then serve on the table.

title: Kluski z Makiem (Dumplings with Poppy Seeds)
body:
## Ingredients

- poppy seeds
- half pound poppy seeds
- quarter pound sugar or honey

## Directions

1. Prepare and cook dumplings as described above
2. Mix half pound of previously scalded poppy seeds in a bowl with quarter pound sugar or honey
3. Pour this over the dumplings, mix well and serve immediately

title: Kluski z Wątróbki (Liver Dumplings)
body:
## Ingredients

- calf liver
- one roll soaked in milk for one hour
- three eggs
- one dry roll grated finely
- one large onion, parboiled and finely chopped
- salt and pepper to taste
- butter
- onion

## Directions

1. Peel the calf liver of membranes, carefully remove veins, chop very finely and pass through a sieve.
2. Soak one roll in milk for one hour, beat in three eggs, add one dry roll grated finely, and one large parboiled onion finely chopped, and salt and pepper to taste.
3. Mix everything properly with the liver and form oblong dumplings by hand.
4. Cook in a wide pot, remove with a slotted spoon, place carefully on a serving platter and pour with browned butter with onion.
5. Such dumplings can also be added to broth.

title: Knedle ze Słoniną (Dumplings with Bacon)
body:
## Ingredients

- half quart grated roll
- one large cup hot milk
- four egg yolks
- salt to taste
- quarter pound bacon, finely chopped
- two egg whites
- one large cup wheat flour

## Directions

1. Pour one large cup of hot milk over half quart of grated roll and let sit for a few minutes
2. Add four egg yolks and season with salt to taste
3. Fry quarter pound of finely chopped bacon until crispy, cool it and add to the batter along with the cracklings
4. Add two beaten egg whites while gradually sprinkling in one large cup wheat flour
5. Mix carefully, turn onto a table and shape into dumplings the size of a walnut, dusting with flour so they don't stick
6. Cook in a large amount of water in a large pot
7. When they float to the surface and puff up well, remove with a slotted spoon onto a serving plate and drizzle with rendered bacon

title: Kokilki z Resztek Zająca (Hare Ramekins from Leftovers)
body:
## Ingredients

- leftover hare pieces
- one tablespoon good butter
- half cup broth or bouillon
- flour
- one or two tablespoons sour cream
- a few mushrooms
- grated cheese, preferably Parmesan

## Directions

1. Remove all bones from leftover hare pieces and chop finely
2. Melt one tablespoon good butter in a saucepan
3. Thin out with half cup broth or bouillon and thicken with a little flour
4. Bring to a good boil
5. Add chopped meat, then add one or two tablespoons sour cream and a few mushrooms
6. Stew under cover until the sauce is reduced by half
7. Mix everything well
8. Place in small molds
9. Sprinkle grated cheese, preferably Parmesan, on top
10. Place in a hot oven until the top is browned
11. Serve

title: Kompot z Fig i Daktyli (Fig and Date Compote)
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup of figs
- one cup of dates
- water
- sugar as optional

## Directions

1. Wash a cup of figs and a cup of dates thoroughly in warm water
2. Remove the pits from the dates and chop the figs finely together with the dates
3. Put into a pot, add a little water and cook covered for about half an hour, adding only enough water to prevent burning
4. Usually sugar is not added as both dates and figs are sufficiently sweet, but if you wish the compote in sweet syrup, add one cup of sugar ten minutes after boiling and add more water

title: Kompot z Jabłek (Apple Compote)
body:
## Ingredients

- several tart apples
- half a quart of water
- half a pound of sugar
- cinnamon
- lemon zest

## Directions

1. Peel several tart apples, cut into quarters and remove the seeds, then place in cold water so they do not blacken
2. Boil half a quart of water with half a pound of sugar, add cinnamon and lemon zest and cook the apples in this syrup, taking care that they do not fall apart
3. This compote can be colored with the juice of any berries, or served naturally

title: Kompot z Jabłek z Cytryna (Apple and Lemon Compote)
body:
## Ingredients

- six medium tart apples
- a heaping cup of sugar
- half a cup of water
- half a lemon sliced

## Directions

1. Peel and cut six medium tart apples into quarters
2. Bring a heaping cup of sugar and half a cup of water to boil, and when the sugar is completely dissolved, add the cut apples and half a lemon sliced into pieces
3. Cook until the apples are soft, being careful not to crush them, but the quarters should remain whole

title: Kompot z Moreli i Fig (Apricot and Fig Compote)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a quart of dried apricots
- six large dried figs
- one cup of sugar

## Directions

1. Cook half a quart of dried apricots until soft
2. Scald six large dried figs with hot water, chop and mix with the apricots
3. Add one cup of sugar and cook together for about fifteen minutes, then cool
4. Though this compote is usually served cold, it can also be served hot

title: Konfitury Ananasowa (Pineapple Jam)
body:
## Ingredients

- ripe pineapples
- sugar equal in weight to the chopped fruit
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Peel ripe pineapples and cut into dice; do this in the evening
2. For each pound of chopped fruit, take one pound of sugar, sprinkle this sugar over the pineapples arranged in a bowl and let it stand overnight
3. The next morning, pour everything into a pot and boil until the pineapple is soft and clear, then add some lemon juice
4. Boil once and fill jars
5. Do not add water at all

title: Konfitury z Fig (Fig Preserve)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of figs
- one cup of water
- half pound of sugar
- a little grated nutmeg

## Directions

1. Chop the figs finely and place in a pot.
2. Pour one cup of water over the figs, add half pound of sugar and a little grated nutmeg.
3. Cook on a low fire until the mixture becomes thick.
4. Pour into a dish wetted with water and let cool.
5. Transfer to a tightly sealed jar for storage.

title: Konfitury z Malin (Raspberry Jam)
body:
## Ingredients

- four quarts of raspberries
- two quarts of currant juice
- sugar equal in weight to berries and juice combined

## Directions

1. Put four quarts of raspberries and two quarts of currant juice on fire and cook for three minutes
2. Add sugar equal in weight to the berries and juice and cook for an additional twenty minutes
3. Fill jars and seal tightly

title: Konfitury z Malin i Porzeczek (Jam of Raspberries and Currants)
body:
## Ingredients

- equal quantity of raspberries and currants
- three-quarters pound of sugar for each pound of berries

## Directions

1. Cook berries alone for twenty minutes
2. Add sugar and cook for an additional five minutes from the time it boils

title: Konfitury z Żółtych Pomidorów (Jam from Yellow Tomatoes)
body:
## Ingredients

- small yellow tomatoes
- sugar equal in weight to the tomatoes
- lemon

## Directions

1. Scald the small yellow tomatoes with boiling water and peel them
2. Take sugar equal in weight to the tomatoes
3. Place the peeled tomatoes in a saucepan and boil for about half an hour until the mass is thick
4. For each quart of tomatoes, take one lemon, cut into small pieces, mix with sugar with the addition of the smallest amount of water and let it stand during the cooking of the tomatoes
5. When the tomatoes are cooked, add the lemon syrup to them
6. Boil once, then remove from the fire
7. Fill jars and store

title: Korniszony w Occie (Cucumbers in Vinegar)
body:
## Ingredients

- małe ogóreczki, świeże
- pół filiżanki soli na kwartę wody
- kwarta mocnego octu
- jedna-trzecia kwarty wody
- pół kwarty cukru
- kilka lasek cynamonu
- łyżeczka całych gwoździków

## Directions

1. Wybrać małe ogóreczki, zupełnie świeże
2. Zalać na noc osoloną wodą w stosunku pół filiżanki soli na kwartę wody
3. Na drugi dzień rano odcedzić całą wodę
4. Ponakładać ogórki w słoiki, układając je bardzo ciasno
5. Wlać w kociołek kwartę mocnego octu, jedną-trzecią kwarty wody, pół kwarty cukru
