6. Zawiązać w kawałku płótna kilka lasek cynamonu i łyżeczkę całych gwoździków
7. Zagotować wszystko w kociołku
8. Płynem tym zalać nłożone w słoikach ogórki i szczelnie zamknąć

title: Kotlet Cielęcy Bity (Beaten Veal Cutlet)
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal cutlets cut from the bones
- Salt
- Egg
- Cold water
- Grated bread crumbs, fine and sifted
- Butter, clarified if possible

## Directions

1. Cut the cutlets from the bones so they have a good shape
2. Beat and salt each cutlet
3. Dip each in egg beaten with a spoonful of cold water
4. Coat in fine grated bread crumbs that have been sifted
5. Put a piece of butter on a pan, preferably clarified, heat strongly, then add the cutlets
6. Fry on low heat so they do not brown too much
7. After browning on one side, turn to the other side
8. When cooked, place on a platter and pour the remaining butter from the pan over them

title: Kotlet Cielęcy Siekany (Chopped Veal Cutlet)
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal from the hind quarter and shoulder
- One bread roll
- Milk
- Grated bread crumbs
- Butter
- Egg yolk
- Salt and pepper
- Bone for filling
- Optional: pork for mixture
- Sauce, spinach, or vegetables for serving

## Directions

1. Remove the sinews from the veal, cut into fine pieces, and chop into a uniform mass
2. For every pound and a half of chopped veal, take one bread roll soaked in milk and mix with a spoonful of grated bread crumbs and a small piece of butter
3. Add one beaten egg yolk
4. Mix all thoroughly with the meat into a smooth mass
5. Form into oblong cutlets, place a bone in each, coat in grated bread crumbs, and fry on hot butter
6. Serve with sauce, spinach, or other vegetables
7. The bones and sinews left from the meat can be cooked to flavor the sauce

title: Kotlety Baranie a la Soubisse (Lamb Cutlets à la Soubisse)
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb cutlets
- white onion sauce à la Soubisse

## Directions

1. Make these cutlets in a similar way as beaten cutlets, except that they should be fried on strong heat and very briefly, so that the meat inside is still pink.
2. Serve separately with white onion sauce, called Soubisse.

title: Kotlety Baranie Bite (Beaten Lamb Cutlets)
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb cutlets
- salt
- pepper
- flour
- butter
- vegetables
- white onion sauce à la Soubisse

## Directions

1. Debone the cutlets, leaving however some fat on them. Then beat each one, salt it, dust with flour and fry in browned butter on strong heat.
2. Serve with various vegetables, and the best sauce for lamb cutlets is white onion sauce à la Soubisse, which should be made thick and poured around the cutlets, not on them.

title: Kotlety Baranie Siekane (Chopped Lamb Cutlets)
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb meat
- salt
- pepper
- soaked roll
- one egg yolk per two pounds of meat
- bread crumbs
- butter

## Directions

1. Take lamb meat - these can be scraps left over from roasts or beaten cutlets - debone it, chop finely, add salt and pepper to taste, soaked roll, one egg yolk per two pounds of meat, mix well and form into flat cutlets.
2. Into these cutlets place small cubes, sprinkle with grated bread crumbs and fry in clarified butter.
3. Make these cutlets thin, as they shrink during frying and become considerably thicker.

title: Kotlety Bite (Beaten Cutlets)
body:
## Ingredients

- chuck steak
- salt
- butter
- grated horseradish
- potatoes

## Directions

1. Cut the chuck steak into cutlets and remove the thick bones, leaving only a small piece of rib on each
2. Beat lightly and shape into cutlet form, keeping the fat on them
3. Salt and place on a pan with hot butter
4. Start frying on high heat to quickly brown
5. After browning one side, flip to the other side and brown it equally, then finish frying on low heat
6. When serving, arrange the cutlets with grated horseradish and potatoes, preferably fried in the French style, or fried together with the cutlets in the pan

title: Kotlety po Niemiecku (German-Style Cutlets)
body:
## Ingredients

- pork cutlets
- salt
- pepper
- flour
- butter
- onion

## Directions

1. Season the prepared cutlets with salt and pepper.
2. Coat with flour and fry in butter over high heat just until lightly browned.
3. Arrange the browned cutlets in a pot, placing onion slices between layers.
4. Cover and braise over low heat until tender.
5. Serve with mashed potatoes, either arranged around them or served separately.

title: Kotlety Pożarskie (Pozharsky Cutlets)
body:
## Ingredients

- Young hen
- One pound white veal
- One quarter pound beef marrow
- One egg yolk
- Egg
- Bread crumbs
- Clarified butter
- Mushroom sauce or lemon slices

## Directions

1. Prepare and clean a young hen as for roasting, remove the skin from the breast and cut from the white meat flat oblong strips in the form of cutlets
2. Mince one pound of white veal, add to it one quarter pound of beef marrow and one egg yolk and mix well
3. Spread a layer of this meat on a work surface, place on it a slice of chicken, on top again a layer of meat and form neat oblong cutlets, insert pieces of chicken wings, salt them
4. Dip in beaten egg and then in bread crumbs and fry on hot clarified butter over moderate heat
5. Serve at table with mushroom sauce, sharp sauce or with slices of lemon

title: Kotlety Wieprzowe (Pork Cutlets)
body:
## Ingredients

- pork cutlets
- salt
- egg
- water
- bread crumbs
- butter or lard

## Directions

1. Beat the cutlets well, giving them a neat shape.
2. Salt them.
3. Dip in beaten egg mixed with water.
4. Coat with bread crumbs.
5. Fry in hot butter or lard.
6. Alternatively, instead of dipping in egg, simply salt and coat with flour.

title: Kotlety Wieprzowe Siekane (Chopped Pork Cutlets)
body:
## Ingredients

- lean pork meat
- soaked bread roll
- egg yolk
- salt
- pepper
- bread crumbs
- butter or lard

## Directions

1. Chop the pork meat finely.
2. Add soaked bread roll.
3. For every two pounds of meat, add one egg yolk, salt and pepper to taste.
4. Mix everything thoroughly.
5. Form flat cutlets from the mixture.
6. Coat with bread crumbs.
7. Fry in hot butter or lard.

title: Kotlety z Móżdżku (Brain Cutlets)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal brain
- cold water
- salted water
- vinegar, one tablespoon
- egg
- bread roll
- butter

## Directions

1. Soak veal brain for an hour in cold water, then carefully remove all veins and membranes.
2. Cover the deveined brain with salted water and add one tablespoon of vinegar, place on heat and bring to a boil, then immediately drain the water.
3. Cut the brain into flat round pieces, dip them in beaten egg, coat with grated bread and fry in clarified butter until both sides are browned.

title: Kotlety z Pieczeni (Cutlets from Roast)
body:
## Ingredients

- Leftover roasted veal, preferably with kidney
- One bread roll, soaked
- One whole egg
- One egg yolk
- Half a spoonful of butter
- Salt and pepper
- Butter for frying

## Directions

1. Chop the leftover roasted veal very finely
2. For every pound of chopped meat, take one soaked bread roll, one whole egg, one egg yolk, half a spoonful of butter, salt, and pepper to taste
3. Mix everything carefully
4. Form into thin round cutlets and fry in butter until browned

title: Kotlety z Ryb (Fish Cutlets)
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- flour
- milk or water
- salt
- pepper
- egg yolks
- olive oil
- cooked or fried fish
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Brown a spoonful of butter with a spoonful of flour in a pot, add two-thirds of a cup of milk, or water if milk is lacking, and bring to a boil once, then add salt and pepper to taste and remove from heat.
2. Beat in two egg yolks.
3. Pour onto an olive oil-greased plate and mix in about a cup of cold cooked or fried fish, broken into small pieces.
4. Place in a cold place.
5. When completely cooled, turn out onto a work surface and form the mixture into cork-shaped pieces, dip in grated breadcrumbs, then in beaten egg, and again in breadcrumbs.
6. Fry until golden in hot fat.
7. It is best to fry them in a wire basket immersed in boiling fat.
8. The breadcrumbs must be grated very finely.

title: Kotlety z Ryżem (Cutlets with Rice)
body:
## Ingredients

- cold roasted meat of any kind, one large onion, total two cups
- one cup cold cooked rice
- salt and pepper to taste
- egg for binding
- one beaten egg for coating
- grated bread or breadcrumbs for coating
- butter for frying
- tomato sauce for serving

## Directions

1. Chop cold roasted meat with one large onion until you have two cups total
2. Mix with one cup cold cooked rice
3. Add salt and pepper to taste
4. Add enough egg to make a fairly thick mixture
5. Place in a cold location for half an hour
6. Spread on a work surface and flatten with a rolling pin
7. Cut with a knife into cutlet shapes
8. Dip each cutlet in beaten egg
9. Coat with grated bread or breadcrumbs
10. Fry until well browned
11. Serve on the table with tomato sauce

title: Kotlety ze Szpikiem (Cutlets with Marrow)
body:
## Ingredients

- Tender veal
- Beef marrow
- Salt and pepper
- Bread crumb soaked
- Egg yolk
- Water
- Clarified butter

## Directions

1. Select a tender piece of veal, remove sinews thoroughly, and chop finely
2. For every half pound of meat, take a quarter pound of beef marrow
3. Mix the marrow with the meat and pound together in a mortar, carefully removing any remaining sinews
4. After pounding into a mass, salt it, add a large spoonful of soaked bread crumb, mix, and form into thin small cutlets
5. Brush with egg yolk beaten with a little water, sprinkle with grated bread, and fry on clarified butter
6. Pour broth as sauce and garnish with lemon slices

title: Kotlety ze Śledzi (Herring Cutlets)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 herring
- 1 large onion
- butter
- 1 bread roll
- milk
- 1 egg yolk
- pepper to taste

## Directions

1. Soak herring in cold water for 24 hours, clean, remove all bones and chop into a mass
2. For 1 pound of herring mass, peel and grate 1 large onion and fry it in a pan with butter
3. Soak 1 bread roll in milk and squeeze it out
4. Add 1 egg yolk and pepper to taste
5. Put everything in a mortar and mix until the mass becomes white and fluffy

title: Kotlety ze Śledzi (Herring Cutlets)
body:
## Ingredients

- herring
- one large onion
- butter
- one bread roll
- one egg yolk
- pepper to taste
- flour
- egg
- water

## Directions

1. Soak herring in cold water for twenty-four hours, clean, remove all bones and chop into a mass
2. For one pound of herring mass, peel and grate one large onion and fry it in a pan with butter
3. Soak one bread roll in milk and squeeze it out, add one egg yolk and pepper to taste
4. Place all ingredients into a bowl and mix until the mass becomes white and fluffy
5. Turn out onto a floured surface and shape into oblong cutlets
6. Dip the cutlets in egg beaten with water and fry in clarified butter
7. Serve with brown caper or gherkin sauce

title: Kołduny Litewskie (Lithuanian Dumplings)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound sirloin, finely chopped but not minced
- 1 pound kidney fat, minced
- salt
- pepper
- 2 large onions, minced
- broth
- meat scraps
- 1 quart flour
- 1 egg
- water

## Directions

1. Remove sinews from the sirloin and chop very finely without mincing.
2. Mince the kidney fat, add salt and pepper to taste, and mix with the meat.
3. Mince the onions, place in a pan with a little broth, the meat scraps, and braise covered until the onion is tender.
4. Pound in a mortar and pass through a sieve.
5. Add to the prepared meat and mix well.
6. Take one quart of flour, one egg, and enough water to make a fairly soft dough.
7. Knead and roll out as thin as possible.
8. Place mounds of filling the size of a walnut on the dough and seal the edges well.
9. Wrap the dough loosely around the filling, as otherwise the dumplings may burst during cooking.
10. Bring water to a boil in a wide pan and drop in the dumplings.
11. When they float to the surface, they are done; do not cook longer as the meat will harden.
12. Remove with a slotted spoon and serve immediately.
13. Some make these dumplings with fatty lamb, proceeding in exactly the same way as with beef dumplings.

title: Krem Bananowy (Banana Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- half of a box of gelatin
- half a cup cold water
- three large cups milk
- one cup sugar
- three bananas
- sour cream, whipped with sugar or plain

## Directions

1. Soak the contents of half a box of gelatin in half a cup of cold water.
2. Bring three large cups of milk to a boil with one cup of sugar, then pour off a sufficient amount of this milk to dissolve the gelatin.
3. When all the gelatin dissolves, pour it into the rest of the milk and boil for ten minutes, then remove from heat.
4. When it cools so that it is barely warm, but the gelatin has not yet set, peel three bananas and crumble them with a fork, then place in the milk and mix well, and then set in a cold place, preferably on ice.
5. This cream should be made in the evening for use the next day.
6. Serve with sour cream beaten with sugar, or with plain raw sour cream and sugar.

title: Krem Kawowy (Coffee Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a quart of cream
- half a pound of powdered sugar
- two cups of strong coffee
- eight gelatin leaves

## Directions

1. Beat half a quart of cream with half a pound of powdered sugar into stiff meringue
2. Cook two cups of strong coffee and dissolve eight gelatin leaves in it; when the coffee cools, pour it into the beaten cream, mix thoroughly and pour into a mold rinsed with water and sprinkled with sugar and set on ice until serving
3. Tea cream is made in the same way, taking strong tea essence instead of coffee

title: Krem Malinowy (Raspberry Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart of raspberries
- juice of one whole lemon
- quarter pound of powdered sugar
- six gelatin leaves, soaked previously in cold water
- half a cup of hot milk
- half a quart of cream
- quarter pound of powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Press one quart of raspberries through a sieve, add juice from one whole lemon and quarter pound of powdered sugar
2. To six gelatin leaves, soaked previously in cold water, pour half a cup of hot milk and stir until the gelatin completely dissolves, then pour into the raspberry juice and mix
3. Beat half a quart of cream with quarter pound of powdered sugar and then slowly pour the raspberry juice with gelatin into this cream, stirring continuously
4. Beat with a whisk as long as the cream begins to thicken, then pour into a mold rinsed and sprinkled with sugar and set on ice to set
5. Serve at table with small cakes
6. In a similar way one can prepare cream using any other berries

title: Krokiety Wieprzowe (Pork Croquettes)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound lean pork
- a pinch of powdered sage leaves
- one-half teaspoon salt
- black pepper
- flour for coating
- egg for coating
- fat for frying
- flour (for sauce)
- one-half cup milk
- salt and pepper (for sauce)

## Directions

1. Mince very finely one pound of lean pork
2. Add a pinch of powdered sage leaves, one-half teaspoon of salt and some ground black pepper
3. Mix into a smooth mass and form small oval patties
4. Fry on hot fat until well browned on both sides
5. For the sauce: to the fat in which the croquettes were fried, add one tablespoon of flour and mix well while holding the pan over the heat
6. Add one-half cup of milk, salt and pepper to taste
7. Pour this sauce over the croquettes

title: Krokiety z Cielęciny i Ostryg (Croquettes of Veal and Oysters)
body:
## Ingredients

- One cup raw oysters
- One cup cold roasted or cooked veal
- One spoonful of butter
- Three spoonfuls of grated bread or bread crumbs
- Two egg yolks
- One spoonful of grated onion
- Salt and pepper
- Egg for dipping
- Grated bread for coating
- Hot fat for frying

## Directions

1. Chop the veal and oysters very finely
2. Add pepper and salt to taste, then the egg yolks, butter, bread, and onion
3. Mix thoroughly
4. Form into oblong small cutlets from this mass
5. Dip in beaten egg, coat in grated bread, and fry on hot fat until golden

title: Krokiety z Kartofli i Ryżu (Potato and Rice Croquettes)
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup cold cooked potatoes
- one cup cold cooked rice
- salt and pepper to taste
- celery salt, optional
- half a cup milk
- flour
- hot fat for frying

## Directions

1. Grate one cup of cold cooked potatoes into a mass
2. Add one cup of cold cooked rice
3. Season with salt, pepper, and celery salt if desired
4. Pour in half a cup of milk and mix everything into a smooth mass
5. Transfer to a board and form into small oblong patties
6. Coat in flour and fry in hot fat

title: Krokiety z Kury (Chicken Croquettes)
body:
## Ingredients

- leftover roasted or cooked chicken
- chicken meat
- butter
- flour
- milk
- salt
- pepper
- egg
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Remove the meat from bones and chop finely.
2. For each cup of chicken meat, take a spoon of butter and mix on smooth mass with half a spoon of flour.
3. Heat half a cup of milk and thicken it with the butter and flour mixture.
4. Cook until thickened, then add the chopped chicken meat and season with salt and pepper to taste.
5. Mix well and form small flat cutlets.
6. Dip them in beaten egg and then in grated breadcrumbs.
7. Fry in fat or butter.
8. Note: turkey meat can be used in the same way instead of chicken.

title: Krokiety z Mięsa i Ryżu (Meat and Rice Croquettes)
body:
## Ingredients

- any roasted or cooked meat, finely chopped
- one cup chopped meat
- one cup cooked rice
- half cup milk
- two spoonfuls butter
- salt
- pepper
- one beaten egg
- grated bread
- fat for frying

## Directions

1. Chop any roasted or cooked meat very finely
2. Heat half cup milk and add chopped meat and rice, two spoonfuls butter, salt and pepper to taste
3. Mix well, and when mass boils, add one well-beaten egg, remove from heat and mix everything again
4. Place in cool location
5. When mass is completely cooled, place on work surface and form small cutlets
6. Dip in beaten egg and then in grated bread and fry in hot fat until browned

title: Krokiety z Móżdżku (Brain Croquettes)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal brain
- salted water
- butter, one tablespoon
- flour, one tablespoon
- two egg yolks
- salt and white pepper
- bread roll
- egg whites
- hot fat

## Directions

1. Carefully clean the veal brain so no veins or membranes remain and blanch in salted water.
2. Boil one tablespoon of butter with one tablespoon of flour, beat in two egg yolks, add the blanched brain and mash everything into a smooth mass with salt and white pepper added.
3. Spread this mass on a work surface sprinkled with grated bread and form into long small rolls.
4. Beat the remaining whites to a stiff foam and dip the croquettes in it and coat with grated and sifted bread; repeat this several times until the croquettes are evenly coated in bread.
5. Then drop them into hot fat in a pan and fry until evenly browned on all sides.
6. Serve on a platter lined with a napkin and garnish with sprigs of fresh parsley.

title: Krokiety z Resztek (Croquettes from Leftovers)
body:
## Ingredients

- various leftovers of meat from several days
- cooked potatoes or cooked rice from the previous day, at a ratio of one part rice or potatoes to two parts meat
- one beaten egg
- one tablespoon grated bread or breadcrumbs
- salt and pepper to taste
- one tablespoon tomato sauce
- cold milk if needed
- hot lard for frying

## Directions

1. Take various meat leftovers
2. Remove meat from bones and chop finely
3. Add cooked potatoes or cooked rice, using one part rice or potatoes for every two parts meat
4. Add one beaten egg
5. Add one tablespoon grated bread or breadcrumbs
6. Add salt, pepper, and one tablespoon tomato sauce
7. If the mixture is too dry, pour in some cold milk
8. Form small round cutlets from the mixture
9. Dip each cutlet first in beaten egg, then in grated breadcrumbs
10. Fry in hot lard until well browned
11. Sauce can be made by adding a little flour to the lard in which the croquettes were fried

title: Krokiety z Ryb (Fish Croquettes)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup milk
- 2 tablespoons flour
- 1 tablespoon butter
- 1 egg yolk
- 1 tablespoon chopped green parsley
- 1/2 teaspoon onion juice
- a little nutmeg
- 2 cups cooked and cooled fish, finely chopped
- 1 beaten egg
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Bring 1 cup of milk to a boil and thicken with 2 tablespoons of flour mixed with 1 tablespoon of butter
2. Boil, stirring constantly, until thickened
3. Remove from heat and add 1 beaten egg yolk, 1 tablespoon of chopped green parsley, 1/2 teaspoon of onion juice, a little nutmeg and 2 cups of any cooked and cooled fish, finely chopped
4. Mix everything properly, spread on a board and form small cutlets from this mixture
5. Dip each cutlet first in beaten egg and then in breadcrumbs
6. Fry in very hot fat until browned

title: Krokiety z Ryżu (Rice Croquettes)
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup cooked rice
- one egg
- one tablespoon butter
- one tablespoon sugar
- half teaspoon salt
- pinch of nutmeg
- half cup milk
- beaten egg for coating
- grated bread for coating
- hot fat for frying

## Directions

1. Take one cup of cooked rice, one egg, one tablespoon butter, one tablespoon sugar, half teaspoon salt and a pinch of nutmeg
2. Boil half cup of milk, add the rice, sugar, salt and nutmeg
3. Boil once more, then add the beaten egg, mix and place in a cool place
4. When completely cooled, form small cutlets
5. Dip in beaten egg and then in grated bread
6. Fry until golden in hot fat

title: Krokiety z Szynki (Ham Croquettes)
body:
## Ingredients

- two large boiled potatoes
- one cup finely minced cooked ham
- one tablespoon butter
- a pinch of red pepper
- one cup grated breadcrumbs
- two eggs
- grated breadcrumbs (for coating)
- lard for frying

## Directions

1. Mash two large boiled potatoes while hot
2. Add one cup of finely minced cooked ham, one tablespoon of butter, a pinch of red pepper, one cup of grated breadcrumbs, and two eggs
3. Mix everything well with the potatoes
4. When the mixture cools slightly, form small oval patties
5. Coat with grated breadcrumbs, dip in beaten egg, then coat again in grated breadcrumbs
6. Fry in a pan filled with hot lard for two to three minutes no longer
7. Drain on paper

title: Krokiety z Łososia (Salmon Croquettes)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 small can of salmon
- 1 cup milk
- 1 tablespoon flour
- 1 large tablespoon butter
- salt to taste
- 2 well-beaten eggs
- 1 beaten egg
- breadcrumbs
- butter for frying

## Directions

1. Remove salmon from 1 small can, discard bones and chop finely
2. Bring 1 cup of milk to a boil and thicken with 1 tablespoon of flour mixed with 1 large tablespoon of butter
3. Add the chopped salmon to the milk, salt, stir and bring to a boil
4. Then beat in 2 well-beaten eggs and immediately remove from heat and place in a cold location until the mixture cools
5. When completely cooled, form small cutlets, dip each in beaten egg and then in breadcrumbs
6. Fry in butter in a pan

title: Krokiety ze Słodkich Kartofli (Sweet Potato Croquettes)
body:
## Ingredients

- sweet potatoes
- salt
- two eggs
- half a tablespoon butter
- nutmeg, grated
- one teaspoon chopped fresh parsley
- beaten egg
- grated bread crumbs
- hot fat for frying

## Directions

1. Boil sweet potatoes in salted water until tender
2. Drain the water and mash them into a mass
3. Beat two eggs and add to the potatoes
4. Add half a tablespoon butter, salt to taste, and a pinch of grated nutmeg
5. Add one teaspoon of chopped fresh parsley and mix everything
6. Place in a saucepan on low heat
7. Stir constantly until the mass begins to pull away from the sides of the saucepan
8. Transfer to a board and place in a cool place
9. When completely cooled, form into rolls three inches long and one inch thick
10. Dip each in beaten egg and then in grated bread crumbs
11. Fry in hot fat

title: Krupnik (Pearl Barley Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef bones and meat scraps
- vegetables
- 1 cup pearl or barley groats
- butter
- beef broth

## Directions

1. It is most appropriate to serve krupnik on the day when there is roast beef for dinner, as all pieces of meat unsuitable for roasting can be used for krupnik
2. Cook broth as usual from bones and meat scraps
3. Cook finely chopped vegetables separately
4. Cook 1 cup of barley or pearl groats separately with the addition of a few tablespoons of fat from the broth
5. When the groats are sufficiently softened and separate, add 1 tablespoon of butter, mix and dilute with cooked broth
6. To the cooked vegetables add some fresh parsley, place everything in a dish and cover with krupnik
7. Remember that an important condition for good krupnik is cooking the groats separately, because if you sprinkle the broth with groats immediately, the krupnik will be white and sticky

title: Krupnik Amerykański (American Barley Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds veal bones
- two quarts cold water
- half a cup pearl barley
- one cup hot cream
- salt and pepper to taste
- meat patties as garnish

## Directions

1. Take two pounds of veal bones, cover with two quarts of cold water and cook on very low heat for three hours.
2. Collect the scum and strain.
3. Rinse half a cup of pearl barley in cold water and pour into a pot and cover with the prepared broth, then cover and cook on low heat until the barley softens.
4. Remove one-third of the barley and place in another pot, pass the rest through a sieve and pour everything onto the whole barley.
5. Add one cup of hot cream, add salt and pepper to taste and serve at table.
6. Meat patties are served as a garnish to this soup.
7. Instead of veal bones, one can use bones left from a roasted chicken or turkey.

title: Krupnik Amerykański (American Barley Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds veal bones
- 2 quarts cold water
- 1/2 cup pearl groats

## Directions

1. Take 2 pounds of veal bones, cover with 2 quarts of cold water and cook on very low heat for three hours
2. Collect the scum and strain
3. Rinse 1/2 cup of pearl groats in cold water and add to the pot

title: Krupnik Amerykański na Mleku (American Barley Soup with Milk)
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb bones, preferably from the shoulder
- one small veal bone
- three quarts cold water
- four tablespoons pearl barley
- chopped onion, some amount
- several peppercorns
- nutmeg, some amount
- one clove
- one or two celery stalks
- one quart good milk
- one tablespoon butter
- one tablespoon flour
- salt to taste
- small croutons from white bread

## Directions

1. Take lamb bones (preferably from the shoulder) and a small veal bone, cover with three quarts of cold water, add four tablespoons of pearl barley, some chopped onion, several peppercorns, a bit of nutmeg, one clove and one or two celery stalks, then cook on very low heat for four to five hours.
2. Then strain.
3. Separately bring one quart of good milk to a boil with one tablespoon of butter and one tablespoon of flour.
4. Pour the milk into the strained broth, salt to taste and pour into a dish lined with small croutons from white bread.

title: Krupnik Angielski (English Barley Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef broth
- vegetables as customary
- pearl barley
- burnt sugar for coloring
- finely chopped parsley

## Directions

1. Prepare beef broth with vegetables added as usual.
2. Toward the end, add barley and cook until it is soft.
3. Before serving, color with burnt sugar, add finely chopped parsley and serve.

title: Krupnik na Ogonach (Barley Soup with Ox Tail)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 ox tail
- cold water
- vegetables
- barley groats
- garlic sauce

## Directions

1. Cut one ox tail into eight to ten pieces
2. Cover with cold water and cook
3. When the scum stops appearing, carefully collect it
4. Add all kinds of vegetables that are on hand
5. Add barley groats and cook for three hours on low heat
6. When the meat is cooked, remove it and serve separately in garlic sauce
7. Pour the soup into a dish

title: Krupnik na Ogonach (Barley Soup with Oxtail)
body:
## Ingredients

- oxtail, cut into 8 to 10 pieces
- cold water
- vegetables as available
- pearl barley
- garlic sauce

## Directions

1. Cut the oxtail into 8 to 10 pieces and cover with cold water and cook.
2. When the scum appears, collect it carefully, and continue until it stops appearing.
3. Add all available vegetables and sprinkle in barley and cook for three hours on low heat.
4. When the meat is cooked, remove it and add separately in garlic sauce, and pour the soup into a dish.

title: Krupnik Postny (Lenten Pearl Barley Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- pearl barley
- mixed vegetables (włoszczyzna)
- butter
- salt
- two egg yolks
- sweet sour cream
- parsley

## Directions

1. For four people, cook a quarter-quart of fine pearl barley with whole mixed vegetables so it can be discarded
2. Add a spoonful of butter and salt to taste
3. When the barley is cooked, beat two egg yolks in a bowl with a cup of sweet sour cream, add finely chopped parsley and then pour the soup over it, constantly stirring

title: Kumys (Kumiss)
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart fresh unskimmed milk
- half cup fresh buttermilk
- four pieces white sugar or one tablespoon granulated sugar
- yeast (optional substitute for buttermilk)

## Directions

1. Pour half cup of fresh buttermilk into one quart of fresh unskimmed milk and add four pieces of white sugar or one tablespoon of granulated sugar
2. Pour into a pitcher and stir until all the sugar dissolves
3. Place in a warm place for ten hours
4. After ten hours, when the milk thickens, mix it well so it becomes a uniform liquid, which is best accomplished by pouring from one pitcher to another several dozen times
5. Pour into clean bottles, cork and tie the cork with wire or string
6. Place in a warm place for twenty-four hours
7. Before use, shake the bottle well several times
8. Instead of buttermilk, yeast can be used and the kumiss will have a slightly different taste

title: Kura Parowana (Steamed Chicken)
body:
## Ingredients

- young chicken weighing three or four pounds, not fatty
- several stalks of celery
- one large onion
- three cups of grated bread or bread
- one tablespoon of chopped parsley
- one teaspoon of salt
- half teaspoon of white pepper
- two pinches of red pepper
- one beaten egg
- one tablespoon of butter
- water

## Directions

1. Select a young chicken, not fatty. Cut off the head and then pull the skin from the neck as far as possible and cut the neck close to the body without damaging the skin.
2. Remove the crop through the neck without damaging the skin on the breast.
3. Clean and dry the inside.
4. Chop several stalks of celery, one large onion and add three cups of grated bread or bread, one tablespoon of chopped parsley, one teaspoon of salt, half teaspoon of white pepper, two pinches of red pepper, one beaten egg and one tablespoon of butter.
5. Mix everything into a smooth mass and fill the chicken inside and also the breast, putting the stuffing through the skin to the neck, which should then be wrapped on the back and sewn or fastened.
6. Put the rest of the stuffing inside the chicken, then sew it up, tie the legs to the body and steam in a double pan for two hours or longer until the meat is completely tender, keeping only a small amount of water in the lower part of the pan.
7. Place on a deep platter and pour over the following sauce: Brown two tablespoons of butter with two tablespoons of flour; add half teaspoon of salt, quarter teaspoon of white pepper, a pinch of red pepper; mix, then slowly pour in half a quart of water over which the chicken was steamed, stirring constantly.
8. Separately cook half a quart of finely chopped celery in a very small amount of water; just before serving pour the celery into the sauce, mix and pour over the chicken.

title: Kura po Wirginijsku (Virginia-Style Chicken)
body:
## Ingredients

- medium chicken, three to four pounds
- water
- potatoes (one per person)
- fresh corn kernels, scraped from cobs with a sharp knife
- white beans
- tomatoes
- salt
- pepper
- rice, cooked

## Directions

1. Clean and quarter the chicken.
2. Place in a small amount of water and cook for fifteen minutes.
3. Add potatoes (one per person), a handful of fresh corn kernels scraped from the cob, a handful of white beans, three tomatoes, a teaspoon of salt, and a quarter teaspoon of pepper.
4. Cook on low heat under a cover for one and a half hours.
5. Serve on a deep platter with the vegetables and sauce; serve cooked rice separately.

title: Kura Smażona (Fried Chicken)
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken weighing about four pounds
- chicken liver
- one tablespoon milk
- one teaspoon salt
- pinch of pepper
- one egg
- grated bread
- fat for frying
- thin slices of lemon

## Directions

1. Divide the chicken into six pieces and flatten each piece, giving it the shape of a cutlet.
2. Take the liver and pound it into a paste with one tablespoon of milk, one teaspoon of salt and a pinch of pepper.
3. Beat one egg, dip the quartered chicken in it, then spread the liver paste on it, dip in egg again, and then coat with grated bread.
4. Fry until well browned in a pan or deep skillet filled with fat.
5. Place on a platter and arrange thin slices of lemon around it.

title: Kura Smażona (Fried Chicken)
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken, about four pounds
- chicken liver
- milk
- salt
- pepper
- egg

## Directions

1. Cut the chicken weighing about four pounds into six pieces and flatten each piece, shaping them like cutlets.
2. Take the liver and pound it into a paste with a spoon of milk, a teaspoon of salt, and a pinch of pepper.
3. Beat one egg and prepare remaining ingredients for breading.

title: Kura z Kwaśną Kapustą (Chicken with Sauerkraut)
body:
## Ingredients

- small young chicken
- salt
- pepper
- flour
- boiled potatoes
- slices of smoked bacon
- one quart of sauerkraut
- cold water

## Directions

1. Clean the small young chicken and dry the inside, then sprinkle the outside with salt, pepper and a good amount of flour.
2. Pound boiled potatoes into a mass and while hot stuff the chicken with them.
3. Take a deep pan with a tight cover (clay dishes are best), place a few slices of smoked bacon on the bottom, put the chicken on top and again a few slices of bacon on top.
4. Take one quart of sauerkraut, which must be fresh and not too sour, rinse it with cold water and place it on the chicken so that it is completely covered with cabbage.
5. Place in a moderately hot oven, pour in one cup of water and bake for three hours, watching carefully so it does not dry out, so water should be added from time to time.
6. When serving, place the chicken in the center of the platter and surround it with sauerkraut.

title: Kura z Makaronem po Chińsku (Chinese-Style Chicken with Noodles)
body:
## Ingredients

- fine noodles (vermicelli)
- salted water
- sharp tomato sauce
- small chicken, about two pounds
- celery stalks
- large onion (preferably Bermuda)
- salt
- white pepper
- red pepper
- ginger powder
- cinnamon
- cloves
- butter

## Directions

1. Cook the noodles in salted water, then drain.
2. Place the drained noodles in a small pot and add half a cup of sharp tomato sauce.
3. Roast or cook the chicken and shred both white and dark meat into match-stick sized pieces.
4. Chop four large celery stalks and finely chop one large onion, preferably Bermuda.
5. Add salt, white and red pepper, and a pinch of powdered ginger, cinnamon, and cloves.
6. Mix everything together, place in a buttered pot, and fry over heat, constantly stirring, until everything turns brown.
7. Transfer to a serving dish and serve immediately.

title: Kurczęta Duszone (Braised Chickens)
body:
## Ingredients

- young chickens
- salt
- butter
- grated bread

## Directions

1. Prepare the chickens as usual and salt them.
2. Heat one tablespoon of butter in a pan, but do not brown it, then put the chickens in and brown them on all sides on very high heat.
3. Then place with the pan in the oven, add a bit more butter, cover with a lid and bake for half an hour.
4. When they are almost baked, put grated bread on a plate and roll the chickens in it, put them back in the pan and continue baking, turning frequently so the bread lightly browns.

title: Kurczęta Pieczone (Roasted Chickens)
body:
## Ingredients

- young chickens
- bread
- butter
- chopped parsley
- salt
- flour
- one egg
- grated and sifted bread
- milk

## Directions

1. After slaughtering, pluck the chickens immediately, clean them and hang them in a cool place for at least twelve hours so they become firm.
2. Do not scald them, but pluck them dry, as scalded ones have a completely different and worse taste.
3. After preparing, make a stuffing from bread, butter and chopped parsley, stuff the chickens, salt them and sprinkle generously with flour.
4. Beat one egg on a plate, dip the chickens in it and immediately sprinkle with grated and sifted bread so thickly that the meat is covered everywhere.
5. Place the prepared chickens in a pan, put a piece of butter on each one, and put in a hot oven for forty or forty-five minutes.
6. Alternatively instead of bread with butter and parsley, you can make a stuffing by mixing with egg yolk some bread previously soaked in milk; then add butter and parsley.

title: Kurczęta po Hiszpańsku (Chickens Spanish Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- young chickens
- flour
- raw butter
- tomatoes
- sieve

## Directions

1. Prepare the chickens as usual for roasting, lightly sprinkle with flour, spread with raw butter and put in a hot oven, but for no longer than ten minutes, then remove immediately and let them cool.
2. Stew several raw tomatoes in a pan, allowing two large tomatoes for each chicken, then pass through a sieve so that all seeds and skins remain on it.

title: Kurczęta po Hiszpańsku (Spanish-Style Chicken)
body:
## Ingredients

- Chicken
- Flour
- Butter
- Raw tomatoes (2 large tomatoes per chicken)
- Paprika

## Directions

1. Prepare the chicken as usual for roasting and lightly dust with flour
2. Smear with raw butter and place in a hot oven for no longer than ten minutes, then remove and let cool
3. Stew several raw tomatoes in a pot, calculating two large tomatoes for each chicken, then pass through a sieve so that all the seeds and skins remain on it
4. Cut the cooled chicken in half, place in a pot, pour over the sieved tomatoes, place good butter on top with a little paprika and stew under a cover until tender
5. Arrange on a serving platter, pour over the sauce which must be thick, and serve at table

title: Kurczęta po Wiedeńsku (Chickens Viennese Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- young chickens
- salt
- flour
- one egg
- grated bread
- hot fat for frying
- parsley sprigs
- lemon slices

## Directions

1. Clean the young chickens as usual, cut them into quarters, salt them and sprinkle lightly with flour.
2. Dip each quarter in beaten egg, coat with grated bread and fry in hot fat, of which there should be enough to completely cover the chickens.
3. After frying, garnish with parsley sprigs and lemon slices and serve.

title: Kurczęta w Słoninie (Chickens in Bacon)
body:
## Ingredients

- young chickens
- salt
- thin but wide slices of young, unsalted bacon
- string
- butter
- grated bread
- lettuce or cucumber salad

## Directions

1. Prepare the chickens as usual, then salt them and cover completely with thin but wide slices of young, unsalted bacon and tie with string.
2. Place the prepared chickens in a pan and bake in a moderately hot oven.
3. After baking, unwrap the string and serve the chickens with the bacon, which should be browned.
4. If one does not like bacon, discard it and pour the chickens with butter browned with grated bread.
5. Serve such chickens with lettuce or cucumber salad.

title: Kuropatwy Pieczone (Roasted Partridges)
body:
## Ingredients

- partridges
- thin slices of lard
- butter
- grated bread
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Pluck and clean carefully, discard the offal
2. Stuff the breasts with thinly sliced lard
3. Roast, continually basting with butter
4. Shortly before removing from the oven, sprinkle with grated bread, brown and pour over browned butter
5. Can be stuffed with a forcemeat made from grated bread and butter, with a small addition of lemon juice

title: Lamb Sauce (Sharp Sauce for Lamb)
body:
## Ingredients

- one onion
- one small carrot
- a piece of ham
- bay leaf
- several peppercorns
- celery stalk
- butter
- two tablespoons vinegar
- some broth
- sour cream (optional, for wild sauce variation)

## Directions

1. Chop finely one onion, one small carrot, and a piece of ham. Add a bay leaf, several peppercorns, and a celery stalk.
2. Brown all of this in butter. Then add two tablespoons of vinegar and some broth and stew on low heat until tender.
3. Press through a sieve and pour into the sauce that remained on the roasting pan from the lamb roast and bring to a boil once more.
4. This sauce is called sharp. To make wild sauce, add several tablespoons of sour cream to it.

title: Legumina Bursztynowa (Amber Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a quart of milk
- half a cup of cornmeal
- half a quart of sliced apples
- half a cup of molasses
- quarter teaspoon of salt
- one quart of milk
- sugar
- cream

## Directions

1. Bring half a quart of milk to a boil, add half a cup of cornmeal and half a quart of apples sliced into pieces, mix and add half a cup of molasses and a quarter teaspoon of salt.
2. Remove from heat, add one quart of milk, mix well and pour all the mass into a baking mold, place in a gentle oven and let it bake for three hours.
3. Remove from the oven and cool.
4. Serve cold with sugar and cream.

title: Legumina Cytrynowa (Lemon Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- three eggs
- one cup sugar
- juice of one large lemon
- grated lemon zest
- one quart milk
- half quart grated buns or white bread
- preserves or jam
- two whole eggs
- two tablespoons sugar

## Directions

1. Beat three eggs with one cup sugar
2. Add juice from one large lemon and grated zest
3. Add one quart milk and half quart grated buns or white bread
4. Mix everything together and bake in a baking tin in a moderately hot oven
5. Remove from oven and cover the top with preserves or jam
6. Beat strongly two whole eggs with two tablespoons sugar
7. Arrange the beaten eggs and sugar on top of the jam
8. Put in the oven for a moment until browned
9. Serve to the table

title: Legumina Cytrynowa (Lemon Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- three eggs
- one cup of sugar
- juice from one large lemon
- grated peel of lemon
- one quart

## Directions

1. Beat three eggs with one cup of sugar, add juice from one large lemon and the grated peel, one quart

title: Legumina Kartoflana (Potato Starch Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- eight egg yolks
- quarter pound of sugar
- half a lemon
- peel of one whole lemon
- sugar cubes
- remaining egg whites
- two ounces of potato starch
- butter for greasing
- half a quart of fresh sour cream
- fine sugar
- vanilla

## Directions

1. Beat eight egg yolks with a quarter of sugar until white, then squeeze half a lemon, rub the peel of a whole lemon against three sugar cubes, which then pound and also add.
2. Beat the remaining egg whites into a stiff foam and add slowly to the yolks, while slowly sprinkling potato starch, of which use two ounces.
3. After mixing, grease a mold or form with butter, pour the mixed mass and put into a moderately hot oven for twenty minutes.
4. When it rises and browns, test with a toothpick or match: if the mass inside is dry and does not stick to the match, it is done.
5. Then turn out onto a platter and cut horizontally with a wide knife.
6. Beat half a quart of fresh sour cream with fine sugar and vanilla, spread this mixture thickly on the bottom half of the pudding, cover with the top half, and arrange the rest of the cream on top and serve to the table.
7. This proportion is for eight people.

title: Legumina Orzechowa (Walnut Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of walnuts in their shells
- eight egg yolks
- half a pound of sugar
- stiffly beaten egg whites from the remaining whites
- two ounces of potato starch
- butter for greasing the mold

## Directions

1. Peel the walnuts from their shells and pound them in a mortar into a paste.
2. Whip eight egg yolks with half a pound of sugar in a bowl.
3. Add the walnuts gradually and continue to grind for another half hour.
4. When the entire mass is well ground, beat a stiff foam from the remaining egg whites and add this foam one spoonful at a time.
5. Also add two ounces of potato starch, sprinkling it in by small spoonfuls at a time and mixing.
6. When everything is mixed this way, place it in a mold greased with butter for baking and put it into a moderately hot oven for forty to forty-five minutes.
7. After removing from the oven, cover with preserves or fruit jelly and serve on a platter or serving dish.

title: Legumina Parzona (Steamed Legume Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a quart of milk
- quarter pound of clarified butter
- two ounces of sugar
- one cup of potato starch or half a quart of wheat flour
- eight egg yolks
- one and a half tablespoons of sugar
- a little nutmeg
- salt to taste
- hot water
- fruit juice, preferably raspberry

## Directions

1. Pour half a quart of milk into a pot, add quarter pound of clarified butter and two ounces of sugar, and heat until well boiling.
2. Add one cup of potato starch or half a quart of wheat flour, pouring gradually and stirring constantly.
3. Cook until the dough pulls away from the sides of the pot, then remove from heat and let cool.
4. When completely cool, slowly beat in eight egg yolks one by one with a spoon.
5. Add one and a half tablespoons of sugar, a little nutmeg, and salt to taste.
6. After mixing well, pour into a mold greased with butter and place the mold in a pot filled with hot water to a depth of one and a half to two inches.
7. Cook on a strong fire for one to two hours, adding hot water as it evaporates.
8. Alternatively, bake in a hot oven for about half an hour.
9. Serve with fruit juice, preferably raspberry.

title: Legumina Pomarańczowa (Orange Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart milk
- two tablespoons cornmeal
- water
- one and a half cups sugar
- three egg yolks
- juice of three oranges
- grated zest of one orange
- three egg whites

## Directions

1. Bring one quart milk to a boil
2. Add two tablespoons cornmeal mixed with a little water
3. Add one and a half cups sugar and three beaten egg yolks
4. Cook for three minutes
5. Squeeze juice from three oranges and grate zest from one, then add
6. Mix well and place in a baking pan
7. Cover with thick meringue made from three egg whites
8. Put in the oven to brown

title: Legumina z Brzoskwiń (Peach Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- three cups of milk
- two tablespoons of cornmeal
- cold water
- one tablespoon of butter
- three egg yolks
- half a cup of sugar
- canned peaches
- two egg whites
- three teaspoons of fine sugar
- peach syrup

## Directions

1. Bring three cups of milk to a boil and immediately add two tablespoons of cornmeal mixed with a little cold water, then cook for a short time until it thickens slightly.
2. Remove from heat, add one tablespoon of butter and three egg yolks beaten well with half a cup of sugar.
3. Drain the syrup from a tin of marinated peaches, place them on a baking sheet for baking pastries and pour the previously cooked mass over them.
4. Then put into a hot oven for ten minutes.
5. After baking, remove from the oven and cover the top with foam from two egg whites beaten stiffly with three teaspoons of fine sugar and put back in the oven to brown.
6. Serve to the table with peach syrup, to which add sugar if it is not sweet enough.

title: Legumina z Chleba (Bread Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- four egg yolks
- peel of one lemon
- one quart of milk
- half a quart of grated bread
- two tablespoons of sugar
- butter for greasing
- two egg whites
- four tablespoons of sugar

## Directions

1. Beat four egg yolks well and add to them the grated peel of one lemon.
2. Into one quart of milk sprinkle half a quart of grated bread, mix and add the beaten yolks and two tablespoons of sugar.
3. After mixing, pour into a tin mold greased with butter and place in a moderately hot oven.
4. Remove from the oven as soon as the mass thickens well, taking care not to keep it in the oven too long, as water will appear.
5. After removing from the oven, pile thick foam from two egg whites beaten with four tablespoons of sugar on top, put back into the oven and remove immediately when the whites brown on top.

title: Legumina z Chleba i Jabłek (Bread and Apple Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a small loaf of white bread
- butter
- several sour apples
- sugar

## Directions

1. Cut half a small loaf of white bread into thin slices and spread thinly with butter
2. Peel, remove seeds, and cut several sour apples into small pieces
3. Take a baking tin and line it with bread
4. Place a layer of apples on top, then sprinkle with sugar
5. Continue layering until the pan is full
6. Put into the oven and bake until the apples are completely soft
7. Serve usually with cream separately

title: Legumina z Chleba i Jabłek (Bread and Apple Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a small loaf of white bread
- butter
- several firm apples
- sugar
- cream

## Directions

1. Cut half a small loaf of white bread into thin slices and spread thinly with butter.
2. Peel, remove seeds and cut into small pieces several firm apples.
3. Take a tin baking mold and line it with bread, then place a layer of apples, then sprinkle with sugar and so on until the dish is full, then put into the oven and bake until the apples are completely soft.
4. Cream is usually served separately with this pudding.

title: Legumina z Makaronu (Noodle Legume Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of flour
- two or three eggs
- cream
- sugar
- fresh butter
- orange zest
- Method 2: four egg yolks
- quarter pound of sugar
- one tablespoon of butter

## Directions

1. Method 1: Knead one pound of flour with two or three eggs, roll out thin, and cut like regular noodles. Cook in abundant water. After draining, mix the noodles with cream, sugar, and fresh butter, adding finely chopped orange zest fried in sugar. Grease a pot or mold with butter, place the noodles in it, and bake for half an hour.
2. Method 2: Cook noodles made from one pound of flour as usual, drain, and add four egg yolks beaten with quarter pound of sugar and one tablespoon of butter, mixing well. Finally add the foam from beaten egg whites, mix lightly, and place in the oven for one hour. Both proportions above are for six people.

title: Legumina z Naleśników (Pancake Soufflé)
body:
## Ingredients

- six egg yolks
- two egg whites for foam
- two additional egg whites
- flour
- butter
- milk
- water
- salt
- quarter pound almonds
- quarter pound sugar
- melted butter
- quarter pound chocolate
- regular sugar

## Directions

1. Make pancake batter as usual but using six egg yolks instead of four, and making foam from two egg whites instead of four, mixing with the batter and reserving the remaining two egg whites
2. Should make about twenty pancakes
3. After frying, cut pancakes first into strips, then into small cubes
4. Grate quarter pound almonds, add quarter pound sugar and a few tablespoons melted butter, mix with the chopped pancakes, then arrange on a large metal plate or platter
5. Grate quarter pound chocolate, beat the remaining four egg whites into stiff foam and mix with the chocolate, then pour this mixture over the arranged pancakes on the platter
6. Sprinkle with regular sugar on top and place in a hot oven but with heat only from above
7. After twenty minutes serve to the table
8. Instead of the almond mixture, may use baked apples pressed through a sieve; the apple mixture should then be beaten with sugar and arranged on the chopped pancakes, then baked in the oven as above

title: Legumina z Ryżu i Jabłek (Rice and Apple Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound of rice
- water
- butter
- quarter pound of sugar
- eight firm apples
- preserves or currants with sugar and cinnamon

## Directions

1. Blanch half a pound of rice and cook it in water, then add butter, and finally add a quarter pound of sugar and mix well.
2. Peel eight firm apples and hollow out the center so as to remove all the seeds, then place them in a mold, pour a small amount of water and cook, but not until soft, only halfway.
3. Fill the hollows in the apples with preserves or currants with sugar and cinnamon.
4. Grease a mold or form with butter, spread a layer of rice on the bottom, arrange the prepared apples on the rice, cover with the rest of the rice, sprinkle with a little sugar mixed with cinnamon and put into a moderately hot oven for half an hour.
5. Alternatively, instead of hollowing out, peel the apples, cut them into slices raw and then layer them between the rice, sprinkling with sugar mixed with cinnamon, with the addition of currants and butter.
6. This proportion is for six people.

title: Legumina z Ryżu i Pomarańcz (Rice and Orange Pudding)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound of rice
- one large tablespoon of butter
- juice from four medium-sized oranges
- juice from one lemon
- peel of one orange
- sugar
- four egg whites
- sugar for beating

## Directions

1. Blanch and cook half a pound of rice until soft, with the addition of one large tablespoon of butter, then set aside to cool.
2. When completely cooled, squeeze into the rice the juice from four medium-sized oranges and one lemon.
3. Rub the peel of one orange against sugar, then pound it and add it, also add another quarter pound of sugar or more, depending on the sweetness of the oranges, then mix everything thoroughly.
4. Arrange on a platter or tin plate, pour foam from four egg whites beaten with sugar and bake in a gentle oven so that heat comes from above only.
5. When it browns well on top, serve to the table.
6. This proportion is for eight people.

title: Lemon Beer (Piwo Cytrynowe)
body:
## Ingredients

- one large lemon
- one gallon of hot water
- one ounce of ground ginger
- one pound of sugar
- one cup of yeast

## Directions

1. Slice one large lemon and pour one gallon of hot water over it
2. Add one ounce of ground ginger and one pound of sugar
3. Stir until sugar dissolves and then cool
4. Pour in one cup of yeast and set at room temperature for fermentation, which lasts about eighteen hours
5. When fermentation is complete, pour the clear liquid into bottles and cork

title: Lemon Liqueur (Nalewka Cytrynowa)
body:
## Ingredients

- three lemons with skin
- one quart of strong brandy
- one pound of sugar in pieces

## Directions

1. Slice three lemons with the skin into slices
2. Pour one quart of strong brandy over them and set in a cool place
3. After six days, squeeze out all the liquid from the lemons, strain and add one pound of sugar in pieces
4. When the sugar dissolves, pour into bottles and cork

title: Lemoniada (Lemonade)
body:
## Ingredients

- half pound sugar
- half quart water
- lemon peels
- lemon juice (to taste)
- mineral or regular water
- red sour wine (optional)
- thin lemon slices for serving

## Directions

1. Make a syrup from half pound sugar and half quart water, add several lemon peels and let it sit for one hour
2. Remove the peels, add lemon juice to taste
3. Add mineral or regular water and serve
4. Red sour wine can be added and the lemonade will be pink with a slightly different flavor
5. When serving, place a thin lemon slice in each glass

title: Lukier Czekoladowy (Chocolate Icing)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 ounce chocolate
- 1 teaspoon powdered sugar
- Cooked icing (as described above)

## Directions

1. Melt the chocolate
2. Add powdered sugar and mix well
3. Add enough cooked icing to achieve desired color

title: Lukier Gotowany (Cooked Frosting)
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup of sugar
- one-third cup of water
- one-eighth teaspoon of cream of tartar
- one egg

## Directions

1. Cook over low heat one cup of sugar with one-third cup of water and one-eighth teaspoon of cream of tartar until a small portion of this mass dropped in cold water forms a soft ball
2. Then beat one egg well and pour the above mass into it in the thinnest stream, constantly stirring
3. Spread on the cake with a knife dipped in warm water

title: Lukier Gotowany (Cooked Icing)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup sugar
- 1/3 cup water
- 1/8 teaspoon cream of tartar
- 1 egg

## Directions

1. Cook sugar, water, and cream of tartar over low heat until a small amount of the mixture dropped in cold water forms a soft ball
2. Beat one egg well
3. Pour the sugar mixture into the egg in the thinnest stream possible, stirring constantly
4. Spread on cake with a knife dipped in warm water
5. Chopped almonds or nuts may be added to this icing

title: Lukier na Prędce (Quick Icing)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 egg white
- 1 teaspoon lemon juice
- 1 small cup sugar

## Directions

1. Mix egg white, lemon juice, and sugar into a mass
2. Beat strongly for five minutes
3. Spread on cake immediately after removing it from the oven while hot

title: Lukier Pomarańczowy (Orange Icing)
body:
## Ingredients

- Yellow part of orange peel
- 3 teaspoons lemon juice
- 1 egg white
- 1 small cup sugar

## Directions

1. Rub the yellow part of orange peel
2. Pour over three teaspoons of lemon juice and let sit for half an hour
3. Strain through cloth and squeeze out juice
4. For every teaspoon of juice obtained, take one egg white and one small cup of sugar
5. Mix everything well and beat strongly for five minutes
6. Spread on cake with a knife dipped in warm water

title: Lukier Twardy (Hard Icing)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup powdered sugar
- 1 tablespoon lemon juice
- 1 tablespoon boiling water

## Directions

1. Beat the powdered sugar, lemon juice, and boiling water strongly until a smooth, half-transparent mass forms
2. Cover the cake while hot, immediately after removing from the oven

title: Lukier Złoty (Golden Icing)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 egg yolks
- 1 cup sugar
- 1/2 teaspoon Jamaican rum

## Directions

1. Beat egg yolks, sugar, and rum into a smooth mass
2. If not thick enough, add more sugar
3. Spread on cake with a knife dipped in warm water

title: Majonez Provençal (Provençal Mayonnaise)
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken
- mixed vegetables (włoszczyzna)
- large bread loaf
- fat
- flour
- mayonnaise
- crayfish tails
- calf's foot jelly
- crayfish necks

## Directions

1. Cook the chicken with vegetables as usual and cut into equal-sized pieces; remove bones and skin as much as possible.
2. Take a large loaf of bread, trim the crust from all sides, and fry it on a large griddle until hardened.
3. Cool the bread, then mix a little flour with fat and coat the bread so the fat hardens on the bread but does not soak in, making it look like it is glazed.
4. Make mayonnaise following the method for 'Mayonnaise with Chicken' but make it thicker.
5. Dip each piece of chicken in the mayonnaise and arrange the pieces on the bread standing as vertically as possible.
6. Surround with crayfish tails, calf's foot jelly, crayfish necks, and similar garnishes.

title: Majonez z Kur (Mayonnaise with Chicken)
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked chicken
- good clarified butter
- flour
- chicken broth
- oil
- salt
- sugar
- lemon or vinegar

## Directions

1. Cook the chicken as for a stew.
2. For two chickens, take a quarter pound of good clarified butter and heat with two spoons of flour.
3. Mix with a quart of chicken broth (with all fat removed) and cook together until thickened.
4. Cool and beat in a bowl until it becomes like sour cream.
5. Slowly pour in half a pound of oil, adding it by the spoonful and constantly stirring with a whisk.
6. Add salt and continue whisking until the sauce becomes white and thick.
7. Add the juice of a whole lemon or enough vinegar to make the sauce sufficiently sour.
8. Add a teaspoon of fine sugar and pour over the chicken arranged on a platter.

title: Majonez z Ryb (Fish Mayonnaise)
body:
## Ingredients

- pike or sandacz weighing at least eight to ten pounds
- various vegetables
- salt
- gelatin
- egg whites
- vinegar
- burnt sugar
- red gelatin
- oil (one and one-half to two pounds for a ten-pound fish)
- egg yolks
- white pepper
- mushrooms
- cornichons
- cooked vegetables (carrots, peas, potatoes)
- lettuce
- capers

## Directions

1. Use a pike or sandacz weighing no less than eight to ten pounds.
2. Clean the fish and carefully split it through the belly with a sharp knife.
3. Carefully remove the backbone without damaging the skin.
4. Fold the fish back together so it looks whole.
5. Prepare a court-bouillon (smak) from various vegetables, heavily salted, and cool it.
6. Place the fish in a long pan so it fits completely.
7. Pour the cold vegetable court-bouillon over it.
8. Cook on a low fire until tender.
9. Remove from heat and place in a cool location where it must stay until the next day.
10. On the next day, remove the fish and place it on another platter.
11. To the remaining court-bouillon add gelatin and bring to a boil.
12. Beat two egg whites with a spoon of water, add vinegar and slowly pour this into the gelatin mixture, continuously beating with a whisk.
13. Strain the gelatin and court-bouillon mixture, then place on fire and boil until it clears.
14. Remove from heat and strain through dense cloth into a deep platter and cool to make an aspic for coating the fish.
15. This aspic can be made in two or more colors, using burnt sugar for yellow coloring and red gelatin for pink color.
16. For a ten-pound fish, take one and one-half to two pounds of oil.
17. Beat two egg yolks in a bowl, mix them well.
18. Add salt and a pinch of white pepper.
19. Slowly pour the oil while stirring vigorously.
20. When half a pound of oil has been incorporated, add another beaten egg yolk, a few spoons of oil, and then a fourth egg yolk.
21. Continue stirring and pouring the oil in drops until all is used.
22. Do this in a cool place.
23. When the mayonnaise is thick, add strong vinegar to taste and stir in one teaspoon of fine sugar.
24. Cover the entire fish arranged on a platter with this mayonnaise.
25. Around it arrange aspic, neatly sliced mushrooms, cornichons and cooked vegetables such as carrots, green peas, potatoes etc., which should be cut into cubes, stars and other neat shapes.
26. These vegetables are best cut raw and cooked in heavily salted water.
27. Arrange lettuce leaves around the platter.
28. Cut the aspic with a serrated knife into triangles.
29. Sprinkle capers over the mayonnaise on top.

title: Majonez ze Śledzia (Herring Mayonnaise)
body:
## Ingredients

- one large fat herring
- two or three large potatoes
- one egg yolk
- two tablespoons oil
- mustard
- salt
- mushrooms
- gherkins
- capers

## Directions

1. Soak one large fat herring in cold water for eighteen hours, then clean, remove skin and all bones and slice thinly
2. Cook two or three large potatoes, peel and slice thinly
3. Beat one egg yolk with two tablespoons of oil, a little mustard and salt
4. On a small plate, layer herring, then potatoes, alternating until a small cake is formed
5. Cover the entire cake with mayonnaise so it is completely covered
6. Decorate with mushrooms, gherkins, capers, etc.
7. Serve for breakfast

title: Makaron (Pasta)
body:
## Ingredients

- four eggs
- flour, about one pound

## Directions

1. Beat four eggs and knead them with as much flour as they will take, about one pound, and the dough should be as hard as possible.
2. Roll out as thinly as possible and leave on the work surface for half an hour or longer to dry.
3. Then roll it into a log and cut very thinly.
4. Scatter the cut pasta loosely on the work surface for at least an hour.
5. Shortly before serving the soup or broth, bring water to a boil in a pot, add some salt and drop in the pasta.
6. When it floats to the surface, it is a sign that it is cooked.
7. Pour it onto a colander, and when the water drains, put it in a bowl and pour broth over it.
8. It can also be cooked in broth and will be even better than cooked in water, but then the broth will not be as clear.

title: Makaron z Jabłkami (Pasta with Apples)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound flour
- three eggs
- six medium apples
- one tablespoon butter
- sugar
- cinnamon
- bread crumbs, grated

## Directions

1. Make pasta in the usual way with half a pound of flour and three eggs, roll out very thinly and cut very fine like thin threads and fairly short, then scatter in a thin layer on a baking sheet and place in a hot oven, and remove only when well browned.
2. Cook six medium-sized apples with one tablespoon of butter and such an amount of sugar to obtain a very sweet marmalade, but not too thick.
3. Grease a baking dish with butter and thickly sprinkle with grated bread crumbs, then place a layer of pasta on the bottom, then a layer of apple marmalade and so on, until all the pasta and marmalade are used; it is best to have three layers of pasta and three of marmalade.
4. Place the prepared dish in the oven for fifteen minutes, just to heat it well, then turn out onto a serving platter and generously sprinkle with sugar mixed with cinnamon.
5. This proportion is for six people.

title: Makaron z Szynką w Cieście (Pasta with Ham in Pastry)
body:
## Ingredients

- half pound of flour
- one egg yolk
- one quarter pound of butter
- one teaspoon of sugar
- half pound of Italian pasta
- salted water
- one quarter pound of cooked ham, sliced thin
- one quarter pound of good butter
- one tablespoon of grated cheese, preferably Parmesan

## Directions

1. Mix half pound of flour, one egg yolk, one quarter pound of butter, and one teaspoon of sugar into short pastry.
2. Grease a pot or mold with butter and line it with this pastry.
3. Break half pound of Italian pasta into pieces, cook in salted water, and mix with one quarter pound of cooked ham cut into thin strips, add one quarter pound of good butter and one tablespoon of grated Parmesan cheese, and mix everything perfectly.
4. Place this mixture in the lined pot, cover from the top with pastry, and put in a moderately hot oven.
5. When well browned, remove from the oven and arrange on a serving platter.
6. Serve as a main dish or as small pastries after soup.

title: Makaron Zapiekany z Szynką (Baked Pasta with Ham)
body:
## Ingredients

- half pound of flour
- three eggs
- salted water
- half pound of ham
- one tablespoon of butter
- one tablespoon of sour cream
- butter for greasing the pot
- grated bread

## Directions

1. Make stiff dough from half pound of flour and three eggs, roll out thinly and cut into pasta pieces.
2. Cook in salted water and strain through a colander.
3. Cut half pound of ham into thin strips and place in a bowl with the pasta, add one tablespoon of butter and one tablespoon of sour cream, mix well, and place in a pot greased with butter and sprinkled with grated bread.
4. Put in the oven and when well browned, turn out onto a serving platter and serve at table.

title: Maliny Konserwowane (Preserved Raspberries)
body:
## Ingredients

- sixteen quarts of raspberries
- eleven pounds of sugar
- two quarts of water

## Directions

1. Fill jars with berries and arrange in a large steamer
2. Cook on steam for half an hour
3. Make a syrup from sugar and water and cook it for five minutes
4. Uncover the steamer and immediately pour the syrup over the berries
5. Jars should be full

title: Manhattan Cocktail
body:
## Ingredients

- one-third quart of vodka
- one-third quart of Italian vermouth wine
- one-third quart of water
- one and a half liqueur glasses of Peyschaud or Angostura Bitters
- one liqueur glass of syrup

## Directions

1. Mix well, pour into a bottle and cork

title: Marchew (Carrots)
body:
## Ingredients

- young or older carrots
- butter
- salt
- sugar
- flour
- sweet cream

## Directions

1. Young carrots after scraping should be cut into quarters with the center intact; older carrots are best cut into thin strips, discarding the center.
2. Pour water in a pan and bring to a boil, then drain the water.
3. Pour in fresh hot water but little, add butter, salt to taste and a little sugar.
4. Braise covered; when half-cooked, mix some butter with flour and season the carrots with it.
5. Just before serving pour in a few spoonfuls of sweet cream, bring to a boil and serve immediately.

title: Marmolada Brzoskwiniowa (Peach Jam)
body:
## Ingredients

- ripe peaches
- three-quarters pound sugar per pound of peaches
- one cup water per pound of sugar

## Directions

1. Peel ripe peaches, remove pits, and chop into small pieces
2. For each pound of chopped peaches, take three-quarters pound of sugar
3. For each pound of sugar, pour in one cup of water and bring to a boil
4. Skim the surface
5. Add the peaches and cook on high heat until soft
6. Mash into pulp and continue cooking until the mixture is thick
7. Pour into jars or glasses, seal tightly and store

title: Marmolada Mieszana (Mixed Jam)
body:
## Ingredients

- three lemons
- three oranges
- three grapefruits
- three quarts water per quart of prepared fruit
- sugar, measure for measure with the mass

## Directions

1. Wash the fruits carefully, dry them, cut off the ends
2. Chop the fruits together with the skin into thin slices
3. Discard all veins and thick membranes
4. For each quart of chopped fruit, take three quarts of water
5. Pour everything into a pot, cover and let stand for twenty-four hours
6. After this time, place on heat and cook for one hour
7. Add sugar in the same measure as the mass
8. Cook on very low heat until a thick jelly forms
9. Remove from heat, fill jars or glasses, seal with paraffin and store

title: Marmolada Rabarbarowa (Rhubarb Jam)
body:
## Ingredients

- three pounds rhubarb stalks
- two pounds sugar
- two lemons, sliced into small pieces
- one ounce bitter almonds, blanched and split in half

## Directions

1. Place all ingredients in a pot
2. Cook until thick
3. Fill glasses and store

title: Marmolada z Rabarbaru (Rhubarb Marmalade)
body:
## Ingredients

- one and a half pounds of rhubarb stalks
- one pound of sugar
- two or three bitter almonds blanched and cut in half
- one lemon cut into small pieces

## Directions

1. Peel the outer fibers and cut into one-inch pieces one and a half pounds of rhubarb stalks, add one pound of sugar, two or three bitter almonds blanched and cut in half and one lemon cut into small pieces
2. Put all this in a pot and cook until the mass is thick
3. This excellent marmalade can be kept for a long time without spoiling

title: Marmolada z Rajskich Jabłuszek (Crabapple Jam)
body:
## Ingredients

- crabapples
- water
- three-quarters pound sugar per pound of apples
- three lemons per pound of apples

## Directions

1. From nice crabapples, remove the core along with the seeds
2. Pour enough water to barely cover and cook until soft
3. Strain through a thin strainer and set aside the liquid
4. Pour a small amount of water over the apples and cook
5. Push through a colander
6. Pour the liquid from the cores into the apple mass
7. Place everything on the heat and cook until the mass is thick
8. Add three-quarters pound sugar per pound of apples and three lemons
9. Mix and cook for forty-five minutes more
10. Fill jars or glasses and seal with paraffin

title: Martini Cocktail
body:
## Ingredients

- one-third quart of Tom Gin
- one-third quart of Italian vermouth wine
- one-third quart of water
- two small liqueur glasses of orange bitters

## Directions

1. Add two small liqueur glasses of orange bitters, mix, pour into a bottle and cork

title: Marynata z Dojrzałych Ogórków (Marinated Mature Cucumbers)
body:
## Ingredients

- sześć dużych żółtych, dojrzałych ogórków
- sól
- sześć czerwonych pieprzów
- jeden średni korzeń chrzanu
- funt nasienia gorczycy
- wrzący ocet

## Directions

1. Sześć dużych żółtych, dojrzałych ogórków przekrajać wzdłuż na połówki
2. Wybrać łyżką wszystkie ziarnka wraz z miazgą
3. Pokrajać w paski wielkości palca
4. Posypać solą i postawić na całą noc aby sól wyciągnęła wszystek nadmiar soku
5. Nazajutrz rano wyjąć ogórki z soli i wytrzeć starannie
6. Wybrać ziarnka z sześciu czerwonych pieprzów i pokrajać strąki na wąskie paski, długości cala
7. W ten sam sposób pokrajać jeden średni korzeń chrzanu
8. Wziąć funt nasienia gorczycy
9. Układać ogórki w dużym słoju, przekładając pieprzem, chrzanem i gorczycą
10. Zalać wrzącym octem i postawić w chłodnem miejscu aż do trzeciego dnia
11. Na trzeci dzień rano ocet znowu zagotować
12. Marynatą napełnić małe słoiki
13. Zalać wrzącym octem
14. Szczelnie zamknąć lub zakorkować i przechować

title: Masło Pietruszkowe (Parsley Butter)
body:
## Ingredients

- three tablespoons of butter
- one tablespoon of lemon juice
- one and a half tablespoons of finely chopped fresh parsley
- half a teaspoon of salt
- pepper to taste

## Directions

1. Work the butter in a bowl with sour cream
2. add lemon juice, finely chopped fresh parsley, salt and pepper
3. mix everything well
4. spread thickly over boiled fish on a serving platter
5. serve immediately to the table

title: Masło Pietruszkowe (Parsley Butter)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 tablespoons butter
- 1 tablespoon lemon juice
- 1 1/2 tablespoons finely chopped green parsley
- 1/2 teaspoon salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Beat 3 tablespoons butter into cream in a mortar
2. Add 1 tablespoon lemon juice, 1 1/2 tablespoons finely chopped green parsley, 1/2 teaspoon salt and some pepper
3. Mix everything well
4. This sauce is used for fish, best boiled fish, in the following way: Place the boiled fish on a platter and spread this sauce thickly over the top
5. Serve immediately

title: Masło Sardelowe (Sardine Butter)
body:
## Ingredients

- sardines or good herring
- half a pound of good butter

## Directions

1. clean the sardines or herring carefully, wash them, remove the bones and pound them to a paste in a mortar
2. take half a pound of good butter for half a pound of sardines or herring
3. pound together in a mortar
4. press through a fine sieve and serve
5. chopped fresh parsley or chives may be added if desired
6. this butter may be kept in a cool place for several weeks

title: Melony Marynowane (Pickled Melons)
body:
## Ingredients

- melons (cantaloupe)
- three pounds of sugar
- half a quart of white vinegar
- cinnamon sticks
- cloves

## Directions

1. Peel melons (cantaloupe), remove seeds and pulp and cut into long pieces
2. For seven pounds of prepared melons, take three pounds of sugar and half a quart of white vinegar
3. Put vinegar with sugar on fire, add a few cinnamon sticks and a few cloves
4. Cook for ten or fifteen minutes
5. Place the sliced melons and cook until transparent, which will take a few hours
6. Fill jars and seal tightly

title: Mizerya po Francusku (Cucumber Salad, French Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- pair of large cucumbers
- one tablespoon vinegar
- two tablespoons oil
- half teaspoon salt
- red pepper

## Directions

1. Peel a pair of large cucumbers and place them in cold water (preferably with ice) for one hour
2. Remove and slice into thin slices
3. Place in a bowl
4. Mix together one tablespoon of vinegar, two tablespoons of oil, half teaspoon of salt, and a pinch of red pepper
5. Pour this sauce over the cucumber slices

title: Mizerya ze Śmietaną (Cucumber Salad with Sour Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- pair of large cucumbers
- sour cream
- vinegar
- white pepper
- lettuce, optional

## Directions

1. Prepare cucumbers the same way as in Mizerya po Francusku
2. Instead of oil, use sour cream
3. Instead of red pepper, use white pepper
4. Optionally mix the sliced cucumbers with lettuce in equal proportions
5. Pour the sour cream and vinegar sauce over everything

title: Mostek Cielęcy Nadziewany (Stuffed Veal Breast)
body:
## Ingredients

- Thick veal breast
- Cold water
- Salt
- Young bacon, quarter pound
- One large onion
- Veal liver, half pound
- Pepper
- Fresh parsley, chopped
- Nutmeg
- Two whole eggs
- Butter

## Directions

1. Soak the thick breast in cold water for an hour, then rinse and salt it
2. Chop a quarter pound of young bacon into a mass, grate one large onion, and fry together with the bacon without allowing it to brown
3. Remove the crusts from two bread rolls and soak them in sweet milk
4. Chop veal liver very finely, add pepper, salt, chopped fresh parsley, nutmeg, two whole eggs, and a spoonful of butter
5. Mix everything thoroughly
6. Stuff the breast under the membrane with this filling and roast first in a hot oven, then in a moderately hot oven, basting continuously with butter
7. Do not add water

title: Mostek Cielęcy po Hiszpańsku (Veal Breast Spanish Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal breast, about three pounds
- white bread, one pound
- marinated tomatoes, half a quart
- onion, one tablespoon finely chopped
- celery, one tablespoon chopped
- salt
- red pepper

## Directions

1. Take a veal breast weighing about three pounds and make a pocket between the ribs and meat for stuffing.
2. Remove all crust from a pound of white bread and crumble and soak the center in half a quart of marinated tomatoes.
3. Add one tablespoon of finely chopped onion, one tablespoon of chopped celery, salt to taste and a bit of red pepper; mix everything well.
4. Stuff the veal with this stuffing and roast in a hot oven.
5. Roasting should take two hours.

title: Mostek Cielęcy z Pomidorami (Veal Breast with Tomatoes)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal breast
- marinated tomatoes
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. After washing the veal breast, dry it with a damp cloth and place it in a roasting pan and put in a very hot oven to quickly brown the meat on top.
2. Remove from the oven, add salt and pepper and cover with a quart of marinated tomatoes, then put back in the oven until the meat is completely cooked.
3. Place on a platter along with the tomatoes and serve immediately on the table.

title: Mostek Cielęcy z Pomidorami (Veal Breast with Tomatoes)
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal breast
- Salt and pepper
- Marinated tomatoes, half a quart

## Directions

1. Wash the veal breast, wipe it with a damp cloth, place in a roasting pan, and put in a very hot oven to quickly brown the meat on top
2. Remove from the oven, add salt and pepper, and cover with half a quart of marinated tomatoes
3. Return to the oven until the meat is completely roasted
4. Place on a platter with the tomatoes and serve immediately

title: Mostek Cielęcy z Ryżem (Veal Breast with Rice)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal breast
- rice
- veal broth
- butter
- bread roll

## Directions

1. Drain the meat and cover the rice with veal broth, add a tablespoon of butter and braise until the rice is soft.
2. Just before serving, place the breast in the rice and heat through.
3. Serve on the table arranging the breast in the center and rice around it.
4. Can be poured with bread roll browned in butter and grated.

title: Mostek Cielęcy z Ryżem (Veal Breast with Rice)
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal breast
- Various vegetables
- Salt
- Rice, quarter pound for four persons
- Hot water
- Veal broth
- Butter
- Grated bread

## Directions

1. Scald the veal breast, then cook until tender with various vegetables and sufficient salt
2. For four persons, take a quarter pound of rice, scald with hot water, bring to a boil, drain the water, and cover the rice with veal broth
3. Add a spoonful of butter and braise until the rice is tender
4. Just before serving, place the breast in the rice and heat through
5. Serve with the breast in the center and rice around it
6. Can be garnished with bread crumbs browned in butter

title: Muffins na Drożdżach (Yeast Muffins)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 tablespoon sugar
