- 0.5 teaspoon salt
- 2 tablespoons butter
- 2 cups boiled milk
- 1 cake yeast
- 3 cups wheat flour
- 2 eggs

## Directions

1. Put 1 tablespoon sugar, 0.5 teaspoon salt, and 2 tablespoons butter in a bowl and pour over 2 cups boiled milk and mix.
2. When cooled to lukewarm temperature, add 1 cake of yeast, crumbled into small pieces, and stir until the yeast dissolves completely.
3. Add 3 cups wheat flour, beat well, and set in a warm place.
4. When the dough rises well, add 2 well-beaten eggs and 1 cup flour, mix, and let rise again.
5. Fill forms with dough and place in a hot oven for twenty to twenty-five minutes.

title: Móżdżek na Parze (Veal Brain Steamed Mold)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal brain
- salted water
- vinegar, one tablespoon
- cream, half a quart
- four egg yolks
- salt and pepper to taste
- nutmeg, a pinch
- egg whites
- butter
- flour
- sharp caper sauce or gherkin sauce

## Directions

1. Peel the raw brain of veins and membranes and blanch in salted water with the addition of one tablespoon of vinegar, place on a bowl and mash.
2. Add half a quart of cream, four egg yolks, salt and pepper to taste and a pinch of nutmeg; at the end beat the whites to a stiff foam and mix with the mass.
3. Grease a mold or form with butter, sprinkle with a little flour, place in another mold with hot water, put the brain mass in and cook on steam for about an hour.
4. After cooking carefully place on a platter and pour with melted butter; separately serve sharp caper or gherkin sauce.

title: Móżdżek na Parze (Veal Brain Steamed)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal brain, one pound
- cold water
- salt
- three eggs
- butter, one teaspoon
- milk, one tablespoon
- salt and pepper to taste

## Directions

1. Soak one pound of veal brain for twenty minutes in cold water, remove and peel off the membrane, then place in salted boiling water and cook for ten minutes.
2. After cooking remove and place again in cold water, and when completely cooled, break into small pieces.
3. Beat well three eggs and mix with the brain, also add one teaspoon of butter, one tablespoon of milk, salt and pepper to taste, then place on heat and braise until the eggs are well set.

title: Móżdżek po Wiedeńsku (Veal Brain Viennese Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal brain
- cold water
- salted water with vinegar and roots
- egg
- bread roll
- butter
- lemon
- parsley

## Directions

1. Soak the brain in cold water for half an hour or an hour, then remove the membranes and blanch in salted water with vinegar and roots.
2. After boiling immediately remove and cut into slices, shaping them like cutlets, dredge each in grated bread, dip in beaten egg, coat with grated bread and fry in butter until browned.
3. When browned, place in a hot oven for a few minutes and then arrange on a platter and garnish with lemon slices and sprigs of fresh parsley.

title: Nalewka Miętowa (Mint Liqueur)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a quart of thick syrup made with sugar in pieces and very little water
- one quart of strong spirits
- one-eighth ounce of mint oil

## Directions

1. Make half a quart of thick syrup using sugar in pieces and very little water
2. Pour one quart of strong spirits into the syrup and add one-eighth ounce of mint oil and mix well

title: Nalewka Pomarańczowa (Orange Liqueur)
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart of strong brandy
- one cup of orange juice
- half a pound of sugar in pieces
- zest from three oranges (yellow part only)

## Directions

1. Take one quart of strong brandy and add one cup of orange juice, half a pound of sugar in pieces and zest from three oranges; use only the yellow part of the zest, scraped with a sharp knife, discard the white part
2. Pour everything into a jar or bottle and cork it tightly, then place in a cool place for three days, stirring several times each day
3. After three days strain the liquid, add more sugar if not sweet enough, pour into bottles and cork

title: Naleśniki (Polish Pancakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- salt
- flour
- milk
- water
- egg whites
- clarified butter or lard for cooking
- sugar
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Add butter, salt, flour, and then milk mixed with water to make a thin batter that pours easily
2. Beat egg whites until stiff and fold into the batter
3. Heat a pan and brush with clarified butter or lard using a feather
4. Pour enough batter to cover the pan completely and quickly tilt to spread evenly
5. Keep pan on high heat
6. When pancake is cooked on one side, circle it with a knife and flip briefly to the other side, then remove to a plate
7. When all batter is cooked, fold pancakes into triangles by folding in half once, then folding through the middle again
8. Place four at a time back in the pan and brown on both sides in clarified butter
9. Sprinkle with sugar and cinnamon and serve
10. This proportion should yield sixteen to twenty pancakes

title: Naleśniki (Thin Pancakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup flour
- four egg yolks
- half tablespoon clarified butter
- salt to taste
- half quart milk
- half quart water
- egg whites

## Directions

1. Mix the egg yolks with the butter and add salt
2. Add the flour and then the milk mixed with water
3. Make a thin batter that will pour
4. Finally beat the egg whites to stiff peaks and fold into the batter
5. Heat a pan and grease it with a feather dipped in clarified butter or with a piece of lard rind
6. Pour enough batter onto the pan to cover it completely, working quickly
7. Tilt and rotate the pan so the batter spreads evenly, keeping the pan on high heat
8. When the pancake is cooked on one side, run a knife around the edge and flip it briefly to the other side
9. Place on a plate

title: Naleśniki Przekładane (Filled Polish Pancakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- regular pancakes
- jam, marmalade, preserves, or curd cheese beaten with butter, egg yolks and sugar

## Directions

1. Make regular pancakes as described above
2. After cooking, brush each one with jam, marmalade, preserves, or curd cheese beaten with butter, egg yolks and sugar
3. The jam should be thick
4. Fold and fry on both sides until browned
5. Serve to the table

title: Naleśniki Zapiekane (Baked Layered Pancakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- regular pancakes
- four egg yolks
- quarter pound sugar
- cup sour cream
- vanilla or lemon extract
- butter
- grated bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Make regular pancakes as described previously
2. Beat four egg yolks until white with quarter pound sugar, add cup of sour cream, season with vanilla or lemon extract and mix well into a uniform mass
3. Take a baking dish about the width of the pancakes, grease well with butter and sprinkle with grated bread crumbs
4. Spread the egg yolk and cream mixture on the pancakes and layer in the dish as follows: spread one pancake and place on the bottom, then place another pancake unspread on top, then another spread pancake and cover with an unspread one, continue layering until the dish is full, with two pancakes per layer and filling between
5. Place in a hot oven for half an hour
6. Temperature is important, as if the oven is too cold or too hot, the filling will not bake but only melt
7. Alternatively, instead of the above filling, use fine sugar moistened with lemon juice and sprinkled with a little grated lemon zest

title: Nektar Malinowy (Raspberry Nectar)
body:
## Ingredients

- four quarts of red raspberries
- one quart of yellow vinegar (cider vinegar)
- two pounds of sugar per quart of obtained liquid

## Directions

1. Pour four quarts of red raspberries with one quart of yellow vinegar (cider vinegar) and place in a cool place for four days
2. After this time strain, and for each quart of obtained liquid add two pounds of sugar, place on fire and boil for twenty minutes
3. Serve diluted with water and ice
4. In tightly corked bottles this juice can be kept for a quite long time

title: Niemieckie Knedle (German Dumplings)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 pound fresh lard
- a couple of onions
- two rolls
- a few tablespoons of broth
- two whole eggs
- salt and pepper to taste

## Directions

1. Chop the fresh lard into small cubes and finely chop a couple of onions and fry them together with the lard so that the lard is only half fried
2. Chop two rolls finely, add to the lard, mix well and continue frying, stirring constantly, until the bread is dry
3. Pour in a few tablespoons of broth, beat in two whole eggs, add salt and pepper to taste and mix everything into a thick mass
4. Make balls the size of a walnut and cook them in broth or soup

title: Niemieckie Knedle (German Knedle)
body:
## Ingredients

- quarter pound fresh lard
- couple of onions
- two rolls

## Directions

1. Cut a quarter pound fresh lard into small cubes, chop a couple of onions finely and fry together with the lard so the lard is only half cooked.
2. Chop two rolls finely, add to the lard, mix well and continue frying, stirring constantly.

title: Nóżki Cielęce w Cieście (Veal Feet in Batter)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal feet
- vegetables
- flour, one tablespoon
- three egg yolks
- cream, two tablespoons
- egg whites
- hot fat
- spinach
- sauce made from preserves or cherry juice

## Directions

1. Cook the carefully cleaned veal feet with various vegetables the same way as for aspic.
2. When completely soft, cut each in half between the hooves, salt them, sprinkle with flour and arrange on a platter, place in a warm place so they do not cool.
3. For four feet beat three egg yolks with one tablespoon of flour, add about two tablespoons of cream, and finally mix in the remaining whites beaten to a stiff foam.
4. Dip the feet in this batter and fry in hot fat until well browned.
5. Instead of batter, the feet can be dipped in beaten egg and then coated in grated bread and fried in butter.
6. The best vegetable to serve with such feet is spinach, and sauce is served made from preserves or cherry juice.

title: Nóżki Cielęce w Galarecie (Veal Feet in Aspic)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal feet
- water
- vegetables
- beef fat
- pepper
- salt
- egg whites

## Directions

1. Carefully clean the veal feet, remove the thick bones, cover with water in a pot and bring to a boil, then immediately drain the water.
2. Rinse the feet, place back in the pot, cover with soft warm water, add various vegetables, a piece of beef fat and pepper, then cook with constant skimming for three to four hours; do not add salt as they will blacken.
3. After cooking remove the feet, salt the remaining broth and clarify with whites beaten with water, which should be poured into the broth and well brought to a boil.
4. Cut the removed feet into small pieces, salt them, arrange in a mold or salad bowl and cover with the clarified strained broth, stir and place in a cold place until set.
5. Serve with vinegar and oil. It is best to take four veal feet and two pork feet, as aspic made from only veal feet is not as tasty.

title: Ogórki Duszone (Braised Cucumbers)
body:
## Ingredients

- four large cucumbers
- boiling water
- one and a half cups sour cream
- half tablespoon butter
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Peel four large cucumbers and cut into slices half an inch thick
2. Place in a pot and cover with just enough boiling water
3. Cook for fifteen minutes or until tender, but they should not fall apart
4. Drain the water
5. In a separate pot pour one and a half cups sour cream, half tablespoon butter, salt and pepper to taste
6. Place on fire and when it just boils, throw in the cooked cucumbers
7. Boil a few times to heat the cucumbers thoroughly
8. Serve on the table
9. These prepared cucumbers can also be served on bread slices, like asparagus

title: Olatki z Ryżu (Rice Wafers)
body:
## Ingredients

- two cups cold cooked rice
- one cup milk
- one cup flour
- one teaspoon baking powder
- half teaspoon salt
- two teaspoons sugar
- one egg
- fat for the pan

## Directions

1. Mash two cups of cold cooked rice into a mass, mix well with one cup of milk.
2. Add one cup of flour sifted with one teaspoon of baking powder, half a teaspoon of salt, two teaspoons of sugar, and one egg.
3. Mix to a loose batter and place spoonfuls on a greased pan.
4. Fry until nicely golden.
5. Make the wafers small, four or five on one pan.
6. Instead of sweet milk, you can use the same amount of sour milk, but then do not use baking powder, use half a teaspoon of soda instead.

title: Omlet z Jabłkami (Omelet with Apples)
body:
## Ingredients

- six large apples
- one cup sugar
- one teaspoon butter
- one cup grated bread
- three eggs
- butter for the pan

## Directions

1. Cook six large apples until soft and press through a sieve
2. While hot, add one cup sugar and one teaspoon butter, mix well and cool
3. Melt one tablespoon of butter in a frying pan, add one cup grated bread and fry until golden
4. Beat three eggs, add to the cooled apple mixture and mix well
5. Butter a baking pan, sprinkle the fried bread on the bottom and sides
6. Add the apple mixture on top, then more grated bread on the top and place in a moderately hot oven for half an hour
7. This omelet is usually served cold with sugar and sour cream, but can also be served hot

title: Omlet z Kalafiorami (Omelet with Cauliflower)
body:
## Ingredients

- cauliflower
- salted water
- four eggs
- small teaspoon cornstarch

## Directions

1. Boil cauliflower in salted water until tender
2. Drain and break into small pieces
3. Beat four eggs with a small teaspoon of cornstarch
4. Add the cauliflower and fry in a pan until the eggs set
5. Fold in half and serve on the table

title: Omlet z Kartofli (Potato Omelet)
body:
## Ingredients

- six medium potatoes
- water
- one small teaspoon salt
- one and a half tablespoons butter
- half a cup hot milk
- ground pepper
- one tablespoon butter for the pan

## Directions

1. Peel and cook six medium potatoes
2. Drain the water and mash the potatoes to a paste
3. Then beat with a spoon until it becomes almost foamy
4. Add one small teaspoon of salt, one and a half tablespoons of butter, half a cup of hot milk and some ground pepper
5. Mix everything well
6. Place on a pan in which one tablespoon of butter has been melted first
7. Spread the potato mixture evenly over the whole pan, cover with a lid and fry on very low heat or in a hot oven for about fifteen minutes until it browns nicely on the bottom
8. Then fold the omelet in half and immediately transfer to a hot serving dish and serve

title: Omlet z Ostryg (Oyster Omelet)
body:
## Ingredients

- six eggs
- one cup of milk
- salt and pepper
- one half teaspoon or more of chopped fresh parsley
- a dozen large oysters
- one tablespoon of melted butter

## Directions

1. Beat six eggs and separate the whites from the yolks
2. Beat the whites until stiff and the yolks separately
3. Pour one cup of milk into the beaten yolks, add salt, pepper and one half teaspoon or more of chopped fresh parsley
4. Chop a dozen large oysters finely and add to the milk, pouring slowly and mixing well
5. After mixing, add one tablespoon of melted butter
6. Fold in the stiffly beaten egg whites gently
7. Melt one tablespoon of butter in a large skillet and pour in the egg and oyster mixture
8. Fry over gentle heat without stirring; when the whole mixture is well set, turn onto a hot plate and serve

title: Omlet z Rybą (Omelet with Fish)
body:
## Ingredients

- four eggs
- half a pound cooked or fried cold fish, deboned and crumbled
- salt
- pepper
- green parsley

## Directions

1. Beat four eggs, add to them half a pound of any cooked or fried cold fish, deboned and crumbled into small pieces
2. Mix well and add salt and pepper to taste, and some chopped green parsley
3. Melt half a tablespoon of butter in a frying pan, pour in the eggs with fish
4. Fry on low heat until the entire mass sets
5. Fold in half, place on a hot serving platter and serve

title: Omlet z Serem (Cheese Omelet)
body:
## Ingredients

- five egg yolks
- five tablespoons milk
- half a small teaspoon salt
- two pinches pepper
- remaining egg whites
- butter
- half a cup cheese, grated

## Directions

1. Beat five egg yolks well, add five tablespoons milk, half a small teaspoon salt and two pinches pepper
2. Beat the remaining egg whites until stiff peaks form and gently mix with the yolks
3. Butter a large frying pan well, heat it thoroughly and pour in the eggs
4. Cover with a lid and fry on low heat until the bottom is well browned
5. Then put in the oven for a minute to dry the omelet slightly on top
6. After removing from the oven, grate half a cup of cheese and sprinkle on top of the omelet
7. Fold the omelet in half and serve immediately

title: Omlet ze Szparagami (Omelet with Asparagus)
body:
## Ingredients

- dozen or so asparagus spears
- salted water
- four or five eggs
- butter

## Directions

1. Cook a dozen or so asparagus spears in salted water until tender, about fifteen minutes
2. Cut the tender ends into small pieces
3. Beat four or five eggs and pour onto a frying pan in which one tablespoon of butter has been melted and place on low heat
4. When the eggs set, arrange the asparagus on top
5. Fold the omelet in half so that the asparagus are in the middle, and serve immediately

title: Omlet ze Szpinakiem (Omelet with Spinach)
body:
## Ingredients

- spinach leaves
- water
- salt
- sour cream
- four eggs
- butter

## Directions

1. Carefully clean spinach leaves of roots and rinse in several waters
2. Plunge into a pot of boiling water and cook, then pour onto a sieve so all water drains
3. Chop finely, season with salt and add a little sweet sour cream
4. Beat four eggs, beating them only for one minute
5. Pour onto a frying pan with hot butter, stir a few times with a fork and fry until the eggs set
6. Then place the hot spinach on top, arranging it lengthwise
7. Fold the omelet in half so the spinach is in the middle, and serve on the table

title: Orange Liqueur (Nalewka Pomarańczowa)
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart of strong brandy
- one cup of orange juice
- half a pound of sugar in pieces
- zest from three oranges

## Directions

1. Take one quart of strong brandy and add one cup of orange juice
2. Add half a pound of sugar in pieces and zest from three oranges, using only the yellow part of the peel, scraped with a sharp knife, discarding the white part
3. Pour all this into a jug or jar and cork tightly
4. Set in a cool place for three days, stirring a few times each day
5. After three days strain the liquid and add more sugar if not sweet enough
6. Pour into bottles and cork

title: Oranżada (Orangeade)
body:
## Ingredients

- four large oranges
- one and a half quarts of water with ice
- sugar to taste

## Directions

1. Cut four large oranges in half and extract with a spoon all the pulp from seven halves, and grate the eighth with its zest; remove all seeds, add one and a half quarts of water with ice and add sugar to taste

title: Ostre Masło do Śniadania (Sharp Breakfast Butter)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound of fresh butter
- one tablespoon of grated green cheese, finely grated
- one small teaspoon of French mustard
- one small glass of good rum

## Directions

1. mix with a wooden spoon into a uniform mass
2. arrange nicely on a butter dish and serve to the table

title: Ostrygi Duszone na Grzankach (Oysters Stewed on Toast)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a quart of oysters with their liquid
- one tablespoon of butter
- one tablespoon of grated bread
- half a teaspoon of salt
- a little pepper
- a pinch of red pepper
- one teaspoon of lemon juice
- thin slices of white bread
- butter for toast
- lemon slices

## Directions

1. Pour half a quart of oysters with their liquid into a saucepan and place over heat
2. As soon as they come to a boil, add one tablespoon of butter, one tablespoon of grated bread, one half teaspoon of salt, a little pepper, a pinch of red pepper and one teaspoon of lemon juice
3. Mix everything and bring to a boil; when boiled for one minute, remove from heat
4. Make toast from thin slices of white bread, butter them and arrange the cooked oysters on top
5. Arrange on a platter, surrounding with lemon slices

title: Ostrygi Smażone (Fried Oysters)
body:
## Ingredients

- large oysters
- salt
- pepper
- flour
- egg
- bread
- fat or lard
- paper
- parsley
- lemon

## Directions

1. Drain and wipe large oysters almost dry
2. Sprinkle with salt and pepper and coat in flour, then dip in beaten egg, coat in grated bread
3. Fry in a deep pan filled with hot fat or lard
4. When done, remove and place on paper to absorb excess fat
5. Arrange on a napkin or plate, decorate with parsley sprigs and lemon slices
6. Serve immediately

title: Ostrygi z Cielęciny (Calf's Oysters)
body:
## Ingredients

- calf meat from the leg
- olive oil
- breadcrumbs
- eggs
- hot fat
- salad leaves
- tartar sauce
- salad

## Directions

1. Take calf meat from the leg and cut out small pieces in the shape of oysters
2. Take each piece of meat prepared in this manner individually
3. Dip in olive oil, then in breadcrumbs, then in beaten egg and again in breadcrumbs
4. Throw into hot fat and fry until well browned
5. Arrange on a serving platter lined with salad leaves
6. Serve separately with tartar sauce and salad

title: Ostrygi Zapiekane (Baked Oysters)
body:
## Ingredients

- two cups of grated bread or crumbs
- half a teaspoon of salt
- a pinch of pepper
- two cups of oysters
- one and one half cups of milk
- one tablespoon of butter
- grated bread for topping
- small pieces of butter

## Directions

1. Take two cups of grated bread or crumbs, add one half teaspoon of salt and a pinch of pepper
2. Pour in two cups of oysters and mix well
3. Add one and one half cups of milk and one tablespoon of butter and mix everything thoroughly
4. Pour into a cake mold, sprinkle grated bread on top
5. Place small pieces of butter on top and bake in the oven for one hour

title: Ostrygi ze Słoniną (Oysters with Bacon)
body:
## Ingredients

- large oysters
- smoked bacon
- salt and pepper
- wooden toothpicks or skewers

## Directions

1. Choose large oysters, salt and pepper them
2. Cut smoked bacon into thin, long slices
3. Wrap each oyster in a slice of bacon and secure with a wooden toothpick or skewer
4. Heat an empty skillet over high heat until very hot
5. Place wrapped oysters in the skillet and fry for about two minutes, shaking constantly to avoid burning
6. Sprinkle with chopped fresh parsley and serve

title: Ostrygi à la Newburg (Oysters à la Newburg)
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup of cream
- one tablespoon of flour
- one large tablespoon of butter
- three hard-boiled egg yolks
- a few teaspoons of cream
- salt and red pepper
- about thirty oysters

## Directions

1. Steam one cup of cream in a double boiler
2. Mix one tablespoon of flour with one large tablespoon of butter and add to the cream
3. Rub three hard-boiled egg yolks to a smooth paste with a few teaspoons of cream and add to the sauce
4. Mix everything well, keeping it over heat, then add salt to taste and some red pepper
5. Cook about thirty oysters in their own liquid until the edges curl
6. Drain the oysters and add them to the sauce
7. Serve immediately on a platter

title: Oszczędny Rosół Cielęcy (Economical Veal Broth)
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds veal from the neck
- water
- 1 medium onion, sliced
- 1/2 cup rinsed rice
- 1 tablespoon flour
- 2 tablespoons water
- salt and pepper to taste
- small croutons

## Directions

1. Cover 2 pounds of veal from the neck with water and cook until the meat is tender
2. Remove meat from bones and set aside, place bones back in the broth and cook for about 2 more hours on low heat, then strain and pour back into the pot
3. Add 1 medium sliced onion, 1/2 cup of rinsed rice and 1 tablespoon of flour previously mixed in 2 tablespoons of water
4. Place on fire and cook until rice is done
5. Add salt and pepper to taste and serve with small croutons
6. From the meat that was removed from the bones, cook a separate stew separately, thus you have two dishes at once

title: Owoce z Kremem (Fruits with Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- two oranges
- three or four bananas
- sugar
- one cup of cream
- one egg white
- half cup of fine sugar

## Directions

1. Peel and slice the oranges and bananas, mix together and sprinkle with sugar.
2. Arrange on a serving platter or individual plates.
3. Take one cup of cream, one egg white, and half cup of fine sugar; divide the sugar into two equal parts.
4. Beat one part of sugar with the egg white until foamy.
5. Beat the other part of sugar with the cream.
6. Mix the two mixtures together and pour the cream over the fruit.

title: Ozór na Szaro (Tongue in Gray Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- one cleaned beef tongue
- water
- various vegetables
- a few bay leaves
- about a dozen peppercorns
- one tablespoon sugar
- one tablespoon butter
- one tablespoon flour
- one half cup wine
- one tablespoon vinegar
- lemon zest
- blanched and sliced almonds

## Directions

1. Place the cleaned tongue in a pan
2. Cover with water
3. Add various vegetables, bay leaves, and peppercorns
4. Boil until tender
5. Immediately remove the skin
6. Slice diagonally
7. Moisten one tablespoon sugar with water and brown in a pan
8. Add one tablespoon butter
9. Add one tablespoon flour, previously cooked
10. Mix with the sugar and brown together
11. Distribute with broth from cooking the tongue
12. Add one half cup wine, one tablespoon vinegar, and some lemon zest
13. Strain and add blanched and sliced almonds
14. Pour this sauce over the tongue arranged in a pan
15. Bring to a boil and serve on the table

title: Ozór po Polsku (Polish-Style Beef Tongue)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef tongue
- vegetables
- roots
- gray sauce
- raisins
- Italian pasta or potatoes

## Directions

1. Cook beef tongue as usual with vegetables and roots until completely tender
2. Remove, peel off the skin, and slice diagonally
3. Arrange on a platter and pour gray sauce with raisins over it
4. Arrange Italian pasta or potatoes around it

title: Ozór po Polsku (Tongue Polish Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef tongue
- vegetables and roots for cooking
- gray sauce with raisins
- Italian pasta or potatoes for garnish

## Directions

1. Cook the beef tongue as usual with vegetables and roots
2. When completely tender, remove and peel the skin
3. Slice diagonally
4. Arrange on a platter
5. Pour with gray sauce with raisins
6. Surround with Italian pasta or potatoes

title: Ozór Wołowy a la Milanaise (Beef Tongue à la Milanaise)
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh beef tongue
- salt
- vegetables for broth
- one large onion
- butter
- one cup rice
- mushrooms, finely chopped
- broth from cooking the tongue
- salt and pepper to taste
- one cup grated Swiss cheese

## Directions

1. Cook fresh beef tongue in salted water with vegetables as for broth, about one and a half hours
2. Remove, peel off the skin, and arrange on a platter
3. Surround with hot rice prepared as follows: chop a large onion and brown it in butter until golden
4. Add one large cup of rice and a few finely chopped mushrooms
5. Bring to a boil for two minutes, then add one quart of broth from cooking the tongue
6. Stir and cook for fifteen minutes, adding more broth if needed, and cook another fifteen minutes, about half an hour total
7. At the end add salt and pepper to taste and one cup of grated Swiss cheese, stir and arrange around the tongue

title: Ozór z Chrzanem (Beef Tongue with Horseradish)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef tongue
- vegetables
- horseradish, grated
- butter
- sour cream
- salt

## Directions

1. Cook beef tongue with vegetables as usual
2. Remove, peel off the skin, and slice thinly
3. Arrange on a platter
4. Take a handful of grated horseradish and brown it with butter
5. Add sour cream, bring to a boil, and pour this sauce over the tongue arranged on the platter

title: Ozór z Chrzanem (Tongue with Horseradish)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef tongue
- vegetables for cooking
- grated horseradish
- butter
- sour cream

## Directions

1. Cook the beef tongue with vegetables as usual
2. Remove and peel off the skin
3. Slice thinly
4. Arrange on a platter
5. Take a handful of grated horseradish
6. Brown with butter
7. Season with sour cream
8. Bring to a boil
9. Pour this sauce over the tongue arranged on the platter

title: Paj Cytrynowy (Lemon Pie)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup flour
- pinch of salt
- 1 teaspoon baking powder
- sweet cream, as needed for dough consistency
- 1 tablespoon cornstarch
- 1 cup sugar
- 1 cup boiling water
- 1 tablespoon melted butter
- juice and zest of 1 lemon
- 2 egg yolks
- 2 egg whites
- 1 teaspoon powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Sift flour with salt and baking powder, add enough sweet cream to make a thick dough and mix quickly
2. Roll out the dough and line a pie plate with perforated bottom, then bake
3. Mix cornstarch with sugar and pour in boiling water, stirring constantly
4. Place on heat and simmer slowly for three minutes
5. Remove from heat, add melted butter, lemon juice and zest, then two well-beaten egg yolks
6. Fill the baked crust with this mixture and bake in a moderately hot oven
7. Beat two egg whites with a teaspoon of powdered sugar until stiff, spread on the baked pie and return briefly to the oven to brown

title: Paj Rabarbarowy (Rhubarb Pie)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 cups rhubarb stalks, chopped into small pieces
- 1 tablespoon flour
- 1 cup sugar
- 1 teaspoon butter
- pie crust dough

## Directions

1. Line a pie plate with dough and sprinkle with sugar mixed with flour, using half of the amount
2. Place chopped rhubarb on top
3. Sprinkle with the remaining flour and sugar mixture
4. Scatter butter, broken into small pieces, on top
5. Cover with a second layer of rolled-out dough and seal the edges firmly
6. Bake in a moderately hot oven for approximately three quarters of an hour

title: Paj Rabarbarowy (Rhubarb Pie)
body:
## Ingredients

- three cups of rhubarb stalks, cut into small pieces
- one tablespoon of flour
- one cup of sugar
- one teaspoon of butter
- pie dough
- sugar

## Directions

1. Line a sheet with dough and sprinkle with sugar mixed with flour, using half the amount

title: Paj z Jabłek (Apple Pie)
body:
## Ingredients

- five medium tart apples
- half a cup of sugar
- half a teaspoon of cinnamon
- nutmeg
- one teaspoon of butter
- rolled out pie dough

## Directions

1. Peel five medium tart apples, cut each into eight pieces and remove the seeds
2. Line a baking sheet with rolled out dough, add to the apples half a cup of sugar, half a teaspoon of cinnamon and a pinch of grated nutmeg, mix and arrange on the sheet, scatter one teaspoon of butter, broken into small pieces on top, cover with another layer of rolled out dough, seal the edges firmly and place in a moderately hot oven for forty to forty-five minutes

title: Paj Żurawinowy (Cranberry Pie)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1.5 cups cranberries
- 1 cup sugar
- 1 egg
- 2 tablespoons water
- 1 teaspoon flour
- lemon extract for flavoring
- 2 egg whites
- 1 teaspoon powdered sugar

## Directions

1. Carefully sort, wash and chop the cranberries
2. Add sugar, one egg, two tablespoons water, a teaspoon of flour and lemon extract for flavor, mix everything together
3. Bake with bottom crust only
4. Cover with meringue made from two egg whites beaten with a teaspoon of powdered sugar

title: Pandowdy (Apple Pandowdy)
body:
## Ingredients

- several tart apples
- sugar
- cinnamon
- one cup of water
- half cup of rye flour
- half cup of cornmeal
- a pinch of salt
- boiling water

## Directions

1. Peel the apples, remove the seeds, and slice them.
2. In a deep baking dish, layer apple slices on the bottom and sprinkle generously with sugar mixed with a little cinnamon.
3. Alternate layers of apples and sugar until the dish is nearly full, then pour one cup of water over.
4. Mix half cup of rye flour and half cup of cornmeal with a pinch of salt and enough boiling water to make a thick dough.
5. Cover the apples tightly with this dough and place in a slow oven for four hours.
6. Remove from the oven, break the crust in several places with a fork, cover with a plate, and bake for another two hours.
7. Remove and let cool.
8. Serve with cream and sugar; should be prepared in the evening for use the next day.

title: Paprykarz z Cielęciny (Veal Paprikash)
body:
## Ingredients

- two medium onions
- butter
- veal meat
- flour
- paprika to taste
- several spoonfuls of marinated tomatoes
- some broth
- sour cream (as substitute for tomatoes)

## Directions

1. Chop two medium onions finely and brown them with butter in a pan.
2. Cut the veal meat into cubes, dust with flour, add to the browned onions, salt and add paprika to taste.
3. Add several spoonfuls of marinated tomatoes and some broth.
4. Stew on low heat until the meat is tender.
5. If desired, sour cream may be used instead of tomatoes.
6. The paprikash may be served alone or with pyza (see 'Dishes with Flour and Eggs').

title: Pasztet Amerykański (American Pâté)
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds lean beef without bones, cut for steak
- water to barely cover the meat
- one teaspoon salt
- pepper to taste
- a pinch of nutmeg
- one large tablespoon tomato sauce (catsup)
- one beaten egg
- butter for frying
- parsley for garnish
- one tablespoon butter for sauce
- one cup milk
- two tablespoons water
- one teaspoon flour
- salt and pepper to taste for sauce

## Directions

1. Place the beef in a flat pan
2. Add enough water to barely cover the meat
3. Braise covered, basting with hot water as it evaporates, until the meat is very tender
4. Chop the cooked meat as finely as possible, almost into a paste
5. Add one teaspoon salt, pepper, a pinch of nutmeg, and one large tablespoon tomato sauce
6. Add one beaten egg
7. Mix everything well
8. Form thin patties about three inches long and wide
9. Fry in butter until browned
10. Sprinkle hot platter with parsley
11. Arrange the fried meat on the platter
12. For the sauce: add one tablespoon butter, one cup milk, two tablespoons water, one teaspoon flour, salt and pepper to the sauce left from frying the meat
13. Mix and bring to a boil
14. Pour the sauce over the meat
15. Mushroom sauce also works well

title: Pasztet Cielęcy po Amerykańsku (American-Style Calf's Pâté)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound fresh calf meat, finely chopped
- half pound pork, finely chopped
- two beaten eggs
- two tablespoons grated breadcrumbs or bread
- one large onion, finely chopped
- one teaspoon chopped parsley
- half cup sour cream or milk
- salt to taste
- red pepper
- bacon slices
- bay leaf

## Directions

1. Chop very finely one pound of fresh calf meat and half pound of pork
2. Add two beaten eggs, two tablespoons of grated breadcrumbs or bread, one large finely chopped onion, one teaspoon of chopped parsley, half cup of sour cream or milk, salt to taste and a little red pepper
3. Mix everything well
4. Fill a loaf pan with this mixture
5. Place a few slices of bacon and a bay leaf on top
6. Place in a moderately hot oven for half an hour

title: Patyczki Królewskie (Royal Sticks)
body:
## Ingredients

- white bread
- butter

## Directions

1. Cut white bread into slices about one-third of an inch thick
2. Remove the crust
3. Spread with butter
4. Cut each slice into three strips
5. Place in a hot oven until browned

title: Peach Liqueur (Nalewka Brzoskwiniowa)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of ripe peaches
- one quart of strong spirit
- one pound of sugar

## Directions

1. Crush one pound of ripe peaches without damaging the pits
2. Then place with the pits and pour one quart of strong spirit over them and let stand in a cool place for a whole day
3. Then strain and add one pound of sugar, pour into bottles and cork
4. Can be colored with burnt sugar

title: Perlice Pieczone (Roasted Guinea Fowl)
body:
## Ingredients

- Guinea fowl
- Lard, thinly sliced
- Fat or butter

## Directions

1. Pluck and prepare like a chicken, then lard the breast with thinly sliced lard and roast in a hot oven, basting frequently with fat or butter
2. After placing on a serving platter pour over with browned butter
3. For them to be tender, guinea fowl should hang for a week in winter after being killed, and three days in summer

title: Petrawka z Ryby (Fish Ragout)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 tablespoon butter
- 1 tablespoon flour
- milk
- 1 small teaspoon salt
- 1/2 teaspoon pepper
- a pinch of red pepper
- a little nutmeg
- 1 teaspoon powdered ginger (optional)
- 1 large cup cold fried or cooked fish, crumbled into pieces the size of a hazelnut
- parsley leaves

## Directions

1. Place 1 tablespoon of butter in a saucepan and brown it slightly
2. Add 1 tablespoon of flour, brown together and add enough milk to form a rather thin sauce
3. Add 1 small teaspoon of salt, 1/2 teaspoon of pepper, a pinch of red pepper, a little nutmeg and 1 teaspoon of powdered ginger if desired
4. Mix everything well, bring to a boil and add 1 large cup of cold fried or cooked fish, crumbled into pieces the size of a hazelnut
5. Place on heat and cook for 10 minutes
6. Arrange on a hot serving platter lined with parsley leaves and serve at table

title: Pieczeń Barania (Roast Lamb)
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb hind quarter
- vinegar
- garlic cloves
- salt

## Directions

1. Take the hind quarter for roast, clean of excess fat, veins and membranes, then beat fairly strongly.
2. Scald with vinegar, lard with garlic cloves, salt and let rest for about an hour.
3. Then place in a roasting pan and roast in a medium oven, basting with the liquid sauce from the pan.
4. Shortly before serving, sprinkle with flour and brown.

title: Pieczeń Barania Duszona (Braised Lamb Roast)
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb roast, prepared as for roasting
- sour cream

## Directions

1. Prepare the lamb roast as above, place in the oven and roast until half done.
2. Then remove, place in a pan and braise on low heat until tender.
3. Toward the end of braising, flavor with sour cream.
4. If the meat is fatty, excess fat should be removed from the roasting pan before placing the roast in the pan.

title: Pieczeń Barania jak Sarnina (Lamb Roast Like Venison)
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb back, or other lamb meat
- onion slices
- vinegar
- pepper
- English herbs
- bay leaves
- juniper berries
- salt
- lard
- butter
- flour
- sour cream

## Directions

1. For this dish, the back of lamb works best, that is the upper part on both sides of the spine, although other parts of meat can also be used for this purpose.
2. Clean the meat of all veins and trim all fat so that only meat is visible everywhere.
3. Place the prepared meat in a flat vessel; cover entirely with onion slices, pour with vinegar boiled with pepper, English herbs, bay leaves and a little juniper berries and place in a cool cellar for three to four days.
4. Then remove from vinegar, rub slightly with juniper berries, salt, lard with thinly sliced lard and let rest for an hour.
5. Then place in a roasting pan, baste with butter at first with a little vinegar, then sprinkle with flour, and shortly before serving flavor generously with sour cream.

title: Pieczeń Barania po Bretońsku (Breton Lamb Roast)
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb shoulder
- garlic
- butter or oil
- salt
- pepper
- mixed vegetables (various cut vegetables)
- white beans, cooked sour

## Directions

1. Beat the lamb shoulder strongly and rub garlic around it. Brush with butter or oil, salt and pepper, and let it rest for an hour and a half.
2. After this time, place on a roasting pan, pour fat over it and put in a very hot oven to brown as quickly as possible, but so that the roast does not release its juices.
3. After browning, add various chopped vegetables, pour butter over and put in the oven. Bake only enough so that it does not cook completely inside, and it will be more juicy.
4. Serve with white beans cooked sour.

title: Pieczeń Barania po Myśliwsku (Lamb Roast Hunter Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb loin
- vegetables
- vinegar
- spices
- juniper berries
- one onion
- one small carrot
- ham piece
- bay leaf
- peppercorns
- celery stalk
- butter
- sour cream

## Directions

1. Marinate lamb loin for four days, adding to the marinade in addition to vegetables, vinegar and spices, also some juniper berries.
2. After removing from the marinade, roast the roast as usual, slice into thin slices, arrange on a serving platter and pour with the following sauce:
3. Finely chop one onion, one small carrot, a piece of ham, add a bay leaf, several peppercorns and a celery stalk; brown all this in butter, then add two tablespoons vinegar, some broth and braise on low heat until tender.
4. Then strain through a sieve, pour into the sauce that remained on the roasting pan from the lamb roast and boil once more.
5. This sauce is called sharp; to have a wild sauce, a few tablespoons of sour cream should be added to it.

title: Pieczeń Cielęca (Roast Veal)
body:
## Ingredients

- hind quarter of veal
- cold water
- young bacon
- salt
- butter

## Directions

1. The best roast and generally every dish made from veal is from a calf aged five to nine weeks.
2. For the roast, take the hind quarter, rinse in cold water, remove the membrane and cut off the bone found underneath near the tail.
3. Lard densely with young bacon, salt it and let it rest for two hours.
4. Place in a roasting pan and roast in a hot oven for a while until it browns well, then finish roasting in a moderately hot oven.
5. It is advised to never pour water over veal roast, but only fat, which should be poured over frequently during roasting.
6. A medium-sized roast cooks for about an hour and a half.
7. When serving, pour over with browned butter. The best roast is naturally made on a spit.

title: Pieczeń Cielęca au Naturel (Veal Roast au Naturel)
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal
- Salt
- Carrots, sliced
- Onion, sliced
- Celery, sliced
- Water
- Butter
- Flour
- Broth
- Lemon
- Sour cream

## Directions

1. Wash and salt the veal and arrange it in a roasting pan
2. Add sliced carrots, onion, and celery
3. Roast until tender, basting initially with water, then with the drippings that accumulate in the pan
4. When nearly done, mix a piece of butter with flour, place in the roasting pan, dilute with broth, and season with lemon
5. Strain the sauce and pour it over the veal arranged on a platter

title: Pieczeń Cielęca au Naturel (Veal Roast au Naturel)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal
- salt
- carrots
- onions
- celery
- water
- butter
- flour
- broth
- lemon

## Directions

1. Arrange the rinsed and salted veal in a roasting pan. Add some carrots, onions and celery sliced into pieces and roast until tender.
2. Pour with water at first, then with the drippings from the pan.
3. When serving, mix a piece of butter with flour, place in the roasting pan, mix with broth and at the end season with lemon.

title: Pieczeń Cielęca jak Sarnina (Veal Roast Like Venison)
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal
- Vinegar
- Juniper berries, crushed
- Young bacon
- Butter
- Sour cream

## Directions

1. Scald a nice piece of veal with light vinegar, having first rubbed and dusted it with crushed juniper berries
2. Place in a dish and let it stand in a cold place for two to three days
3. Remove from the dish, rinse in the same vinegar, lard with young bacon, and roast in a medium oven as you would any roast, basting frequently with butter
4. At the end of roasting, season with sour cream

title: Pieczeń Cielęca po Amerykańsku (American Style Veal Roast)
body:
## Ingredients

- piece of veal from the leg
- bread or bread crumbs
- water
- butter
- salt
- pepper
- thyme
- bacon
- flour

## Directions

1. Take a piece of veal from the leg and remove the bone.
2. Soak one cup of grated bread or bread crumbs in a little water, add one tablespoon of butter, a quarter teaspoon of salt, some pepper and a bit of powdered thyme. Mix into a paste and fill with this stuffing the place where the bone was.
3. Roll the meat and tie with twine. Rub the top with salt, pepper and flour.
4. Place in a roasting pan, put a couple of bacon slices on top, add water and roast in a moderately hot oven.
5. Baste frequently to avoid burning; for this purpose the meat can also be covered with paper.
6. Roast for about two hours.

title: Pieczeń Cielęca z Biszamelem (Veal Roast with Béchamel)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal roast
- béchamel sauce
- Parmesan cheese
- young butter
- flour
- sour cream
- egg yolks

## Directions

1. Roast the meat as usual. When it is almost done, cut it deeply and interlard with béchamel sauce mixed with Parmesan.
2. Pour the same béchamel sauce over the top and place in the oven for five to ten minutes until it browns.
3. To prepare the béchamel: Take a quarter pound of young butter, bring to a boil with one tablespoon flour, mix in a half cup of sour cream, cook down to half.
4. Then season with three egg yolks beaten previously with one tablespoon of cold sour cream, and then add a quarter pound of grated Parmesan.

title: Pieczeń Cielęca Zawijana (Rolled Veal Roast)
body:
## Ingredients

- small quarter of veal
- salt
- nutmeg
- lemon
- young bacon
- vinegar
- good sour cream

## Directions

1. Butterfly the small quarter of veal lengthwise so the bones are loosened. After removing the bones, pound the meat well and season with salt and a bit of nutmeg.
2. Sprinkle inside with lemon, using one large or one and a half medium lemons. Over the lemon, lay finely sliced young bacon to cover the entire inner surface.
3. Roll tightly into a log and tie with twine.
4. Place the rolled roast in a salad bowl or dish, sprinkle with good vinegar and place in a cold pantry for two days. Every day it should be turned several times.
5. After two days, remove from the dish, salt well and let it rest in salt for about three hours.
6. Then place on a roasting pan and bake in a moderately hot oven, basting frequently with butter. At the end of roasting, season with one tablespoon of good sour cream to form a creamy sauce.
7. Serve sliced.

title: Pieczeń Huzarska (Hussar Roast)
body:
## Ingredients

- several pounds of roast beef, preferably sirloin
- salt and pepper to taste
- fat for basting
- two medium onions
- butter
- white bread, grated
- sauce from roasting

## Directions

1. Pound a several-pound piece of beef strongly, salt it and roast in a hot oven, basting frequently with fat
2. When well browned and half cooked, remove and cut diagonally with a sharp knife
3. Prepare the stuffing: roast two medium onions, grate them, add butter, salt and pepper to taste, and enough grated white bread to make a fairly thick mass, mix well
4. Fill the cuts with this stuffing
5. Place the roast in a roasting pan, pour the sauce that formed during roasting over it, cover with a lid, and braise on low heat for half an hour

title: Pieczeń Huzarska (Hussar Roast)
body:
## Ingredients

- piece of beef suitable for braising
- salt

## Directions

1. Beat a several-pound piece of roast, preferably suitable for braising, strongly
2. Salt it and sear in hot

title: Pieczeń po Portugalsku (Portuguese Roast)
body:
## Ingredients

- a piece of tender beef
- various vegetables, cut into cubes
- several potatoes
- a few sliced tomatoes
- some green peas
- broth

## Directions

1. Brown the piece of tender beef on all sides in a roasting pan
2. Add various vegetables cut into cubes, several potatoes, a few sliced tomatoes, and some green peas
3. Pour broth over and braise on low heat until tender
4. When the meat is tender, remove from the pan, slice it, arrange on a platter and pour the remaining sauce with vegetables over it

title: Pieczeń Siekana (Minced Meat Roast)
body:
## Ingredients

- one and a half pounds tender beef, minced finely
- half a pound minced pork
- two tablespoons minced beef suet from the kidney
- two rolls, skinned and soaked in cold water, or the same amount of white stale bread, pressed well of water
- salt and pepper to taste
- one roasted and finely minced onion
- two egg yolks
- a few tablespoons young bacon, cut into cubes
- flour
- sour cream

## Directions

1. Mince one and a half pounds tender beef very finely, add half a pound minced pork and two tablespoons minced beef suet from the kidney
2. Mix, then mince again
3. Add two rolls skinned and soaked in cold water, or the same amount of white stale bread pressed well of water
4. Add salt and pepper to taste, one roasted and finely minced onion, two egg yolks and a few tablespoons young bacon cut into cubes
5. Mix it all and form into an oblong loaf
6. Place on a buttered baking sheet and put in a hot oven for half an hour, basting frequently during roasting with the drippings from the meat
7. Before serving, thicken the sauce with flour and sour cream

title: Pieczeń Siekana Nadziewana Kartoflami (Minced Meat Roast Stuffed with Potatoes)
body:
## Ingredients

- minced meat prepared as for pieczeń siekana
- hot mashed potatoes
- flour
- sour cream

## Directions

1. Prepare the meat exactly as for the roast stuffed with buckwheat
2. Instead of buckwheat, use hot mashed potatoes
3. Serve with a sauce made from flour and sour cream

title: Pieczeń Siekana Nadziewana Kaszą (Minced Meat Roast Stuffed with Buckwheat)
body:
## Ingredients

- minced meat prepared as for pieczeń siekana
- bacon
- half a quart coarse buckwheat groats
- water
- flour
- sour cream
- butter

## Directions

1. Prepare the meat as described in the pieczeń siekana recipe above
2. Fry half a quart coarse buckwheat groats with bacon, then let cool
3. Dampen a tablecloth with water, spread the meat on it in a thin round slice, arrange the buckwheat lengthwise in the center, roll into an oblong loaf, dust or coat with flour and place on a hot buttered skillet
4. Brown on high heat, then place the skillet in a very hot oven for half an hour or slightly longer
5. Before serving, thicken the sauce with flour and sour cream

title: Pieczeń Ułańska (Uhlan Roast)
body:
## Ingredients

- meat
- salt and pepper to taste
- vegetables
- cold water
- three tablespoons horseradish
- butter
- one tablespoon sugar
- two egg yolks
- grated bread
- fresh parsley or dill, finely chopped
- flour
- sour cream

## Directions

1. Salt and pepper the meat to taste, brown it on all sides with vegetables, cover with cold water and braise on low heat under a lid
2. When the meat is half-braised, remove it from the pan and cut deeply along or diagonally
3. Prepare the stuffing: mix three tablespoons horseradish, some butter, one tablespoon sugar, two egg yolks, and enough grated bread to make a fairly thick mass, add salt, pepper and finely chopped fresh parsley or dill, mix well
4. Layer the meat with this stuffing
5. Place the prepared roast in a clean roasting pan
6. In the pan where the meat was braised, put one tablespoon butter and one tablespoon flour, brown them, then dilute with sour cream, bring to a boil, strain, pour this sauce over the roast and braise on low heat until tender, being careful that the stuffing does not leak out

title: Pieczeń Wołowa (Roasted Beef Steak)
body:
## Ingredients

- piece of beef suitable for roasting or cross-cut
- young lard, thinly sliced
- butter
- salt

## Directions

1. Lard the meat with thinly sliced young lard
2. Place on a roasting pan, put a piece of butter on top
3. Place in a very hot oven
4. During roasting, baste frequently with the drippings
5. When one side is sufficiently browned, turn over to the other side
6. Salt only fifteen minutes before serving
7. A four-pound piece of meat cooks for forty to forty-five minutes
8. For smaller pieces of two to three pounds, first sear in butter on a very high heat on the stove, then place in the oven on the roasting pan

title: Pieczeń Wołowa Duszona (Braised Beef Roast)
body:
## Ingredients

- piece of beef suitable for braising
- lard, cut into thick pieces
- pepper
- salt
- butter
- water
- onion
- dried mushrooms
- peppercorns
- flour or grated bread
- tomatoes (optional)

## Directions

1. Lard the meat with fairly thick pieces of lard sprinkled with pepper
2. Salt and let sit for one or two hours
3. Melt a tablespoon of butter in a roasting pan
4. When it is brown, place the meat and on high heat brown it all around without covering
5. The pan should be such a size that the meat fits snugly
6. Pour water over the meat, add sliced onion, a few dried mushrooms, and a few peppercorns
7. Braise under cover on very low heat for two to four hours depending on the size of the roast
8. Be careful that there is never too much water, otherwise the meat will boil rather than braise, and will be neither as juicy nor as tasty
9. Shortly before serving, a few tomatoes can be added and braised together
10. After removing the meat, thicken the remaining sauce with a little flour or grated bread
11. Tomatoes can be omitted and the sauce thickened with flour only
12. Serve separately any vegetables, preferably braised in butter

title: Pieczeń z Chrzanem (Beef with Horseradish)
body:
## Ingredients

- piece of beef suitable for roasting
- salt
- butter
- water
- onion
- fresh grated horseradish
- butter
- grated bread roll
- sugar
- salt and pepper
- egg yolks
- lemon
- twine

## Directions

1. Beat a piece of beef well, salt it, and sear in a roasting pan in hot butter
2. When it is well browned all around, pour water over it, add some onion, and braise under cover on low heat
3. When completely tender, remove from the pan and cut lengthwise through the center to three-quarters of the meat's thickness and open it
4. Line both edges of the cut with thin slices of lemon, peeled of skin and seeds
5. Prepare the filling: take three tablespoons of freshly grated horseradish, two tablespoons of butter, one tablespoon of grated bread roll, one teaspoon of sugar, salt and pepper to taste, and two egg yolks; mix everything into a smooth mass
6. Place the filling between the lemon slices in the cut
7. Tie the meat with twine, place back in the pan, and braise for half an hour or forty minutes
8. Thicken the sauce with flour and sour cream, simmer together briefly, and serve

title: Pieczone Banany (Baked Bananas)
body:
## Ingredients

- several bananas
- one lemon
- three tablespoons sugar
- one tablespoon melted butter

## Directions

1. Peel and cut in half lengthwise several bananas, place them in a baking pan and sprinkle with the following syrup: squeeze the juice from one lemon, add three tablespoons of sugar and one tablespoon of melted butter and mix well.
2. With this syrup you should only dampen the bananas and place in a moderately hot oven for half an hour, but during this time you must baste them every few minutes with the syrup until it is all used.

title: Pierogi Leniwe (Lazy Dumplings)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound fresh cottage cheese
- four egg yolks
- one tablespoon butter
- salt to taste
- one tablespoon wheat flour
- one egg white
- hot butter
- browned roll

## Directions

1. Carefully press one pound of fresh cottage cheese to remove whey and mix in a bowl with four egg yolks and one tablespoon butter
2. Add salt to taste and one tablespoon wheat flour and mix well
3. Add the foam from one egg white and mix lightly
4. Turn onto a table thickly dusted with flour
5. Roll into a long cylinder, notch with a knife and cut into diagonal pieces
6. Cook in a very large pot full of boiling water
7. When they float to the surface, remove with a slotted spoon and arrange on a platter
8. Drizzle with hot melted butter with browned roll and serve

title: Pierogi z Grzybami (Mushroom Pierogi)
body:
## Ingredients

- one quarter pound of dried mushrooms
- hot water
- two onions
- one tablespoon of butter
- a handful of grated bread
- salt and pepper
- flour
- egg
- water

## Directions

1. Pour hot water over one quarter pound of dried mushrooms, wash well, cook until tender, and chop very finely.
2. Peel and chop two onions, fry in one tablespoon of butter, and add to the mushrooms along with a handful of grated bread, salt, and pepper to taste, mixing everything well.
3. Knead dough from flour, egg, and water as usual for pierogi.
4. Place heaps of the mushroom mixture on the dough and cut out small pierogi with a glass, sealing the edges well.
5. Cook in a large pot of boiling water and arrange on a serving platter, pouring melted butter over them.

title: Pierogi z Grzybami i Kapustą (Mushroom and Cabbage Pierogi)
body:
## Ingredients

- one quarter of flour
- one egg
- water
- one small head of fresh cabbage
- salted water
- several dried mushrooms
- one herring
- one tablespoon of butter
- one tablespoon of chopped onion
- pepper

## Directions

1. Make dough from one quarter pound of flour, one egg, and enough water so it is not too stiff, and roll out thinly on a work surface.
2. Shred a small head of fresh cabbage and cook in salted water, then drain and chop finely.
3. Cook several dried mushrooms separately until tender and chop finely, and also chop very finely one well-soaked herring that has been cleaned of skin and bones.
4. Fry one tablespoon of butter with the same amount of chopped onion, then add the cabbage and braise until completely tender, then add the mushrooms and herring, mix everything well, and add pepper to taste.
5. Place heaps of this mixture on the rolled out dough, cut out small pierogi with a glass, seal the edges carefully, and cook in boiling water like any other pierogi.
6. Serve on a platter poured with melted butter.

title: Pierogi z Jagodami (Berry Pierogi)
body:
## Ingredients

- flour
- egg
- water
- berries
- sugar
- egg white

## Directions

1. Knead flour with egg and water into a stiff dough.
2. Roll out very thinly on a work surface and place small heaps of berries on it.
3. Sprinkle the berries with sugar, wrap the dough around them, and seal the edges well by first brushing them with egg white so they do not come apart during cooking.
4. Leave on the work surface for half an hour so the berries release their juices.
5. Boil a large pot of water and drop the pierogi into it, cooking until they float to the surface, then carefully remove with a slotted spoon and arrange on a serving platter.
6. Serve with sugar and sour cream.
7. If made with cherries, remove the pits and drain excess juice first.

title: Pierogi z Kartofli ze Śliwkami (Potato Pierogi with Plums)
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart of floury potatoes
- one tablespoon of milk
- one whole egg
- one egg yolk
- half pound of sifted wheat flour
- ripe plums with pits

## Directions

1. Peel and cook one quart of floury potatoes in separate water, mash with a pestle in a bowl, pour in one tablespoon of milk, and beat in one whole egg and one egg yolk.
2. Mash to a uniform mixture and then add half pound of sifted wheat flour and knead into a not-very-thick dough.
3. Flour your hands, take small pieces of dough, stretch them out, and wrap one ripe plum with its pit in each piece, taking care that the layer of dough around the plum is thin and even.
4. Drop the prepared dumplings into a large pot filled with water and cook, then arrange on a serving platter, pour hot butter over them, and serve.

title: Pierogi z Powidłami (Plum Butter Pierogi)
body:
## Ingredients

- flour
- egg
- water
- plum butter or jam
- water for sealing
- butter
- sugar
- cinnamon
- sour cream

## Directions

1. Knead dough from egg and water and roll out very thinly on a work surface.
2. Place small heaps of plum butter or jam on it, cut out small pierogi with a glass, brush the edges with water and seal firmly.
3. Cook in a large pot full of water, carefully remove with a slotted spoon onto a serving platter, pour butter over them, sprinkle with sugar mixed with cinnamon, and serve passing sour cream separately on the side.

title: Pierogi z Serem (Cheese Pierogi)
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart of flour
- one egg
- water as needed
- cheese
- butter
- egg yolk
- sugar
- chives, finely chopped (optional)
- raisins (optional)
- sour cream

## Directions

1. Knead a not-very-stiff dough using one quart of flour, one egg, and as much water as needed.
2. Roll out the dough and place small heaps of cheese on it, which should be well-mixed with butter, egg yolk, and sugar.
3. Cover with dough and cut out small pierogi using a glass or small cup.
4. Drop into boiling water in a large pot, and when they float to the surface, remove with a slotted spoon and arrange on a serving platter.
5. Pour melted butter over them and serve, passing sour cream separately on the side.
6. If desired, finely chopped chives or raisins can be added to the cheese.

title: Pierogi ze Szpinakiem (Spinach Pierogi)
body:
## Ingredients

- flour
- egg
- water
- spinach
- salted water
- butter
- grated cheese

## Directions

1. Knead ordinary dough from egg and water as above.
2. Place heaps of spinach that has been cooked in salted water and then carefully mashed in a bowl or passed through a sieve on the dough.
3. Wrap and cook the same way as other wrapped pierogi, and when serving pour hot butter over them and sprinkle with grated cheese.

title: Pierożki z Mięsem (Meat Pierogi)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound cooked beef, minced
- 1/4 pound young, previously cooked bacon, minced
- 1 tablespoon butter
- 2 onions, finely minced (preferably roasted)
- a few mushrooms, minced (if available)
- 1 cup grated bread
- 1 or 2 egg yolks
- salt and pepper to taste
- 1/2 quart flour
- 1 egg
- water

## Directions

1. Put 1 tablespoon of butter in a small pan, and when it melts, add about 2 finely minced onions (preferably roasted), fry briefly and immediately remove from heat.
2. Mix with the minced meat; if there are mushrooms, mince a few and add them along with some of the broth in which they were cooked.
3. Add 1 cup of grated bread, 1 or 2 egg yolks, salt and plenty of pepper, and mix everything well.
4. Knead 1/2 quart flour and 1 egg into dough with enough water to make it sufficiently soft.
5. Roll out and cut into diagonal squares.
6. Place walnut-sized portions of meat filling (or slightly larger) on each square, fold and seal the edges.
7. Drop the prepared pierogi into boiling water.
8. When they float to the surface, they are done.
9. Arrange on a platter and pour melted butter with toasted bread, or young bacon, over them.

title: Pierożki z Mięsem (Meat Piroshki)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound cooked beef, minced
- 1/4 pound young previously cooked bacon, minced
- 1 tablespoon butter
- 2 onions, finely minced (preferably roasted)
- mushrooms, minced (if available)

## Directions

1. Mince together the cooked beef and the quarter pound of young previously cooked bacon.
2. Place a tablespoon of butter in a pan and when it melts, add the two finely minced onions (preferably roasted) and fry briefly.
3. Remove from heat and mix with the minced meat.
4. If mushrooms are available, mince a few and add them along with some of the sauce in which they were cooked.

title: Pierożki z Ostryg (Oyster Patties)
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup of flour
- half a cup of lard
- salt
- half a cup of very cold water
- one tablespoon of flour
- one cup of milk
- one teaspoon of lemon juice
- a pinch of nutmeg
- one small cup of oysters

## Directions

1. Mix one cup of flour, one half cup of lard, salt and one half cup of very cold water to make a dough
2. Roll out fairly thin and line small tin molds called patty pans
3. Mix one tablespoon of flour with one cup of milk; dissolve the flour in a little cold milk and heat the rest
4. Add the heated milk to the flour mixture and bring to a boil
5. Add one teaspoon of lemon juice and a pinch of nutmeg, then add one small cup of oysters
6. Salt to taste and bring to a boil once
7. Remove from heat and place four oysters in each mold lined with dough
8. Cover the top with the same dough and bake in a hot oven

title: Pikle Francuskie (French Pickle)
body:
## Ingredients

- pek zielonych pomidorów
- sześć dużych cebul
- filiżanka soli
- kwarta octu
- trzy funty cukru
- ćwierć funta nasienia białej gorczycy
- po jednej łyżeczce sproszkowanych gwoździków
- łyżeczka sproszkowanego imbieru
- łyżeczka czerwonego pieprzu
- dwie łyżeczki musztardy

## Directions

1. Pokrajać w plasterki pek zielonych pomidorów i sześć dużych cebul
2. Włożyć w gliniany słój
3. Posypać filiżanką soli i postawić w chłodnem miejscu na noc
4. Na drugi dzień rano odcedzić słoną wodę
5. Dodać kwartę octu, trzy funty cukru, ćwierć funta nasienia białej gorczycy, po jednej łyżeczce sproszkowanych gwoździków, imbieru i czerwonego pieprzu, oraz dwie łyżeczki musztardy
6. Postawić na ogniu i gotować piętnaście minut, licząc od czasu zagotowania
7. Ponakładać w słoiki i szczelnie zamknąć

title: Pikle Hiszpańskie (Spanish Pickles)
body:
## Ingredients

- twelve cucumbers
- one large head of cabbage
- twelve onions
- twelve mango fruits (six red and six green)
- one pound of brown sugar
- celery seeds
- mustard seeds
- one gallon of vinegar
- yellow ginger (turmeric powder)

## Directions

1. Chop twelve cucumbers finely and salt overnight
2. Chop one large head of cabbage
3. Chop twelve onions and twelve mango fruits (six red and six green) finely and salt
4. Let stand for five hours, then press out the salt properly
5. Add one pound of brown sugar, celery seeds, and mustard seeds
6. Cover with one gallon of vinegar
7. Add yellow ginger (turmeric powder)
8. Put on fire and when the entire mixture cooks well, fill jars and seal tightly

title: Pikle Indyjskie (Indian Pickle)
body:
## Ingredients

- pół pęka zielonych pomidorów
- sześć średnich ogórków
- mała główka kapusty
- kilka cebul
- trzy pęczki selerów
- cztery strąki zielonego pieprzu
- filiżanka soli
- pół filiżanki gorczycy w ziarnkach
- pół filiżanki nasienia selerów
- półtorej filiżanki brunatnego cukru
- dwie kwarty octu

## Directions

1. Posiekać drobno pół pęka zielonych pomidorów, sześć średnich ogórków, małą główkę kapusty, kilka cebul, trzy pęczki selerów, cztery strąki zielonego pieprzu
2. Dodać filiżankę soli
3. Niech postoi dwanaście godzin
4. Wycisnąć z soli
5. Dodać pół filiżanki gorczycy w ziarnkach, pół filiżanki nasienia selerów, półtorej filiżanki brunatnego cukru i dwie kwarty octu
6. Postawić na ogniu i gotować do miękkości
7. Napełnić słoiki i szczelnie zamknąć

title: Pikle Krajane (Sliced Pickle)
body:
## Ingredients

- trzy tuziny dużych ogórków
- tuzin białych cebul
- sól
- kwarta octu
- woda
- filiżanka białego cukru
- trzy łyżki białej gorczycy
- dwie łyżki mielonego białego pieprzu
- pół kwarty białego octu
- cztery łyżki utartego chrzanu

## Directions

1. Obrać i pokrajać w cienkie plasterki trzy tuziny dużych ogórków
2. Obrać tuzin białych cebul i pokrajać
3. Nasolić osobno ogórki i cebule
4. Niech tak stoją przez noc
5. Nazajutrz wyjąć z soli
6. Zmieszać ogórki z cebulą
7. Zalać kwartą octu, zmieszanego na pół z wodą
8. Postawić na ogniu i niech dojdzie do punktu wrzenia — ale nie gotować
9. Ocet z wodą odcedzić
10. Dodać filiżankę białego cukru, trzy łyżki białej gorczycy, dwie łyżki mielonego białego pieprzu
11. Zalać pół kwartą białego octu
12. Postawić na ogniu i mieszać aż dojdzie do temperatury wrzenia, ale nie gotować
13. Zdjąć z ognia
14. Dodać cztery łyżki utartego chrzanu
15. Nakładać w słoiki na gorąco i hermetycznie zamknąć

title: Pikle Siekane (Chopped Pickle)
body:
## Ingredients

- kwarta dojrzałych pomidorów
- dwie filiżanki selerów
- tuzin białych cebul
- dwa strąki czerwonego pieprzu
- filiżanka soli
- kwarta octu
- cztery funty brunatnego cukru
- dwie uncye ziarnek gorczycy

## Directions

1. Posiekać każde osobno, kwartę dojrzałych pomidorów, dwie filiżanki selerów, tuzin białych cebul, dwa strąki czerwonego pieprzu
2. Posypać to filiżanką soli
3. Postawić w chłodnem miejscu na dwadzieścia cztery godziny
4. Zagotować kwartę octu z czterema funtami brunatnego cukru
5. Ostudzić
6. Wycisnąć pomidory z soli
7. Dodać dwie uncye ziarnek gorczycy
8. Zalać przegogtowanym octem z cukrem
9. Napełnić słoiki i zakręcić pokrywki

title: Pikle w Musztardzie (Mustard Pickle)
body:
## Ingredients

- równa ilość malutkich ogóreczków, białych cebulek i kalafiorów, aby razem uczyniło pek
- filiżanka soli
- woda
- pół galona żółtego octu
- pół funta suchej musztardy
- łyżka żółtego imbieru
- dwie i pół łyżki proszku indyjskiego
- dwie łyżki imbieru
- łyżeczka czerwonego pieprzu

## Directions

1. Przesypać solę, biorąc do tego soli jedną filiżankę
2. Zalać taką ilością wody, aby wszystko przykryć
3. Postawić w chłodnem miejscu na całą noc
4. Na drugi dzień rano wyrzucić na sito
5. Wodę odcedzić i pikle wytrzeć do sucha
6. Wziąć pół galona żółtego octu
7. Zmieszać pół funta suchej musztardy, łyżkę żółtego imbieru, dwie i pół łyżki proszku indyjskiego, dwie łyżki imbieru i łyżeczkę czerwonego pieprzu
8. Dodać do tego trochę zimnego octu i rozmieszać na gładką masę
9. Resztę octu postawić na piecu
10. Wlać do niego masę z korzeni i mieszać nieustannie aż się zagotuje
11. Płynem tym zalać pikle
12. Postawić na dwa dni; codziennie należy parę razy dobrze wszystko wymieszać
13. Ponakładać w kwartowe słoiki, zamknąć szczelnie i przechować

title: Pikle z Oliwą (Pickles with Oil)
body:
## Ingredients

- one hundred medium cucumbers
- one and a half quarts of white onions
- three ounces of ground white pepper
- three ounces of white mustard seeds
- one ounce of celery seeds
- two cups of good oil
- a small piece of alum
- cold vinegar

## Directions

1. Slice cucumbers into equal-sized pieces without peeling
2. Salt them and let them stand for three hours
3. Drain the salted water that has collected; if cucumbers are too salty, rinse with cold water
4. Slice the white onions into equal-sized pieces
5. Add ground white pepper, white mustard seeds, and celery seeds
6. Mix in good oil
7. Add a small piece of alum dissolved in warm water
8. Cover with cold vinegar and seal the jars hermetically

title: Pikle z Pomidorów (Tomato Pickle)
body:
## Ingredients

- ten pounds of ripe tomatoes
- four pounds of sugar
- one quart of vinegar
- one tablespoon of ground cinnamon
- one tablespoon of ground cloves

## Directions

1. Scald ten pounds of ripe tomatoes with boiling water, peel and cut into slices, then add four pounds of sugar, one quart of vinegar, one tablespoon of ground cinnamon and one tablespoon of ground cloves
2. Place everything on fire and cook until thick, stirring frequently, then fill jars and close tightly

title: Pikle z Pomidorów (Tomato Pickle)
body:
## Ingredients

- ten funtów dojrzałych pomidorów
- cztery funty cukru
- kwarta octu
- łyżka mielonego cynamonu
- łyżka mielonych gwoździków

## Directions

1. Sparzyć wrzącą wodą dziesięć funtów dojrzałych pomidorów
2. Obrać i pokrajać w plasterki
3. Dodać cztery funty cukru, kwartę octu, łyżkę mielonego cynamonu i łyżkę mielonych gwoździków
4. Postawić na ogniu i gotować aż będzie gęste, często mieszając
5. Napełnić słoiki i szczelnie zamknąć

title: Pineapple Liqueur (Likier Ananasowy)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of ripe pineapple
- one quart of white arrack
- sugar to taste

## Directions

1. Slice one pound of ripe pineapple into thin slices
2. Pour one quart of white arrack over it and set in a cool place for two days
3. Then strain, add sugar to taste and store in a corked bottle

title: Piramidki Kartoflane (Potato Pyramids)
body:
## Ingredients

- boiled potatoes
- butter
- hot sour cream or milk
- salt
- pepper
- one beaten egg

## Directions

1. Mash boiled potatoes and add some butter, hot sour cream or milk, salt and pepper to taste
2. Beat with a spoon for about ten minutes
3. Add a beaten egg and mix well
4. Form small pyramids from this mixture
5. Bake in a hot oven until well browned
6. After removing from the oven, brush around with butter and serve on a hot serving dish

title: Piław Turecki (Turkish Pilaf)
body:
## Ingredients

- tomatoes
- one cup tomato liquid from cooked and strained tomatoes
- one cup strong broth
- salt
- pepper
- one large onion, grated or finely chopped
- one cup well-rinsed rice
- one tablespoon butter

## Directions

1. Cook tomatoes and then press through a sieve
2. For every cup of strained tomato liquid, take one cup of strong broth, add plenty of salt and pepper and one large grated or finely chopped onion
3. Bring to a boil and then add one cup of well-rinsed rice
4. Cook further, stirring frequently with a fork, until the rice absorbs all the liquid
5. Add one tablespoon butter and mix, then cook in steam for twenty minutes
6. This rice is served as a vegetable dish

title: Piław Turecki (Turkish Pilaf)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of rinsed rice
- water
- fatty lamb breast or other meat
- butter
- small onions

## Directions

1. Pour water over one pound of rinsed rice and cook for ten minutes, then throw it onto a sieve so the water drains well.
2. Chop fatty lamb breast or other meat into pieces the size of cutlets, salt it and place a layer of meat in a braising pan on melted butter, then a layer of rice, then meat again and so on until everything is used, interspersing these layers with small onions that have been previously blanched with boiling water.
3. Place the meat and rice arranged in this way on low heat and cover tightly with a lid so that as little steam escapes as possible.
4. After fifteen minutes check the pan and, if necessary, pour in a tablespoon of cold water so it does not burn. Repeat this watering every few or several minutes until the rice is completely tender, which will take about two hours.

title: Placek do Kawy na Prędce (Quick Coffee Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- one-quarter cup butter
- one cup sugar
- one egg, beaten
- one-half cup milk
- one-quarter teaspoon salt
- one and one-half cups flour
- one heaping teaspoon baking powder
- small pieces of butter for topping
- sugar and cinnamon for topping

## Directions

1. Cream one-quarter cup butter with one cup sugar
2. Add one beaten egg
3. Add one-half cup milk
4. Add one-quarter teaspoon salt and one and one-half cups flour sifted several times with one heaping teaspoon baking powder
5. Mix into a smooth dough
6. Pour into shallow pans to no more than one inch depth
7. Scatter small pieces of butter on top
8. Sprinkle with sugar and cinnamon
9. Bake in a moderately hot oven

title: Placek Imbierowy (Ginger Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- two cups flour
- one-half cup sugar
- one-half cup molasses
- one-third cup butter
- one teaspoon baking soda
- one teaspoon powdered ginger
- one teaspoon cinnamon
- one teaspoon salt

## Directions

1. Mix two cups flour, one-half cup sugar, one-half cup molasses, one-third cup butter, one teaspoon baking soda, one teaspoon powdered ginger, one teaspoon cinnamon, and one teaspoon salt together (first dissolve the soda in the molasses)
2. Bake on a flat sheet so the layer is no thicker than three-quarters inch, at most one inch
3. After baking, cut with a hot knife into square pieces and serve hot

title: Placek Szkocki (Scottish Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- two cups sugar
- one cup butter
- flour as needed

## Directions

1. Cream two cups sugar with one cup butter
2. Add enough flour as the butter will take and mix into a thick dough
3. Turn out onto a work surface and roll to one-half inch thickness
4. Cut with a knife into small squares
5. Prick each square with a fork
6. Bake in a moderately hot oven

title: Placek z Jabłkami (Apple Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- one-half quart flour
- one and one-half teaspoons baking powder
- one teaspoon salt
- two tablespoons butter
- one egg
- two-thirds cup milk
- four large sour apples
- sugar and cinnamon for topping

## Directions

1. Mix one-half quart flour well with one and one-half teaspoons baking powder and one teaspoon salt
2. Rub in two tablespoons butter
3. Add one egg and two-thirds cup milk
4. Mix everything carefully and spread on a baking sheet to a thickness of one-half inch
5. Peel four large sour apples, cut into eight pieces, and arrange in rows on the dough
6. Sprinkle with sugar and a bit of powdered cinnamon
7. Bake in a hot oven for twenty-five minutes
8. Can be served with cream and sugar, or without

title: Placki Anyżowe (Anise Cakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- eight eggs
- one pound sugar
- one tablespoon anise seed
- flour as needed

## Directions

1. Beat eight eggs with one pound sugar for one-half hour, beating always in one direction only
2. Add one tablespoon anise seed and enough flour to make a medium-thick dough
3. Turn out onto a work surface and roll thin
4. Cut out small round cakes
5. Place in a hot oven until browned

title: Placki Kartoflane (Potato Cakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked potatoes
- half cup flour per heaping cup of potatoes
- one tablespoon milk
- half teaspoon salt
- butter for serving

## Directions

1. Mash cooked potatoes into a smooth mass.
2. For each heaping cup of potatoes, take half a cup of flour, one tablespoon of milk, and half a teaspoon of salt.
3. Mix everything well, place on a work surface, and roll out to the thickness of an inch.
4. Cut small circles from the dough, prick with a fork, and bake in a hot oven.
5. Serve hot with butter.

title: Placki Kukurydzane No. 1 (Corn Bread No. 1)
body:
## Ingredients

- three eggs
- two and a half cups sweet milk
- two and a half cups white corn meal
- two teaspoons baking powder
- one teaspoon salt
- half cup wheat flour
- one tablespoon melted lard

## Directions

1. Beat three eggs well and add two and a half cups of sweet milk
2. Sift together to mix well two and a half cups of white corn meal with two teaspoons of baking powder, one teaspoon of salt, and half cup of wheat flour
3. Add one tablespoon of melted lard and mix
4. Pour the batter into a form and bake in a moderately hot oven
5. Melt the lard in the form in which it is to be baked

title: Placki Kukurydzane No. 2 (Corn Bread No. 2)
body:
## Ingredients

- half quart sour milk
- three eggs
- half teaspoon salt
- one cup corn meal
- one teaspoon soda
- hot water
- half cup butter

## Directions

1. Take half quart of sour milk, three well beaten eggs, half teaspoon of salt, one cup of corn meal, one teaspoon of soda dissolved in a small amount of hot water, and half cup of butter
2. Melt the butter in the form in which it is to be baked
3. Mix into smooth dough, pour into the form and bake

title: Placki Kukurydzane No. 3 (Corn Bread No. 3)
body:
## Ingredients

- one egg
- two heaping tablespoons sugar
- one large tablespoon butter
- one cup sour milk
- one cup corn meal
- one cup wheat flour
- one teaspoon soda
- salt to taste

## Directions

1. Beat one egg with two heaping tablespoons of sugar and one large tablespoon of butter
2. Add one cup of sour milk, one cup of corn meal, one cup of wheat flour, one teaspoon of soda, and salt to taste
3. Mix well and bake

title: Placki Kukurydzane No. 4 (Corn Bread No. 4)
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup corn meal
- one tablespoon sugar
- one large tablespoon butter
- three tablespoons cooked pumpkin
- two eggs
- pinch of salt
- one cup sour milk
- one teaspoon soda

## Directions

1. Mix properly one cup of corn meal, one tablespoon of sugar, one large tablespoon of butter, and three tablespoons of cooked pumpkin
2. Add two beaten eggs, a pinch of salt, one cup of sour milk, one teaspoon of soda dissolved in a small amount of warm water
3. Mix everything properly and place in the oven for half an hour

title: Placki Kukurydzane No. 5 (Corn Bread No. 5)
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup corn meal
- one cup wheat flour
- half teaspoon salt
- half teaspoon soda
- half cup finely chopped beef suet
- two cups sour milk

## Directions

1. Mix one cup of corn meal, one cup of wheat flour, half teaspoon of salt, and half teaspoon of soda
2. Add half cup of finely chopped beef suet and two cups of sour milk and mix perfectly
3. Pour into a form and place in a moderately hot oven for half an hour

title: Placki z Pasternaku (Parsnip Cakes)
body:
## Ingredients

- parsnip roots
