- one and a half cups cooked parsnip mass
- one egg
- one tablespoon flour
- salt
- pepper
- butter or fat for frying

## Directions

1. Cook parsnip roots until soft and then mash into a mass.
2. Take one and a half cups of this mass, add one egg, one tablespoon of flour, salt, and some pepper.
3. Mix well and form into thin cakes.
4. Fry on a pan in butter or fat, browning nicely on both sides.

title: Polewka Cebulowa (Onion Broth)
body:
## Ingredients

- four onions, finely chopped
- one large spoon of butter
- one and a half quarts of water
- salt and pepper to taste
- a handful of parsley
- one quart of milk
- one large spoon of cornmeal
- one spoon of cold water
- three spoons of butter

## Directions

1. Chop four onions finely and fry in one large spoon of butter, being careful however not to brown the onion, then add one and a half quarts of water, salt and pepper to taste and cook for an hour, together with a handful of parsley
2. Strain and add one quart of milk, beaten with one large spoon of cornmeal mixed in one spoon of cold water
3. Cook for about ten more minutes, and just before serving add three spoons of butter

title: Polewka Letnia (Summer Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- two large potatoes
- hot water
- two tomatoes
- one onion
- a handful of green corn kernels scraped with a knife
- two quarts of cold water
- one tablespoon of butter
- salt and pepper to taste
- flour (optional, for thickening)

## Directions

1. Peel and slice two large potatoes
2. Pour hot water over them and boil for no more than ten minutes, then strain the water
3. Chop two tomatoes and one onion finely, add a handful of green corn kernels scraped with a knife
4. Place along with the potatoes in a pot and pour two quarts of cold water over them
5. Boil until everything is soft, then press through a sieve
6. Pour the soup back into the pot, add one tablespoon of butter, salt and pepper to taste and serve
7. For a thicker soup, mix a small amount of flour with cold water and add to the pot

title: Polewka na Burakach (Beet Broth)
body:
## Ingredients

- several red beets
- half a quart of milk
- salt and pepper to taste
- one spoon of butter
- one spoon of flour
- half a cup of sour cream

## Directions

1. Grate several red beets on a grater and strain so as to extract all the juice, which pour into a pan and bring to a boil
2. Separately bring half a quart of milk to a boil and pour into the beet juice, also add salt and pepper to taste
3. Brown one spoon of butter with one spoon of flour in a pan and add gradually to the soup, stirring constantly, bring to a boil and serve
4. The soup will be more tasty by adding half a cup of sour cream

title: Polewka z Grzanek (Bread Crumb Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- quarter pound white bread
- butter
- flour
- water
- milk
- salt
- pepper
- two egg yolks

## Directions

1. Break quarter pound white bread into small pieces and fry in a pan with a large spoonful of butter
2. Add a grated spoonful of flour and continue frying, constantly stirring, until the flour is well browned
3. Bring a quart of water to boil and pour gradually into the pan, stirring quickly
4. Then add half quart of milk
5. Salt and add pepper to taste
6. Just before serving, add two well beaten egg yolks

title: Polewka z Kapusty (Cabbage Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- small cabbage head
- water
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Take a small cabbage head, remove all green leaves and chop the white ones, pour a quart of hot water over them and cook for half an hour

title: Polewka z Kapusty (Cabbage Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- one small head of cabbage
- one quart of water
- half quart of good hot milk
- salt and pepper to taste
- two tablespoons of butter
- one tablespoon of flour

## Directions

1. Take a small head of cabbage and remove all the green leaves, chop the white leaves and pour one quart of water over them
2. Boil for half an hour or a little longer, then strain
3. The strained cabbage is used as a vegetable
4. Pour half of the strained water back into the pot
5. Add half a quart of good hot milk, salt and pepper to taste
6. Brown two tablespoons of butter with one tablespoon of flour in a pan, then add to the soup
7. Mix well, boil for about five minutes and serve

title: Polewka z Piwa Zabielana (Beer Soup with Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart of beer
- piece of rye bread crust
- quarter cup of sour cream
- four egg yolks
- one tablespoon of cold beer
- one tablespoon of fine sugar
- bread cut into cubes and dried
- curd cheese
- a couple of tablespoons of sugar with cinnamon

## Directions

1. Bring to a boil under a cover one quart of beer with a piece of rye bread crust
2. Separately, beat in a pot a quarter cup of sour cream with four egg yolks, one tablespoon of cold beer, and one tablespoon of fine sugar
3. When the beer boils, pour it slowly, constantly stirring, into the pot with the sour cream and egg yolks
4. Place on fire so that it becomes warm, but does not boil
5. Pour into a bowl in which bread cut into cubes and dried, curd cheese, and a couple of tablespoons of sugar with cinnamon are placed first
6. If sour cream is lacking, one can make it with egg yolks alone, but then take more, namely six yolks for four persons

title: Polewka z Serwatki (Whey Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart of fresh whey
- a few tablespoons of wholemeal flour
- sour cream
- salt to taste
- croutons

## Directions

1. Boil 1 quart of fresh whey
2. Mix wholemeal flour with sour cream
3. Slowly add the cream mixture to the whey, constantly stirring
4. Add salt to taste
5. Serve with croutons

title: Polędwica Pieczona (Roast Tenderloin)
body:
## Ingredients

- tenderloin
- lard for larding
- butter

## Directions

1. Lard and stuff the tenderloin with lard
2. Place it on a baking dish with hot butter
3. At first, brown it on strong heat over an open flame, and when it becomes browned, place it in a hot oven
4. Various vegetables can be served with the tenderloin, though small potatoes fried are the best

title: Polędwica Pieczona (Roasted Tenderloin)
body:
## Ingredients

- tenderloin
- lard for larding
- hot butter
- various vegetables
- potatoes fried raw
- Italian pasta
- mushrooms

## Directions

1. Remove sinews and lard the tenderloin
2. Place on a roasting pan with hot butter
3. Initially sear on high heat over an open flame until browned
4. When browned, place in a hot oven
5. Serve with various vegetables, preferably raw fried potatoes, Italian pasta, and mushrooms

title: Polędwica w Cieście (Tenderloin in Pastry)
body:
## Ingredients

- tenderloin
- salt
- French pastry dough
- butter

## Directions

1. Remove sinews and clean the tenderloin of fat
2. Salt it and let it rest for one hour
3. Place in a very hot oven and bake for half an hour
4. Make French pastry and roll it thin
5. Cool the tenderloin so only the surface cools slightly while the inside remains hot
6. Wrap it entirely in pastry so no meat is visible anywhere
7. Place in a hot oven and when the pastry is well browned, the meat is also done
8. The tenderloin baked this way will be red inside

title: Pomidory Faszerowane (Stuffed Tomatoes)
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup finely grated bread crumbs
- one tablespoon finely chopped fresh parsley
- one tablespoon melted butter
- half a teaspoon salt
- two beaten eggs
- red pepper
- six large tomatoes

## Directions

1. Mix one cup of finely grated bread crumbs, one tablespoon of finely chopped fresh parsley, one tablespoon of melted butter, half a teaspoon of salt, two beaten eggs, and a pinch of red pepper into a smooth mass
2. Hollow out six large tomatoes with a spoon
3. Fill with the above mass
4. Bake in a moderately hot oven for twenty minutes

title: Pomidory Faszerowane Mięsem (Tomatoes Stuffed with Meat)
body:
## Ingredients

- large tomatoes
- cooked beef, pork, or both
- grated bread crumbs
- egg yolk
- salt
- pepper
- tomato pulp
- garlic, grated, optional
- butter or oil

## Directions

1. Cut off the tops of large tomatoes with a sharp knife
2. Remove the center with a spoon
3. Pass the removed mass through a sieve so that all the seeds remain on the sieve
4. Take cooked meat (which may be beef, pork, or both)
5. Add grated bread crumbs, egg yolk, salt and pepper to taste, the sieved tomato mass, and a pinch of grated garlic if desired
6. Fill the hollowed tomatoes with this mixture
7. Cover with the tops cut from the tomatoes
8. Place in a baking dish or form generously buttered or oiled
9. When well baked, serve at table

title: Pomidory Nadziewane Ogórkami (Tomatoes Stuffed with Cucumbers)
body:
## Ingredients

- tomatoes, one per person
- pair of cucumbers
- oil
- vinegar
- salt
- pepper
- lettuce leaves

## Directions

1. Pour boiling water over the tomatoes and peel off the skin
2. Scoop out the center of each tomato with a small spoon
3. Peel and chop the cucumbers not too finely
4. Make French sauce from oil, vinegar, salt and pepper
5. Mix the chopped cucumbers with the French sauce
6. Fill the hollowed tomatoes with this mixture
7. Pour the same French sauce over the top
8. Serve each tomato on its own small plate on a lettuce leaf

title: Poncz z Mleka (Milk Punch)
body:
## Ingredients

- zest from four large lemons (yellow part)
- one quart of brandy or rum
- juice of four lemons
- one and a half pounds of sugar in pieces
- two grated nutmegs
- one quart of water
- one quart of fresh milk

## Directions

1. Scrape the yellow part of zest from four large lemons and pour one quart of brandy or rum over it, then place in a cool place for a whole day
2. Add juice of four lemons, one and a half pounds of sugar in pieces, two grated nutmegs and one quart of water; boil one quart of fresh milk and also pour it in, then strain everything through cloth, cool and serve
3. This punch can be kept in corked bottles for several months without spoiling

title: Postna Zupa Amerykańska (American Meatless Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- three medium carrots
- same amount of turnips
- three onions
- sufficient amount of butter
- handful of finely chopped celery
- salt and pepper to taste
- two cloves
- some parsley
- pinch of grated nutmeg
- three quarts of water

## Directions

1. Peel and finely chop three medium carrots, the same amount of turnips, and three onions
2. Fry these vegetables in a pan with sufficient butter until everything is nicely browned
3. Toward the end of frying, also add a handful of finely chopped celery
4. Put into a pot and add salt and pepper to taste, as well as two cloves, some parsley, and a pinch of grated nutmeg
5. Pour in three quarts of water and cook over a low fire for three hours
6. Serve to the table

title: Potrawka Brazylijska (Brazilian Ragout)
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds calf meat, cut into small pieces
- one pound salted bacon, cut into slices
- water
- three tablespoons vinegar
- one or two onions, cut into slices
- one and a half pounds carrots, cut into small pieces
- salt and pepper to taste

## Directions

1. Place in a pot two pounds of calf meat cut into small pieces and one pound of salted bacon cut into slices
2. Pour four quarts of water and add three tablespoons of vinegar
3. Place on fire and cook for one hour
4. Skim well and add one or two onions cut into slices, with one and a half pounds of carrots cut into small pieces, salt and pepper to taste
5. Pour the meat along with the broth onto a baking sheet and place in a moderately hot oven for two hours

title: Potrawka Meksykańska (Mexican Stew)
body:
## Ingredients

- roasted chicken from previous day (or boiled chicken)
- chicken meat, skinned and boned
- raw ham, cut into small pieces with fat
- butter
- chopped green parsley
- pepper
- cooked rice
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Chop the chicken meat very finely.
2. For each cup of chicken meat, take half a cup of raw ham cut into small pieces with fat.
3. Fry the ham with a spoon of butter for a few minutes.
4. Add the chopped chicken meat, a teaspoon of chopped green parsley, and some pepper.
5. When everything is well mixed, add two large cups of cooked rice.
6. Keep on heat for a moment while stirring with a spoon until well heated.
7. Transfer to a platter and sprinkle with a small amount of lemon juice.

title: Potrawka z Baraniny (Lamb Stew)
body:
## Ingredients

- thin bacon slices
- various vegetables and roots
- bay leaf
- salt
- pepper
- lamb meat
- water
- vinegar
- flour
- broth

## Directions

1. Line the bottom of a braising pan with thin bacon slices. Then place various chopped vegetables, roots, a bay leaf, salt and pepper to taste, and on top place lamb meat.
2. Pour water halfway up, add a tablespoon of vinegar and stew on low heat until tender.
3. After cooking, remove the meat and arrange on a serving dish. Thicken the sauce with flour, thin with broth, bring to a boil once, strain and pour over the meat.

title: Potrawka z Cielęciny (Calf's Ragout)
body:
## Ingredients

- calf meat (breast is best)
- water
- vegetables (carrots, celery, etc.)
- salt
- butter
- flour
- fresh parsley, finely chopped
- asparagus or cauliflower, cooked and cut into small pieces
- crayfish necks
- calf's brains
- butter for frying
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Take a whole piece of calf meat, pour boiling water over it and bring to a boil
2. Immediately remove, rinse in cold water and cut into pieces
3. Place the cut meat in a pot, pour fresh hot water, add vegetables and salt
4. Cook until the meat is tender
5. When the meat is cooked, arrange on a serving platter and cover to keep warm
6. Bring one tablespoon of butter with one tablespoon of flour to a boil, dilute with the broth, add finely chopped green parsley
7. You can also add cooked and cut into small pieces asparagus or cauliflower, or crayfish necks
8. Pour this sauce over the arranged meat on the platter
9. To serve the ragout elegantly, you can make small delicate cutlets from calf's brains, fry them in butter until brown and arrange them around the ragout

title: Potrawka z Cielęciny po Indyjsku (Indian-Style Calf's Ragout)
body:
## Ingredients

- one medium onion
- butter
- two cups marinated tomatoes or fresh tomatoes
- one teaspoon curry powder
- two pounds calf meat, cut into pieces
- salt and pepper to taste
- three and a half cups boiling water
- flour
- rice

## Directions

1. Cut one medium onion into slices and place in a pot with one tablespoon of butter and brown it
2. Add two cups of marinated tomatoes or the same amount of fresh ones, one teaspoon of curry powder and two pounds of calf meat cut into pieces
3. Add salt and pepper to taste
4. Pour three and a half cups of boiling water and place on fire
5. Cook covered on low heat for one and a half hours
6. Then thicken with one tablespoon of flour mixed with a little water
7. Cook rice until thick
8. Pour the ragout onto a large serving platter and arrange rice around it

title: Potrawka z Gołębi (Pigeon Stew)
body:
## Ingredients

- fat pigeons
- butter
- onions
- sour apples
- mushrooms
- lemon juice
- broth or bouillon
- butter
- flour
- sour cream

## Directions

1. Clean and dress the fat pigeons, cut them into quarters, and place them in a pot with a generous amount of butter, then braise on low heat covered until soft
2. Remove the pigeons, and in the remaining butter in the pot, place several onions cut into thin slices and braise until soft, then add some chopped sour apples, a few mushrooms, lemon juice from half a lemon, and dilute with broth or bouillon, then bring to a boil
3. In a separate pot, brown 1 tablespoon of butter with 1 tablespoon of flour and dilute with the sauce, then add the pigeons which should be previously soaked in sour cream, cook for about ten minutes and serve

title: Potrawka z Główki Cielęcej (Ragout from Calf's Head)
body:
## Ingredients

- calf's head
- hot water
- vegetables (carrots, celery, etc.)
- butter
- flour
- broth
- sugar
- caramel
- lemon
- Madeira wine
- egg
- breadcrumbs
- parsley sprigs

## Directions

1. After carefully cleaning the calf's head of hair, split it in half
2. Remove the tongue and brains separately and cook them
3. Discard the eyes and jaws
4. Pour hot water and cook like any calf meat with the addition of vegetables
5. When the meat is tender, remove from broth, cut into nice pieces and arrange on a serving platter
6. Pour with the following sauce: brown one tablespoon of butter, brown with one tablespoon of flour, dilute with broth, add one teaspoon of sugar, a little caramel for color, squeeze half a lemon and pour one glass of Madeira wine
7. The brains should not be cooked, but right after scalding cut into small cutlets, dip in egg, sprinkle with grated breadcrumbs and fry in butter
8. Arrange these cutlets around the ragout and serve to the table

title: Potrawka z Jarzynami (Beef Stew with Vegetables)
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds lean beef without bone, or two and a half to three pounds with bone
- salt
- pepper
- flour
- half cup carrots
- half cup turnips
- one medium onion
- two cups potatoes
- water

## Directions

1. Wipe meat with damp cloth and cut into one-inch cubes
2. Sprinkle with salt, pepper and small amount of flour
3. If meat has fat, trim it with knife and render in pan, then add meat and fry on strong heat until slightly browned
4. Place everything in pot, cover with quart of water and cook one and a half hours on low heat
5. Cut half cup carrots and half cup turnips into half-inch cubes, slice one medium onion and add to meat, cook another half hour
6. Cut two cups potatoes into quarter-inch thick slices and cook separately in salted water until halfway cooked, then add to stew fifteen minutes before serving
7. Thicken stew with two spoonfuls of flour mixed previously in cold water, or omit if you prefer thinner sauce

title: Potrawka z Kaczki (Duck Stew)
body:
## Ingredients

- duck
- salt
- brown sauce, caper sauce, or gherkin sauce

## Directions

1. Prepare and clean the duck, then salt it and place in a hot oven and leave only until it browns on top
2. After roasting, slice into pieces, place in a saucepan, cover with brown sauce, caper sauce, or gherkin sauce, add the sauce that dripped during roasting and braise over low heat until tender
3. When serving, arrange the meat on a platter and cover with sauce

title: Potrawka z Kaczki z Jabłkami (Duck Stew with Apples)
body:
## Ingredients

- various vegetables like pasta
- duck, cut into pieces
- broth
- sour apples
- two large onions
- butter
- flour
- caramel

## Directions

1. Shred various vegetables like pasta, then blanch with boiling water, drain this water, then add fresh water and bring to a boil, then add the duck cut into pieces and cook
2. When the meat is almost cooked, peel and slice several sour apples and two large onions, cover with broth from the duck and braise under a cover in a separate pan until the apples and onions soften
3. When the duck is completely tender, remove it from the pan with the broth, in that same pan brown one spoon of butter with one spoon of flour, mix with the broth, color with caramel; strain the cooked apples with onion through a sieve and add to this sauce, mix well and pour over the duck arranged on a platter
4. If the sauce is too sour, add a little sugar

title: Potrawka z Kurcząt (Chicken Stew)
body:
## Ingredients

- Chicken
- Various vegetables and greens
- Water
- Flour
- Butter
- Green parsley, finely chopped
- Cauliflower and other vegetables

## Directions

1. Clean the chicken as usual and cut into quarters
2. Place in a pot, add various greens and vegetables, pour with water and boil until tender, which should take half an hour from the time it comes to a boil
3. When tender, work together one spoonful of flour with one spoonful of butter, dilute with broth from the chicken, add some finely chopped green parsley and boil until it thickens
4. Then add chopped cauliflower etc., warm together with the chicken and arrange on a serving platter

title: Potrawka z Kurcząt z Agrestem (Chicken Stew with Gooseberry)
body:
## Ingredients

- Chicken
- Vegetables and greens
- Water
- Unripe gooseberry (half a quart per two chickens), removed from stems
- Sugar
- Butter

## Directions

1. Prepare the chicken stew as above, but do not let it cook until very tender
2. For two chickens throw in half a quart of unripe gooseberry removed from stems and boil until tender
3. Then add one spoonful of sugar, place in one spoonful of butter and boil a little longer until the sauce thickens
4. Flour is not needed

title: Potrawka z Kurcząt z Garnka (Chicken Stew from the Pot)
body:
## Ingredients

- Chicken
- Various vegetables and greens
- Butter
- Mushrooms
- Salt
- Green parsley, chopped
- Flour
- Cooked vegetables

## Directions

1. Prepare and clean the chicken as usual, cut into quarters and place in a pot, preferably earthenware with a wide bottom
2. Add various vegetables and greens, one spoonful of butter, a few small mushrooms, add salt to taste, cover with a lid and stew very slowly, preferably not on fire but in a hot oven
3. After half an hour throw in chopped green parsley, shake the pot several times and stew further until tender, which altogether should take about an hour
4. Finally thicken the sauce with flour, bring to a boil, then pour out the chicken and arrange on a platter and pour over the sauce, and sprinkle on top with finely chopped cooked vegetables

title: Potrawka z Kury z Kluseczkami (Chicken Stew with Noodles)
body:
## Ingredients

- fat young chicken
- water
- mixed vegetables
- flour
- eggs

## Directions

1. Prepare a fat young chicken as usual and place it in a pot large enough to contain it whole.
2. Add enough water to cover the chicken and add mixed vegetables.
3. Cook covered for about two hours on low heat until tender.
4. Prepare noodles (kluseczki), preferably the day before, by rolling them very thin and cutting diagonally.
5. Dry them on a sieve.
6. Cook the noodles in the chicken broth without skimming the fat.
7. After removing the chicken, cut it into pieces.

title: Potrawka z Kury z Kluseczkami (Chicken Stew with Noodles)
body:
## Ingredients

- fat young chicken
- water
- mixed vegetables (włoszczyzna)
- flour dough for noodles
- green parsley

## Directions

1. Prepare the chicken as usual and place it in a pot so that it fits completely.
2. Pour enough water to cover the chicken and add mixed vegetables.
3. Cook covered on low heat for about two hours until tender.
4. Make noodles the day before, rolling them very thin and cutting them diagonally; dry them on a sieve.
5. Remove the chicken from the broth and cut into pieces.
6. Place the meat in the center of a platter, sprinkle with chopped green parsley.
7. Cook the noodles in the chicken broth without removing the fat.
8. Arrange the cooked noodles around the meat and pour the remaining broth over everything.
9. Note: Add enough water before cooking so that no additional water needs to be added during cooking. Remove the meat and strain the broth before returning it to the pot and adding the noodles.

title: Potrawka z Nerek Wołowych (Beef Kidney Stew)
body:
## Ingredients

- one beef kidney
- salted water
- one onion, finely chopped or grated
- pinch of sage
- one cup boiling water
- one tablespoon cornstarch
- salt and pepper to taste
- two hard-boiled eggs

## Directions

1. Chop one beef kidney into small pieces, place in a pan, cover with salted water, and cook for half an hour
2. Drain the kidneys on a colander
3. Return to the pan and add one finely chopped or grated onion, a pinch of sage, and one cup of boiling water
4. Bring to a boil, then thicken with one tablespoon of cornstarch
5. Add salt and pepper to taste and cook a few minutes more
6. Before serving, chop two hard-boiled eggs and stir into the stew

title: Potrawka z Nóżek Cielęcych (Ragout of Calf's Feet)
body:
## Ingredients

- calf's feet
- flour
- butter
- broth or strong bouillon
- sugar
- currants, washed and pitted
- lemon
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Cook the calf's feet in the same manner as for aspic and cut into pieces
2. Arrange the pieces on a serving platter
3. To prepare the gray sauce: brown one tablespoon of flour with one tablespoon of butter until it colors
4. Dilute with broth or strong bouillon and bring to a boil
5. Soak a few pieces of sugar in water, or use one tablespoon of regular sugar, brown it in a pan, pour into the sauce and add a handful of washed currants without pits
6. Squeeze in half a lemon, or pour in one tablespoon of good vinegar and bring together to a boil
7. Before serving, place a few thin slices of lemon in the sauce
8. Pour the sauce over the arranged meat on the platter

title: Potrawka z Nóżek Cielęcych (Veal Feet Stew)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal feet
- vegetables
- flour, one tablespoon
- butter, one tablespoon
- sugar
- currants
- salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Cook the veal feet the same way as for aspic and cut into pieces, arrange on a platter and pour with the following gray sauce:
2. Fry one tablespoon of flour with one tablespoon of butter until browned, dilute with broth or strong bouillon and bring to a boil.
3. Soak a few pieces of sugar in water or about one tablespoon of regular sugar, brown it in a pan, pour into the sauce and add a handful of cleaned dried currants without pits.

title: Potrawka z Ostryg (Oyster Stew)
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart oysters
- water
- butter
- crackers
- salt
- pepper
- cream

## Directions

1. Drain one quart of oysters through a colander, catching the sauce in a bowl
2. Add half a cup of water to the sauce, bring to boil and strain
3. Add one tablespoon butter and one heaping tablespoon crushed crackers, salt and pepper to taste
4. Place on fire and when it boils, add the oysters and simmer until the edges begin to curl
5. Add half a cup of boiled cream and serve immediately

title: Potrawka z Ostryg (Oyster Stew)
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart of oysters
- half a cup of water
- one tablespoon of butter
- one heaping tablespoon of crumbled crackers
- salt and pepper to taste
- half a cup of boiled cream

## Directions

1. Drain one quart of oysters in a colander, collecting the liquid in a bowl
2. Add half a cup of water to the oyster liquid, bring to a boil and strain
3. Add one tablespoon of butter and one heaping tablespoon of crumbled crackers, salt and pepper to taste
4. Place over heat and when it comes to a boil, add the oysters and cook until the edges begin to curl
5. Pour in half a cup of boiled cream and serve immediately

title: Potrawka z Pieczeni Cielęcej (Ragout from Leftover Roast Calf)
body:
## Ingredients

- leftover roast calf meat, cut into cubes
- one and a half cups calf meat
- three heaping tablespoons butter
- two heaping tablespoons flour
- one cup milk
- two hard-boiled eggs
- salt to taste
- white pepper
- red pepper

## Directions

1. Cut the leftover roast calf meat from the previous day into cubes
2. In a pot melt three heaping tablespoons of butter, brown in it two heaping tablespoons of flour and slowly add one cup of milk, constantly stirring
3. When it comes to a boil, add the calf meat, as well as two hard-boiled eggs with yolks and whites finely chopped and cut into pieces
4. Cook for three minutes, then season with salt to taste, add a little white pepper and a pinch of red pepper

title: Potrawka z Pieczeni z Agrestem (Stew of Leftover Roast with Gooseberries)
body:
## Ingredients

- leftover lamb roast from previous day
- butter
- flour
- sauce from remaining roast
- underripe gooseberries
- sugar
- or pickled cucumbers diced

## Directions

1. This stew is made from leftover lamb roast from the previous day. Melt a tablespoon of butter in a braising pan, brown it and thicken with a tablespoon of flour.
2. Add, stirring, the sauce from the remaining lamb roast, after collecting all the fat, bring to a boil and add a large cup of underripe gooseberries, cleaned of stems, and also a tablespoon of sugar and cook together.
3. Chop the lamb roast, place in the sauce, warm together and serve.
4. Instead of gooseberries one can equally well use pickled cucumbers cut into cubes.

title: Potrawka z Pieczonego Indyka (Stewed Roasted Turkey)
body:
## Ingredients

- leftover roasted turkey
- butter
- one cup broth or bouillon
- half a lemon
- pepper
- powdered ginger
- grated bread

## Directions

1. Slice the remaining roasted turkey into pieces
2. Place in a saucepan with one spoon of butter, one cup of broth or bouillon, squeeze half a lemon, add a little pepper and some powdered ginger, bring to a strong boil
3. Then add the meat and a handful of grated bread, heat well and serve

title: Potrawka z Pomidorami (Stew with Tomatoes)
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb breast or shoulder
- water
- mixed vegetables
- tomato sauce

## Directions

1. For this stew, lamb breast or shoulder is best. Chop the meat into pieces, pour water over, bring to a boil and skim.
2. Then drain this water, pour in fresh water, add various vegetables and cook until tender.
3. When the meat is completely tender, pour tomato sauce over it and stew for several more minutes on low heat.

title: Potrawka z Prosięcia (Piglet Stew)
body:
## Ingredients

- one piglet
- water
- one tablespoon flour
- one tablespoon butter
- vinegar
- caramel (for color)
- currants without pits (optional)
- sweet sliced almonds (optional)

## Directions

1. Clean the piglet and cut into pieces
2. Split the head crosswise, leaving the brain undisturbed in the upper part
3. Place in a pan, cover with water and cook until tender
4. Remove the meat
5. Make a roux by browning one tablespoon of flour and one tablespoon of butter in a pan
6. Add a few tablespoons of vinegar, a little caramel for color, and if desired some large pitted currants and sweet sliced almonds
7. Boil everything together
8. Add the meat and adjust the seasoning

title: Potrawka z Prosięcia (Pork Stew)
body:
## Ingredients

- cleaned piglet, cut into pieces
- head of piglet
- tablespoon of flour
- tablespoon of butter
- several tablespoons of vinegar
- caramel for color
- large raisins without seeds
- sweet almonds, sliced
- water
- salt and pepper to taste

## Directions

1. Clean the piglet and cut it into pieces; split the head across, leaving the brain untouched in the upper part.
2. Place in a pot, cover with water and cook until tender.
3. Remove the meat from the pot.
4. In the pot, brown a tablespoon of flour and a tablespoon of butter together.
5. Add several tablespoons of vinegar, a small amount of caramel for color, and if desired, large raisins without seeds and sliced sweet almonds.
6. Boil everything together, then add the meat.
7. Add the broth remaining from cooking the piglet and bring to a boil.
8. Arrange on a serving dish, garnish with lemon slices, pour sauce over it and serve.

title: Potrawka z Suszonej Wołowiny (Dried Beef Stew)
body:
## Ingredients

- one half pound dried beef
- one teaspoon butter
- one teaspoon flour
- two small cups milk
- chopped fresh parsley

## Directions

1. Cut the dried beef into thin slices
2. Tear into small pieces and chop finely
3. Melt one teaspoon of butter in a pan
4. Fry with one teaspoon of flour until the flour browns
5. Pour two small cups of milk onto the pan
6. Stir and bring to a boil
7. Add the chopped meat to the boiling milk
8. Stew on low heat until everything thickens slightly
9. Add chopped fresh parsley
10. Serve on the table
11. This stew is served for breakfast in American homes

title: Potrawka z Wołowej Wątroby (Beef Liver Stew)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound beef liver
- one large tablespoon butter
- one cup milk
- one teaspoon cornstarch
- one half teaspoon salt
- a pinch of red pepper
- one teaspoon chopped fresh parsley
- one teaspoon sharp tomato sauce (optional)

## Directions

1. Cut the beef liver into half-inch cubes
2. Melt a large tablespoon of butter in a pan
3. Add the liver and fry until well browned
4. Heat one cup of milk
5. Dissolve one teaspoon of cornstarch in a small amount of water
6. Add the cornstarch mixture to the hot milk
7. Add one half teaspoon salt, a pinch of red pepper, and one teaspoon chopped fresh parsley
8. Cook until the mixture becomes smooth
9. Pour the sauce over the liver arranged on a platter
10. If desired, add one teaspoon of sharp tomato sauce

title: Potrawka z Zająca lub Królika (Hare or Rabbit Stew)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 hare or rabbit
- water
- salt
- pepper
- 1 onion, sliced
- 1 cup milk
- 1 tablespoon butter

## Directions

1. Quarter the hare or rabbit and cook until soft in enough water to barely cover the meat, with the addition of salt, pepper, and one onion sliced into pieces
2. When the meat is soft, place on a platter; to the sauce remaining in the pot add 1 cup of milk with 1 tablespoon of butter, bring to a boil for about three minutes, and pour this sauce over the hare on the platter

title: Powidła Pigwowe (Quince Paste)
body:
## Ingredients

- quinces
- sugar in the proportion of one and a half quarts per quart of grated mass

## Directions

1. Peel and grate the quinces on a grater
2. Take one and a half quarts of sugar for each quart of grated mass
3. First make a syrup from the sugar with the addition of the smallest amount of boiling water
4. Then pour in the grated quinces and boil on a strong fire until the mass thickens
5. For six quarts of this paste, one bunch of quinces is required

title: Powidła z Daktyli (Date Paste)
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds of dates
- two cups of water
- one pound of sugar
- a piece of butter
- one quarter of grated nutmeg

## Directions

1. Pit two pounds of dates
2. Add two cups of water, one pound of sugar, a piece of butter and one quarter of grated nutmeg
3. Mix everything and place on the fire
4. Boil, stirring frequently, until a thick mass forms
5. Fill glasses and cover with buttered paper

title: Powidła z Fig (Fig Paste)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of peeled and carefully washed figs
- one cup of water
- half a pound of sugar
- half of grated nutmeg

## Directions

1. Chop very finely one pound of peeled and carefully washed figs
2. Add one cup of water, half a pound of sugar and half of grated nutmeg
3. Place in a saucepan and cook to a thick mass
4. Fill small glasses, seal tightly and store

title: Powidła z Rodzenków (Currant Paste)
body:
## Ingredients

- one large pound of currants without seeds
- one cup of water
- half a pound of sugar
- half of grated nutmeg

## Directions

1. Carefully sort and wash one large pound of currants without seeds
2. Chop them, add one cup of water, half a pound of sugar and half of grated nutmeg
3. Mix everything and boil until it breaks down into a thick mass
4. Fill glasses and seal tightly

title: Prażucha (Browned Buckwheat and Wheat Flour Porridge)
body:
## Ingredients

- half quart of buckwheat flour
- half quart of wheat flour
- one quart of milk
- salt
- lard

## Directions

1. Pour half quart of buckwheat flour and half quart of wheat flour onto a skillet
2. Place on very low heat and stir constantly with a spoon until the flour browns well
3. Then sift it and crush any lumps with a spoon
4. Bring one quart of milk to a boil in a small pot
5. Pour the browned flour into the boiling milk, salt it and cook briefly on low heat, stirring constantly, until the mass stops sticking to the pot
6. Remove from heat and shape on a platter using a spoon dipped in lard
7. Pour fried bacon over it

title: Prosię Pieczone (Roasted Piglet)
body:
## Ingredients

- one piglet
- salt
- butter (for the first stuffing method)
- pork lungs
- young pork fat
- raw liver
- three beaten eggs
- salt and pepper
- one large baked and grated onion
- one-half pound grated breadcrumbs
- a pinch of marjoram
- currants (optional)
- buckwheat groats (for second stuffing method)
- water
- pork fat (for second stuffing method)
- salt and pepper
- marjoram
- lard or pork fat (for basting)

## Directions

1. Clean the piglet and soak in cold water for one hour
2. Remove from water and rub with salt on the surface
3. Prepare the first stuffing: boil the piglet's lungs with a piece of young pork fat and mince as finely as possible together with raw liver
4. Add two tablespoons of melted butter, three beaten eggs, salt and pepper to taste, one large baked and grated onion, one-half pound of grated breadcrumbs, and a pinch of marjoram
5. Mix everything well; add a handful of small currants if desired
6. Alternative second stuffing: for a small piglet, take one-half quart of buckwheat groats, place in a clay pot and cover with the same amount of boiling water
7. Add one-half pound of pork fat cut into small cubes and fried; salt and mix well
8. Place on hot heat until the groats begin to thicken, then place the pot in a moderately hot oven for one-half hour
9. Cook the lungs until tender and the liver only briefly, then mince both together very finely
10. Add salt, pepper and a pinch of grated marjoram, mix with the groats
11. Stuff the piglet with either mixture and sew
12. Place in the oven on a baking pan, baste with lard and turn so it browns evenly on all sides

title: Prosię w Galarecie (Piglet in Aspic)
body:
## Ingredients

- young piglet, carefully cleaned
- mixed vegetables
- roots
- onion
- pepper
- several tablespoons of strong vinegar
- salt to taste
- egg whites
- caramel
- vinegar and oil for serving
- grated horseradish with sour cream for serving

## Directions

1. Carefully clean the young piglet and cut it into pieces as for a stew.
2. Place in a pot and bring to a boil.
3. After boiling and careful skimming, add mixed vegetables, some roots, onion, pepper, several tablespoons of strong vinegar, and salt to taste.
4. Cook until the meat is tender.
5. When the meat is tender, remove all pieces.
6. Clarify the remaining sauce with egg whites, cool slightly, and pour over the meat arranged in a salad bowl, making sure no bones protrude.
7. Cool completely.
8. To make the aspic a yellow color, tint it with caramel.
9. Serve at table with vinegar and oil, or with grated horseradish with sour cream.

title: Pszenne Muffins (Wheat Muffins)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 egg
- 1 large tablespoon butter
- 0.5 teaspoon salt
- 1 cup sour milk
- 1.5 cups wheat flour

## Directions

1. Beat 1 egg, 1 large tablespoon butter, 0.5 teaspoon salt, 1 cup sour milk, and 1.5 cups wheat flour together well.
2. Pour into forms and bake.

title: Pulpety do Barszczu (Dumplings for Borscht)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound cooked meat left over from broth from the previous day
- 1/4 pound fresh beef suet from the kidney
- 1/4 pound grated roll
- one baked and finely chopped onion
- salt and pepper to taste

## Directions

1. Chop the cooked meat and 1/4 pound fresh beef suet from the kidney very finely
2. Add 1/4 pound grated roll, one baked and finely chopped onion, salt and pepper to taste
3. Mix everything thoroughly in a bowl, then lay out on a board sprinkled with flour
4. Work this mass into round rolls, flatten them a little with your hand and place them in boiling water in a deep pan or shallow pot, and cook covered
5. After cooking, place on a colander and when they have drained from water, cut them diagonally with a knife into one-inch pieces
6. Place them in a bowl and pour borscht over them

title: Pulpety Smażone (Fried Dumplings)
body:
## Ingredients

- any cold beef, which can be roast or cooked beef
- a considerable amount of grated onion
- salt and pepper to taste
- a couple of tablespoons of broth or any soup
- one tablespoon of flour
- one egg
- hot lard for frying

## Directions

1. Chop any cold beef finely, add a considerable amount of grated onion, salt and pepper to taste
2. Add a couple of tablespoons of broth or any soup, place on heat, thicken with one tablespoon of flour and cook for five minutes
3. Remove from heat and when it cools, make balls the size of a walnut, dip them in egg and fry in hot lard until brown
4. Then add them to soup

title: Pulpety Smażone (Fried Meatballs)
body:
## Ingredients

- cold cooked beef (roast or boiled beef)
- grated onion, a generous amount
- salt and pepper to taste
- a couple of tablespoons of broth or soup
- a tablespoon of flour
- egg
- hot lard for frying

## Directions

1. Mince the cold cooked beef into a mass
2. Add a generous amount of grated onion, salt and pepper to taste
3. Add a couple of tablespoons of broth or soup, place on heat
4. Thicken with a tablespoon of flour and cook for five minutes
5. Remove from heat and let cool
6. Shape into balls the size of an Italian walnut
7. Dip in egg and fry in hot lard until browned
8. Serve in soup

title: Pulpety Smażone z Szynki (Fried Ham Patties)
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup finely minced cooked ham
- one egg
- one cup any meat broth
- one-quarter teaspoon dry mustard (ground mustard)
- one teaspoon Worcestershire sauce
- one tablespoon butter
- one teaspoon flour
- one egg (for coating)
- flour for the batter
- two tablespoons melted butter (for batter)
- one cup warm water (for batter)
- hot fat for frying

## Directions

1. Heat the broth and add flour mixed with butter
2. Add the minced ham and remaining ingredients and bring to a boil
3. Pour in one beaten egg, mix well and remove from heat to cool
4. When cooled completely, form small balls from the mixture
5. Dip each ball separately in thin batter
6. Fry in hot fat in a pan; use plenty of fat
7. For the batter: mix one cup of flour with two tablespoons of melted butter, add one egg and one cup of warm water

title: Pulpety z Kartofli (Potato Meatballs)
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked potatoes boiled in salted water
- butter
- salt
- pepper
- celery salt
- one beaten egg yolk
- fresh parsley, finely chopped
- beaten egg
- grated bread crumbs
- hot fat for frying

## Directions

1. Mash cooked potatoes that were boiled in salted water while they are still hot
2. For every half-quart of potatoes, add one tablespoon butter, half a teaspoon salt, some pepper, and some celery salt
3. Mix everything well and set aside to cool
4. When the mass has cooled, add one beaten egg yolk and mix well in a bowl
5. Add one teaspoon of finely chopped parsley and mix
6. Form the mass into balls or small sticks
7. Dip in beaten egg
8. Coat with grated bread crumbs and fry in hot fat
9. Fry for only one minute
10. Arrange on a serving dish in the shape of a pyramid and serve immediately

title: Pulpety z Kartofli (Potato Meatballs)
body:
## Ingredients

- several cold cooked potatoes
- a tablespoon of fresh parsley
- two egg yolks and one egg white
- grated bread crumbs
- hot fat for frying

## Directions

1. Grate the cold cooked potatoes into a mass
2. For half a quarter of potatoes, add a tablespoon of fresh parsley, two egg yolks and one egg white
3. Beat into a smooth mass
4. Shape small balls from the mixture
5. Roll in grated bread crumbs
6. Fry until browned in hot fat
7. Place in a bowl and pour soup over them

title: Pulpety z Mięsa (Meat Dumplings)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pound meat
- a piece of beef suet the size of a chicken egg, preferably from the kidney
- roll soaked in water
- salt and pepper to taste
- chopped green parsley
- a pinch of nutmeg
- two eggs

## Directions

1. Chop any tender meat very finely
2. Take a piece of beef suet the size of a chicken egg, preferably from the kidney, chop it very finely and then beat it into a mass
3. Add the roll soaked in water, salt and pepper to taste, chopped green parsley and a pinch of nutmeg, and beat in two eggs
4. Mix everything well, dust your hands with flour and make small balls the size of a walnut from this mass
5. Cook them in water
6. These dumplings are usually served with borscht

title: Pulpety z Móżdżku (Brain Dumplings)
body:
## Ingredients

- one calf's brain
- one tablespoon of butter
- four egg yolks
- salt and pepper to taste
- fat for frying

## Directions

1. Take one calf's brain, remove the membrane and cook it in salted water with the addition of a few tablespoons of vinegar
2. Beat one tablespoon of butter in a mortar until creamy, then add the brain cut into pieces and stripped of veins and beat together well
3. Beat in the four egg yolks one at a time, beating well after each, along with salt and pepper to taste
4. Beat the remaining four egg whites into a stiff foam, mix with the beaten mass and beat everything again until thick
5. Heat a considerable amount of fat in a pan, take on a spoon as round pieces of mass as possible and throw them into the fat
6. When the dumplings brown, remove them with a spoon and place in a bowl already filled with soup
7. The dumplings should not be placed in an empty bowl because they will be squashed

title: Pulpety z Ryb (Fish Meatballs)
body:
## Ingredients

- any fish
- salt and pepper to taste
- a small spoon of butter
- one grated onion
- milk
- one bread roll
- flour for dusting
- water for cooking

## Directions

1. Remove skin from the fish and extract the meat
2. Chop the meat, add salt and pepper to taste
3. Melt a small spoon of butter in a pan, when it boils add one grated onion
4. Boil again and remove from heat
5. Soak one bread roll in milk and squeeze it out
6. Add to the chopped fish, chop everything again, mix well and place on a work surface
7. Dust with flour and shape into finger-length cylinders
8. Drop into boiling water and cook for about twenty minutes
9. After cooking, drain on a colander and cool
10. Cut into thick slices and place in a bowl before pouring soup over them
11. These same meatballs can also be served separately as a dish with small noodles and white cream sauce

title: Pulpety z Szynki (Ham Dumplings)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 cup grated roll or white bread
- 1/2 cup milk
- one egg white
- two tablespoons of butter
- some chopped parsley
- a lot of pepper
- 2/3 cup cooked ham

## Directions

1. Cook 1/2 cup grated roll or white bread in 1/2 cup milk into a mass
2. Remove from heat and add one egg white beaten into a stiff foam, two tablespoons of butter, some chopped parsley and a lot of pepper
3. Chop 2/3 cup cooked ham finely, mix with the previously made mass and beat or chop everything into a mass
4. Press this mass through a fine sieve
5. Make oblong dumplings from this mass and cook them in soup

title: Pulpety z Wątróbki (Liver Dumplings)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound cooked calf's liver
- 1/4 pound finely chopped beef suet from the tenderloin
- 1/2 quart grated roll
- two eggs
- salt and pepper to taste
- some chopped green parsley

## Directions

1. Grate the cooked calf's liver and press it through a sieve
2. Add to it 1/4 pound finely chopped beef suet from the tenderloin, 1/2 quart grated roll, two eggs, salt and pepper to taste and some chopped green parsley
3. Mix everything well, lay out on a board and dust your hands with flour
4. Make round dumplings the size of a walnut
5. Place them in boiling broth or water and cook for three minutes
6. Remove with a spoon and place in a bowl

title: Pulpety z Łoju (Veal Fat Dumplings)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pound fresh beef suet, preferably from the tenderloin
- 1/2 quart grated roll
- one tablespoon of broth
- two whole eggs
- finely chopped fresh parsley
- a pinch of nutmeg

## Directions

1. Chop the fresh beef suet very finely, remove all veins and membranes, then beat it in a mortar into a mass
2. Add 1/2 quart grated roll, one tablespoon of broth, two whole eggs, some finely chopped fresh parsley and a pinch of nutmeg and mix everything well
3. Dust your hands with flour and make round balls the size of a walnut from this mass
4. Cook them in broth

title: Pulpety ze Szpiku (Marrow Dumplings)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 pound marrow
- two whole eggs
- 1/4 pound finely grated and sifted roll
- pepper and salt to taste

## Directions

1. Beat the marrow well in a mortar until it is similar to sour cream
2. Beat in two whole eggs one at a time, beat everything well together
3. Then gradually sprinkle in 1/4 pound finely grated and sifted roll, add pepper and salt to taste and mix well
4. Lay this mass out on a board and roll out a long piece
5. Cut it into pieces the size of a walnut, then shape each piece until it is round
6. Throw into boiling water
7. When cooked, remove carefully with a spoon and place in a bowl filled with soup
8. These dumplings cannot be placed in an empty bowl because they will stick together
9. If the dumplings are to be served with borscht or sorrel soup, they can be cooked in the soup

title: Pulpety ze Szpinaku (Spinach Dumplings)
body:
## Ingredients

- some spinach
- grated roll in equal amount to the spinach
- salt to taste
- a pinch of regular pepper and red pepper
- egg white

## Directions

1. Cook some spinach in salted water, chop it very finely and add the same amount of grated roll
2. Season well with salt and add a pinch of regular pepper and red pepper
3. Add enough egg white to dampen the mass well, then let it stand for fifteen minutes
4. Make balls the size of a hazelnut and cook them in soup
5. Cook for five minutes
6. Serve three or four dumplings per soup bowl

title: Pure z Jablek (Apple Puree)
body:
## Ingredients

- several sour apples
- water
- sugar to taste
- one egg white for every six apples
- cream for serving

## Directions

1. Wash several sour apples carefully, add a little water in a pot and cook until completely soft
2. Drain the water and press the apples through a sieve
3. Add sugar to taste to the resulting mass and mix well, beat one egg white to stiff peaks for every six apples and also mix with the apple mass, then place on ice and leave until serving time
4. Serve cold with cream

title: Puree Clamart (Clamart Puree)
body:
## Ingredients

- handful of cooked asparagus
- small can of marinated green peas
- one onion
- a couple of potatoes
- half a quart of water
- salt and pepper to taste
- two tablespoons of butter
- three egg yolks
- milk if needed

## Directions

1. Take a handful of cooked asparagus (may be leftovers from the previous day), a small can of marinated green peas, one onion, and a couple of potatoes
2. Pour in half a quart of water and cook until everything is completely soft
3. Strain through a sieve; if the soup is too thick, add milk
4. Add salt and pepper to taste, two tablespoons of butter, and three egg yolks
5. Do not cook further after adding the yolks
6. Serve with croutons cut into cubes

title: Puree Maigre (Meatless Puree)
body:
## Ingredients

- one onion
- handful of white cabbage
- water
- half a cup of rice
- one small turnip
- one carrot
- a couple of tablespoons of celery
- one tablespoon of green parsley
- one quart of cold water
- one cup of tomatoes from a can or fresh
- pinch of baking soda
- one tablespoon of flour
- one tablespoon of butter
- salt and pepper to taste
- one cup of good milk

## Directions

1. Peel and finely chop one onion, finely chop a handful of white cabbage, pour water over them and cook for twenty minutes, then drain the water
2. Rinse half a cup of rice and soak in cold water for two hours
3. Peel and grate one small turnip and one carrot on a grater
4. Chop a couple of tablespoons of celery and one tablespoon of green parsley
5. Pour all of the above with one quart of cold water, place on fire and cook for one hour from when it comes to a boil
6. Take one cup of tomatoes from a can, or the same amount of fresh ones, add to the soup and cook for another half hour
7. Strain everything through a fine sieve or colander
8. Pour back into the pot, place on the fire, add a pinch of baking soda, mix one tablespoon of flour with one tablespoon of butter and bring to a boil
9. Add salt and pepper to taste, and after five minutes remove from heat, add one cup of good milk, and serve to the table

title: Puree z Grochu (Pea Puree)
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart of dried peas
- soft water
- one tablespoon butter
- one blanched onion
- a little parsley
- one tablespoon good butter
- a quarter tablespoon flour
- one cup sweet cream
- salt and pepper to taste

## Directions

1. Pour one quart of ordinary dried peas into a pan, cover with soft water and bring to a boil. After skimming, drain the water, rinse the peas in fresh water and then cover with another hot water and cook until completely soft.
2. During cooking add one tablespoon of butter, one blanched onion and a little parsley.
3. When the peas are well cooked, mash them with a roller, then press through a fine sieve; if too thick, add some hot water.
4. Place one tablespoon of good butter in a pan and brown lightly with a quarter tablespoon of flour, and when it thickens, dilute with one cup of sweet cream, add to the pressed peas, mix well, heat on hot fire and serve.

title: Puree z Jarzyn (Vegetable Puree Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- one large onion
- two potatoes
- butter
- two tablespoons washed rice
- one carrot
- broth or water
- two tablespoons corn starch
- milk
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Chop finely one large onion and two potatoes.
2. Brown the onion in a pan with two spoons of butter and place in a pot.
3. Add the potatoes, two spoons of washed rice, one finely chopped carrot.
4. Pour in one quart of broth or water and simmer until all vegetables are tender.
5. Press everything through a sieve and pour back into the pot.
6. Mix two spoons of corn starch in half a cup of milk and pour into the soup.
7. Season with salt and pepper to taste and serve.

title: Puree z Kalafiorów (Cauliflower Puree Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- ordinary broth from beef bones, veal, or offal
- vegetables for broth
- two large cauliflowers
- cold water
- salt
- flour
- butter
- egg yolks
- cold water

## Directions

1. Cook ordinary broth, which may be made from beef bones, veal, or offal.
2. After the broth is cooked and vegetables added in whole form, take two large cauliflowers, clean them, place in a pot, cover with cold water, salt, and bring to a boil once, then drain the water.
3. Break the cauliflowers into pieces and place in the broth, cooking until completely tender.
4. Remove all the vegetables from the broth.
5. Press the broth together with the cauliflower through a sieve or colander, then pour back into the pot and bring to a boil.
6. Brown a spoon of flour with a spoon of butter and thicken the boiling soup with this mixture.
7. Beat two egg yolks well with a spoon of cold water, pour into a serving bowl and onto this slowly pour the hot soup, stirring constantly with a spoon.
8. This soup is usually served with small pastries or croutons with cheese.

title: Puree z Kartofli (Potato Puree)
body:
## Ingredients

- mealy potatoes
- salted water
- cold water
- one cup milk
- salt
- one tablespoon butter

## Directions

1. Peel and cook mealy potatoes in salted water
2. Cover the potatoes with cold water, never hot
3. After cooking drain the water and place the potatoes on the heat for a few minutes to dry
4. Then rub them to a paste
5. For each quart of such potatoes heat one cup of milk with salt and one tablespoon of butter
6. When the milk is hot, but should not boil, pour it over the potatoes and beat strongly with a spoon for at least three minutes until the potatoes become well white
7. Then serve at table
8. The method above is American; the European method differs in that instead of milk sour cream is used, not heated, and the pot with potatoes during beating is placed in another pot filled with hot water

title: Puree z Kartofli (Potato Puree)
body:
## Ingredients

- four large potatoes
- one onion
- a couple of celery stalks
- a couple of sprigs of parsley
- two quarts of water
- salt and pepper to taste
- one tablespoon of flour
- two heaping tablespoons of good butter
- one cup of good milk

## Directions

1. Cook four large potatoes, one onion, a couple of celery stalks, and a couple of sprigs of parsley in two quarts of water
2. Strain through a sieve
3. Pour back into a pot, add salt and pepper to taste, and one tablespoon of flour mixed with two heaping tablespoons of good butter
4. Pour one cup of good milk into a bowl first, then pour the soup into it

title: Puree z Kury (Chicken Puree Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- leftover cooked chicken from previous day
- rice, cooked
- butter or fat
- flour
- water
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Remove meat from bones and chop into small pieces.
2. For each cup of meat, take one cup of cooked rice and mash together into a paste.
3. Place the chicken mixture in a pan with a large spoon of butter or fat and brown it.
4. Sprinkle a spoon of dry flour and fry until the flour is lightly browned.
5. Pour in two quarts of boiling water and add the rice.
6. Season with salt and pepper to taste and simmer over low heat until everything is cooked enough to pass through a fine sieve.
7. Press through a fine sieve.
8. Pour back into the pan and bring to a boil, adding a little boiling water if the soup is too thick.

title: Puree z Marchwi (Carrot Puree Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- several carrots
- butter
- broth, cooked previously
- sour cream
- egg yolks
- salt
- pepper
- bread cubes for croutons

## Directions

1. Scrape several carrots, chop finely and braise in butter for several minutes.
2. Pour in previously cooked broth and cook until tender.
3. Strain the broth and press the remaining carrot through a sieve.
4. Mix with sour cream beaten with egg yolks, then add to the broth.
5. Mix well, bring to a boil and serve at table with croutons.
6. Alternatively, this soup may be thickened with cooked rice instead of sour cream.

title: Puree z Marchwi (Carrot Puree)
body:
## Ingredients

- peeled and cooked carrots
- milk
- salt
- flour
- butter
- pepper

## Directions

1. Pass peeled and cooked-until-tender carrots through a sieve.
2. For half a quart of this puree take a cup of milk and some salt.
3. Mix and place on the fire; when it begins to boil, add two tablespoons of flour mixed with a small amount of cold milk.
4. Cook for several minutes, constantly stirring.
5. At the end add a teaspoon of butter and a pinch of pepper and serve.

title: Puree z Zielonego Groszku (Green Pea Puree Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart shelled green peas
- water
- beef broth, cooked previously
- butter
- flour
- egg yolks
- small bread cubes
- butter for frying

## Directions

1. Pour water over one quart of shelled peas and cook until tender.
2. Press through a fine sieve and dilute with previously cooked beef broth.
3. After stirring well, thicken the soup with a spoon of butter rubbed with half a spoon of flour and bring to a boil.
4. Remove from heat, beat two egg yolks and mix into the soup.
5. Pour into a serving bowl.
6. Serve separately with small bread cubes fried in butter.

title: Puree ze Sztokfisza i Kartofli (Stockfish and Potato Puree)
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 medium potatoes
- 1 tablespoon butter
- 1 cup hot milk
- salt and pepper to taste
- 1 tablespoon butter
- 1 tablespoon flour
- 1/2 quart milk
- 1 slice onion
- 1/2 quart previously cooked and finely crumbled stockfish

## Directions

1. Cook 6 medium potatoes and grate them well
2. Add 1 tablespoon of butter while hot, 1 cup of hot milk, salt and pepper to taste
3. Beat the mixture well and spread it on a serving platter so that there is only a thin layer of potatoes in the middle of the platter but considerably more around the edges, forming a wall around the platter
4. Bring 1/2 quart of milk to a boil with one slice of onion
5. Mix 1 tablespoon of butter with 1 tablespoon of flour, then add 1 tablespoon of hot milk, mix properly and pour into the boiling milk
6. Add 1/2 quart of previously cooked and finely crumbled stockfish to this milk, stir and cook for 10 minutes from the moment it boils
7. Pour onto the platter lined with potatoes
8. The potato walls must be high enough that the fish with milk cannot spill over them

title: Pyzy (Polish Potato and Dough Dumplings)
body:
## Ingredients

- half quart of wheat flour
- one cup of milk
- one packet of yeast
- two egg yolks
- one whole egg
- half tablespoon of butter
- salt

## Directions

1. Mix half quart of wheat flour with one cup of milk and one packet of yeast
2. When it rises, beat in two egg yolks and one whole egg
3. Add half tablespoon of butter and salt to taste
4. Mix well
5. When it begins to rise again, turn out onto a floured work surface
6. Stretch by hand to the thickness of an inch
7. Cut out round discs with a glass as if making doughnuts
8. Spread on the work surface and when they rise, drop into boiling water
9. Cook uncovered and when they float to the surface, remove with a slotted spoon
10. Pour melted butter or fried bacon over them and serve immediately on the table, as they will fall; when eating do not cut them with a knife as they will become tough, but tear them with a fork

title: Pâté z Ostryg (Oyster Pâté)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a quart of oysters
- one cup of milk
- one cup of cold milk
- one cup of grated bread
- one tablespoon of butter
- pepper

## Directions

1. Bring half a quart of oysters to a boil with one cup of milk
2. Mix one cup of cold milk with one cup of grated bread
3. Pour this mixture into the oysters as soon as they come to a boil
4. Stir and cook for two minutes
5. Remove from heat and add one tablespoon of butter and some pepper
6. Pour into a buttered cake mold and bake in the oven until well browned

title: Pączki z Kartofli (Potato Fritters)
body:
## Ingredients

- three tablespoons flour
- half a teaspoon baking powder
- salt
- three large cooked potatoes
- two beaten eggs
- one tablespoon chopped fresh parsley
- hot fat for frying

## Directions

1. Sift together three tablespoons flour, half a teaspoon baking powder, and salt to mix well
2. Grate three large cooked potatoes on a grater
3. Add the grated potatoes to the flour
4. Add two beaten eggs and one tablespoon of chopped fresh parsley
5. Mix everything into a dough
6. Take small spoonfuls and fry in hot fat, using plenty of fat in the pan
7. After frying, place on paper to absorb the fat

title: Pączki z Sera (Cheese Fritters)
body:
## Ingredients

- three ounces flour
- one ounce butter
- half teaspoon salt
- pinch of red pepper
- half cup water
- three ounces grated cheese
- one egg yolk
- one egg white
- fat for frying

## Directions

1. Rub together in a bowl three ounces of flour with one ounce of butter, with the addition of half a teaspoon of salt and a pinch of red pepper.
2. Slowly pour in half a cup of water, stirring constantly, then add three ounces of grated cheese mixed with one egg yolk.
3. Beat the egg white to stiff peaks and mix with the dough at the very end.
4. Take spoonfuls and drop into hot fat, which must be abundant, and fry until golden, the same way as regular fritters.

title: Pączki z Sera (Cheese Puffs)
body:
## Ingredients

- three ounces flour
- one ounce butter
- half teaspoon salt
- pinch of red pepper
- half cup water
- three ounces grated cheese
- one egg yolk
- one egg white

## Directions

1. Mix flour with butter in a bowl
2. Add salt and red pepper
3. Gradually pour in half cup water while stirring constantly
4. Mix in grated cheese combined with one egg yolk
5. Beat egg white until stiff and fold into the mixture at the very end
6. Scoop with a spoon and drop into boiling hot fat
7. Fry until golden brown like doughnuts

title: Pączki ze Śniegiem (Donuts with Snow)
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup milk
- two cups flour
- one-half teaspoon salt
- one cup dry clean snow
- lard for frying

## Directions

1. Mix one cup milk with two cups flour to make a thick smooth dough
2. Add one-half teaspoon salt and one cup dry clean snow and mix quickly with the dough
3. Heat plenty of lard in a pan until very hot
4. Using a spoon, drop dough into the hot lard and fry until the donuts brown well
5. Keep the bowl of dough in a cold place while frying
6. The snow makes the donuts very puffy, light, and better than any other method

title: Quenelle z Szynką (Quenelle with Ham)
body:
## Ingredients

- one and one-half cups finely minced cooked ham
- one and one-half cups cooked potatoes sliced thin
- one tablespoon butter
- one tablespoon flour
- one-half quarter of cream
- pepper
- two eggs
- grated breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Mince the ham very finely and slice the cooked potatoes thinly
2. Fill a baking mold alternating layers of potatoes and ham
3. Sprinkle the ham lightly with pepper
4. Melt one tablespoon of butter in a pan, mix in one tablespoon of flour
5. Add one-half quarter of cream very slowly while stirring constantly, and a pinch of pepper
6. Bring to a boil and remove immediately
7. Beat two eggs and mix first with two tablespoons of the sauce, then pour the eggs into the sauce, mix and pour over the layered ham and potatoes in the mold
8. Sprinkle with grated breadcrumbs on top and place in a hot oven for about fifteen minutes
9. This dish is usually served for breakfast

title: Rabarbar Konserwowany (Preserved Rhubarb)
body:
## Ingredients

- young rhubarb stalks
- cold water

## Directions

1. Select young stalks and chop into half-inch pieces
2. Pack into jars nearly to the top
3. Cover with clean cold water and stir with a silver fork to bring up all air bubbles to the surface
4. Fill jars with water so it overflows
5. Apply new rubber rings and tighten the lids securely
6. Store in a dry, cool, and dark place, setting the jars upside down
7. If any jars leak, use them as soon as possible

title: Rabarbar Zapiekany (Baked Rhubarb)
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- grated bread or breadcrumbs
- rhubarb stalks
- sugar

## Directions

1. Grease a deep baking dish with butter and on the bottom spread a layer of grated bread or breadcrumbs about an inch thick
2. Peel the outer fibers of the rhubarb stalks, cut into small pieces and spread a layer of the same thickness on the grated bread and sprinkle generously with sugar, then alternately add layers of grated bread and rhubarb with sugar until the dish is full, with grated bread on top

title: Racuszki (Fried Batter Puffs)
body:
## Ingredients

- half quart flour
- cup sour cream
- two whole eggs
- salt
- pound of lard or fat for frying
- bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Sift half quart flour, add cup of sour cream, two whole eggs and a bit of salt and beat everything well, beating for at least twenty minutes
2. Heat a pound of fat in a baking dish or deep pan; the fat must be hot enough that a piece of bread crust thrown in sizzles immediately
3. As soon as the fat is sufficiently heated, take batter by spoon and pour onto the fat in the form of small pancakes
4. When they brown, remove with a fork, drain well from the fat and place on paper so the remaining fat soaks in, then arrange on a platter and serve to the table
5. Serve separately with berry or fruit juice

title: Rizi Bizi (Rice and Peas)
body:
## Ingredients

- one quarter pound of rice
- water
- good butter
- water
- one half quart of fresh peas
- salt
- boiling water
- a few mushrooms
- one tablespoon young butter
- one tablespoon grated Parmesan
- salt to taste
- butter
- Parmesan cheese

## Directions

1. Cover rice with water and bring to a boil, then drain the water and braise the rice covered in good butter, pouring a little water so it does not burn.
2. Shell one half quart of fresh peas, throw into a large pan with salted boiling water and cook until tender.
3. Cook a few mushrooms and cut into thin strips, then mix everything together, add one tablespoon of young butter, one tablespoon of grated Parmesan, salt to taste and place in a deep buttered clay bowl and put in a hot oven for a few minutes just to heat through.
4. Then transfer to a plate and sprinkle generously with Parmesan on top. This is an Italian dish, but common in other countries as well.

title: Roast Beef (Roasted Beef)
body:
## Ingredients

- large piece of rib meat
- salt
- pepper
- mustard
- potatoes

## Directions

1. Rub the rolled meat with salt, pepper, and a little mustard
2. Place in a hot oven
3. During roasting, baste frequently with the drippings in the pan
4. One hour before it is cooked, surround the meat with peeled potatoes and roast together
5. The sauce can be thickened with flour or served as is

title: Roast Beef Parowany (Steamed Roast Beef)
body:
## Ingredients

- piece of cross-cut beef with two ribs
- salt
- flour

## Directions

1. Remove the bones, roll the meat, and tie with twine
2. Rinse with boiling water
3. Place the meat in a roasting pan of such height that the meat almost reaches the top
4. Pour boiling water one inch deep
5. Cover tightly and place in a moderately hot oven for two and a half to three hours
6. After one hour of cooking, sprinkle with about one teaspoon of salt
7. When the meat is cooked, remove the cover and leave in the hot oven for about ten minutes
8. To make the sauce: remove the meat from the pan, add one and a half tablespoons of flour to the remaining sauce and brown on the fire, then serve separately or pour over the meat

title: Roasted Turkey (Pieczony Indyk)
body:
## Ingredients

- young and fat turkey, ten to twelve pounds
- baking soda
- salt
- two dozen chestnuts
- turkey liver
- parsley, finely chopped
- onion
- pepper
- two egg yolks
- five small pork sausages
- butter
- grated bread or white bread
- smoked bacon, thinly sliced
- flour

## Directions

1. Select a young and fat turkey weighing ten to twelve pounds
2. After plucking, singe over a sheet of lit paper, holding the turkey carefully to avoid tearing the innards
3. Wash carefully in four waters; add one teaspoon of baking soda to the third water
4. Dry thoroughly both inside and outside, then rub the inside with a little salt
5. Roast two dozen chestnuts and peel them
6. Pound eight of these chestnuts and the turkey liver in a mortar into a paste, add finely chopped parsley, onion, salt and pepper, and two egg yolks, mix into a paste and stuff the cavity and sew it shut
7. Fry five small pork sausages in butter until half cooked; add one cup of grated bread or white bread, one large spoon of butter, salt and pepper to taste and the remaining whole chestnuts, stuff the turkey body with this filling and sew it shut with strong twine
8. Tie the legs and wings to the body; brush with butter and wipe with salt and pepper, then coat with flour
9. Place in a roasting pan and cover with thin slices of smoked bacon; place in a moderately hot oven
10. Roast for twenty minutes per pound of turkey, so for a twelve-pound turkey roast for four hours
11. During roasting, frequently baste and turn so that it browns evenly on all sides
12. Cook the giblets separately in salted water, then chop very finely and add to the sauce remaining in the pan after removing the turkey

title: Rolada z Prosięcia (Pork Roulade)
body:
## Ingredients

- dressed and cleaned piglet
- pork meat
- one pound of finely chopped veal
- liver of the piglet
- salt and pepper to taste
- nutmeg flower
- young pork fat
- two small bread rolls
- three whole eggs
- cooked ham with fat
- cooked young pork fat
- hard-boiled eggs
- water
- salt
- vinegar
- mixed vegetables

## Directions

1. Cut the prepared and cleaned piglet lengthwise along the belly, discard the head and legs.
2. Remove from the bones and lay the skin on the table, skin side down.
3. Scrape all meat from the bones and head, chop finely, and mix with one pound of finely chopped veal.
4. Add the chopped liver of the piglet.
5. Season with salt, pepper, and a small amount of nutmeg flower.
6. Fry this mixture for half an hour in young pork fat on low heat, being careful not to brown the fat.
7. Remove from heat and cool slightly.
8. Add two small bread rolls (moistened and squeezed of water), three well-beaten whole eggs, and mix everything thoroughly.
9. Pass through a fine sieve.
10. Spread this farce on the laid out piglet.
11. Layer with slices of cooked ham with fat, or cooked young pork fat.
12. Layer with hard-boiled eggs, sliced.
13. Add another layer of farce.
14. Roll up, sew closed, wrap in a cloth, and cook in salted water with vinegar and mixed vegetables for two hours.
15. Remove from heat and cool slightly, then remove and press down with a board or stone to flatten, and cool completely.
16. When completely cold, slice and serve with vinegar and oil.
17. Note: If storing the roulade for longer during summer, do not add eggs or ham.
18. The remaining sauce in the pot should be clarified with egg whites, reheated, strained through cloth and cooled; this aspic is used to coat the sliced roulade.

title: Root Beer
body:
## Ingredients

- five quarts of water
- one and a half cups of thick molasses
- one ounce of sassafras bark
- one ounce of wintergreen bark
- one ounce of sarsaparilla root
- half a cup of yeast
- two gallons of water

## Directions

1. Boil five quarts of water, add one and a half cups of thick molasses and stir until dissolved
2. Set aside for three hours
3. After this time add one ounce each of sassafras bark, wintergreen bark, and sarsaparilla root, all ground into powder
4. Then add half a cup of yeast, add two gallons of water and set at room temperature for twelve hours for fermentation
5. When fermentation is complete, pour the clear liquid into bottles and cork tightly

title: Rosół (Beef Broth)
body:
## Ingredients

- half pound beef per person, or one pound per person for a stronger broth
- water
- parsley with roots
- leeks
- carrots
- onion
- beef fat

## Directions

1. Do not rinse the meat, as this removes significant nutrients. Instead, immediately pour water over it in a pot, preferably soft water, and simmer without skimming for three and a half to four hours. Cook over low heat, pouring in enough water at the start so that you do not need to add more during cooking, as this would diminish the quality of the broth. When cooking on very low heat, half a quart of water per person is sufficient. After the broth has cooked for about an hour and the foam stops appearing, pour in a spoonful of cold water, which will cause fat to rise to the surface. Skim this fat with a spoon. Then add whatever vegetables are available: parsley with roots, leeks, carrots, and onion. The onion can first be roasted on a sheet in the oven, which enhances the flavor of the broth. Before serving, let it rest off the heat for ten minutes, skim off the remaining fat, then strain through a sieve into a serving bowl, in which a little finely chopped parsley is placed.

title: Rosół Julienne (Beef Broth with Julienne Vegetables)
body:
## Ingredients

- three-quarters pound beef, preferably from the cross-rib, per person
- some veal, in small pieces
- some chicken giblets
- beef fat, a few spoonfuls
- onions, roasted
- carrots, one or two
- water
- cooked green peas, one spoonful
- spinach leaves cut into strips, or asparagus, cauliflower, or brussels sprouts

## Directions

1. Chop finely together the veal, giblets, and small pieces of beef, about half a pound total. Add several spoonfuls of beef fat and place in a pot over heat with just enough water so it does not scorch. When well browned, add a couple of roasted onions and one or two finely chopped carrots, allowing them to brown together. Keep moistening with water to prevent scorching. Cook the meat separately as usual for broth. Once the pieces with onion and carrot are well browned, add them with their juices to the broth and cook everything together. Separately, chop various vegetables finely into thin strips, place in another pot, and braise over very low heat, moistening gradually with broth. When both the meat and vegetables are completely cooked, add one spoonful of previously cooked green peas or strips of spinach leaves; alternatively, use asparagus, cauliflower, or brussels sprouts. Serve without any other additions.

title: Rosół na Prędce (Quick Broth)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound lean beef, finely chopped
- 1 carrot, finely sliced
- 1 small rutabaga, finely sliced
- 1 onion, finely sliced
- 1 clove
- 1/2 bay leaf
- 1 quart water
- salt and pepper to taste
- 1 tablespoon tomato sauce (optional)
- burnt sugar for color (optional)

## Directions

1. Chop finely 1 pound of lean beef; slice finely 1 carrot, 1 small rutabaga and 1 onion, also add 1 clove and 1/2 bay leaf
2. Cover vegetables with 1 quart of water and cook for 20 minutes, then strain
3. Pour the strained liquid back into the pot, add the meat and cook on low heat for 1 hour with salt and pepper to taste
4. You can also add 1 tablespoon of tomato sauce and burnt sugar for color
5. Serve on the table with the meat

title: Rosół Orleański (Beef Broth Orleans)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef for strong broth
- chicken or hen meat, deboned and raw
- butter, preferably crayfish butter
- soaked bread roll
- salt and pepper
- nutmeg, a pinch
- egg yolks, two per half pound of mixture
- cooked green peas, a few spoonfuls
- cooked rice, fried in butter, a few spoonfuls

## Directions

1. Prepare a strong beef broth as usual. Separately, make the following stuffing from poultry, preferably from a hen: finely chop the raw deboned poultry. Heat butter (preferably crayfish butter) and add the chopped meat to it, allowing it to braise for about half an hour. Then add as much soaked bread roll as you have meat, salt and pepper to taste, a pinch of nutmeg, and finally for every half pound of this mixture, beat in two egg yolks and mix everything well. On a work surface, form two dozen oblong dumplings from this mixture. Drop them into boiling water and after they are cooked, remove them with a spoon and place in a serving bowl. In addition, place a few spoonfuls of cooked green peas and the same amount of cooked rice that has been fried in butter in the bowl, then pour the broth over everything.

title: Rosół Owsiany (Oat Broth)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef bone
- 2 quarts cold water
- 1/2 cup rice soaked in water until softened
- 3 tablespoons oatmeal
- salt and pepper to taste

## Directions

1. Cover beef bone with 2 quarts of cold water and cook on low heat for 3 hours
2. Half an hour before serving, add 1/2 cup of rice soaked in water until softened, 3 tablespoons of oatmeal, salt and pepper to taste
3. Cook for another half hour and serve

title: Rosół Selerowy (Celery Broth)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef broth with vegetables
- celery, cut into small cubes

## Directions

1. Prepare beef broth with vegetables as usual, and shortly before serving, add celery cut into small cubes and cook until completely soft.

title: Rosół Selerowy (Celery Broth)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef for broth
- vegetables
- celery root, cut into small cubes

## Directions

1. Cook broth on beef with vegetables as usual
2. Shortly before serving, add celery root cut into small cubes and cook until completely tender
3. Serve the meat from this broth dressed with cooked vegetables as a meat course

title: Rosół Szkocki (Scottish Broth)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 pounds lamb from the neck
- 2 quarts cold water
- 2 carrots
- 1 rutabaga
- 1 or 2 onions
- 2 tablespoons pearl barley
- salt and pepper to taste
- parsley, finely chopped

## Directions

1. Soak 3 pounds lamb from the neck in cold water for 1 hour, then remove, trim all fat, and cut meat into small pieces
2. Place meat in a pot, cover with 2 quarts of cold water and bring to boil
3. Skim well, then cook for 1.5 hours
4. Cut 2 carrots, 1 rutabaga, 1 or 2 onions into pieces and add to the soup along with 2 tablespoons of pearl barley, salt and pepper to taste
5. Continue cooking for 3 more hours
6. Serve sprinkled with finely chopped parsley

title: Rosół z Cielęciny (Veal Broth)
body:
## Ingredients

- at least 1/2 pound veal per person
- water

## Directions

1. First scald the veal with boiling water and leave it in the water for a few minutes, then drain the water; otherwise the broth will become white
2. Proceed the same as with beef broth, except that veal broth cooks much faster, only about 1.5 hours
3. Salt it only just before serving, otherwise it will turn red
