4. Serve only with rice, barley groats mixed with egg, or ribbon noodles

title: Rosół z Lanemi Kluseczkami (Beef Broth with Ribbon Dumplings)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef broth with vegetables
- flour, two spoonfuls
- eggs, two whole
- cold water

## Directions

1. Prepare broth from beef meat with various vegetables, strain it, pour it back into the pot, and place over heat. Beat two spoonfuls of flour with two whole eggs and cold water to make a thin batter. Form a paper cone and pour the batter through it onto the simmering broth.

title: Rosół z Siekanego Mięsa (Broth with Minced Meat)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 heaping tablespoon good butter
- 1 medium onion, sliced
- 2 pounds lean beef, finely chopped
- 1 quart cold water
- 1 egg white, well beaten
- 1 eggshell
- 1 tablespoon cold water
- a few cloves
- a pinch of nutmeg
- salt and pepper to taste

## Directions

1. Melt butter in a pot, add sliced onion of medium size and braise on low heat under a lid until the onion lightly browns, stirring from time to time
2. Add 2 pounds of finely chopped lean beef to the browned onion, pour in 1 quart of cold water, cover with a lid and cook on very low heat for 3 hours, then strain and pour back into the pot
3. Add 1 well-beaten egg white, 1 eggshell, 1 tablespoon of cold water, a few cloves, a pinch of nutmeg, salt and pepper to taste
4. Cook for 5 minutes, then strain again and serve

title: Rosół z Łanemi Kluseczkami (Beef Broth with Ribbon Noodles)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef for broth
- various vegetables
- 2 tablespoons flour
- 2 whole eggs
- cold water

## Directions

1. Cook broth on beef with addition of various vegetables, strain it, pour back into the pot and place on fire
2. Beat 2 tablespoons flour with 2 whole eggs and cold water to make a thin batter
3. Make a paper cone and pour the batter through it into the boiling broth

title: Rosół ze Szczawiem (Sorrel Broth)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef for broth
- handful of sorrel leaves
- eggs

## Directions

1. Cook strong broth on beef
2. Pour off half a quart of this broth into a separate pot and cook a handful of sorrel leaves in it
3. Pour the broth and sorrel leaves into a bowl and mix
4. Cook eggs in their shells in water, one per person, arrange on plates and then pour soup onto each plate

title: Rosół ze Słodką Kapustą (Beef Broth with Sweet Cabbage)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef broth
- cabbage, one quarter head per person
- small potatoes, finely diced
- water for cooking cabbage

## Directions

1. Prepare ordinary clear broth. Separately, cook cabbage that has been cut into quarters and previously parboiled, allowing one quarter head per person. Cook the cabbage until completely soft and without a lid so the bitterness can evaporate. When the cabbage is cooked, drain it and place in a serving bowl along with a portion of finely diced cooked potatoes and pour broth over it. Some also use sour cream with this broth, which is served separately and added to the plate at the table.

title: Rozbratel Wiedeński (Viennese Schnitzel)
body:
## Ingredients

- thin slice of meat (preferably first-cut or cutlet)
- 1/2 tablespoon butter
- 1 onion, parboiled or roasted
- salt

## Directions

1. Cut a thin slice of meat (the best is first-cut or cutlet) and beat it strongly into a round slice the size of a plate and salt it.
2. Put 1/2 tablespoon of butter into a pan, and when it is well browned, place the meat in it and fry on very high heat for 2 minutes on one side.
3. Turn to the other side.
4. Have a parboiled or roasted onion ready, and when the meat is turned to the other side, place this onion on the edge of the pan and fry it so that it just softens well but does not brown.
5. The entire frying should not last longer than 5 minutes, as the quality of schnitzel depends on quick frying.

title: Rumiana Zupa z Manny (Brown Semolina Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal bone
- two quarts water
- sliced carrot
- onion
- several celery stalks
- parsley, some amount
- salt
- several peppercorns
- one egg
- a pinch of salt
- three tablespoons semolina
- one tablespoon butter

## Directions

1. Place a veal bone in two quarts of water, and when it comes to a boil, remove the scum and add sliced carrot, onion, several celery stalks, some parsley and salt, as well as several peppercorns.
2. Cook until it reduces to nearly half.
3. Break one egg with a pinch of salt and gradually add three tablespoons of semolina to it, continually beating until a thick dough forms.
4. In a pan dissolve one tablespoon of butter and place the semolina by the spoonful on it and fry: when it browns on one side, turn it over to the other.
5. When both sides have been browned in this way, add to the soup that has been removed from the heat and let it sit for fifteen minutes, then place on heat and cook for fifteen minutes, then serve.

title: Rumsztyk z Cebulą (Rump Steak with Onion)
body:
## Ingredients

- sirloin, stew meat, or rump, cut into thick square pieces
- salt
- pepper
- butter
- onion, thinly sliced
- potatoes

## Directions

1. The best meat for rump steak is sirloin, stew meat, or rump.
2. Cut the meat into thick square pieces.
3. Season with salt and pepper.
4. Fry in hot butter in a pan without over-cooking.
5. Toward the end of frying, add plenty of thinly sliced onion and fry it together with the meat.
6. When serving, surround with potatoes.

title: Ryba Pieczona po Amerykańsku (Fish Roasted American Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- fish weighing four to six pounds
- salt
- pepper
- bacon
- butter
- water
- parsley

## Directions

1. Take any fish weighing four to six pounds.
2. Clean it, wipe dry, salt inside and outside, stuff and sew.
3. With a sharp knife, make diagonal cuts two inches apart, arranged in a zigzag pattern.
4. Place the fish on a roasting pan.
5. Insert sliced bacon into the cuts.
6. Sprinkle with salt and pepper and place in the oven.
7. When it begins to brown, baste with butter and water every ten minutes.
8. When roasted (taking about an hour), remove the bacon and place on a platter.
9. Garnish with parsley sprigs.
10. To prevent the fish from sticking to the pan, you can place a couple of strips of clean cloth under it and lift it with the cloth after roasting.
11. Prepare the stuffing using one of three methods below: Method 1 - Soak one cup of grated bread or white bread in cold water then squeeze out the water; sauté three spoons of chopped onion in white fat; add one teaspoon of chopped parsley, one teaspoon of capers, one teaspoon of chopped cornichons, salt and pepper to taste and mix well. Method 2 - Chop very finely half a pound of lean veal and two ounces of smoked bacon; add one quarter cup of soaked and then squeezed out bread, one teaspoon of chopped onion, one teaspoon of chopped parsley, salt and pepper to taste and mix. Method 3 - Sauté one spoon of very finely chopped onion in two spoons of butter in white fat; add one cup of soaked and then squeezed out bread, one teaspoon of chopped green parsley, one beaten egg, salt and pepper to taste and mix carefully.

title: Ryba z Kwaśną Kapustą (Fish with Sour Cabbage)
body:
## Ingredients

- fish (tench preferred, or carp)
- sour cabbage
- butter
- flour
- onion
- sour cream
- salt

## Directions

1. For this dish, tench is best, but since tench is not found in America, any other fish can be used, even carp.
2. Cook the sour cabbage after rinsing it thoroughly in cold water so it is not too sour.
3. Season with butter mixed with flour and roasted and grated onion.
4. Cut the fish into small pieces, not thick slices, remove all bones, salt and set aside for an hour.
5. Place the fish in the cabbage, pour in sour cream, mix and braise on low heat under a cover until tender, being careful not to let it burn.

title: Ryba Zapiekana z Makaronem (Fish Baked with Pasta)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup cold cooked fish (preferably stockfish)
- 1 cup cold cooked Italian pasta, broken into inch-sized pieces
- butter
- salt and pepper to taste
- 1/2 tablespoon butter
- milk to cover the mixture
- breadcrumbs
- 3 tablespoons grated cheese

## Directions

1. Mix 1 cup of cold cooked fish (preferably stockfish) with 1 cup of cold cooked Italian pasta broken into inch-sized pieces
2. Butter a baking dish or mold
3. Place the fish and pasta mixture in the dish
4. Add salt and pepper to taste, as well as 1/2 tablespoon of butter and enough milk to moisten the entire mixture
5. Sprinkle a layer of breadcrumbs on top, then 3 tablespoons of grated cheese
6. Place in the oven and bake until well browned
7. Remove from oven and serve immediately at table

title: Ryby a la Nelson (Fish a la Nelson)
body:
## Ingredients

- tench, pike or pike-perch
- salt
- pepper
- butter
- onion
- mushrooms
- Italian pasta
- water

## Directions

1. For this dish, tench, pike or pike-perch is best. After cleaning the fish, cut it into pieces, remove all bones, salt and pepper it at least an hour before use.
2. Place a spoonful of butter on the bottom of a pot.
3. On top, place thinly sliced onion that has been previously scalded with boiling water, a few cooked mushrooms cut into thin slices.
4. Place the fish on top, then add Italian pasta, broken into small pieces and cooked in salted water.
5. Add a little more butter on top.
6. Cover tightly and braise for no more than half an hour, frequently shaking the pot.
7. Instead of pasta, chopped potatoes can be used, and the taste will be different but equally good.

title: Ryby Smażone (Fried Fish)
body:
## Ingredients

- fish
- salt
- beaten egg
- butter or fat

## Directions

1. Salt the fish an hour before frying.
2. Wipe off excess moisture with clean blotting paper.
3. Dip in beaten egg and fry in hot butter or fat, using plenty of fat.
4. Instead of butter, fat can be used equally well.
5. Serve the fried fish dry, without pouring butter on it, sprinkled only with finely chopped green parsley.

title: Ryby w Pomidorowym Sosie (Fish in Tomato Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- fish fillets
- salt
- pepper
- lard
- flour
- onion
- tomatoes from a can
- water
- cloves
- bay leaf
- garlic
- white bread
- butter
- Worcestershire sauce

## Directions

1. Fry four large fish steaks about half an inch thick in a pan until golden brown.
2. In another deep pan, brown half a spoonful of lard with the same amount of flour and one finely chopped onion; when the onion browns well, add a cup of tomatoes pressed through a sieve from a can.
3. Place on heat and cook for seven minutes from the moment it boils.
4. Place the fried fish in the tomatoes, add half a cup of boiling water, add salt and pepper to taste, a few cloves, one bay leaf and, if desired, a clove of garlic.
5. Braise on very low heat for several minutes, stirring carefully.
6. Toast four slices of white bread for croutons, spread with butter and arrange on a serving dish.
7. Place a piece of fish on each crouton, pour the sauce over it and serve.
8. This sauce will be more delicious if, just before serving, a spoonful of Worcestershire sauce is added to it, which is sold in all grocery stores.

title: Ryż Gotowany (Cooked Rice)
body:
## Ingredients

- a quarter pound of rice
- hot water
- cold water
- butter, about a quarter tablespoon

## Directions

1. Pour hot water over a quarter pound of rice in a pan and place on heat
2. As soon as it boils, immediately remove from heat and drain the water
3. Pour cold water over it, add a little butter (about a quarter tablespoon), and cook until the rice is soft
4. Stir frequently to prevent sticking
5. It is best to cook in a double boiler as there is no danger of sticking
6. When cooked, place in a bowl and pour soup over it
7. Whoever likes it can sprinkle grated Parmesan or other cheese on the rice

title: Ryż Par Excellence (Rice Par Excellence)
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup rice
- three and a half cups salted water
- butter
- cream or syrup

## Directions

1. Rinse one cup rice in several waters
2. Pour into a saucepan and add three and a half cups salted water
3. Cook for twenty minutes, counting from when it comes to a boil
4. Strain the rice through a colander
5. Grease the saucepan with butter
6. When water has drained from the rice, return the rice to the saucepan
7. Place one tablespoon of butter on top
8. Cover with a lid and place in a warm spot
9. When the butter melts, serve to the table
10. Serve with cream or syrup

title: Ryż po Turecku (Turkish Rice)
body:
## Ingredients

- three cups strong beef broth
- three tomatoes
- one cup coarse rice
- one teaspoon salt
- one quarter teaspoon pepper
- one tablespoon butter

## Directions

1. Pour three cups of strong beef broth into a saucepan and bring to a boil
2. Chop three tomatoes into small pieces, add to the broth and cook for five minutes, then strain and pour the broth back into the saucepan
3. Rinse one cup of coarse rice and spread on a table or paper to dry, then add to the broth and cook on low heat until all the broth is absorbed
4. Add one teaspoon salt, one quarter teaspoon pepper and one tablespoon butter, mix well and serve at the table

title: Ryż po Turecku (Turkish-Style Rice)
body:
## Ingredients

- three cups strong beef broth
- three tomatoes
- one cup coarse rice
- one teaspoon salt
- quarter teaspoon pepper
- one tablespoon butter

## Directions

1. Pour three cups of strong beef broth into a pot and boil
2. Cut three tomatoes into small pieces and throw into the broth
3. Cook for five minutes, then strain and pour the broth back into the pot
4. Rinse one cup of coarse rice and spread on a tableboard or paper to dry
5. Throw it into the broth and cook on low fire until it absorbs all the broth
6. Add one teaspoon salt, quarter teaspoon pepper and one tablespoon butter
7. Mix well and serve on the table

title: Ryżowe Muffins (Rice Muffins)
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup cooked rice
- half tablespoon sugar
- one tablespoon melted butter
- one cup milk
- half tablet of pressed yeast
- wheat flour as needed

## Directions

1. Mix one cup of cooked rice, half tablespoon of sugar, and one tablespoon of melted butter perfectly
2. Boil one cup of milk and cool to lukewarm temperature, then dissolve half a tablet of pressed yeast in it
3. Pour into the rice and add enough wheat flour to obtain fairly thick dough
4. Place in a warm place
5. When well risen, add three beaten eggs, mix, and fill molds halfway
6. When it has risen well again, place in a hot oven for ten minutes
7. The molds must be well buttered

title: Różowa Polewka na Mleku (Pink Milk Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- flour
- butter
- milk
- salt
- nutmeg
- cooked beets
- potato dumplings (pulpety z kartofli)

## Directions

1. In a pot, make a roux of one spoonful of flour with one spoonful of butter, being careful not to brown it, constantly stirring
2. When a uniform mass forms, slowly pour in one quart of milk while constantly stirring and bring to a boil
3. Add salt and some grated nutmeg
4. Just before serving, grate a cooked red beet, strain this mass through a sieve, take a full spoonful and mix it into the soup to give it color
5. The best addition to this soup is potato dumplings

title: Sago in Wine
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart of boiling water
- 1/4 pound of white sago
- 1 bottle of white or red sour wine
- 3/4 pound of sugar
- cinnamon in pieces
- lemon peel
- several cloves
- several slices of lemon without seeds

## Directions

1. Pour 1 quart of boiling water into a pot
2. Add 1/4 pound of white sago and cook, constantly stirring, until soft
3. Pour in 1 bottle of white or red sour wine
4. Add 3/4 pound of sugar, some cinnamon in pieces, lemon peel, and several cloves
5. Boil everything together
6. Pour into a bowl into which you have first placed several slices of lemon without seeds
7. This proportion is for eight people

title: Sago na Winie (Sago in Wine)
body:
## Ingredients

- quarter pound of white sago
- one quart of boiling water
- one bottle of white or red sour wine
- three quarters pound of sugar
- cinnamon
- lemon zest
- cloves
- lemon slices

## Directions

1. Pour a quarter pound of white sago into one quart of boiling water and cook, constantly stirring, until soft
2. Pour one bottle of white or red sour wine over it
3. Add three quarters pound of sugar, some cinnamon pieces, lemon zest and a few cloves
4. Bring everything to a boil together and pour into a bowl into which you have first placed several lemon slices without seeds
5. The above proportion is for eight people
6. Tapioca may be prepared in the same way

title: Salcyfie Smażone (Fried Salsify)
body:
## Ingredients

- salsify roots
- egg
- salt
- flour
- milk

## Directions

1. Take a couple of salsify roots, scrape them, cut into small pieces and cook in water until tender.
2. Mash into a paste and add one well-beaten egg, some salt, and a tablespoon of flour.
3. Add enough milk to make the batter fairly thin.
4. Take the batter with a spoon, place on a pan and fry like pancakes.

title: Sandacz po Parysku (Pike-Perch à la Parisienne)
body:
## Ingredients

- sandacz (pike-perch)
- salt
- butter
- flour
- heavy cream
- egg yolks
- parmesan cheese, grated
- bread roll

## Directions

1. Clean the pike-perch, split it in half, remove the backbone, salt it and let it sit for an hour.
2. Then place the fish on hot but not browned butter and braise on very low heat until tender, then remove from heat and let it cool.
3. Make a roux by cooking a tablespoon of good butter with a tablespoon of flour on low heat; dilute with a quarter measure of thick cream and cook on very low heat until thickened.
4. Remove from heat, add two beaten egg yolks and one ounce of grated parmesan and mix well.
5. Break the cooled fish into small pieces and arrange half on a buttered porcelain platter or metal mold, pour the sauce over it, then add the other half of the fish and pour the remaining sauce over it.
6. Sprinkle one ounce of grated parmesan on top, pour browned butter with breadcrumb over it and place in the oven to brown around the edges, then serve on the table with the platter or mold.

title: Sandacz po Turecku (Pike-Perch à la Turque)
body:
## Ingredients

- sandacz (pike-perch)
- salt
- oil
- white onion
- fresh parsley
- pepper
- lemon juice or vinegar

## Directions

1. Clean the pike-perch, cut it into rings, salt it and let it sit for a couple of hours.
2. Pour plenty of oil onto a pan and place on heat; when it is well heated, carefully place the fish removed from salt and cook on very low heat, turning, until tender, being careful not to let it burn or brown.
3. When the fish is done, finely chop white onion and fresh parsley, mix together and sprinkle the platter; on the onion with parsley place the fried fish and sprinkle the top generously also with parsley and onion, and a pinch of pepper.
4. Finally pour lemon juice or good vinegar over it. Such fish is best the day after it is prepared.

title: Sandacz w Koperkowym Sosie (Pike-Perch in Dill Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- sandacz (pike-perch)
- onion
- vegetables (miscellaneous)
- butter
- flour
- fresh dill

## Directions

1. Clean and split the pike-perch, then fill the inside with onion sliced into strips and leave in a cool place for several hours.
2. Just before use, discard the onion, rinse the fish thoroughly, cut it into rings and pour cooled stock made from vegetables over it, then cook on strong heat until tender.
3. Carefully remove the fish and arrange on a platter.
4. Place in a pan a tablespoon of butter, rubbed raw with a tablespoon of flour, brown it lightly and then dilute with the stock in which the fish was cooked, cook for a couple of minutes.
5. Add plenty of chopped fresh dill and pour this sauce over the fish arranged on the platter.

title: Sandacz z Jajami (Pike-Perch with Eggs)
body:
## Ingredients

- sandacz (pike-perch)
- salt
- vegetables (miscellaneous)
- onion
- eggs
- fresh parsley
- butter

## Directions

1. Clean the pike-perch, salt it and let it sit in salt for an hour.
2. Cook a stock from miscellaneous vegetables, onion, etc. and let it cool.
3. Remove the fish from salt, cut it into rings, place in a pan or dish, pour the cooled vegetable stock over it, cook on high heat until tender.
4. Carefully remove the fish and arrange on a platter, placing one ring next to another to form the whole fish.
5. Cook a couple of eggs hard, chop them, add plenty of chopped fresh parsley and toss onto melted butter, which should not be browned.
6. When it has warmed slightly, pour this sauce over the fish arranged on the platter.

title: Sandacz z Rakowem Masłem (Pike-Perch with Crayfish Butter)
body:
## Ingredients

- sandacz (pike-perch)
- vinegar
- roots and herbs
- salt
- butter
- crayfish butter
- lemon
- potatoes

## Directions

1. Wipe the cleaned pike-perch with cloth and place in cold vinegar boiled with roots and salt for an hour.
2. Half an hour before serving, butter an oblong baking dish, arrange the pike-perch, pour melted butter or oil over it and place in a moderately hot oven for half an hour, basting with melted fat at least every five minutes.
3. When cooked, carefully slide it onto a platter and coat with melted crayfish butter so that the entire fish is covered.
4. Then arrange with lemon slices and small boiled potatoes.

title: Sarnina (Venison Roast)
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh venison
- vinegar with roots, vegetables and onion
- lard for larding
- butter
- sour cream

## Directions

1. Allow fresh venison to age several days at minimum to tenderize, or soak in vinegar that has been boiled with roots, vegetables and onion for several days
2. Remove from vinegar and remove sinews
3. Lard with lard
4. Roast in a roasting pan, initially basting with butter, then with sour cream
5. Roast no more than two hours, or less if the meat is small
6. Before serving, slice the meat into strips and then reassemble so the roast retains its original form

title: Sałata a la Zamojski (Salad à la Zamojski)
body:
## Ingredients

- salad greens
- one thin but wide slice of bread
- garlic
- butter
- two hard-boiled egg yolks
- one teaspoon of strong mustard
- two tablespoons of oil
- one tablespoon of strong vinegar
- white pepper

## Directions

1. Sort, wash and drain the salad as usual
2. Cut a thin but wide slice of bread, rub it with garlic and fry in butter
3. Place this toast on a salad bowl and put the salad on top
4. Cover with a plate and let it stand for one hour
5. After one hour, take two hard-boiled egg yolks, add one teaspoon of strong mustard, two tablespoons of oil, one tablespoon of strong vinegar and a little white pepper
6. Crush all of this well into a uniform paste and pour this sauce over the salad
7. Mix and only then salt to taste, discarding the slice of toast beforehand

title: Sałata Macedońska (Macedonian Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup of cold boiled potatoes
- half cup of celery
- some finely shredded raw cabbage
- some cooked green beans
- a small chopped onion
- lettuce leaves
- two tablespoons of oil
- one tablespoon of vinegar
- half teaspoon of salt
- pepper to taste

## Directions

1. Slice one cup of cold boiled potatoes
2. Chop half a cup of celery into cubes
3. Add some finely shredded raw cabbage, some cooked green beans and a small chopped onion
4. Mix together and place into a salad bowl lined with lettuce leaves
5. Mix two tablespoons of oil, one tablespoon of vinegar, half a teaspoon of salt and pepper to taste together and pour this sauce over the salad

title: Sałata Owocowa w Galarecie (Fruit Salad in Jelly)
body:
## Ingredients

- two oranges
- four bananas
- one cup of sugar
- half a box of gelatin
- water
- cream whipped with sugar

## Directions

1. Peel and slice the oranges and bananas thinly into a serving dish.
2. Add one cup of sugar and mix.
3. Dissolve half a box of gelatin in water and pour over the arranged fruit.
4. Place in a cold place for several hours until the jelly sets.
5. When completely set, cut into large pieces and top each with a dollop of cream whipped with sugar.
6. Instead of oranges and bananas, other fruits and berries may be used.

title: Sałata po Francusku (French-Style Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- salad greens
- one egg
- one tablespoon of strong vinegar
- two tablespoons of oil
- half teaspoon of sugar
- salt

## Directions

1. Sort and wash the salad greens carefully
2. Drain of water and place in a napkin, then holding by the four corners, shake the napkin vigorously so all moisture remains on it
3. Hard-boil one egg and remove the yolk into a small bowl
4. Add one tablespoon of strong vinegar, two tablespoons of oil, half teaspoon of sugar and some salt
5. Mix this thoroughly with a fork
6. Pour this sauce over the salad, mix well and let stand quietly for ten minutes
7. Then serve on the table, making sure all salad leaves are covered with sauce

title: Sałata z Cielęciny (Veal Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- leftover cold roasted veal
- lettuce leaves
- French sauce or mayonnaise sauce

## Directions

1. Take leftover cold roasted veal and carefully remove all fat and browned skin
2. Chop the white meat into small cubes and arrange on a salad bowl lined with lettuce leaves
3. Pour French sauce made of oil, vinegar, salt and pepper, or mayonnaise sauce over it

title: Sałata z Fasoli Strączkowej (Green Bean Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- green string beans
- salt
- water
- one teaspoon grated onion
- one teaspoon lemon juice
- one teaspoon salt
- one teaspoon fine sugar
- two pinches pepper
- two pinches dry mustard
- one tablespoon oil

## Directions

1. Cook green string beans in salted water
2. Drain and set aside to cool
3. When completely cold, arrange on a salad bowl
4. For the sauce: mix together one teaspoon grated onion, one teaspoon lemon juice, one teaspoon salt, one teaspoon fine sugar, two pinches pepper, two pinches dry mustard and one tablespoon oil
5. Pour the sauce over the beans

title: Sałata z Homarów (Lobster Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup of lobster meat
- oil
- vinegar
- pepper
- a large head of lettuce
- half cup of mayonnaise sauce
- olives, capers, etc.

## Directions

1. Take one cup of lobster meat, break it into small pieces and pour oil with vinegar and pepper over it, as with chicken salad
2. Tear a large head of lettuce into small pieces an hour after preparing the lobster meat and mix together
3. Add half a cup of mayonnaise sauce, mix everything together and arrange on a salad bowl lined with whole lettuce leaves
4. You can garnish the top with olives, capers, etc.
5. Salads from other crustaceans are prepared in the same way

title: Sałata z Jaj (Egg Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- six eggs
- cold water
- lettuce leaves
- half cup vinegar
- two tablespoons grated bread
- one teaspoon mustard
- one teaspoon celery seed
- one tablespoon butter
- pepper
- or two tablespoons butter
- one tablespoon lemon juice
- one tablespoon finely chopped pickled cucumbers
- salt
- a little grated lemon zest

## Directions

1. Cook six eggs hard and plunge into cold water
2. When completely cool, peel and slice very thinly, best sliced lengthwise
3. Line a salad bowl with lettuce leaves and arrange the sliced eggs on top
4. For the first sauce option: mix together half cup vinegar, two tablespoons grated bread, one teaspoon mustard, one teaspoon celery seed, one tablespoon butter and some pepper
5. For the second sauce option: melt two tablespoons butter, add one tablespoon lemon juice, one tablespoon finely chopped pickled cucumbers, salt and pepper to taste and a little grated lemon zest; mix and pour
6. Pour one of the sauces over the eggs

title: Sałata z Kalafiorów (Cauliflower Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- one large cauliflower
- two quarts salted water
- one egg yolk
- one quarter teaspoon salt
- three tablespoons oil
- one tablespoon vinegar or lemon juice
- one quarter teaspoon dry mustard

## Directions

1. Cook one large cauliflower in two quarts salted water until tender, which should take about half an hour or slightly longer
2. Drain water and place cauliflower in a cool place
3. When completely cool, break into small pieces and arrange nicely in the center of a large salad bowl
4. In a small bowl, beat one egg yolk, add one quarter teaspoon salt and mix; add drop by drop, while continuously stirring, three tablespoons oil and one tablespoon vinegar or lemon juice, and one quarter teaspoon dry mustard; mix until smooth
5. Pour this sauce over the cauliflower
6. Alternatively, French salad dressing or mayonnaise sauce may be used instead

title: Sałata z Kartofli i Śledzia (Potato and Herring Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes in their skins
- water
- one good herring
- one medium onion
- two tablespoons oil
- one tablespoon strong vinegar
- pepper

## Directions

1. Cook potatoes in their skins as usual for potato salad, drain water and let cool
2. When completely cold, slice or dice
3. For one quart of diced potatoes, take one good herring, clean, wash thoroughly, peel off skin and bones and dice along with the milt
4. Grate on a grater or chop very finely one medium onion
5. Mix everything together and add two tablespoons oil, one tablespoon strong vinegar and pepper to taste

title: Sałata z Kartofli No. 1 (Potato Salad No. 1)
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart potatoes
- one raw egg yolk
- salt
- white pepper
- one quarter pound oil
- vinegar
- finely chopped chives
- finely chopped parsley

## Directions

1. Cook one quart potatoes unpeeled, cool, peel and slice thinly
2. Thoroughly mix one raw egg yolk with salt and white pepper
3. Slowly add one quarter pound oil while continuously stirring; when the mixture thickens, add vinegar to taste
4. Add finely chopped chives and parsley
5. Pour this sauce over the potatoes in a salad bowl, mix gently and let sit undisturbed for several hours so the potatoes absorb the dressing, then serve

title: Sałata z Kartofli No. 2 (Potato Salad No. 2)
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart unpeeled potatoes
- water
- one quarter pound fatty smoked bacon
- one onion
- half cup hot water
- half cup hot vinegar
- one large teaspoon salt
- black pepper
- two tablespoons oil

## Directions

1. Cook one quart unpeeled potatoes, drain water and place briefly on hot stove to dry
2. Peel and cut into thin slices while hot
3. Cut one quarter pound fatty smoked bacon into small cubes and fry on low heat
4. Finely chop one onion and add half cup hot water, half cup hot vinegar and one large teaspoon salt; mix in a bowl and add some black pepper
5. When the bacon is well fried and the cracklings browned, add two tablespoons oil, mix and carefully pour into the bowl with vinegar and water, stirring gently to avoid splattering; mix together
6. Pour this over the potatoes arranged in a salad bowl, mix everything and serve immediately

title: Sałata z Kartofli No. 3 (Potato Salad No. 3)
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart cooked cold potatoes
- one large onion
- three tablespoons oil
- one tablespoon vinegar
- half teaspoon salt
- three pinches pepper
- finely chopped fresh parsley

## Directions

1. Slice one quart cooked cold potatoes into uniform pieces
2. Chop one large onion very finely
3. Mix three tablespoons oil, one tablespoon vinegar, half teaspoon salt and three pinches pepper
4. Pour this sauce over the potatoes and mix everything well
5. May add some chopped fresh parsley

title: Sałata z Kartofli No. 4 (Potato Salad No. 4)
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked potatoes
- three hard-cooked eggs
- one large onion
- salt
- pepper
- one egg yolk
- one cup vinegar
- two thirds cup sour cream

## Directions

1. Chop cooked potatoes fairly coarsely
2. For one quart of potatoes, hard cook three eggs and chop them; finely chop or grate on a grater one large onion
3. Layer in a salad bowl: a layer of potatoes mixed with sauce, then a layer of chopped eggs mixed with onion, add salt and pepper to taste, then another layer of potatoes, and so on until all ingredients are used
4. For the sauce: beat one egg well and mix with one cup vinegar; place on heat and bring to a boil a couple of times, stirring; remove to cool
5. When completely cool, beat two thirds cup sour cream and mix with the vinegar
6. Use this sauce for the layered salad

title: Sałata z Kury (Chicken Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- white meat of roasted chicken - two cups
- half teaspoon of salt
- quarter teaspoon of pepper
- four tablespoons of oil
- two tablespoons of vinegar
- two cups of celery
- one cup of mayonnaise sauce
- lettuce leaves
- olives or gherkins

## Directions

1. Chop the white meat of roasted chicken into half-inch cubes or smaller, about two cups of meat
2. Put half a teaspoon of salt and a quarter teaspoon of pepper into a bowl, mix and then pour in four tablespoons of oil very slowly, constantly stirring, and finally two tablespoons of vinegar
3. Place the meat in a salad bowl, pour this sauce over it and let it sit for an hour
4. Chop two cups of celery into cubes and add to the meat
5. Pour one cup of mayonnaise sauce over it, mix and place on a salad bowl lined with lettuce leaves
6. Pour about one tablespoon of mayonnaise sauce on top and garnish with olives or gherkins
7. The above proportion is for six people

title: Sałata z Marchwi (Carrot Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- about three carrots
- one boiled egg yolk
- one tablespoon of butter
- a few tablespoons of vinegar
- salt and pepper to taste
- lettuce leaves

## Directions

1. Cook about three carrots in a large quantity of water until tender; when half cooked, drain the water and add fresh water
2. After cooking, remove them from water and cool completely
3. Chop into half-inch pieces and place in a salad bowl lined with lettuce leaves
4. Rub one cooked egg yolk through a sieve with one tablespoon of butter, add a few tablespoons of vinegar, salt and pepper to taste and pour this sauce over the carrots

title: Sałata z Ogórków i Kartofli (Cucumber and Potato Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- two large cucumbers
- a couple of potatoes
- half teaspoon of salt
- two pinches of pepper
- three tablespoons of oil
- quarter teaspoon of onion juice
- one tablespoon of vinegar

## Directions

1. Peel two large cucumbers and plunge into cold water for an hour
2. Chop the cucumbers into fairly thin slices and also chop a couple of potatoes in a similar way; the potatoes should be half the amount of the cucumbers
3. Mix together and pour the following sauce over them: Mix half a teaspoon of salt, two pinches of pepper, three tablespoons of oil, a quarter teaspoon of onion juice and one tablespoon of vinegar well together, taking all these ingredients separately in the order given above; pour the oil very slowly, constantly stirring

title: Sałata z Ostryg (Oyster Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- half quart of oysters with their juice
- the same amount of chopped celery
- salt and pepper to taste
- mayonnaise sauce
- lettuce

## Directions

1. Pour half a quart of oysters with their juice into a pan, place on fire and cook until they plump up
2. Drain all the liquid and chop the oysters
3. Mix with the same amount of chopped celery, add salt and pepper to taste
4. Pour mayonnaise sauce over it and serve at table
5. Instead of celery, you can also use chopped lettuce

title: Sałata z Owoców (Fruit Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- three oranges
- two bananas
- two figs
- two tablespoons of sugar

## Directions

1. Peel three oranges, remove the seeds and chop into small cubes
2. Peel and slice two bananas
3. Rinse and chop two figs into small pieces
4. Mix everything together with two tablespoons of sugar and arrange on a salad bowl

title: Sałata z Pomarańcz i Winogron (Orange and Grape Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- three oranges
- half pound of large white grapes (preferably Malaga)
- half cup of sugar

## Directions

1. Peel three oranges, remove seeds and chop into cubes
2. Rinse half a pound of large white grapes (the best are called Malaga), cut each in half and remove the seeds
3. Add half a cup of sugar and mix everything well
4. This salad is usually served in orange skins, peeled from the oranges and cut in half

title: Sałata z Pomidorów (Tomato Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- four large tomatoes
- two medium cucumbers
- lettuce leaves
- chives, finely chopped
- fresh parsley
- oil
- vinegar
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Pour hot water over the tomatoes, then peel off the skin
2. Slice or cut the tomatoes into pieces
3. Peel and slice the two cucumbers
4. Mix the tomatoes and cucumbers together
5. Arrange on a plate lined with lettuce leaves
6. Sprinkle finely chopped chives and fresh parsley over top
7. Pour French sauce made from oil, vinegar, salt and pepper over everything

title: Sałata z Ryby (Fish Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- any cooked or fried fish
- vinegar
- water
- roots and herbs for boiling
- mayonnaise sauce or tartare sauce
- lettuce leaves

## Directions

1. Take any cooked or fried fish, carefully peel off skin and bones, break into small pieces
2. Pour over cooled boiled vinegar with roots for one hour
3. After one hour, drain from vinegar, arrange on a salad bowl and pour with mayonnaise or tartare sauce, and arrange lettuce leaves around it

title: Sałata z Sardynek i Selerów (Salad with Sardines and Celery)
body:
## Ingredients

- small box of sardines in oil
- celery stalks, twice the amount of sardines
- salt
- pepper
- vinegar
- one cup of milk
- half a teaspoon of flour

## Directions

1. Remove sardines from oil and lay them on paper towels, turning them so all oil is absorbed
2. Dice the celery stalks and sprinkle with salt and pepper, then pour vinegar over them
3. Arrange the seasoned celery in the center of a glass plate in pyramid form
4. Arrange the sardines around the celery
5. Heat one cup of milk and season it with half a teaspoon of flour, pepper and salt
6. Remove from heat and cool completely
7. Pour this sauce over the arranged celery and sardines

title: Sałata z Szynki (Ham Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup cooked ham
- one cup small pickled cucumbers
- three hard-boiled eggs
- one tablespoon vinegar
- one tablespoon butter
- one teaspoon sugar
- one teaspoon mustard
- one egg yolk
- red pepper

## Directions

1. Trim all fat and chop the cooked ham into one cup of pieces
2. Slice the pickled cucumbers into thin slices
3. Slice the three hard-boiled eggs
4. Layer the ham and cucumbers in a bowl, arranging eggs on top
5. Heat one tablespoon of vinegar with one tablespoon of butter
6. Add one teaspoon of sugar, one teaspoon of mustard, one egg yolk, and some red pepper
7. Cook everything for about three minutes, then cool
8. Pour this sauce over the layered ham and vegetables

title: Sałata z Ćwildą (Salad with Beets)
body:
## Ingredients

- lettuce leaves
- red beets
- oil
- vinegar
- pepper
- or mayonnaise sauce

## Directions

1. Wash lettuce leaves and dry them, place in a salad bowl
2. Cook a couple of red beets, peel and slice them
3. Cut the slices into pretty stars, hearts and similar shapes
4. Arrange in the salad bowl so that the beets are in the middle and lettuce around them
5. Pour oil with vinegar and pepper, or mayonnaise sauce over everything

title: Sałata z Ćwildą (Salad with Beets)
body:
## Ingredients

- salad greens
- a few red beets

## Directions

1. Wash salad leaves, dry them and place in a salad bowl
2. Cook a few red beets, peel them and cut into slices from which then cut nice stars, hearts and other shapes

title: Sałata ze Sardynek (Sardine Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- one small can of sardines
- a large handful of lettuce
- four hard-boiled egg yolks
- six tablespoons of oil
- two tablespoons of vinegar
- one small teaspoon of salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Take one small can of sardines, remove the fish, rinse off the oil and carefully remove all the bones
2. Wash and tear a large handful of lettuce into small pieces, place in a salad bowl and mix with the sardines
3. Chop finely four hard-boiled egg yolks and also place in the salad bowl
4. Mix six tablespoons of oil, two tablespoons of vinegar, one small teaspoon of salt and some pepper together and pour this sauce over the salad
5. Mix everything together well and serve at table

title: Sałata ze Sardynek i Selerów (Sardine and Celery Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- one small can of sardines in oil
- celery stalks
- salt
- pepper
- vinegar
- one cup of milk
- half tablespoon of flour

## Directions

1. Take one small can of sardines in oil, remove the fish and lay them on blotting paper, turning them so that all the oil is absorbed
2. Chop the celery stalks into cubes and take twice as much as the amount of sardines
3. Sprinkle with salt and pepper and pour vinegar over it
4. Arrange the prepared celery in the middle of a salad bowl, preferably glass, in the form of a pyramid, and arrange the sardines around the celery
5. Heat one cup of milk, season it with half a tablespoon of flour, pepper and salt, remove from heat and cool

title: Sałata ze Śmietaną (Salad with Sour Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- salad greens
- one cup of thick sour cream
- a few tablespoons of vinegar
- salt
- pepper
- a little sugar

## Directions

1. Sort, wash and drain the salad as above
2. Prepare the dressing as follows: Take one cup of thick sour cream, a few tablespoons of vinegar, salt and pepper to taste, and a little sugar
3. Beat all of this in a small bowl
4. Pour this sauce over the salad and mix well

title: Sałata ze Świeżej Kapusty (Fresh Cabbage Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- white or red cabbage
- boiling water
- vinegar
- oil
- vinegar
- sugar

## Directions

1. Shred white or red cabbage as finely as possible
2. Plunge into boiling water, and as soon as it boils, immediately drain the water and squeeze the cabbage
3. Place in a salad bowl and pour with vinegar; let sit half an hour
4. Drain the vinegar, then pour the cabbage with the following sauce: for one small head of cabbage take two tablespoons oil, one tablespoon vinegar and half tablespoon sugar

title: Sałata ze Świeżej Kapusty (Fresh Cabbage Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- white or red cabbage
- vinegar
- two tablespoons of oil
- one tablespoon of vinegar
- half tablespoon of sugar

## Directions

1. Shred the cabbage as finely as possible
2. Throw it into boiling water, and as soon as it comes to a boil, immediately drain the water and squeeze out the cabbage
3. Place in a salad bowl and pour vinegar over it and let it sit for half an hour
4. Then drain the vinegar
5. For a small head of cabbage, take two tablespoons of oil, one tablespoon of vinegar and half a tablespoon of sugar; mix well and pour over the cabbage
6. Prepare the above sauce an hour before serving the salad; after pouring the sauce, serve immediately at table
7. American version: Heat half a cup of vinegar with one tablespoon of butter in a double boiler; beat one egg yolk with one tablespoon of flour and one tablespoon of sugar, add two tablespoons of sour cream, pour it all into the vinegar and cook stirring until it becomes a smooth mass; beat the egg white, mix with the sauce and immediately pour over the finely shredded and salted cabbage arranged in a salad bowl
8. Or instead of the above sauce, if you want to prepare the salad quickly, pour French sauce made only of vinegar, oil and pepper

title: Schab Pieczony (Roasted Pork Loin)
body:
## Ingredients

- pork loin, weighing at least four pounds
- salt
- marjoram
- apples

## Directions

1. Select a piece weighing at least four pounds, as smaller pieces will dry out too much in the oven and taste poorly.
2. Rub the pork loin with salt and a little marjoram.
3. Place in a medium oven and roast without basting.
4. Near the end of cooking, add a couple of peeled apples cut into quarters.
5. If the pork loin is from a young pig with skin, score the skin in a crosshatch pattern and roast as above.

title: Selery na Biało (Celery in Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- celery stalks (white parts only)
- salted water
- half quart milk
- one onion sliced thin
- one large cup grated bread or white bread
- one tablespoon butter
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Wash and cut celery stalks into one-inch pieces, using only the white parts
2. Take two cups of cut celery and cook to tenderness in salted water
3. In a separate pot pour half quart of milk and throw in one onion cut into thin slices
4. Cook on low fire until the onion is completely tender
5. Strain the milk
6. Add to the milk one large cup of grated bread or white bread and the cooked celery
7. Also add one tablespoon butter and salt and pepper to taste
8. Mix, boil for about three minutes and serve

title: Selery z Marchwią (Celery with Carrot)
body:
## Ingredients

- carrots sliced thin
- celery cut into small cubes in equal quantity to carrots
- salted water
- half quart sweet milk
- one teaspoon cornstarch
- cold milk
- salt
- pepper
- one tablespoon butter

## Directions

1. Slice carrots thinly and cut equal quantity of celery into small cubes
2. Cook the carrots to tenderness in salted water, then drain the water
3. Cook the celery in another pot
4. When both celery and carrot are tender, drain the water and mix both vegetables
5. Add half quart of sweet milk
6. Mix one teaspoon cornstarch in a small amount of cold milk and add
7. Add salt and pepper to taste and one tablespoon butter
8. Mix everything, place on fire, boil once and serve on the table

title: Siekanka na Zimno (Cold Ground Beef)
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds lean raw beef, finely chopped
- one teaspoon salt
- quarter teaspoon pepper
- half teaspoon sage
- two spoonfuls melted butter
- two heaping spoonfuls grated bread
- two beaten eggs

## Directions

1. Chop two pounds lean raw beef very finely
2. Add one teaspoon salt, quarter teaspoon pepper, half teaspoon sage and two spoonfuls melted butter
3. Grate bread very finely and take two heaping spoonfuls, add two beaten eggs
4. Mix everything well and form into one long loaf and place in oven

title: Siekanka na Zimno (Cold Meatloaf)
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds lean raw beef
- one teaspoon salt
- one quarter teaspoon pepper
- one half teaspoon sage
- two tablespoons melted butter
- two heaping tablespoons finely grated bread
- two beaten eggs

## Directions

1. Chop the beef very finely
2. Add salt, pepper, sage, and melted butter
3. Grate bread very finely and take two heaping tablespoons
4. Add two beaten eggs
5. Mix everything well
6. Form the mixture into one long loaf and place on a baking sheet
7. Bake in the oven, basting frequently during baking
8. Remove from the oven and place in a cold location
9. When completely cooled, slice and serve as an appetizer
10. This meatloaf can also be served hot

title: Siekanka Przysmażana (Pan-Fried Meatloaf)
body:
## Ingredients

- leftover roasted meat from the previous day or several types of various meats
- salt and pepper to taste
- celery stalk with leaves
- cooked potatoes, mashed, equal in quantity to the meat
- butter or lard for frying

## Directions

1. Remove all fat from the meat
2. Chop the lean meat finely
3. Add salt and pepper to taste
4. Add celery stalk with leaves
5. Add mashed cooked potatoes equal in quantity to the meat
6. Mix everything well
7. Spread on a work surface
8. Form thin cutlets or one flat patty from the mixture
9. Fry in butter or lard in a pan until both sides are well browned

title: Siekanka z Jaj i Kartofli (Hash of Eggs and Potatoes)
body:
## Ingredients

- six medium potatoes
- six eggs
- one tablespoon butter
- two tablespoons sour cream or milk
- salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Peel and boil six medium potatoes.
2. Drain the water and let cool.
3. Hard boil six eggs for fifteen minutes.
4. Plunge into cold water and let cool.
5. Chop the eggs and potatoes together, not too finely.
6. Season with salt and pepper.
7. Place in a small pan with one tablespoon of butter and two tablespoons of sour cream or milk.
8. Put on the fire, stir slowly, and when it comes to a boil, serve immediately to the table.

title: Siekanka z Jajkiem (Ground Meat with Egg)
body:
## Ingredients

- any cold roasted meat, finely chopped
- half quarter-weight of chopped meat in grated bread
- salt
- pepper
- butter
- eggs

## Directions

1. Chop finely any cold roasted meat
2. For each portion, use small cup of grated bread, salt and pepper to taste, mix well
3. Divide this mass into as many portions as there are people, place on small saucers
4. Into each break one egg, add half teaspoon butter and place in oven
5. When egg begins to set, sprinkle slightly with grated bread
6. Remove from oven and serve immediately

title: Siekanka z Pomidorów, Sposób 1 (Tomato Chutney, Method 1)
body:
## Ingredients

- a bunch of green tomatoes
- two pounds of common raisins
- one pound of currants
- five pounds of brown sugar
- one tablespoon of cinnamon
- one tablespoon of cloves
- three nutmegs
- quarter cup of vinegar

## Directions

1. Chop a bunch of green tomatoes
2. Add two pounds of common raisins, one pound of currants, five pounds of brown sugar
3. Add cinnamon, cloves, and nutmegs, which must be ground
4. Cook for about three hours, and when almost soft, add quarter cup of vinegar
5. Fill jars and seal

title: Siekanka z Pomidorów, Sposób 2 (Tomato Chutney, Method 2)
body:
## Ingredients

- a bunch of green tomatoes
- half a bunch of peeled and chopped apples
- one pound of common raisins
- one pound of currants
- two cups of finely chopped beef suet
- one chopped lemon
- three pounds of sugar
- one cup of vinegar
- two tablespoons of cinnamon
- half a teaspoon of cloves
- half a teaspoon of nutmeg

## Directions

1. Scald a bunch of green tomatoes with boiling water, peel and chop
2. Scald with boiling salted water and drain the water
3. Add half a bunch of peeled and chopped apples, one pound of common raisins, one pound of currants
4. Add two cups of finely chopped beef suet, one chopped lemon, three pounds of sugar, one cup of vinegar
5. Add cinnamon, cloves, and nutmeg
6. Cook for half an hour
7. Fill jars, seal tightly and store

title: Skorupki Rakowe Nadziewane (Stuffed Crayfish Shells)
body:
## Ingredients

- couple of dozens of crayfish
- salt and pepper
- a tablespoon of raw butter
- a tablespoon of grated bread roll
- chopped parsley
- nutmeg
- two egg yolks

## Directions

1. Cook a couple of dozens of crayfish with salt and pepper
2. When cool, remove meat from the necks and legs and chop finely
3. Mix with a tablespoon of raw butter, a tablespoon of grated bread roll
4. Add some chopped parsley, nutmeg, and salt to taste, and beat in two egg yolks
5. Mix everything into a smooth mass and fill the emptied and cleaned crayfish shells with this stuffing
6. Cook the prepared shells in crayfish soup, or they can be lightly fried in butter

title: Smażona Kura z Rosołu (Fried Chicken from Broth)
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked chicken from broth
- one egg
- grated bread
- hot fat for frying
- broth
- butter
- flour

## Directions

1. After cooking the broth with the chicken, remove the meat onto a colander so it dries completely.
2. Take one piece at a time, dip it in beaten egg and then in grated bread.
3. Fry on hot fat for about ten minutes until browned, watching carefully so as not to burn it.
4. Place on a platter and pour over a sauce made from broth, butter and flour.

title: Soczewica (Lentils)
body:
## Ingredients

- one half quart of lentils
- water
- one tablespoon butter
- one half tablespoon flour
- a little vinegar
- burned sugar
- fried sausages or slices of smoked meat

## Directions

1. Cover half a quart of lentils with water, bring to a boil, drain this water, rinse the lentils, cover with other hot water and cook until tender.
2. When completely soft, bring to a boil on white one tablespoon of butter with half a tablespoon of flour and add, also add a little vinegar and burned sugar, mix well and serve on the table, arranging fried sausages or slices of smoked meat around it, which can be cooked in the lentils.

title: Sok Winogronowy (Grape Juice)
body:
## Ingredients

- ten pounds grapes
- water

## Directions

1. Remove ten pounds of grapes from stems, place in an enameled pot, add some water and cook until they fall apart
2. Strain through thick cloth, return the juice to the pot
3. Add three pounds of sugar, then bring to a boil
4. Pour into bottles and seal tightly with corks

title: Sos a la Maitre d'Hotel (Maître d'Hôtel Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 tablespoon good butter
- 1 tablespoon flour
- 1/4 cup sweet sour cream
- 1 teaspoon chopped parsley
- several small potatoes
- fish stock (optional)

## Directions

1. Brown 1 tablespoon good butter with 1 tablespoon flour
2. Dilute with 1/4 cup sweet sour cream
3. Add 1 teaspoon chopped parsley
4. Cook several small potatoes, cut them into thin slices while hot and add to the sauce
5. The amount of potatoes should be roughly equal to the amount of sour cream
6. If using this sauce for fish, use less sour cream and add some fish stock instead

title: Sos Amerykański do Ryb (American Fish Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup of milk
- one cup of water
- one tablespoon of flour
- cold water
- two eggs
- salt
- pepper
- two hard-boiled eggs

## Directions

1. Mix one cup of milk with one cup of water and boil
2. Then add one tablespoon of flour previously dissolved in a small amount of cold water
3. Beat two eggs and also add to the sauce
4. Salt and pepper to taste and remove from heat
5. Slice two hard-boiled eggs thinly
6. Arrange the boiled fish on a platter, sprinkle with the sliced eggs, and then pour the sauce over it

title: Sos Bawarski (Bavarian Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 quart milk
- 1 egg
- 1/2 cup sugar
- 1 teaspoon corn starch
- vanilla extract

## Directions

1. Heat 1/2 quart milk
2. Beat 1 egg with 1/2 cup sugar and 1 teaspoon corn starch and add to the milk
3. Cook for a while, constantly stirring, until thickened
4. Add vanilla extract to taste
5. This sauce is used for legumes

title: Sos Beszamel (Béchamel Sauce) - American Recipe
body:
## Ingredients

- one and a half tablespoons of flour
- two tablespoons of butter
- one small cup of boiling water
- one bay leaf
- several sprigs of parsley
- one sprig of thyme
- one small piece of carrot
- half an onion
- a bit of nutmeg
- several peppercorns
- salt
- several tablespoons of broth
- one cup of sour cream

## Directions

1. Brown one and a half tablespoons of flour with two tablespoons of butter in a roux
2. Then pour in one small cup of boiling water
3. Tie together one bay leaf, several sprigs of parsley, and one sprig of thyme and place in the sauce
4. Also add one small piece of carrot, half an onion, a bit of nutmeg, and several peppercorns
5. Salt and pour in several tablespoons of broth
6. Cook everything over low heat for one hour
7. Then strain, add one cup of sour cream, boil, and serve

title: Sos Beszamel (Béchamel Sauce) - European Recipe
body:
## Ingredients

- quarter pound of butter
- one tablespoon of flour
- two tablespoons of broth
- half a quart of sour cream, previously boiled
- four egg yolks
- one tablespoon of sour cream
- a bit of nutmeg

## Directions

1. Fry quarter pound of butter with one tablespoon of flour until browned
2. Dissolve in two tablespoons of broth and then slowly pour in half a quart of previously boiled sour cream
3. Cook until the sauce thickens well, constantly stirring so it does not burn
4. Remove from heat and cool
5. When completely cooled, temper with four egg yolks beaten with one tablespoon of sour cream
6. Add a bit of nutmeg

title: Sos Biały (White Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 tablespoon flour
- 1 tablespoon raw butter
- broth or fish stock
- sour cream
- green dill, chopped

## Directions

1. Mix 1 tablespoon flour with 1 tablespoon raw butter until white
2. Dilute with broth or fish stock
3. Add sour cream and chopped green dill
4. Boil and pour over meat or fish

title: Sos Cebulowy Amerykański (American Onion Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 large white onions
- water
- 1 cup unboiled milk
- 1 tablespoon melted butter
- 1/2 teaspoon salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Peel 5 large white onions, cover with water and cook until completely tender
2. Drain water and mash the onions
3. Boil 1 cup unboiled milk and pour into the pot with onions
4. Add 1 tablespoon melted butter, 1/2 teaspoon salt and some pepper
5. Boil once and serve immediately

title: Sos Chile (Chile Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- two dozen large ripe tomatoes
- two medium onions
- three large green chili peppers
- one quart of vinegar
- half a cup of sugar
- one tablespoon of Jamaican pepper (allspice)
- one tablespoon of cinnamon
- one tablespoon of mace
- one tablespoon of ground cloves
- one tablespoon of celery seed

## Directions

1. Chop all the above finely and add one quart of vinegar, half a cup of sugar, one tablespoon of Jamaican pepper (allspice), one tablespoon of cinnamon, one tablespoon of mace, one tablespoon of ground cloves and one tablespoon of celery seed
2. Cook everything for two hours, stirring frequently, then fill jars with this prepared mixture and store in a cool place

title: Sos Cytrynowy Biały (White Lemon Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- one tablespoon of clarified butter
- one tablespoon of flour
- poultry broth or sweet sour cream
- juice from half a lemon
- a bit of sugar
- two egg yolks

## Directions

1. Beat one tablespoon of clarified butter with one tablespoon of flour until white
2. Dissolve in poultry broth or sweet sour cream
3. Squeeze in juice from half a lemon
4. Add a bit of sugar and pour out
5. To have a more substantial sauce with a yellow color, before serving pour in two beaten egg yolks, warm, and serve
6. This sauce is used for poultry, veal, and fish

title: Sos Cytrynowy Rumiany (Brown Lemon Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- one tablespoon of butter
- one tablespoon of flour
- half a glass of strong broth or poultry broth
- juice from half a lemon
- good half a teaspoon of burnt sugar
- lemon slices

## Directions

1. Brown one tablespoon of butter with one tablespoon of flour
2. Dissolve in half a glass of strong broth or poultry broth
3. Squeeze in juice from half a lemon
4. Add good half a teaspoon of burnt sugar for color
5. Boil, strain through a sieve, and pour over meat on which lemon slices have been previously placed, with the seeds removed

title: Sos Francuski do Sałaty (French Salad Dressing)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 teaspoon salt
- 2 pinches pepper
- 1 tablespoon olive oil
- 2 tablespoons olive oil
- 1 tablespoon good vinegar
- onion or onion juice (optional)

## Directions

1. Put 1/4 teaspoon salt and 2 pinches pepper in a cup
2. Pour in 1 tablespoon olive oil and mix well
3. Add 2 more tablespoons olive oil and 1 tablespoon good vinegar
4. If desired, add some onion to taste, preferably onion juice, of which use 3 or 4 drops

title: Sos Grzybowy (Mushroom Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- several dried mushrooms
- 1 tablespoon butter
- 1 tablespoon flour
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Cook dried mushrooms until tender
2. Brown 1 tablespoon butter with 1 tablespoon flour
3. Dilute with the cooking liquid from the mushrooms
4. Add vinegar to taste and finely chopped mushrooms
5. Boil and serve

title: Sos Kaparowy (Caper Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 tablespoon butter
- 1 tablespoon flour
- 1 glass strong bouillon
- capers
- lemon juice
- 1 small glass white wine
- 1 teaspoon sugar

## Directions

1. Brown 1 tablespoon butter with 1 tablespoon flour
2. Dilute with 1 glass strong bouillon
3. Add capers
4. Press in lemon juice to taste
5. Add 1 small glass white wine and 1 teaspoon sugar
6. Boil and use

title: Sos Korniszonowy (Gherkin Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 tablespoon butter
- 1 tablespoon flour
- bouillon or strong broth
- gherkins, sliced
- broth for cooking gherkins
- caramelized sugar
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Brown 1 tablespoon butter with 1 tablespoon flour and dilute with bouillon or strong broth, keeping on heat and stirring constantly
2. In a separate pot, cook gherkins sliced in broth
3. When tender, pour with the broth into the sauce
4. Add some caramelized sugar for color and some vinegar if the sauce is not sufficiently sour
5. Pour over meat
6. Gherkin sauce is served with meat dishes and cutlets

title: Sos Kreolski (Creole Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- one tablespoon of butter
- two medium onions, finely chopped
- one clove of garlic
- one tablespoon of finely chopped cooked ham
- one tablespoon of flour
- one large cup of canned tomatoes
- salt
- red pepper
- one tablespoon of sugar
- two tablespoons of vinegar

## Directions

1. Melt one tablespoon of butter in a pan and add two medium finely chopped onions and one clove of garlic
2. Fry over low heat until the onion slightly browns
3. Then add one tablespoon of finely chopped cooked ham and one tablespoon of flour and fry a few minutes longer
4. Add one large cup of canned tomatoes (about half the contents of a regular can) along with their juice
5. Add salt and red pepper to taste, one tablespoon of sugar, and two tablespoons of vinegar
6. Mix everything together and cook for fifteen minutes
7. Then pour the sauce over meat

title: Sos Maderowy (Madeira Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound of butter
- two tablespoons of flour
- one quart of strong broth
- one glass of Madeira wine
- marinated truffles, mushrooms, or olives

## Directions

1. Brown half a pound of butter with two tablespoons of flour
2. Dissolve in one quart of strong broth
3. Add one glass of Madeira wine
4. Mix thoroughly and cook covered over low heat until the sauce is thick
5. Then strain through a sieve, holding the roux in a bowl with hot water
6. To such sauce one usually adds sliced marinated truffles, mushrooms, or olives
7. Used for sirloin, veal cutlets, etc.
8. The above proportion is for ten persons

title: Sos Majonezowy bez Oliwy (Mayonnaise Sauce without Oil)
body:
## Ingredients

- one egg yolk
- half teaspoon butter
- hot water
- salt
- red pepper
- one tablespoon butter
- one tablespoon vinegar

## Directions

1. Place one egg yolk and half teaspoon butter in a small pot and set the pot in hot water and constantly stir.
2. When it begins to thicken, add a little salt and a pinch of red pepper, then add another tablespoon of butter, adding slowly and continually stirring.
3. When a sufficiently thick and smooth mass forms, cool it, add one tablespoon of vinegar and the sauce is ready for use.

title: Sos Majonezowy Ostry (Sharp Mayonnaise Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- two egg yolks
- half teaspoon salt
- a pinch of red pepper
- one teaspoon dry mustard
- half cup best olive oil
- three tablespoons vinegar
- half lemon

## Directions

1. Beat two egg yolks into a bowl; adding while mixing half teaspoon of salt, a pinch of red pepper and one teaspoon of dry mustard.
2. Mix well, then add half a cup of best olive oil, pouring slowly and constantly mixing.
3. Lastly pour in three tablespoons of vinegar and juice from half a lemon.
4. All these ingredients should be added one by one and well mixed each time.
5. This sauce is used mainly for salads.

title: Sos Majonezowy Provensal (Provençal Mayonnaise Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- three egg yolks
- salt
- half a pound of oil
- two tablespoons of good vinegar
- one teaspoon of sugar

## Directions

1. Beat three egg yolks in a bowl, salt them, and stir with a wooden spoon for twenty minutes
2. When thickened, add half a pound of oil very slowly, preferably drop by drop, constantly stirring with the spoon
3. When thickened, add about two tablespoons of good vinegar and one teaspoon of sugar
4. Keep in a cool place until time of use

title: Sos Majonezowy Provensal (Provençal Mayonnaise Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- three egg yolks
- salt
- half pound olive oil
- two tablespoons good vinegar
- one teaspoon sugar

## Directions

1. Beat three egg yolks in a bowl, salt and stir with a wooden spoon for twenty minutes.
2. When it thickens, add half a pound of olive oil, pouring very slowly, preferably in drops, constantly stirring with a spoon.
3. When it thickens, add about two tablespoons of good vinegar and one teaspoon of sugar and keep in a cool place until ready to use.

title: Sos Meksykański (Mexican Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- one large onion
- one tablespoon butter
- two tomatoes
- one pod red pepper
- one pod green pepper
- one clove garlic
- half teaspoon celery seed
- quarter teaspoon ground black pepper
- one teaspoon Worcestershire sauce

## Directions

1. Chop a large onion into thin slices and fry in one tablespoon butter until lightly browned.
2. Then add: two tomatoes peeled and chopped into thin slices, one pod of red pepper and one pod of green pepper, chopped finely with the seeds, and stew everything under cover until soft.
3. Then add one finely chopped clove of garlic, half teaspoon of celery seed, quarter teaspoon of ground black pepper and one teaspoon of Worcestershire sauce.
4. Mix and pour over meat.
5. This sauce is used for beefsteaks and other fried meat.

title: Sos Musztardowy (Mustard Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 tablespoon butter
- 1 tablespoon flour
- broth or fish stock
- 1 large teaspoon English mustard
- vinegar
- sugar
- a few slices of lemon

## Directions

1. Brown 1 tablespoon butter with 1 tablespoon flour
2. Dilute with broth or fish stock
3. Add 1 large teaspoon English mustard, some vinegar, sugar, and a few slices of lemon
4. Boil together and serve
5. Use for fish and also for sausage

title: Sos Musztardowy (Mustard Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- one tablespoon of butter
- one tablespoon of flour
- broth or fish stock
- one large teaspoon of English mustard
- vinegar
- sugar
- several slices of lemon

## Directions

1. Brown one tablespoon of butter with one tablespoon of flour
2. Dissolve in broth or fish stock
3. Add one large teaspoon of English mustard, some vinegar, and sugar
4. Add several slices of lemon
5. Boil together and serve
6. Used for fish and also for sausage

title: Sos Ogórkowy (Cucumber Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 tablespoon butter
- 1 tablespoon flour
- cooking liquid from dried mushrooms
- cucumber vinegar
- pickled cucumbers, diced
- 1 teaspoon sugar

## Directions

1. Brown 1 tablespoon butter with 1 tablespoon flour
2. Dilute with cooking liquid from dried mushrooms
3. Add some cucumber vinegar, pickled cucumbers cut into small dice and 1 teaspoon sugar
4. Boil and serve
5. Cucumber sauce is used for meat dishes and herring and potato cutlets

title: Sos Ogórkowy Zimny (Cold Cucumber Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 large cucumbers, not very ripe
- 1 onion
- 1/2 teaspoon grated horseradish
- white mustard seeds
- good vinegar

## Directions

1. Grate 3 large cucumbers on a grater and place the mass on a colander to let excess juice drain
2. Chop 1 onion very finely
3. Add 1/2 teaspoon grated horseradish and some white mustard seeds
4. Mix everything together and add enough good vinegar to make the sauce rather thin
5. This sauce, kept in a cool place, can be stored for a longer time without spoiling

title: Sos Ostry do Zimnego Mięsa (Sharp Sauce for Cold Meat)
body:
## Ingredients

- one egg yolk
- one tablespoon olive oil
- three tablespoons grated horseradish
- two tablespoons sour cream
- juice from one grated raw carrot
- one teaspoon strong vinegar
- salt
- white pepper
- strong cold broth or bouillon

## Directions

1. Mix one egg yolk with one tablespoon olive oil.
2. Add three tablespoons of grated horseradish, two tablespoons of sour cream, juice squeezed from one grated raw carrot, one teaspoon of strong vinegar, salt and white pepper to taste.
3. Mix everything into a uniform mass, dilute with strong cold broth or bouillon and strain through a fine sieve.
4. If the sauce is too sharp, it can be tempered after straining by adding one teaspoon of fine sugar.
5. This sauce is used for cold meat, suckling pig or fish.

title: Sos Owocowy (Fruit Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- one glass of any fruit juice
- half a glass of red wine
- one tablespoon of potato starch or cornstarch
- half a glass of water

## Directions

1. Take one glass of any fruit juice and half a glass of red wine
2. Add one tablespoon of potato starch or cornstarch dissolved in half a glass of water
3. Mix well and boil over low heat, constantly stirring
4. This sauce is for legumes

title: Sos Pieczarkowy (Mushroom Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- mushrooms
- water
- juice of 1/2 lemon
- 1 tablespoon good butter
- 1 tablespoon flour
- sweet sour cream
- sugar
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Remove the outer skin from mushrooms, wash carefully, cut larger ones into round slices and cook in a small amount of water
2. During cooking, press in juice of 1/2 lemon to keep mushrooms white
3. When completely tender, brown 1 tablespoon good butter with 1 tablespoon flour without browning
4. Dilute with sweet sour cream
5. Add the mushrooms along with their cooking liquid and boil together
6. Just before serving, add a pinch of sugar and press in some lemon juice to taste

title: Sos Pomidorowy (Tomato Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- six medium tomatoes
- butter
- broth or bouillon
- one teaspoon sugar
- one teaspoon butter
- a little flour

## Directions

1. Chop six medium tomatoes and stew them in butter, moistening with broth or bouillon.
2. Cook until they break down into a paste, then strain through a sieve so that all the seeds remain in it.
3. Then add one teaspoon of sugar.
4. Make a white roux separately with one teaspoon of butter and a little flour, add to the tomatoes, mix well and dilute with strong broth or bouillon to the right consistency.
5. Finally bring to a boil and pour over meat.

title: Sos Ponczowy (Punch Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- one tablespoon of fresh butter
- one teaspoon of sifted potato starch or cornstarch
- half a quarter of white wine
- quarter pound of fine sugar
- juice from half a lemon
- a good glass of good rum

## Directions

1. Rub one tablespoon of fresh butter with one teaspoon of sifted potato starch or cornstarch
2. Dissolve in half a quarter of white wine
3. Place on fire and cook slowly, constantly stirring, until thickened
4. When the sauce is thick, add quarter pound of fine sugar
5. Squeeze in juice from half a lemon
6. Pour in a good glass of good rum
7. Place again on fire and warm well, stirring thoroughly
8. This sauce is served with legumes

title: Sos Rakowy (Crayfish Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked crayfish shells
- butter
- flour
- good raw butter
- salt
- white pepper
- strong broth or bouillon
- two egg yolks
- one tablespoon sweet cream

## Directions

1. Pound cooked crayfish shells in a mortar, place in a small pot, add butter and stew on gentle heat.
2. Crayfish butter will form on the surface, which should be carefully collected with a spoon.
3. Two tablespoons of this butter boil on gentle heat with one tablespoon flour.
4. Add one tablespoon of good raw butter, salt and white pepper to taste.
5. Dilute with one-quarter portion of strong broth or bouillon, place on gentle heat and cook until the sauce is sufficiently thick.
6. Beat two egg yolks with one tablespoon sweet cream and season the sauce, which should previously be removed from heat so that the yolks do not curdle.

title: Sos Sardelowy lub śledziowy (Anchovy or Herring Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- sardines or herring
- chopped onion
- 1 tablespoon butter
- 1 tablespoon flour
- strong broth or bouillon

## Directions

1. Clean, carefully peel the sardines or herring of bones and skin and chop very finely
2. Add some chopped onion and mix well
3. Melt 1 tablespoon butter in a pan, add the sardine or herring mixture and fry
4. In another pan, brown 1 tablespoon butter with 1 tablespoon flour
5. Dilute with strong broth or bouillon to desired thickness, mix everything together, boil and pour over meat

title: Sos Szary z Rodzenkami (Gray Sauce with Raisins)
body:
## Ingredients

- one tablespoon butter
- one tablespoon flour
- strong broth or bouillon
- a few pieces of sugar
- a handful of large seedless raisins
- half lemon
- or vinegar instead
- a few slices of lemon

## Directions

1. Brown one tablespoon butter with one tablespoon flour, dilute with strong broth or bouillon and bring to a boil.
2. Soak a few pieces of sugar in water and brown in a pan and pour into the sauce, also add a handful of large seedless raisins.
3. Squeeze juice from half a lemon, or instead pour in vinegar and bring to a boil.
4. Just before serving, add a few slices of lemon, from which the seeds should be removed as they give a bitter taste.
5. This sauce is used for tongue, veal feet or fish.
6. If it is to be used for fish, instead of broth dilute it with stock cooked from fish.

title: Sos Szczawiowy (Sorrel Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- sorrel leaves
- salted water
- butter
- strong broth or bouillon
- two tablespoons sour cream
- one tablespoon flour
- one teaspoon sugar

## Directions

1. Carefully peel, wash and chop sorrel leaves.
2. Blanch them in salted water and then stew in butter, moistening with a little strong broth or bouillon.
3. Strain through a sieve, add two tablespoons of sour cream beaten with one tablespoon flour.
4. Add one teaspoon of sugar, bring to a boil and pour over meat.
5. Serve with a piece of meat or veal cutlets.

title: Sos Szczypiorkowy (Chive Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 tablespoon butter
- 1 tablespoon flour
- good broth
- 1 tablespoon vinegar
- handful of green chives, finely chopped

## Directions

1. Brown 1 tablespoon butter with 1 tablespoon flour
2. Dilute with good broth
3. Add 1 tablespoon vinegar and a handful of finely chopped green chives
4. Boil and serve
5. Used for meat dishes

title: Sos Szodonowy (Shoddy Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- five or six egg yolks
- quarter pound of fine sugar
- half a quarter of white wine

## Directions

1. Beat five or six egg yolks strongly with quarter pound of fine sugar
2. Pour in half a quarter of white wine
3. Place over low heat and continue beating with a whisk until thickened, but do not allow to boil

title: Sos Tatarski (Tartar Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- one egg yolk
- one teaspoon French mustard
- salt
- ten hard-boiled eggs
- one pound olive oil
- one lemon
- or two tablespoons strong vinegar
- ten small pickles
- ten marinated mushrooms
- or capers and chives

## Directions

1. Mix one egg yolk with one teaspoon of French mustard and salt in a bowl.
2. Remove yolks from ten hard-boiled eggs, strain through a sieve, add to the mixed yolk and blend.
3. Take one pound of olive oil and pour slowly into the yolks, constantly stirring with a wooden spoon until it thickens.
4. Then squeeze juice from one lemon, or pour in about two tablespoons of strong vinegar.
5. Chop finely ten small pickles and ten marinated mushrooms, add to the sauce and mix.
6. Instead of mushrooms and pickles, capers and chives can be used; the sauce will be different but also good.

title: Sos Truflawy (Truffle Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- one tablespoon of butter
- one tablespoon of flour
- strong broth
- one tablespoon of preserved truffle flavor or three fresh truffles and red wine
- one black plum

## Directions

1. Brown one tablespoon of butter with one tablespoon of flour and dissolve in strong broth, pouring enough so the sauce is gravy-like
2. If truffles are preserved, pour in one tablespoon of their flavor; if fresh, cook three pieces in red wine and pour that wine into the sauce
3. Cook one black plum until soft, mash into a paste, mix with the sauce, and strain through a sieve
4. Slice the truffles into round slices, add to the sauce, and boil

title: Sos z Jabłek (Apple Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- sour apples with skin
- water
- sugar

## Directions

1. Cut sour apples with skin, remove seeds, cover with enough water to barely cover them
2. Cook until completely softened
3. Pass through a sieve
4. Add some sugar and serve
5. If the sauce is too thin, add a little water
6. This sauce is served with schnitzel, pork or lamb cutlets, ducks and geese

title: Sos z Kartofli i Pieczarek (Potato and Mushroom Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- several peeled potatoes
- broth or bouillon
- 1 large tablespoon dried mushrooms
- butter
- flour
- salt
- parsley or dill (optional when serving with meat)

## Directions

1. Slice or cut potatoes into strips and cover with broth or bouillon and cook
2. Scald and braise 1 large tablespoon dried mushrooms in butter
3. Add to the cooked potatoes
4. Season with a heaping teaspoon of butter mixed with a little flour, stir and pour over meat
5. When serving with meat, add some chopped parsley or dill

title: Sos z Kartofli i Pieczarek (Potato and Mushroom Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- several raw potatoes
- broth or bulion
- one large tablespoon of dried mushrooms
- butter
- butter and flour

## Directions

1. Slice several raw potatoes into slices or strips
2. Pour broth or bulion over them and cook
3. Scald one large tablespoon of dried mushrooms and stew in butter
4. Add to the cooked potatoes
5. Bind with a rounded teaspoon of butter mixed with a bit of flour
6. Mix together and pour over meat

title: Sos Śmietankowy Biały (White Cream Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a quart of sour cream
- two tablespoons of sugar
- a small piece of vanilla or some vanilla extract
- six egg yolks
- two tablespoons of sour cream

## Directions

1. Boil half a quart of sour cream with two tablespoons of sugar and vanilla or vanilla extract
2. Beat six egg yolks and two tablespoons of sour cream together strongly
3. Add to the boiled sour cream and warm together over very low heat, constantly stirring, until it begins to thicken
4. This sauce is used for cold legumes

title: Sos Śmietankowy Białyy (White Cream Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- half quart sweet cream
- two tablespoons sugar
- a small piece of vanilla or a little vanilla extract
- six egg yolks
- two tablespoons sweet cream

## Directions

1. Bring to a boil together half quart of sweet cream, two tablespoons of sugar and a small piece of vanilla or a little vanilla extract.
2. Beat six egg yolks and two tablespoons of sweet cream vigorously, add to the boiled cream and heat together until it comes to a boil.

title: Sos Śmietankowy Rumiany (Brown Cream Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- sour cream
- sugar
- vanilla
- one tablespoon of burnt sugar
- six egg yolks
- two tablespoons of cold sour cream

## Directions

1. Boil sour cream with sugar and vanilla as described above
2. Pour in one tablespoon of burnt sugar
3. Beat six egg yolks with two tablespoons of cold sour cream and add to the sauce
4. Then warm the sauce slowly, constantly stirring, until it thickens
5. Cool and serve
6. This sauce prepared this way has both a different color and taste

title: Stokrotki z Jaj (Egg Daisy Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- five hard-boiled eggs
- two tablespoons oil
- small teaspoon lemon juice
- salt
- red pepper
- lettuce leaves

## Directions

1. Hard boil five eggs and place in cold water
2. When completely cooled, peel and slice crosswise into very thin slices, about ten slices per egg
3. Carefully remove the yolks, keeping the egg white rings intact
4. Arrange the egg white rings on a plate lined with lettuce leaves or on a half-dish in a circle like a wreath, with the rings overlapping at the edges
5. Place the yolks in a small bowl and mash them to a smooth paste with two tablespoons of oil and small teaspoon of lemon juice, adding salt and a bit of red pepper
6. Using a small spoon, place a little of this mixture in the center of each white ring
7. This is a decorative salad that looks like a wreath of daisies

title: Stożki z Kartofli (Potato Cones)
body:
## Ingredients

- six large potatoes
- salt
- pepper
- one onion, grated
- two tablespoons milk
- one tablespoon butter

## Directions

1. Boil six large potatoes
