2. Mash them into a mass and add salt and pepper
3. Add one grated onion
4. Take two tablespoons milk and one tablespoon butter and mix everything well
5. Form small cones or pyramids from this mass
6. Place on a baking sheet and put in a hot oven for ten minutes
7. When they are properly browned, serve at table

title: Strudel z Mięsa (Meat Strudel)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 large slice tender beef, beaten very thin
- salt
- pepper
- thin slices of young bacon
- roasted onion
- butter
- grated bread roll
- 1 tablespoon butter
- broth
- 1 tablespoon white wine

## Directions

1. Beat a large slice of tender beef as thin as possible.
2. Season with salt and pepper.
3. Line the entire piece with thin slices of young bacon.
4. Grate roasted onion, add butter and grated bread roll, and mix into a paste.
5. Cover the meat with this filling.
6. Roll up and tie with twine.
7. Brown a tablespoon of butter in a wide pan or on a griddle.
8. Place the meat inside and braise on low heat until well browned.
9. Then add broth and a tablespoon of white wine and braise until tender.

title: Suflet z Jabłek (Apple Soufflé)
body:
## Ingredients

- several tart apples
- two small cups of apple marmalade
- half a pound of sugar
- two egg yolks
- six egg whites
- sugar
- chopped almonds

## Directions

1. Bake several tart apples and press through a colander, then cool
2. When completely cold, take two small cups of this marmalade, add half a pound of sugar and two egg yolks and beat with a spatula in a bowl until white; this should be done in a cool place
3. Beat meringue from six egg whites, carefully mix with the marmalade, arrange on a baking sheet in the shape of a pyramid, sprinkle with sugar and chopped almonds and place in a slow oven for half an hour
4. Proportions for six persons

title: Suflet z Kartofli (Potato Soufflé)
body:
## Ingredients

- two cups grated cooked potatoes
- three tablespoons sour cream
- two egg yolks
- half a teaspoon salt
- two egg whites
- butter for greasing the mold

## Directions

1. Place two cups of grated cooked potatoes in a saucepan
2. Add three tablespoons of sour cream, two egg yolks, and half a teaspoon of salt
3. Place on fire and stir continuously with a spoon
4. When the entire mass is well heated, remove from heat
5. Add two egg whites beaten into a foam and mix lightly
6. Transfer everything to a buttered baking mold
7. Bake in a hot oven until browned

title: Suflet z Moreli (Apricot Soufflé)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a dozen apricots
- quarter pound of sugar
- five egg whites
- apricot preserves

## Directions

1. Peel half a dozen apricots, remove the pits and chop into small pieces, then cook into marmalade with a quarter pound of sugar and let cool
2. Beat five egg whites into stiff meringue and mix a couple of spoonfuls of this meringue with the marmalade, then add all the marmalade to the meringue, mix lightly and arrange on a baking sheet, leveling the top with a knife, and place in a slow oven for twenty minutes
3. After removing from the oven, cover with preserves, preferably also apricot, and serve at table

title: Suflet z Truskawek lub Malin (Strawberry or Raspberry Soufflé)
body:
## Ingredients

- half pound strawberries or raspberries
- half pound sugar
- two egg whites
- four egg whites

## Directions

1. Press half pound strawberries or raspberries (which must be completely ripe and healthy) through a fine sieve
2. Add half pound sugar and two egg whites
3. Beat in a cool place until white
4. Separately, beat stiff meringue from four egg whites and mix with the fruit mixture
5. Arrange on a baking pan, sprinkle with sugar and put in a very slow oven for half an hour

title: Suflet z Truskawek lub Malin (Strawberry or Raspberry Soufflé)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound of strawberries or raspberries, completely ripe and healthy
- half a pound of sugar
- two egg whites
- four egg whites

## Directions

1. Press half a pound of strawberries or raspberries through a fine sieve, which must be completely ripe and healthy; sprinkle in half a pound of sugar, add two egg whites and beat in a cool place until white
2. Separately beat a stiff meringue from four egg whites and mix with the berry mass, arrange on a baking sheet, sprinkle with sugar and place in a very slow oven for half an hour; the oven must be only moderately warm, as in a hot oven the soufflé will fall and become a cake: this condition must be observed when making all soufflés
3. When the soufflé has risen well, remove and serve at table, giving sweet cream separately
4. Proportions for five persons

title: Syrop z Cytryn (Lemon Syrup)
body:
## Ingredients

- twelve lemons (for juice)
- six lemons (for zest)
- six pounds sugar
- minimal amount of water

## Directions

1. Extract juice from twelve lemons, add grated zest from six lemons, strain
2. Add thick syrup made from six pounds of sugar with the smallest amount of water; the syrup should first be cooled
3. Pour into bottles and cork
4. These syrups can be served with water in summer instead of lemonade

title: Syrop z Truskawek lub Malin (Strawberry or Raspberry Syrup)
body:
## Ingredients

- ripe and perfectly healthy berries
- one quart syrup made from sugar and water (for each quart of juice)

## Directions

1. Select ripe and completely healthy berries, place in a piece of clean cloth or bag and crush
2. Press out all the juice into a prepared vessel
3. For each quart of this juice, take one quart of regular syrup made from sugar and water
4. Mix and cook on very low heat for one hour
5. Then pour into bottles and seal hermetically with corks

title: Szarlotka Czekoladowa (Chocolate Charlotte)
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup flour
- baking powder
- salt
- one heaping tablespoon lard
- very cold water
- two cups milk
- one cup sugar
- butter the size of a walnut
- four egg yolks
- three tablespoons cornmeal
- cold milk
- vanilla extract
- whipped sweetened cream

## Directions

1. To one cup flour add a pinch of baking powder, a pinch of salt and one heaping tablespoon lard
2. Mix everything perfectly, then add just enough very cold water to make a stiff dough
3. Divide the dough into two halves and roll each out to the size of a large plate
4. Line the bottom and sides of a deep baking pan with this dough and bake in a hot oven
5. Heat two cups milk
6. Add one cup sugar and a piece of butter the size of a walnut
7. Beat four egg yolks and add to the milk
8. Mix three tablespoons cornmeal in cold milk and pour into the pan with hot milk
9. Cook until it thickens, then add one teaspoon vanilla extract
10. Fill the baked dough in the pans with this mixture
11. Spread sweetened whipped cream on top and serve

title: Szarlotka Kremowa po Bostońsku (Boston Cream Charlotte)
body:
## Ingredients

- three eggs
- one cup sugar
- one and a half cups sifted flour
- one heaping teaspoon baking powder
- salt
- half quart milk
- two eggs
- half cup flour
- half cup sugar
- one tablespoon butter
- vanilla or lemon extract

## Directions

1. Beat three eggs and add one cup sugar
2. Add one and a half cups sifted flour mixed with one heaping teaspoon baking powder and a pinch of salt
3. Mix everything together into dough
4. Divide dough into two halves and pour onto two baking pans
5. Bake in the oven until the cakes brown slightly and take on a dark straw color
6. Remove and cool
7. Bring half quart milk to a boil
8. Beat two eggs and add to them half cup flour and half cup sugar, well mixed
9. Mix everything well and pour into the milk as soon as it comes to a boil
10. Add one tablespoon butter and a little salt and cook on low heat until it thickens
11. Split each previously baked cake in half with a sharp knife
12. Spread cooled cream on the bottom half and cover with the top half
13. Place in a cold spot to cool well
14. It is good to add one teaspoon vanilla or lemon extract to the cream right after cooking

title: Szarlotka z Cielęciny i Szynki (Veal and Ham Charlotte)
body:
## Ingredients

- cold veal from a roast or stew, minced finely to make one and a half cups
- one cup of cooked ham, minced finely
- one small onion, grated
- salt and pepper to taste
- some celery salt
- one egg
- half cup of water
- flour
- lard
- cold water

## Directions

1. Mince finely one and a half cups of cold veal and one cup of cooked ham.
2. Mix them together and add one small onion grated on a grater, salt and pepper to taste, some celery salt, one egg and half cup of water.
3. Mix everything into a smooth mass.
4. Make soft dough from flour, lard and cold water, place on work surface and roll out fairly thinly.
5. Fill a metal plate with the meat mixture, cover the top with the rolled out dough, seal the edges and bake in a moderate oven.

title: Szarlotka z Daktyli (Date Charlotte)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound dates
- cold water
- two cups milk
- three eggs
- salt
- grated nutmeg
- flour
- lard

## Directions

1. Rinse one pound dates in warm water
2. Place in a saucepan and add just enough cold water to barely cover them
3. Let stand overnight
4. Next day, place on the fire and cook until completely soft
5. Remove the pits
6. Add two cups milk, three eggs, a little salt and grated nutmeg
7. Mix everything into a smooth paste
8. Make dough from flour, lard and cold water
9. Line a baking pan with this dough, rolled out quite thin
10. Fill with the date mixture and bake
11. The proportion above is for two large charlottes

title: Szarlotka z Jaj (Egg Charlotte)
body:
## Ingredients

- five hard-boiled eggs
- grated rolls
- butter
- milk
- salt and pepper to taste

## Directions

1. Hard boil five eggs and cut into thin slices.
2. On the bottom of a cake form, spread a layer of grated bread.
3. Then arrange a layer of sliced eggs, salt, pepper to taste, and a few small pieces of butter.
4. Repeat with another layer of bread and eggs and so on until the form is full.
5. Sprinkle grated bread on top and pour over one cup of milk.
6. Bake in a slow oven until everything is well browned.

title: Szarlotka z Suszonych Śliwek (Dried Plum Charlotte)
body:
## Ingredients

- dried plums
- one cup plum puree
- one cup thin cream or half milk and half cream
- one teaspoon cornmeal
- cold milk
- two egg yolks
- one-third cup sugar
- fruit juice
- flour
- lard
- water
- two egg whites
- two tablespoons fine sugar

## Directions

1. Cook dried plums until soft
2. Press through a sieve and take one full cup of the puree
3. Bring one cup thin cream (or half milk and half cream) to a boil
4. When it begins to boil, pour in one teaspoon cornmeal mixed in a little cold milk
5. When it begins to thicken, pour into the plum puree
6. Add two beaten egg yolks and one-third cup sugar
7. It is also good to add a couple of tablespoons of some fruit juice
8. Line a baking pan with soft dough made from flour, lard and water
9. Spread the plum mixture on top and put in a hot oven
10. After baking, remove and cover the top with meringue made from two egg whites beaten with two tablespoons fine sugar
11. Put in a hot oven for one or two minutes
12. When browned, serve to the table
13. This charlotte can also be served cold

title: Szczaw na Jarzynę (Sorrel as a Vegetable)
body:
## Ingredients

- sorrel leaves
- butter
- sour cream
- flour
- salt
- sugar

## Directions

1. Peel and carefully rinse the sorrel leaves, cook in water and chop very finely.
2. Melt plenty of butter in a pan, add sorrel and braise briefly.
3. Beat thick sour cream with some flour and season the sorrel with it.
4. Add salt to taste and a little sugar if too sour.
5. Serve with veal cutlets or a piece of meat.
6. Alternatively, braise sorrel in butter raw without previous cooking.

title: Szczupak Faszerowany po Żydowsku (Stuffed Pike Jewish-Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- pike, at least three pounds
- one soaked roll per pound of meat
- one tablespoon butter
- one large onion
- salt and white pepper
- mixed vegetables

## Directions

1. Clean the pike carefully
2. Slit the belly and remove all meat, leaving only a little near the tail, without damaging the skin
3. Separate the meat carefully from bones and chop very finely
4. Add one soaked roll per pound of meat
5. Melt one tablespoon butter in a pan, add one large previously scalded and grated onion and fry, being careful not to brown
6. Add this to the fish, season with salt and white pepper to taste
7. Mix everything thoroughly into a uniform mass
8. Stuff the pike with this forcemeat, giving it a neat form
9. Place in an oblong pan with plenty of mixed vegetables on the bottom
10. Cover with cold water and boil for one hour
11. When stuffing, be careful not to add too much forcemeat as it swells during cooking and the skin may burst
12. Serve hot with white sauce or parsley sauce, or cold with vinegar and oil
13. Alternatively, cut the pike into large rings first, remove the meat without damaging the skin, chop and season as above, stuff each ring separately and cook as usual

title: Szczupak lub Sandacz Gotowany (Boiled Pike or Sandacz)
body:
## Ingredients

- large pike or sandacz fish
- salt
- various vegetables
- onion
- roots
- flour
- unsalted butter
- sour cream
- lemon juice
- mushrooms (if available)

## Directions

1. Clean and salt the large fish an hour before use.
2. Prepare a court-bouillon (smak) from various vegetables, onion and roots.
3. Cool the court-bouillon and pour it over the fish.
4. Cook until the fish is tender, being careful not to overcook it.
5. In another pan, make a roux by frying one spoon of flour with one spoon of unsalted butter.
6. Dilute the roux with a quarter of the court-bouillon from the cooked fish.
7. Add two spoons of sour cream and some lemon juice to the sauce.
8. If available, add several sliced mushrooms.
9. Place the cooked fish in this sauce, bring to a boil and serve at table.

title: Szczupak lub Sandacz Gotowany (Boiled Pike or Zander)
body:
## Ingredients

- large pike or zander
- mixed vegetables
- onion
- roots
- flour
- unsalted butter
- fish stock

## Directions

1. Clean and salt the large fish one hour before use
2. Cook a stock from mixed vegetables, onion and roots, cool and cover the fish with it
3. Boil until the fish is tender, being careful not to overcook
4. In another pan, make a roux with one tablespoon flour and one tablespoon unsalted butter
5. Thin with a quarter of the stock from cooking the fish

title: Szczupak lub Sandacz z Sosem (Pike or Zander with Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- pike or zander
- mixed vegetables and roots
- butter
- flour
- sour cream

## Directions

1. Cook a stock from mixed vegetables and roots and cool
2. Clean the fish and cut into pieces
3. Cover with the stock and cook until tender
4. Remove the fish and make a white sauce from the remaining stock, adding butter, flour and a few tablespoons of sour cream
5. Alternatively, serve with parsley sauce poured over the arranged fish on a platter

title: Szczupak Pieczony w Słoninie (Pike Roasted in Bacon)
body:
## Ingredients

- large pike
- salt
- white pepper
- young bacon
- cotton thread

## Directions

1. Clean the large pike carefully.
2. Salt and sprinkle it lightly with white pepper.
3. Cut young bacon into broad but very thin slices.
4. Wrap the entire pike with these bacon slices.
5. Tie with cotton thread so the bacon lies smoothly on the fish.
6. Place the prepared pike in a roasting pan.
7. Roast in a moderately hot oven, basting frequently with the drippings from the fish.

title: Szczupak w Galarecie (Pike in Aspic)
body:
## Ingredients

- pike, completely fresh
- salt, roasted
- parsley
- carrot
- onion
- other vegetables
- pepper, a few grains
- egg white
- water

## Directions

1. For good aspic, pike must be absolutely fresh.
2. Clean and cut the fish into bell-shapes and salt with roasted salt, letting it rest for a few hours.
3. Chop parsley, carrot, onion and other vegetables, add a few peppercorns and prepare, preferably in a clay pot.
4. Cook until the broth takes on the color of consommé, then set aside and let cool.
5. Place pike in a pan, cover with cooled vegetable broth and cook for half an hour, then add the rest of the fish and pour in some sauce that formed from the salt and cook until tender.
6. When the fish is cooked, arrange it on a platter and place in a cold location to cool.
7. Continue cooking the remaining broth in the pan over low heat, constantly skimming.
8. When the broth becomes sticky, add egg white beaten with water to clarify it, cook for a moment and strain through dense sieve or cloth.
9. Remove the back bone from each bell-shaped piece of fish, remove the skin and arrange nicely in a salad bowl.
10. Pour the cooked broth over it, pouring slowly through a napkin.
11. Do not decorate with anything, as the aspic flavor will be spoiled.
12. Serve at table with vinegar and oil.

title: Szczupak z Chrzanem (Pike with Horseradish)
body:
## Ingredients

- pike
- salt and pepper
- horseradish root
- butter
- sour cream

## Directions

1. Clean the pike as usual
2. Cut into rings, sprinkle with salt and pepper
3. Grate a good amount of horseradish root and scald with boiling water
4. Put one tablespoon butter in a saucepan, then add the chopped fish
5. Sprinkle with horseradish and cover with sour cream
6. Cover and stew on low heat until tender, which should take at most half an hour
7. During cooking, shake the pan frequently to prevent burning

title: Szczupak z Jarzynami (Pike with Vegetables)
body:
## Ingredients

- parsley roots
- celery roots
- carrot
- large onion
- pike
- butter
- salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Take a couple of thick parsley roots, celery roots, one carrot, one large onion and chop everything very finely, like pasta
2. Scald with boiling water
3. Clean and cut the pike into rings
4. Put one tablespoon butter on the bottom of a saucepan
5. Layer chopped vegetables, then a layer of fish, sprinkle with salt and pepper
6. Continue alternating vegetables and fish, finishing with a piece of butter on top
7. Cover tightly and stew on low heat, shaking the pan, until the fish is tender
8. Remove the fish carefully and arrange on a platter
9. Arrange the vegetables around it

title: Szerbet z Truskawek (Strawberry Sherbet)
body:
## Ingredients

- three-quarters pound ripe strawberries
- three glasses water
- several lemon slices
- one teaspoon orange flower water
- one pound sugar

## Directions

1. Crush three-quarters pound of ripe strawberries into a paste, add three glasses of water, add several slices of lemon and one teaspoon of orange flower water
2. Place in a cool place for two or three hours
3. Put one pound of sugar in another vessel, cover with cloth and strain the strawberry juice through this cloth onto the sugar, press out, stir until the sugar completely dissolves
4. Then place on ice

title: Sznycel Wiedeński (Viennese Cutlet)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal meat
- egg
- cold water
- flour
- butter or lard

## Directions

1. Cut the veal into thin large slices and pound each one quite thin, giving it the shape of a cutlet.
2. Salt and dip in egg beaten with a spoonful of cold water and a spoonful of flour; the layer of this batter should be very thin, but the entire cutlet must be covered with it.
3. Heat a spoonful of butter on a pan or griddle, place the cutlet on it and fry until browned on one side.
4. Turn over to the other side, brown similarly and immediately serve to the table.

title: Sznycle Siekane (Chopped Veal Cutlets)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound chopped beef
- half a pound stale white bread
- water
- one or two onions, finely chopped
- salt and pepper to taste
- grated bread

## Directions

1. Take one pound chopped beef and half a pound stale white bread
2. Soak the bread in water, squeeze out, and mix well with the meat
3. Add one or two finely chopped onions, salt and pepper to taste
4. Mix the entire mass thoroughly and brown over gentle heat
5. Cool slightly and form into small round cutlets
6. Sprinkle with grated bread and fry on medium heat, ensuring they are well-fried but not dried out
7. Garlic may be added if desired

title: Szparagi (Asparagus)
body:
## Ingredients

- asparagus
- butter
- bread roll

## Directions

1. Scrape the asparagus and break off the tough ends
2. Tie them in bundles of eight or ten pieces
3. Throw them into a large pot with hot water
4. When it boils, drain the water and pour in fresh hot water
5. Cook until tender
6. Remove, untie, arrange on a serving dish
7. Pour the tips with butter browned with bread roll
8. Serve good melted butter separately on the side
9. Important: do not overcook the asparagus as it will absorb water and become unpalatable
10. Avoid using wilted asparagus, though it can be refreshed by burying it in earth for two feet; when removed after fifteen or twenty hours it will be fresh again

title: Szparagi z Białym Sosem (Asparagus with White Sauce)
body:
## Ingredients

- asparagus
- water in which asparagus was cooked
- two tablespoons flour
- two tablespoons butter
- salt
- pepper
- bread slices (optional)

## Directions

1. After washing the asparagus, break it into one-inch pieces (do not cut)
2. Discard the ends that do not break easily in the fingers as they are not good
3. Throw into boiling water and cook for fifteen minutes
4. Remove and arrange on a serving dish
5. For the sauce: take one cup of water in which the asparagus was cooked
6. Brown two tablespoons flour with two tablespoons butter
7. Add to the water and boil
8. Add salt and pepper to taste
9. Pour the sauce over the asparagus
10. Some arrange the dish with bread slices first, then place the asparagus on them and pour the sauce over

title: Szpinak po Amerykańsku (American-style Spinach)
body:
## Ingredients

- spinach leaves
- water
- salt and pepper to taste
- 1 tablespoon butter per 1/2 quart spinach
- 1/2 tablespoon flour
- 1/2 cup milk
- hard-boiled eggs

## Directions

1. Sort, clean roots from spinach leaves and wash in several waters.
2. Place in a small pot, pour a small amount of water and cook until tender.
3. Drain on a colander; when water drains, chop very fine, season with salt and pepper to taste.
4. For every 1/2 quart of chopped spinach, take 1 tablespoon butter and 1/2 tablespoon flour.
5. Place in a pan and make a roux, not allowing it to brown.
6. When the flour is made into a smooth paste, add the spinach to the pan and fry for five minutes, stirring with a spoon.
7. Add 1/2 cup milk and cook for another three minutes.
8. Lay on a hot plate.
9. Cook a couple of eggs hard, cut in slices and garnish the spinach on top.

title: Szpinak z Jajkiem (Spinach with Egg)
body:
## Ingredients

- spinach
- salt
- 1 tablespoon butter
- 1 teaspoon sugar
- 1 tablespoon vinegar
- salt to taste
- hard-boiled eggs

## Directions

1. Rinse spinach thoroughly in several waters.
2. Place in a pot and pour a large amount of salted boiling water over it.
3. Cook for twenty-five minutes.
4. Drain and squeeze out the water, chop the spinach very fine.
5. Return to the pot and add 1 tablespoon butter, 1 teaspoon sugar, 1 tablespoon vinegar and salt to taste.
6. Place on heat and stir; cook until a smooth mass is obtained.
7. Lay on a hot plate and garnish with hard-boiled eggs, cut into quarters or slices.

title: Sztufada (Pot Roast)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 to 6 pounds roasting meat
- bacon, coarsely chopped
- butter
- carrots, chopped
- onions, chopped
- black peppercorns
- bay leaves
- 1 glass white wine or a little clear vodka

## Directions

1. Lard a 4 to 6 pound piece of roasting meat generously with coarsely chopped bacon.
2. Place it in a pot with hot melted butter.
3. Turn the meat on all sides over high heat to brown it well on all sides.
4. When well browned, add chopped carrots, onions, a few peppercorns, and a few bay leaves.
5. Add 1 glass of white wine or a little clear vodka.
6. Cover and braise on very low heat for 3 to 4 hours; the heat must be so low that the meat barely simmers.
7. Before serving, dust the roast with flour, turn it a few times in the sauce to thicken it, then place on a platter and pour the strained sauce over it.

title: Sztuka Mięsa Biała (White Boiled Meat)
body:
## Ingredients

- cross-cut meat
- vegetables (carrots, cabbage, celeriac)
- small potatoes
- butter
- flour
- fresh parsley or dill
- broth

## Directions

1. Cook broth with vegetables as usual.
2. When the meat is almost done, remove it from the broth and place it in another pot.
3. Pour a small amount of broth (with all fat removed) over it.
4. Add a large variety of finely cut vegetables and braise covered on low heat for 3/4 hour.
5. Prepare potatoes by blanching a few, cutting them into slices, putting them in a pot, covering with fat broth, and cooking.
6. Then add a little butter mixed with a spoonful of flour and chopped fresh parsley or dill.
7. Serve the meat on a platter with various vegetables cooked in broth (such as cabbage cut into quarters, carrots, celeriac) and the potatoes prepared as above, pouring the sauce over it.

title: Sztuka Mięsa na Zimno (Cold Beef)
body:
## Ingredients

- thick piece of cross-cut beef
- bacon, sliced thick and sprinkled with salt and pepper
- water
- two veal feet
- vegetables for broth
- salt
- caramelized sugar

## Directions

1. Lard thick piece of cross-cut beef with bacon sliced into fairly thick strips and sprinkled with salt and pepper
2. Cover with cold water and cook like broth
3. After cooking, remove meat from broth, place in another pot, cover and let cool
4. Separately cook two veal feet with vegetables, clarify, color with caramelized sugar and chill to aspic
5. When meat is completely cold, slice thinly and surround with veal foot aspic cut into cubes
6. Serve separately with oil and vinegar

title: Sztuka Mięsa Opiekana (Roasted Meat Preparation)
body:
## Ingredients

- meat cooked until tender in broth
- horseradish
- sour cream
- lemon juice
- broth
- salt to taste
- two egg yolks
- sugar
- optional: béchamel sauce or butter and flour sauce with broth
- optional: grated cheese or parmesan

## Directions

1. Remove cooked meat from broth and cut into long pieces, arrange on a metal or ceramic plate
2. Grate several spoonfuls of horseradish on a grater, pour boiling water over it and place in a pot
3. Add two spoonfuls of sour cream, juice from half a lemon, several spoonfuls of broth and salt to taste
4. Bring to boil, then beat two egg yolks and pour into the horseradish
5. Immediately remove from heat, add one teaspoon of sugar and pour this sauce over the meat
6. Place in oven to brown well and serve at table
7. Optional: instead of horseradish sauce, use béchamel sauce or sauce made from butter and flour with addition of several spoonfuls of broth
8. Some prefer to sprinkle with grated cheese or parmesan and then place in oven for browning

title: Sztuka Mięsa Opiekana (Roasted Meat with Horseradish)
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked meat
- horseradish sauce

## Directions

1. Remove meat cooked to tenderness in broth.
2. Cut into oblong pieces and arrange on a glazed or ceramic plate.
3. Pour horseradish sauce prepared in the following manner over it.

title: Sztuka Mięsa Parowana (Steamed Meat)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 to 4 pounds middle cross-cut meat
- young bacon, thick pieces
- butter
- vegetables: parsley, carrots, celeriac, celery, cauliflower, potatoes, and other vegetables as available

## Directions

1. Take a piece of middle cross-cut meat, remove the bones, beat well, and lard it with thick pieces of young bacon.
2. Salt it and let it sit for 1/2 hour.
3. For 3 to 4 pounds of meat, place 1 tablespoon of butter in a pot, lay the meat on it.
4. On top sprinkle a large amount of finely shredded vegetables: parsley, carrots, celeriac, celery, cauliflower, potatoes and any other vegetables available.
5. Place 1/2 tablespoon of butter on top, cover, and place this pot in another pot with boiling water, adding water continuously as it evaporates.
6. Cook in this manner for 5 hours without disturbing the meat.
7. Then place on a platter and pour the sauce with vegetables over it; very little sauce should remain.

title: Sztuka Mięsa po Amerykańsku (American-Style Beef)
body:
## Ingredients

- four pounds beef
- two quarts water
- four cloves
- piece of cinnamon
- salt
- pepper
- small amount of vegetables
- flour
- fresh parsley, finely chopped

## Directions

1. Cook four pounds beef in two quarts water with four cloves, piece of cinnamon, salt, pepper and small amount of vegetables
2. When meat is tender, place on platter
3. Thicken remaining broth in pot with flour mixed previously in cold water
4. Add finely chopped fresh parsley
5. Pour sauce over meat on platter and serve immediately

title: Sztuka Mięsa po Włosku (Italian-Style Beef)
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked beef
- eggs
- grated bread
- grated parmesan
- fat or butter for frying
- caper sauce

## Directions

1. Remove cooked beef from broth and let cool slightly
2. Cut into even slices
3. Beat eggs, add some grated bread and grated parmesan, mix to fairly thin consistency
4. Dip sliced meat in this mixture, coat with grated bread mixed with parmesan
5. Fry in hot fat or butter until well browned on both sides
6. Arrange on platter and pour caper sauce over

title: Sztuka Mięsa z Chrzanem (Beef with Horseradish)
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked beef
- horseradish sauce
- grated bread
- melted butter

## Directions

1. Cut cooked beef into slices and arrange on a platter
2. Pour horseradish sauce over it
3. Sprinkle lightly with grated bread, drizzle with melted butter
4. Place in oven for ten minutes
5. When browned on top, remove and serve immediately

title: Szynka Gotowana (Boiled Ham)
body:
## Ingredients

- ham with skin
- water
- vegetables
- pepper
- bay leaves
- green peas
- horseradish

## Directions

1. Select ham from young pigs that is not too fatty.
2. Always cook ham with its skin on, as ham cooked without skin will be tasteless.
3. Before cooking, wash the ham well in water and soak in cold water for four hours.
4. Place in a pot, cover with fresh cold water.
5. Add some vegetables, pepper, and a couple of bay leaves.
6. Cook until tender, which will take three to five hours depending on size; it is done when the meat separates easily from the bone.
7. If the ham is to be served hot: remove the skin after cooking, separate the meat from the bone, slice the meat and arrange the slices back around the bone on a platter; serve with green peas and horseradish.
8. If the ham is to be used cold, leave it in the cooking water until completely cooled.
9. To carve: with a sharp knife break out the lower bone, then remove the round bone next to it; remove the skin and carve from the side where the bone was; when you reach the thick leg bone, turn the ham over and carve from the other side.

title: Szynka Opiekana (Baked Ham)
body:
## Ingredients

- medium-sized ham
- cold water
- cloves
- bay leaves
- egg
- bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Wash a medium-sized ham in cold water.
2. Place in a pot, cover with cold water.
3. Add six cloves and a couple of bay leaves.
4. Place over heat and cook fifteen minutes per pound of ham.
5. Cook over low heat and skim.
6. After cooking, remove from heat and let cool without removing from the water.
7. When completely cold, remove the skin from the ham.
8. Brush with egg.
9. Sprinkle with bread crumbs.
10. Place on a roasting pan in a hot oven for fifteen to twenty minutes.

title: Szynka po Angielsku na Dziko (English-Style Ham)
body:
## Ingredients

- eight thin slices of cooked ham
- mustard
- red pepper
- one tablespoon butter
- three or four tablespoons red currant jelly

## Directions

1. Spread each slice of thinly cut cooked ham thinly with mustard
2. Sprinkle with red pepper
3. Melt one tablespoon of butter in a pan
4. Add three or four tablespoons of red currant jelly
5. Bring to a boil together
6. Arrange the ham slices in the pan and fry until slightly browned

title: Szynka Wędzona z Cielęciny (Smoked Veal Ham)
body:
## Ingredients

- a large quarter of veal weighing ten to fifteen pounds
- one pound of salt
- half ounce of powdered saltpeter

## Directions

1. Salt the large quarter of veal with one pound of salt and half ounce of powdered saltpeter and place it in a barrel, pressing it down with a stone.
2. It may be placed together with other meat that is being salted.
3. Every day the meat must be turned over and basted with brine so that it does not dry out on any side.
4. Leave the salted veal to lie for two weeks, then smoke it in a smokehouse like any other cured meat.
5. After smoking, it is best to roast it in a very hot oven, having first covered it with rye dough; it bakes for two to three hours, depending on size.
6. Instead of roasting, it may also be boiled like ordinary ham.

title: Szynka z Makaronem (Ham with Pasta)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound Italian pasta
- salt
- one quarter pound cooked ham
- butter
- sour cream
- cheese

## Directions

1. Cook one pound of Italian pasta in salted water until tender.
2. Drain the water and place the pasta in a serving bowl.
3. Cut or chop one quarter pound of cooked ham into small pieces.
4. Add one tablespoon of butter and three tablespoons of thick sour cream.
5. Add a couple of tablespoons of grated cheese.
6. Mix and place the entire mixture in a buttered cake form or roasting pan.
7. Place in the oven for twenty minutes.

title: Szynka z Makaronem (Ham with Pasta)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound Italian pasta
- salted water
- one-quarter pound cooked ham
- one tablespoon butter
- three tablespoons thick sour cream
- a few tablespoons grated cheese
- butter for greasing the mold

## Directions

1. Cook one pound of Italian pasta in salted water until tender
2. Drain the water and place the pasta in a salad bowl
3. Chop one-quarter pound of cooked ham into small pieces or mince it
4. Add one tablespoon of butter and three tablespoons of thick sour cream
5. Add a few tablespoons of grated cheese
6. Mix well and place the entire mixture in a buttered baking mold or baking pan
7. Place in the oven for twenty minutes

title: Słodkie Kartofle po Kreolsku (Sweet Potatoes Creole Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- six firm sweet potatoes
- salt
- butter
- one egg
- one cup grated bread crumbs or white bread

## Directions

1. Peel and cut six firm sweet potatoes in half lengthwise
2. Place in a deep baking form
3. Sprinkle half a teaspoon salt and add one tablespoon butter
4. Beat one egg and mix with one cup of grated bread crumbs or white bread
5. Cover the potatoes arranged in the form with this mixture
6. Bake in a moderately hot oven for one hour

title: Słodkie Kartofle Zapiekane (Baked Sweet Potatoes)
body:
## Ingredients

- sweet potatoes
- butter
- flour
- salt
- pepper
- milk

## Directions

1. Peel and slice sweet potatoes very thinly lengthwise
2. Butter a baking dish or baking form and place a thin layer of sliced potatoes on the bottom
3. Sprinkle with half a teaspoon flour and a little salt and pepper
4. Place another similar layer and continue until the dish is full
5. Scatter small pieces of butter on top
6. Pour in just enough milk to barely moisten everything
7. Put in a moderately hot oven for forty minutes

title: Talarki z Konfiturami (Tart Wafers with Preserves)
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup flour
- one tablespoon lard
- cold water as needed
- thick preserves, jelly, or jam

## Directions

1. Mix one cup flour well with one tablespoon lard and add enough cold water to make a medium dough
2. Turn out onto a work surface and roll thin
3. Cut into strips about three inches wide and six to eight inches long
4. Spread the strips thickly with thick preserves, jelly, or jam
5. Roll up lengthwise into a log and seal the outer edge well
6. Place on a baking sheet and bake in a hot oven
7. After removing from the oven, cut the entire log into slices and serve

title: Tapioka na Mleku (Tapioca in Milk)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 cup of tapioca
- 2 cups of cold water
- 1 quart of milk
- 2 tablespoons of butter
- 1 onion
- a few celery stalks
- 1 tablespoon of flour
- salt and pepper
- a little ground nutmeg

## Directions

1. Soak 1/2 cup of tapioca in 2 cups of cold water for 5-6 hours
2. Cook in a double boiler for 1 hour
3. In another pot, boil 1 quart of milk
4. Chop 2 tablespoons of butter, 1 onion, and a few celery stalks finely
5. Put them in a pan and fry for 10 minutes
6. Add 1 tablespoon of flour and mix well to obtain a smooth paste
7. Add this to the milk
8. Add salt, pepper, and a little ground nutmeg
9. Cook for another 10 minutes
10. Pour the milk into the tapioca and cook together for 1/2 hour
11. Serve immediately

title: Tort Geraniowy (Geranium Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- half cup butter
- one cup sugar
- two-thirds cup cold water
- two cups flour
- one teaspoon baking powder
- four egg whites
- fresh geranium leaves

## Directions

1. Cream half cup butter with one cup sugar
2. Slowly add two-thirds cup cold water while stirring constantly
3. Add two cups flour sifted with one teaspoon baking powder
4. Beat four egg whites into stiff meringue and gently fold into batter
5. Line a form with buttered paper and place several fresh geranium leaves on the bottom
6. Pour batter over the leaves and place in a moderately hot oven
7. After baking, remove cake and clean off the leaves
8. The geranium leaves impart an unusual flavor and aroma to the cake

title: Tort Kolonialny (Colonial Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup butter
- one and a half cups sugar
- three egg yolks
- one teaspoon vanilla extract
- a little nutmeg
- one cup flour
- one and a half teaspoons baking powder
- half cup milk
- three egg whites
- jam or preserves for filling
- one cup raisins without seeds
- quarter cup sweet almonds
- some grated coconut
- one egg white for the filling
- white icing made from sugar, butter, and milk

## Directions

1. Cream one cup butter, gradually add one and a half cups sugar
2. Add three well-beaten egg yolks and one teaspoon vanilla extract and a little nutmeg
3. Sift one cup flour with one and a half teaspoons baking powder and add to the butter
4. Simultaneously pour in half cup milk and mix well
5. Fold in stiff meringue made from three egg whites
6. Divide batter in half and bake two cakes
7. After removing from oven, spread one with jam or preserves
8. Prepare filling by finely chopping one cup raisins without seeds, quarter cup sweet almonds, and some grated coconut; mix with stiff meringue made from one egg white
9. Place filling on top of the first cake
10. Place second cake on top and cover with white icing made from sugar, butter, and milk

title: Tort Kolonialny (Colonial Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup butter
- one and a half cups sugar
- three egg yolks
- one teaspoon vanilla extract
- pinch of nutmeg
- one cup flour
- one and a half teaspoons baking powder

## Directions

1. Beat one cup of butter until creamy, add one and a half cups of sugar, adding slowly, then three well-beaten egg yolks and one teaspoon of vanilla extract, as well as a pinch of nutmeg
2. Sift one cup of flour with one and a half teaspoons of baking powder and add

title: Tort Zwyczajny (Standard Layer Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- half cup of butter
- one cup of sugar
- half cup of milk
- half teaspoon of salt
- two egg yolks
- two cups of flour
- two teaspoons of baking powder
- two egg whites

## Directions

1. Cream half cup of butter with one cup of sugar
2. Add half cup of milk and half teaspoon of salt
3. Add two egg yolks
4. Mix in two cups of flour with two teaspoons of baking powder sifted together
5. Mix well and then add the egg whites beaten to foam, mixing only lightly
6. Divide the batter in half and bake in a moderately hot oven on two plates
7. After removing from the oven, spread some filling on one layer, place the other layer on top, and frost the entire cake

title: Tort Żurawinowy (Cranberry Cake)
body:
## Ingredients

- two cups flour
- two teaspoons baking powder
- half cup lard
- milk
- butter
- sugar
- cooked cranberries

## Directions

1. Sift two cups flour with two teaspoons baking powder and mix in half cup lard
2. Add enough milk to form a soft dough
3. Divide dough in half and roll each into a round cake, using as little flour as possible
4. Place one cake on a baking sheet or tin plate and brush with butter
5. Place second cake on top and put in a hot oven
6. When the cakes are baked and well browned, remove from oven and set aside for five minutes
7. Separate the cakes and place a layer of cranberries cooked with sugar on the bottom cake
8. Place the second cake on top, cover it with a thinner layer of cranberries, and serve hot

title: Uszka do Barszczu (Little Ears for Borscht)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of lean cooked beef
- a quarter pound of cooked bacon
- a tablespoon of butter
- half a tablespoon of chopped roasted onion
- a few cooked mushrooms, finely chopped
- a couple of tablespoons of water in which the mushrooms were cooked
- a couple of tablespoons of grated bread roll
- one or two egg yolks
- salt and pepper to taste
- half a quart of flour
- one egg
- water

## Directions

1. Chop the cooked lean beef and cooked bacon together very finely, almost into a mass
2. Heat a tablespoon of butter in a pan and add half a tablespoon of chopped roasted onion, then remove from heat
3. Place the chopped meat in the pan with butter, add a few finely chopped cooked mushrooms
4. Pour in a couple of tablespoons of the water in which the mushrooms were cooked and add a couple of tablespoons of grated bread roll
5. Add one or two egg yolks and salt and pepper to taste, mix well
6. Make a soft dough from half a quart of flour, one egg and water
7. Roll out fairly thinly and cut into square pieces, but cut diagonally so that when folded in half they form an even triangle
8. Place filling on the dough, fold the two opposite sharp corners together, seal firmly
9. Drop into salted boiling water and cook
10. This proportion is for eight people
11. For fasting soup uszka, make them the same way but use mushrooms and butter instead of meat and bacon

title: Vinaigrette (Vinaigrette Salad)
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked fish or herring
- potatoes
- beets
- hard-boiled eggs
- gherkins
- pickled cucumbers
- marinated mushrooms
- olive oil
- strong vinegar
- egg yolks
- sugar
- mustard
- capers
- red cabbage

## Directions

1. Cook potatoes and slice thinly
2. Cook beets and slice thinly
3. Add a couple of hard-boiled eggs, a couple of gherkins, pickled cucumbers and marinated mushrooms, all sliced
4. Add any cooked fish, cut into small pieces and mix together
5. Add a couple of tablespoons of olive oil and one tablespoon of strong vinegar
6. Arrange on a platter and garnish nicely on top and around with sliced eggs, beets, cucumbers, capers, red cabbage, etc.
7. Separately, rub four egg yolks with four tablespoons of olive oil, add two tablespoons of vinegar, a little sugar and a touch of mustard
8. Pour this sauce over the vinaigrette arranged on the platter

title: Vol au Vent
body:
## Ingredients

- pike or sandacz fish
- salt
- butter, melted
- lemon juice
- vegetables, various chopped
- pepper, a few grains
- flour
- egg yolks, a few beaten
- fish balls (from the Soup Additions section)

## Directions

1. Clean pike or sandacz, fillet, carefully remove all bones, and cut the meat into neat flat pieces about two inches long.
2. Salt the pieces and let them rest in salt for one hour.
3. After one hour, remove from salt, place on a baking sheet, pour generously with melted butter, sprinkle with lemon juice and place over low heat so it slowly fries without browning.
4. Cover the removed head, fins and bones with water, add miscellaneous chopped vegetables and cook well, then strain.
5. Add some butter in which the fish was stewed, enough flour to create a sauce of medium thickness, squeeze some lemon juice, add a few beaten egg yolks and mix everything well with the remainder of the sauce in which the fish was stewed.
6. Finally add several fish balls (see the Soup Additions section).
7. Arrange the fish on a platter and pour the sauce over it, placing a piece of heated French pastry on top.
8. To serve this dish more elegantly, the fish can be interspersed with stewed mushrooms, and with crayfish necks and claws.

title: Wafle (Waffles)
body:
## Ingredients

- three egg yolks
- one quart milk
- one-half cup melted butter
- two teaspoons baking powder
- flour as needed
- egg whites beaten to stiff peaks

## Directions

1. Beat three egg yolks with one quart milk, one-half cup melted butter, two teaspoons baking powder, and enough flour to make a thin batter
2. Fold in egg whites beaten to stiff peaks
3. Bake on a well-greased waffle iron

title: Whiskey Cocktail
body:
## Ingredients

- two-thirds quart of ordinary vodka
- one-third quart of water
- one liqueur glass of Peyschaud or Angostura Bitters
- two liqueur glasses of syrup

## Directions

1. Prepare in the same way as Brandy Cocktail, using ordinary vodka instead of brandy

title: Wielkopostna Zupa Amerykańska (Lenten American Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 tomatoes
- 2 large potatoes
- 2 onions
- 2 quarts of cold water
- 2 tablespoons of fine tapioca
- a little marjoram
- salt to taste
- 2 small cups of milk
- 1/2 tablespoon of flour
- 1/2 tablespoon of butter
- small croutons

## Directions

1. Pour 2 quarts of cold water over 3 tomatoes, 2 large potatoes, and 2 onions
2. Cook until everything is soft
3. Strain through a sieve
4. Add 2 tablespoons of fine tapioca, a little marjoram, and salt to taste
5. Cook for another 20 minutes
6. Separately boil 2 small cups of milk
7. Mix 1/2 tablespoon of flour well with 1/2 tablespoon of butter
8. Add this mixture to the milk
9. Pour into the soup, boil again, and serve with small croutons

title: Wine from Blackberries (Jerzyny Wino)
body:
## Ingredients

- one gallon of ripe blackberries
- one quart of boiling water
- three quarts of cold water
- two pounds of brown sugar

## Directions

1. Crush one gallon of ripe blackberries
2. Pour one quart of boiling water over the crushed mass and set in a cool place for a whole day
3. After this time, strain through cloth and add three quarts of cold water
4. Add two pounds of brown sugar and stir until all sugar dissolves
5. Pour into a jug or clean barrel and set in a cool place
6. The vessel must be loosely closed or corked so that gas can escape
7. Fermentation should proceed very slowly and last from two to three months
8. When fermentation is complete, pour into bottles and cork well

title: Wine from Cherries (Wiśniowe Wino)
body:
## Ingredients

- one gallon of cherries
- one quart of water
- two pounds of sugar per gallon of liquid

## Directions

1. Crush one gallon of cherries without damaging the pits
2. Add one quart of water and set in a cool place for a whole day
3. Then strain and add two pounds of sugar per gallon of liquid
4. Set again in a cool place for a day
5. Then pour into a barrel or jug, close the opening loosely and set in a cool place for fermentation, which should last from three to six months
6. When fermentation is complete, pour into bottles and cork

title: Wino (Wine)
body:
## Ingredients

- completely ripe grapes
- no additives mentioned in complete portion

## Directions

1. Grapes should be completely ripe, undamaged, unspoiled and completely fresh
2. Sort carefully, crush and press out the juice; when crushing, be careful not to crush the seeds, as they would give the wine a bitter taste
3. Pour the pressed juice into an open vessel and place at a temperature between 58 to 68 degrees Fahrenheit for fermentation
4. The higher the temperature, the faster the fermentation, but be careful not to let it turn to vinegar, which can happen if the temperature is too high
5. Fermentation lasts from three to seven days, and when it stops, which can be recognized by the foam ceasing to be released, the liquid should be strained into another vessel immediately, doing this carefully so all the sediment remains on the bottom
6. Pour the obtained liquid into a small barrel and...

title: Wiśnie z Tapioka (Cherries with Tapioca)
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup tapioca
- cold water
- half a quart of boiling water
- one and a half pounds of sour cherries
- one cup of sugar

## Directions

1. Soak one cup of tapioca overnight in cold water.
2. The next day, place in a pot, pour half a quart of boiling water over it, and cook on a low fire until completely transparent.
3. Remove the pits from one and a half pounds of sour cherries, add one cup of sugar, and mix with the tapioca.
4. Bring to a boil once, pour into a serving dish, and place in a cold place.
5. When completely cool, serve with sugar and cream served separately.

title: Wiśniówka (Cherry Liqueur)
body:
## Ingredients

- ten pounds of wild cherries
- one gallon of brandy
- two pounds of sugar

## Directions

1. Remove pits from ten pounds of wild cherries and crush them, then place both the pits and cherries in a clay jar, cover with one gallon of brandy and let stand in a cool place for eight weeks
2. After this time add two pounds of sugar, strain and then filter through paper and pour into bottles
3. Do not use before six weeks after bottling

title: Wołowina Prasowana (Pressed Beef)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef cooked in broth until meat falls from bones
- salt
- pepper
- melted butter
- broth

## Directions

1. Remove meat from hot broth, trim all fat and cut into small pieces
2. Add salt and pepper to taste, melted butter and mix with cup of broth
3. Mix everything well and place in pot or bowl, cover with lid smaller than pot so it enters inside
4. Place weight of several pounds on top, preferably a pressing iron, and place in cold location
5. When well cooled, slice with sharp knife and serve

title: Wątróbka Cielęca Duszona (Braised Veal Liver)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal liver
- milk
- lard bacon for larding
- butter
- onion, chopped finely
- celery
- parsley
- flour
- sour cream

## Directions

1. Soak the veal liver in milk for an hour, remove the membrane and lard it with bacon.
2. Place a piece of butter, finely chopped onion, celery and parsley in a pan, put the liver on top, cover and braise on low heat.
3. When it is half braised, remove it from the pan, salt it, dust with flour, return it to the pan and continue braising until done, which can be recognized by the fact that no blood appears when pricked with a fork.
4. Be careful not to cook too long, as it hardens; it also hardens if salted too early.
5. After cooking, slice it, arrange on a platter and pour over the sauce remaining in the pan with the addition of several spoonfuls of sour cream.
6. Alternative method: Prepare the liver as above and slice it raw, dust with flour and braise on butter in a pan, but very briefly; toward the end of braising add sour cream; salt not immediately but just before adding the cream.

title: Wątróbka Cielęca Pieczona (Roasted Veal Liver)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal liver
- milk
- lard bacon
- butter
- sour cream
- vinegar
- flour
- salt

## Directions

1. Soak the veal liver in milk for an hour, then remove the membrane and lard it with thinly sliced bacon.
2. Place in the oven. At the beginning of roasting, baste with butter, then subsequently with sour cream with the addition of a spoonful of vinegar.
3. Toward the end of cooking, dust with flour and salt.

title: Wątróbka po Indyjska (Veal Liver Indian Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- one spoonful of fat
- one onion, sliced
- one spoonful of chopped fresh parsley
- two or three spoonfuls of finely chopped smoked bacon
- veal liver
- flour mixed with butter
- half cup of boiling water
- juice of half a lemon
- one teaspoonful of curry powder
- one teaspoonful of finely chopped sorrel

## Directions

1. Brown one spoonful of fat on a pan, add one onion sliced, one spoonful of chopped fresh parsley and two or three spoonfuls of finely chopped smoked bacon.
2. Cut the veal liver into pieces the size of a finger.
3. When the fat on the pan begins to boil, add the liver to the pan and fry until well browned, turning frequently.
4. Remove the liver from the pan and place on a hot platter, keeping it warm in the oven.
5. Strain the remaining sauce through a sieve, rinse the pan with hot water and pour the strained sauce back in and place on heat.
6. Add one spoonful of flour mixed with one spoonful of butter and cook, stirring, until the sauce forms a smooth brown mass.
7. Then add half cup of boiling water, juice of half a lemon, one teaspoonful of curry powder and one teaspoonful of finely chopped sorrel.

title: Wątróbka z Jabłkami (Veal Liver with Apples)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal liver
- milk
- lard bacon
- butter
- vinegar
- one large apple
- sugar

## Directions

1. Soak the liver in milk, remove the membrane and lard it as described above; sprinkle with vinegar and let it rest for an hour.
2. Melt a spoonful of butter in a pan, and when it begins to brown, salt the liver, place it in the butter and braise until the liver browns.
3. Then grate one large apple and add it to the liver, along with a teaspoonful of sugar, and continue braising, watching carefully so it does not burn, as the whole liver would burn.
4. As soon as it is tender, serve to the table.

title: Wątróbka à la Nelson (Veal Liver à la Nelson)
body:
## Ingredients

- veal liver
- lard bacon
- flour
- butter
- onion

## Directions

1. Remove the membrane from the liver, lard it with bacon, cut into thick slices and dust with flour.
2. Place a spoonful of butter in a pan, brown it slightly and add the liver, layering with sliced onion, place a little butter on top, cover and braise for about twenty minutes on strong heat.
3. When the liver is half braised, salt it, add a little pepper, shake the pan and then finish braising.

title: Wątróbki z Drobiu z Ryżem (Poultry Livers with Rice)
body:
## Ingredients

- Half a pound rice
- Hot water
- Butter
- Strong broth or bouillon
- Poultry livers
- Salt

## Directions

1. Pour half a pound of rice with hot water and place on the stove, and when it comes to a boil, drain the water
2. Place one spoonful of butter in a pot, then the parboiled rice and add strong broth or bouillon, then place on low heat and boil
3. When the rice is half tender, place the poultry livers cut in half, cover the pot with a lid and stew on low heat until tender
4. Before serving at table one should salt it and add a little good butter

title: Węgorz Marynowany (Marinated Eel)
body:
## Ingredients

- eel
- vegetables (miscellaneous)
- roots and herbs
- white wine
- vinegar
- parsley
- chives
- dill
- vinegar and oil

## Directions

1. Cook stock from miscellaneous vegetables and roots, then strain and pour over the eel cut into pieces and arranged in a pan; there should be only enough stock to barely cover the fish.
2. Place on heat and cook until completely tender; during cooking add one glass of white wine and half a glass of vinegar.
3. Arrange the cooked eel in a stone jar and cover with the stock.
4. When serving at the table, sprinkle with chopped parsley, chives or dill and pour the sauce over it, and serve vinegar and oil on the side.

title: Węgorz Smażony (Fried Eel)
body:
## Ingredients

- eel
- vegetables (miscellaneous)
- egg
- water
- bread crumbs
- flour
- butter

## Directions

1. After skinning and cleaning the eel, cut it into rings, pour stock made from miscellaneous vegetables over it and cook until it is half cooked.
2. Then remove it, dip in egg beaten with water, coat with bread crumbs mixed with flour and fry in hot butter.
3. The best sauce for eel is caper sauce.

title: Węgorz w Galarecie (Eel in Aspic)
body:
## Ingredients

- eel
- salt
- vegetables (miscellaneous)
- crayfish tails and claws
- parsley

## Directions

1. Skin and clean the eel, salt it, cut it into rings and cook in stock made from vegetables, prepared previously and cooled.
2. When the fish is completely tender, remove it; clarify the remaining stock and place in a cold place to cool.
3. When the aspic begins to set, pour a few tablespoons of it onto a small plate, arrange the cooked crayfish tails and claws on it, intersperse with sprigs of parsley, arrange the fish on top and cover with the remaining aspic, then leave undisturbed until it sets.

title: Zacierka Gryczana (Buckwheat Paste Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- half quart buckwheat flour
- one spoonful wheat flour
- salt
- hot water
- fresh milk

## Directions

1. Take half quart buckwheat flour and one spoonful wheat flour, salt it
2. Scald in a bowl with several spoonfuls of hot water and stir until all flour is moistened
3. Bring a quart or more of fresh milk to boil in a pot
4. Quickly place oblong dumplings on a spoon onto the boiling milk
5. When they float to the surface, serve on table

title: Zacierka na Mleku (Milk with Egg Drop Noodles)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart of milk
- 2 whole eggs
- 4 tablespoons of flour
- cold water

## Directions

1. Boil 1 quart of milk
2. Beat 2 whole eggs with 4 tablespoons of flour and enough cold water to obtain thin batter
3. Make a cone from paper and pour the batter through it onto the boiling milk

title: Zając Duszony (Braised Hare)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 hare
- smoked bacon, thinly sliced
- water or broth
- salt to taste
- pepper to taste
- 1 bay leaf
- pepper grains
- 1 onion
- flour
- butter
- lemon

## Directions

1. Clean and rinse the hare well, quarter it, cover with thin slices of smoked bacon, and fry in a pan on high heat so that it browns on top
2. Remove from pan and place in a pot, add about 1/2 quart of water or broth, salt and pepper to taste, add one bay leaf, a few peppercorns, and one whole onion, and braise covered on low heat until the meat is soft
3. Remove the meat to a platter, add some flour and butter to the pot and make a fairly thick sauce, bring to a boil, and pour over the hare, and around it arrange lemon slices

title: Zając Pieczony (Roasted Hare)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 hare, dressed
- bacon, finely chopped
- weak vinegar
- parsley, chopped
- celery, chopped
- carrots, chopped
- onion, chopped
- pepper
- English herbs
- salt
- sour cream
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Dress and lard the hare densely with finely chopped bacon
2. Take weak vinegar, add plenty of chopped parsley, celery, carrots, onion, as well as some pepper and English herbs, and boil it, then cool it
3. Pour this over the hare and let it marinate in this vinegar for thirty-six hours
4. One hour before roasting, remove the hare and salt it, then place in a roasting pan and roast slowly, continuously basting with sour cream
5. If you want a more sour sauce, you can add some of the vinegar in which the hare marinated to the sour cream
6. Roasted red beets are usually served alongside the hare

title: Zając po Indyjsku (Hare Indian-Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cleaned hare
- salted water
- 1 medium onion, thinly sliced
- 3-4 thin slices salted bacon
- 1 cup rice
- 3 cups water
- 1 can preserved tomatoes or 1 cup fresh tomato sauce
- 1 tablespoon butter
- 1 tablespoon sour cream
- salt to taste
- 1/4 teaspoon curry powder
- 1 cup milk
- 1 tablespoon butter
- 1 teaspoon curry powder
- 1 tablespoon flour
- cold milk
- 1/2 lemon, juice

## Directions

1. Clean the hare, disassemble into parts, and soak in salted water for half an hour; remove from water and place in a pot, also add the onion sliced into thin slices and three or four thin slices of salted bacon, and braise covered until the meat is soft, which will take about one hour
2. Separately, place 1 cup of rice in a pot with three cups of water; when the rice is almost soft, pour into it the sauce from one can of preserved tomatoes, about 1 cup, or the same amount of sauce from cooked fresh tomatoes; continue cooking until the rice absorbs all this sauce
3. When the meat is soft, add to the rice 1 tablespoon butter, 1 tablespoon sour cream, salt to taste, and 1/4 teaspoon curry powder, bring to a boil, and place the rice on a platter in such a way that in the center there is only a thin layer and around the edges a high rim
4. In this prepared platter, arrange the hare and the slices of bacon cooked with it, and on top pour the following sauce: To the remaining sauce in the pot in which the hare cooked, add 1 cup milk, 1 tablespoon butter, and 1 teaspoon curry powder, bring to a boil, and then thicken with 1 tablespoon flour mixed in a small amount of cold milk, then squeeze in the juice from half a lemon

title: Zając z Cebulą (Hare with Onion)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 large hare
- cold water
- salt to taste
- warm water
- salt to taste
- 1 tablespoon butter
- 1 tablespoon flour
- 1.5 cups milk
- 2 onions, finely chopped
- salt to taste
- pepper to taste

## Directions

1. Clean a large hare, cover with cold water and let soak for twenty minutes
2. Remove from water, sew or wrap in a piece of thin cloth, place in a pot, cover with warm water, salt to taste, cook on low heat for one and a half hours, then remove from cloth, place on a platter, and pour the following sauce over it: Brown 1 tablespoon butter with 1 tablespoon flour in a pot, add one and a half cups milk and bring to a boil
3. Chop very finely two onions previously cooked in water, add them to the sauce, as well as salt and pepper to taste, and bring to a boil several times

title: Zapiekana Szynka z Jajami (Baked Ham with Eggs)
body:
## Ingredients

- one cup finely chopped cooked ham
- two hard-boiled eggs
- one cup grated rolls or white bread
- one tablespoon melted butter
- one cup milk mixed with two tablespoons cornstarch
- salt and pepper to taste

## Directions

1. Take one cup of finely chopped cooked ham, two hard-boiled eggs, one cup of grated rolls or white bread, one tablespoon of melted butter, one cup of milk mixed with two tablespoons of cornstarch, salt and pepper to taste.
2. Mix the grated bread and butter well.
3. Chop the whites finely.
4. Rub the yolks into a smooth paste.
5. Take a small baking dish and on the bottom spread some of the ham, then bread, whites, and finally yolks until the dish is full.
6. Pour the milk with cornstarch over everything.
7. Place in the oven for half an hour and serve hot.

title: Zrazy a la Nelson (Nelson-Style Beef Rolls)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound sirloin, thinly sliced
- salt
- pepper
- butter
- onion, blanched and thinly sliced

## Directions

1. Beat the beef slices well and season with salt and pepper.
2. Heat a tablespoon of butter in a wide pan until browned.
3. Layer the beef slices, then sprinkle with the blanched onion slices.
4. Repeat with another layer of beef and onion, then add a bit more butter on top.
5. Cover tightly and braise, shaking the pan frequently.
6. Use fairly high heat, as the main requirement is to braise for no longer than twenty minutes, after which they should be tender.
7. If desired, partially boiled potatoes cut into slices can be braised with the rolls.
8. The addition of mushrooms braised together with the meat makes the dish considerably better.

title: Zrazy a la Nelson (Zrazy a la Nelson)
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal, sliced thin
- Rye bread
- Onion, roasted and grated
- Salt and pepper
- English herbs, powdered
- Butter

## Directions

1. Slice the veal into small thin pieces
2. Grate some rye bread, add roasted onion grated on a grater, pepper and salt to taste, and some powdered English herbs
3. Arrange the zrazy in a pot on hot butter, sprinkling with the bread mixture, cover tightly, and place in a hot oven for half an hour
4. Serve from the pot to the table
5. Alternatively, the zrazy can be braised on low heat, but they will not be as good
6. Potatoes cut into quarters can be braised together with the meat

title: Zrazy Amerykańskie (American Roulade)
body:
## Ingredients

- a large but thin slice of round steak, about two pounds
- cooked ham, finely chopped, one cup
- two whole eggs
- one teaspoon mustard
- red pepper
- three tablespoons broth or water
- flour
- lard
- two onions, finely chopped
- three cups water
- salt
- pepper
- two cloves
- fresh parsley, chopped

## Directions

1. Cut a large but thin slice of round steak, about two pounds
2. Finely chop a piece of cooked ham and take one cup of this mass
3. Beat in two whole eggs, add one teaspoon of mustard, a pinch of red pepper and three tablespoons of broth or water
4. Mix it all into a smooth mass and spread it on the meat, then roll it tightly and tie it with twine in several places, but loosely enough that the twine does not cut the meat during cooking
5. Dust the rolled meat with flour and brown it in lard on fairly strong heat, then remove it
6. To the remaining lard in a pot, add two finely chopped onions, fry a little, add two tablespoons of flour, mix well and fry for about three minutes, then pour in three cups of water and bring to a boil
7. Place the meat in a pot of such size that it just fits and cover with the above sauce, add salt and pepper and two cloves, then cover and braise on very low heat for three hours, turning the meat once during this time
8. When serving, strain the sauce and pour it over the meat on a platter, and sprinkle chopped fresh parsley on top

title: Zrazy Angielskie (English-Style Beef Rolls)
body:
## Ingredients

- a few pounds stewing meat or first-quality cross-cut beef, thinly sliced
- salt
- pepper
- 3 roasted onions, minced finely
- 2 tablespoons butter
- 1 tablespoon butter or some chopped kidney fat
- 1.5 cups white wine

## Directions

1. Slice the meat thinly, beat well with a mallet, and lightly salt.
2. Prepare a filling from the three roasted minced onions, two tablespoons of butter, salt, and pepper to taste; mix this thoroughly without cooking.
3. Place a tablespoon of butter or some chopped kidney fat in a pan and heat until browned.
4. Layer the beef rolls alternating with the filling until all are used, with filling on top.
5. Pour 1.5 cups of white wine over everything.
6. Cover tightly and place over high heat; once it comes to a full boil, reduce to low heat and braise until tender.
7. Shake the pan frequently during braising to prevent the meat from sticking.

title: Zrazy Bite (Beaten Beef Rolls)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef for zrazy
- salt
- pepper
- flour
- butter
- onion
- mushrooms
- water
- sour cream

## Directions

1. Cut the meat into thin slices and beat well
2. Season with salt and pepper, coat each in flour and place in a pan with hot butter
3. When browned, add onion cut into slices and some mushrooms
4. Add a little water and braise until the sauce thickens, then add one tablespoon of sour cream and serve

title: Zrazy Faszerowane (Stuffed Beef Rolls)
body:
## Ingredients

- one and a half pounds good beef
- quarter pound kidney fat
- half a large soaked and wrung-out roll
- salt and pepper to taste
- onion
- water
- flour
- butter
- meat broth from cooked bones or sour cream

## Directions

1. Mince one and a half pounds good beef with a quarter pound kidney fat
2. Add half a large soaked and wrung-out roll, salt and pepper to taste, and finely minced onion
3. Mix everything into a paste and form into elongated rolls
4. Moisten with water and coat with flour and drop onto browned butter
5. When browned on both sides, add meat broth from cooked bones or two tablespoons sour cream
6. Braise covered for fifteen minutes

title: Zrazy na Prędce (Quick Beef Rolls)
body:
## Ingredients

- tender veal, thinly sliced
- salt and pepper
- butter
- lemon
- grated roll

## Directions

1. Slice tender veal thinly, beat lightly and sprinkle with salt and pepper.
2. Melt a piece of butter in a pan without letting it brown, add the rolls to the butter and fry until they brown slightly.
3. Squeeze a little lemon juice, sprinkle with grated roll, cover with a lid and braise on low heat for about five minutes.

title: Zrazy na Prędce (Quick Beef Rolls)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound tender beef, preferably sirloin, cut into walnut-sized pieces
- salt
- pepper
- 2 small whole onions
- 1 tablespoon flour
- 1 tablespoon butter
- 1 large cup sweet sour cream

## Directions

1. Cut the beef into pieces the size of a walnut and beat each one very thoroughly.
2. Season with salt and pepper.
3. Add the two small whole onions, flour, and mix everything well together.
4. Heat a tablespoon of butter in a pan or small dish until well browned.
5. Add the prepared rolls and fry over high heat, stirring with a spoon so the meat does not clump together but remains in separate pieces.
6. When the meat loses its red color, pour in a large cup of sweet sour cream.
7. Simmer for about two minutes and serve.

title: Zrazy po Wiejsku (Country-Style Beef Rolls)
body:
## Ingredients

- tender beef
- salt
- pepper
- lard slices
- bay leaves
- water
- grated rye bread

## Directions

1. Cut tender beef into thin slices, beat, season with salt and pepper and arrange in a pan
2. Layer with thin slices of lard and bay leaves
3. Add a small amount of water and braise on low heat until the meat is tender, then add grated rye bread so the sauce is thick
4. These rolls are usually served with potato pancakes

title: Zrazy Polskie Zawijane (Polish Rolled Beef Rolls)
body:
## Ingredients

- beef or cross-cut meat
- salt
- two roasted and grated onions
- one tablespoon butter
- half a cup grated bread
- pepper to taste
- butter for pan
- roasted onion
- broth
- grated bread

## Directions

1. Cut the meat into large but very thin slices and beat them
2. Salt and let rest about fifteen minutes
3. Mix two roasted and grated onions, one tablespoon butter, half a cup grated bread, salt and pepper to taste into a smooth paste
4. Spread this paste on the slices and roll them tightly so they do not unroll
5. Melt a piece of butter in a pan and add the rolled slices, plus roasted onion
6. Braise covered on low heat
7. When browned, add a little broth and some grated bread
8. Braise until tender, which should take about forty-five minutes

title: Zrazy Siekane (Minced Beef Rolls)
body:
## Ingredients

- two pounds good beef, first cross-cut
- fat
- one soaked and wrung-out roll
- two medium roasted and finely minced onions
- salt and pepper to taste
- two egg yolks or one whole egg
- quarter pound young lard
- a few tablespoons broth
- a few dried mushrooms
- two small onions
- one or two tablespoons sour cream

## Directions

1. Mince two pounds good beef with fat, add one soaked and wrung-out roll, two medium roasted and finely minced onions, salt and pepper to taste, and two egg yolks or one whole egg
2. Mix everything well and form eight elongated rolls from this mixture
3. Finely mince a quarter pound young lard and fry in a pan until lightly browned, then add the rolls
4. Brown them well on all sides, then add a few tablespoons broth, a few dried mushrooms which should be soaked in hot water for an hour and finely chopped, and two small onions
5. Braise covered on low heat for fifteen to twenty minutes, then add one or two tablespoons sour cream and braise again for a quarter hour, then transfer to a serving platter

title: Zrazy Wołyńskie (Volhynian Beef Rolls)
body:
## Ingredients

- minced zrazy
- cabbage
- fresh lard slices
- broth

## Directions

1. Prepare minced zrazy as described below and braise so they are half-cooked
2. Braise some cabbage separately, either fresh or sour
3. Coat each roll thickly with cabbage and then cover with slices of fresh lard so the entire roll is covered
4. Arrange the prepared rolls in a pan so they do not touch each other
5. Add a little broth and place in a hot oven without covering the pan
6. When the lard is well browned, the rolls are done
7. Transfer to a serving platter and pour some strong broth or meat stock over them

title: Zrazy Zawijane Amerykańskie (American Rolled Roulade)
body:
## Ingredients

- a large piece of steak, half an inch thick
- one onion, finely chopped
- butter
- one cup grated roll or white bread
- one tablespoon finely chopped onion
- one tablespoon chopped fresh parsley
- salt
- pepper
- one tablespoon butter
- one cup water

## Directions

1. Cut a large piece of steak half an inch thick and cut it into strips six inches long and three to four inches wide
2. Brown one finely chopped onion in a small amount of butter, and when it becomes slightly browned, remove it from heat
3. Add one cup of grated roll or white bread, one tablespoon of finely chopped onion, one tablespoon of chopped fresh parsley, salt and pepper to taste
4. Mix this well and spread this filling on the meat, putting about one tablespoon on each strip, then roll it up, secure with a toothpick and place in a baking dish with one tablespoon of butter and one cup of water for three quarters of an hour
5. After removing from the oven, thicken the sauce with flour and pour it over the roulade on a platter
6. During baking, frequently baste the meat with sauce so it does not dry out

title: Zrazy Zawijane z Kapustą (Beef Rolls with Cabbage)
body:
## Ingredients

- tender beef
- salt
- pepper
- sour cabbage
- lard or butter
- broth or water
- pork lard or pork rind
- tomatoes
- roots
- grated bread
- flour
- boiled potatoes
- fresh parsley or dill

## Directions

1. Cut tender beef into fairly large but thin slices, beat strongly and season with salt and pepper
2. Blanch some sour cabbage in a pan and spread it on the meat, using one large spoonful of cabbage per meat slice, also add a piece of lard or a teaspoon of butter
3. Roll up, tie with string, arrange in a pan and add broth or water
4. Add several slices of lard or pork rind and several chopped tomatoes and roots
5. Braise until tender
6. When the meat is completely tender, transfer to a serving platter, thicken the sauce with flour and pour it over the rolls
7. Around them arrange grated potatoes and sprinkle with chopped fresh parsley or dill

title: Zrazy à la Nelson (Nelson-Style Beef Rolls)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound tenderloin
- salt
- pepper
- butter
- onion

## Directions

1. Cut one pound tenderloin into thin slices, beat well and season with salt and pepper
2. Put one tablespoon butter in a pan, heat until browned and arrange a layer of rolls on it
3. Sprinkle with blanched onion cut into thin slices, then another layer of rolls, onion and a little butter on top, then cover tightly

title: Zrazy à la Stroganoff (Stroganoff-Style Beef Rolls)
body:
## Ingredients

- tenderloin or tender beef
- salt
- butter
- flour
- onion
- sour cream
- paprika
- soy sauce
- bread slices

## Directions

1. Cut the meat into slices, salt and fry in butter
2. Put a piece of butter in a pan, add a little flour and finely minced onion and brown
3. Pour in a few tablespoons sour cream, add a little paprika and one tablespoon soy sauce
4. Arrange the fried rolls on toast slices on a platter and pour the above sauce over them
5. Be careful not to over-fry the rolls

title: Zupa "Cream of Celery" (Cream of Celery Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- One pound of fresh lean beef, chopped fine
- Two quarts of cold water
- Several celery stalks cut into half-inch pieces
- One onion, sliced
- Half cup of washed rice
- Salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Chop fine one pound of fresh lean beef
2. Pour in two quarts of cold water and cook on very low heat for two hours
3. Cut several celery stalks into half-inch pieces
4. Add one sliced onion, half cup of washed rice, salt and pepper to taste
5. Cook another two hours; at this time there should be a good quart of soup (liquid only)
6. Add one cup of good milk and strain
7. Serve
8. A few tablespoons of cooked rice and also cream can be added at serving

title: Zupa "Cream of Pea" (Cream of Pea Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- One cup of green peas (fresh or pickled)
- Water
- Onion, chopped fine
- Half quart of broth or water
- Two tablespoons of butter
- One tablespoon of flour
- Salt and pepper
- Several tablespoons of whipped cream

## Directions

1. Pour one cup of green peas in a pot with just enough water to barely cover them
2. Add finely chopped onion
3. Cook until completely soft
4. Mash to a smooth paste and pour in half quart of broth or water
5. Make a roux with two tablespoons of butter and one tablespoon of flour, being careful not to brown it
6. Add to the soup along with salt and pepper to taste
7. Bring to a boil, strain and serve
8. The quality of this soup can be improved by adding several tablespoons of whipped cream just before serving

title: Zupa "Ox Tail" (Oxtail Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- one beef oxtail
- flour
- one teaspoon of salt
- several peppercorns
- two whole onions
- one carrot
- one turnip
- two stalks of celery
- parsley
- water

## Directions

1. Chop beef oxtail into inch-long pieces, dredge each in flour and place in a pot.
2. Add one teaspoon of salt, several peppercorns, two whole chopped onions, one carrot, one turnip, two stalks of celery and parsley, and cover with water.
3. Cook on a low fire for three hours.
4. Remove the meat, skim all the fat from the soup and then strain it well.
5. Carefully cut the meat from the bones, chop into small pieces, place in a bowl and pour the hot soup over it.

title: Zupa "Ox Tail" Rumiana (Browned Oxtail Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- one beef oxtail
- fat
- three onions
- two carrots
- parsley
- thyme
- one pound of beef
- salt
- pepper
- celery seeds
- flour
- butter

## Directions

1. Chop beef oxtail into pieces about an inch long and fry in fat in a pan until well browned.
2. Remove them and in the same fat fry three finely shredded onions and two carrots.
3. After frying, place these vegetables along with some parsley and thyme in a small cloth bag and put in the pot.
4. Take one pound of beef, cut into thin strips and add along with the previously fried oxtails to the pot with the vegetables.
5. Cover with three quarts of water and cook about four hours, or even longer, on a very low fire.
6. Grate two carrots and add to the soup salt and pepper for taste, and also a little celery seed.
7. Cook for another fifteen minutes, strain and thicken with a little flour browned in butter.

title: Zupa a la Reine (Queen's Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken
- vegetables for broth
- 1/4 pound cooked rice, very soft
- 1 tablespoon good butter
- 2 egg yolks
- broth from chicken
- 1/2 glass Rhine wine (optional)
- small croutons fried in butter

## Directions

1. Cook broth from chicken in the usual way, adding vegetables, but cook longer so the meat separates completely from the bones.
2. Take the white meat and meat from the thighs, chop finely, discarding the skin.
3. Separately prepare 1/4 pound of rice cooked until very soft.
4. Take some meat and rice and pound together in a mortar into a paste, then pass through a sieve, pouring broth over it.
5. Take 1 tablespoon of good butter, beat it with two egg yolks and mix into the soup that has been removed from the heat.
6. Do not cook further, but serve immediately to the table.
7. Some add 1/2 glass of Rhine wine before serving, which is poured into the soup and heated together.
8. Serve with small croutons fried in butter.

title: Zupa Aksamitna (Velvet Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart clear broth
- 1 cup sour cream
- salt to taste
- pepper to taste
- 4 egg yolks
- 4 tablespoons sour cream

## Directions

1. Add 1 cup sour cream to 1 quart of clear broth, season with salt and pepper to taste.
2. Beat 4 egg yolks with 4 tablespoons of sour cream and mix into the soup.
3. This soup is usually served in bouillon cups.

title: Zupa Andalus (Andalusian Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 tomatoes
- 3 onions
- 1 bay leaf
- 1 clove garlic
- English spices
- beef broth
- piece of ham
- fresh dill
- eggs in their shells

## Directions

1. Place tomatoes, onions, bay leaf, garlic clove, and English spices in a clay pot.
2. Stew under a lid until soft, then press through a sieve.
