3. Have beef broth ready and mix with the sieved mass and bring to a boil.
4. Slice ham thinly and fry it in a pan with fresh dill.
5. Place in a serving bowl and pour the soup over it.
6. Serve this soup with eggs in their shells.

title: Zupa Bagration (Bagration Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound veal with bone
- water
- vegetables
- 1 cup cooked rice
- 1 cup milk
- 2 egg yolks
- flour dumplings or pasta

## Directions

1. Pour water over 1 pound of veal with bone and cook.
2. After skimming, add various vegetables and cook until the meat is completely tender.
3. Remove meat from bone and add 1 cup of previously cooked rice.
4. Pound together in a mortar into a paste, then press through a sieve, pouring broth over it.
5. Distribute the obtained paste with broth.
6. When serving, season with 1 cup of milk in which 2 egg yolks have been beaten.
7. Serve as an addition to this soup with sliced dumplings or pasta.

title: Zupa Biała (White Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken or 2 pounds veal
- vegetables
- barley or pearl groats
- small amount of butter
- 1 cup sour cream
- fried croutons
- fresh parsley, chopped

## Directions

1. Cook broth with vegetables from chicken or veal, cooking until the meat is completely tender.
2. Separately cook barley or pearl groats with a small amount of butter until thick, making sure it does not burn and breaks down well, constantly collecting the formed starch in a bowl, as this is the best part.
3. When the groats are completely cooked, press through a fine sieve, pouring broth over it and mixing well.
4. Add 1 cup sour cream and bring to a boil.
5. Serve with croutons fried in butter and chopped fresh parsley.

title: Zupa Cebulowa (Onion Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- one spoon of butter
- one medium onion, finely chopped
- previously cooked broth
- one spoon of flour
- water
- salt and pepper to taste
- stale bread

## Directions

1. Heat one spoon of butter in a pan and add to it one medium onion, finely chopped, then fry, stirring constantly, so as not to burn, until it is well browned
2. Add to previously cooked broth and cook for fifteen minutes
3. Mix one spoon of flour with water so smoothly that there are no lumps, pour into the soup and cook for another five minutes
4. Add salt and pepper to taste; chop stale bread into small cubes, brown them well in an oven, pour into a bowl, pour the soup and serve immediately

title: Zupa Cytrynowa (Lemon Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- one bread roll
- one and a half quarts of water
- a little cinnamon
- zest of one lemon
- three egg yolks
- sugar
- juice of one lemon
- one quarter litre of good white wine
- previously cooked rice

## Directions

1. Pour one and a half quarts of water over one bread roll, add a little cinnamon, then pass through a sieve
2. Grate the zest from one lemon; beat three egg yolks with sugar and press into them the juice of one lemon; add all this to the boiling soup, as well as one quarter litre of good white wine, stirring constantly, and immediately remove from fire and serve with previously cooked rice, which is poured into the bowl

title: Zupa Cytrynowa (Lemon Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- ordinary broth with vegetables
- 1/4 pound rice
- butter
- 1/4 cup sour cream
- 1 lemon
- salt and pepper to taste

## Directions

1. Cook ordinary broth with vegetables, which may be from beef, veal, or offal.
2. Scald 1/4 pound of rice, then cook until it breaks down completely with butter.
3. Beat it well with 1/4 cup of sour cream.
4. Squeeze juice from one lemon, distribute with broth and bring to a boil with seasoning.
5. In the serving bowl, before pouring the soup, place lemon sliced thinly into thin slices, without seeds.

title: Zupa Cytrynowa (Lemon Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- ordinary broth with vegetables
- beef, veal, or offal
- 1/4 pound rice
- butter
- 1/4 cup sour cream
- 1 lemon
- salt and pepper to taste

## Directions

1. Cook ordinary broth with vegetables, which may be from beef, veal, or offal.
2. Scald 1/4 pound of rice, then cook until it breaks down completely with butter.
3. Beat it well with 1/4 cup of sour cream.
4. Squeeze juice from one lemon, distribute with broth and bring to a boil with seasoning.
5. In the serving bowl, before pouring the soup, place lemon sliced thinly into thin slices, without seeds.

title: Zupa Czekoladowa (Chocolate Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart of milk
- two ounces of cocoa or grated chocolate
- sugar
- vanilla or vanilla extract
- two egg yolks
- small croutons or white bread

## Directions

1. Bring one quart of milk to a boil with two ounces of cocoa or grated chocolate, add sugar and vanilla or vanilla extract and pour into a bowl into which two beaten egg yolks have been poured previously, and mix well
2. Serve with small croutons made from bread roll or white bread as an accompaniment to this soup

title: Zupa Francuska (French Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef broth with vegetables
- White bread
- Parmesan or grated Swiss cheese

## Directions

1. Cook broth in the usual way on beef with vegetables
2. Cut white bread into thin square slices
3. Moisten with broth
4. Place in a bowl, sprinkle with parmesan or grated Swiss cheese and put in the oven
5. When browned, remove from the oven, pour broth over it and serve

title: Zupa Grecy (Vegetable Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- two carrots
- one potato
- one turnip
- one quart of water
- one spoon of finely chopped onion
- a little celery salt
- salt and pepper to taste
- half a spoon of butter
- one cup of milk
- one spoon of flour
- cold water
- small croutons

## Directions

1. Peel two carrots, one potato and one turnip and chop into small cubes, pour over with one quart of water and cook until the vegetables are completely soft
2. Remove the vegetables and pass through a sieve, then put the mashed mass back into the water in which it was cooked, add one spoon of finely chopped onion, a little celery salt, salt and pepper to taste, half a spoon of butter and one cup of milk, and then cook for five to ten minutes
3. Finally mix one spoon of flour with cold water, pour into the soup and bring to a boil again
4. Serve with small croutons fried in butter

title: Zupa Grochowa (Pea Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- Half quart of peas
- One quart of soft water
- One pound of overripe pork, or several pork tails, or smoked meat
- One tablespoon of butter
- One tablespoon of flour
- Salt and pepper
- Marjoram

## Directions

1. Pour half quart of peas in one quart of soft water and bring to a boil
2. Strain this water, rinse the peas thoroughly and pour fresh hot water over them, as peas soaked in cold water will not be so soft
3. Separately cook with one pound of overripe pork, or several pork tails, or use smoked meat instead
4. When the peas are completely soft, strain the water and pass the peas through a sieve, pouring broth from the cooking meat over them
5. Make a roux with one tablespoon of butter and one tablespoon of flour
6. Cut the pork into pieces and add to the soup
7. Bring together to a boil and pour into a bowl into which first place croutons fried in butter and a pinch of marjoram

title: Zupa Grzybowa (Mushroom Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- Poultry offal (goose or duck) or beef
- Regular broth with vegetables
- Several mushrooms
- One cup of pearl barley
- One tablespoon of butter
- One quarter of cream

## Directions

1. This soup is usually made on goose or duck offal, though it can be made on beef
2. Cook regular broth with vegetables and several mushrooms
3. Separately, cook one cup of pearl barley, add one tablespoon of butter and beat to whiteness
4. Pour in one quarter of cream, dilute with broth
5. Cut the cooked mushrooms into thin strips
6. Serve on the table

title: Zupa Grzybowa (Mushroom Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- a dozen medium-sized dried mushrooms
- a couple of whole onions
- various vegetables
- salt to taste
- butter
- a small amount of flour
- vinegar to taste
- small dumplings

## Directions

1. Take a dozen medium-sized dried mushrooms, wash carefully, pour over with water and cook for half an hour, then add a couple of whole onions and various vegetables and continue cooking until the mushrooms are completely soft
2. Remove the mushrooms and chop six of them finely, save the rest for the next day for ear-shaped pastries to beet soup or small pies; strain the soup through a sieve, add the chopped mushrooms, salt, thicken with butter mixed with a little flour, add vinegar to taste, bring to a boil and serve with small dumplings which are cooked in this soup
3. This soup can also be prepared without vinegar, and then it is called 'sweet'

title: Zupa Grzybowa Amerykańska (American Mushroom Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- Half quart of fresh white mushrooms
- One quart of broth, preferably from veal or chicken
- One tablespoon of flour
- One tablespoon of butter
- Salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Peel half quart of fresh white mushrooms and cut into cubes
2. Stew them in half quart of broth, preferably from veal or chicken
3. As soon as the mushrooms are cooked, add one quart of broth
4. Make a roux with one tablespoon of flour and one tablespoon of butter
5. Add to the soup, season with salt and pepper to taste and serve

title: Zupa Grzybowa z Kaszką (Mushroom Soup with Groat)
body:
## Ingredients

- dried mushrooms
- mixed vegetables (włoszczyzna)
- butter
- fine groat mixed with egg

## Directions

1. Cook the broth from dried mushrooms with mixed vegetables
2. When the mushrooms are completely soft, strain
3. Salt the broth
4. Add butter
5. Sprinkle in fine groat mixed with egg

title: Zupa Hiszpańska (Spanish Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound white beans
- 1 small head white cabbage
- smoked meat (wędzonka)
- 1 red pepper pod
- salt to taste
- lard (smalec)
- 1 onion
- 1 clove garlic

## Directions

1. Soak 1 pound of white beans overnight
2. The next day, finely chop a small head of white cabbage, some smoked meat, and one red pepper pod
3. Add to the beans and cook for one hour
4. Add salt to taste
5. Melt some lard in a pan and fry one finely chopped onion
6. Add slowly to the soup while constantly stirring
7. Add one crushed clove of garlic, bring to a boil and serve
8. Garlic can be omitted

title: Zupa Hiszpańska (Spanish Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- One pound of white beans
- One small head of white cabbage
- Some smoked meat
- One red pepper pod
- One onion
- A clove of garlic
- Fat

## Directions

1. Soak one pound of white beans overnight
2. The next day, chop fine one small head of white cabbage, some smoked meat, one red pepper pod
3. Add to the beans and cook one hour
4. Add salt to taste
5. Melt some fat in a pan and fry one finely chopped onion on it and add slowly to the soup, constantly stirring
6. Add one minced clove of garlic, bring to a boil and serve
7. Garlic can be omitted

title: Zupa Holsztyńska (Holstein Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound beef
- 1/4 pound smoked lard (słonina)
- water
- various vegetables
- macaroni or noodles (łazankami)
- potatoes

## Directions

1. Pour water over 1 pound of beef and 1/4 pound of smoked lard and cook
2. After skimming, add a generous amount of various vegetables that are on hand
3. Cook until vegetables and meat are completely tender
4. Strain the broth and pour into a dish lined with slices of the smoked lard removed from the soup and cut up, and with macaroni or noodles
5. Whole boiled potatoes can also be served with this soup
6. Serve the meat separately, dressed with vegetables cooked in the soup

title: Zupa Indyjska (Indian Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 medium onion
- butter
- twice the amount of veal as onion, cut into cubes
- 1 tablespoon flour
- 1 teaspoon curry powder
- lemon zest
- beef broth
- 1 large sour apple
- salt and pepper to taste
- separately cooked rice

## Directions

1. Finely chop one medium onion and fry it slightly in butter
2. Add twice the amount of veal cut into cubes as there is onion, and 1 tablespoon of flour
3. Fry everything together
4. Add 1 teaspoon of curry powder and some grated lemon zest for flavor
5. Dilute all of this with previously cooked beef broth
6. Add one large sour apple cut into slices
7. Add salt and pepper to taste
8. Add separately cooked rice
9. Bring everything to a boil once more and serve

title: Zupa Kukurydzana Królewska (Royal Corn Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- two quarts chicken broth
- finely chopped celery
- one cup green corn, scraped with a knife
- a pinch of red pepper
- one cup cream or milk

## Directions

1. Take two quarts of chicken broth and add finely chopped celery, one cup of green corn scraped with a knife and a pinch of red pepper.
2. Cook everything for forty minutes, then add one cup of cream or milk and serve.

title: Zupa Leyigne
body:
## Ingredients

- two carrots
- one onion
- salted water
- one and one-half quarts prepared broth
- half a cup cream
- four egg yolks
- salt and pepper to taste

## Directions

1. Cut two carrots and one onion into thin slices and cook in salted water.
2. Strain and add to one and one-half quarts of previously prepared broth, from which all the fat has been removed.
3. Whip half a cup of cream with four egg yolks.
4. Remove the soup from the heat, add salt and pepper to taste, then pour in the egg yolks, mix well and serve.

title: Zupa Medyolańska (Milanese Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound rice
- one onion
- butter
- prepared broth
- salt and pepper to taste

## Directions

1. Rinse half a pound of rice and cook.
2. Chop one onion finely and fry in butter in a pan.
3. When the onion browns, add the rice to it, mix with the previously prepared broth, place on heat and cook until the rice softens enough that it can be passed through a sieve.
4. After passing through, mix again with the soup, add salt and pepper to taste and serve at table.

title: Zupa Medyolańska (Milanese Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound of rice
- one onion
- butter
- broth
- salt
- pepper
- a few tablespoons of parmesan or grated Swiss cheese

## Directions

1. Rinse half a pound of rice and cook it.
2. Chop one onion finely and fry it in butter in a pan.
3. When the onion becomes golden, add the rice to it, mix with the previously cooked broth, put on the fire and cook until the rice softens so much that it can be pressed through a sieve.
4. After pressing through the sieve, mix again with the soup, add salt and pepper to taste and serve at table, adding a few tablespoons of parmesan or grated Swiss cheese.

title: Zupa Migdałowa (Almond Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pound sweet almonds
- 5 bitter almonds
- milk or water
- 1 quart boiled water
- 5 or 6 ounces sugar
- 1/2 cup rice
- cinnamon (optional)
- sugar (optional)
- raisins (optional)
- ice

## Directions

1. Scald 1/2 pound sweet almonds and 5 bitter almonds, peel them of their skin, chop very finely, then pound in a mortar, moistening with milk or water so the oil does not release.
2. Pour 1 quart of boiled water into the almonds ground into a paste, mix well and strain through a fine sieve or cloth.
3. Pound the remaining pulp again, pour a little of the same liquid and strain through the sieve again.
4. Add 5 or 6 ounces of sugar and mix everything well together.
5. Separately cook 1/2 cup of rice in milk or water - it can be made with cinnamon, sugar and raisins - put in a bowl and pour the soup over it, then place on ice to cool.
6. Note: This soup can also be served hot.

title: Zupa Neapolitańska po amerykańsku (Neapolitan Soup American Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- five cups of cooked broth
- salt
- pepper
- one egg
- flour
- parmesan cheese
- parsley

## Directions

1. Have five cups of cooked broth seasoned with salt and pepper to taste.
2. Knead one egg with enough flour, to which a pinch of salt has been added, to create a dough of medium thickness.
3. Make balls the size of a thimble from this dough and drop them into the boiling broth ten minutes before serving.
4. When serving, sprinkle one tablespoon of parmesan and one teaspoon of finely chopped parsley.

title: Zupa Neapolitańska po europejsku (Neapolitan Soup European Style)
body:
## Ingredients

- one tablespoon of butter
- one tablespoon of flour
- one tablespoon of parmesan cheese
- one cup of sour cream or sweet cream
- two egg yolks
- one tablespoon of cold water
- one quart of good previously cooked broth
- Italian pasta

## Directions

1. Brown one tablespoon of butter with one tablespoon of flour and one tablespoon of parmesan cheese, stirring until a uniform mass is obtained.
2. Dissolve this mixture with one cup of sour cream; if sour cream is unavailable, use sweet cream.
3. Bring to a boil, remove from heat and thicken with two egg yolks beaten with one tablespoon of cold water.
4. Add one quart of good previously cooked broth.
5. Separately have cooked Italian pasta broken into small pieces, place it in a bowl and pour the soup over it.

title: Zupa Nic (Nic Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 egg whites
- 1 tablespoon fine sugar
- 1 quart unboiled milk
- 1/2 cup sugar
- 3 egg yolks
- 2 tablespoons cold milk
- cinnamon stick
- handful of raisins without seeds
- ice

## Directions

1. Whip 3 egg whites into a foam, add 1 tablespoon of fine sugar and mix.
2. Bring 1 quart of unboiled milk to a boil, take the foam of egg whites with a spoon and place on the boiling milk like dumplings. When cooked, remove with a slotted spoon and place in a bowl.
3. Mix 1/2 cup of sugar with 3 egg yolks almost to whiteness, add 2 tablespoons of cold milk. Then pour hot milk onto this mixture, stirring continuously.
4. Add a couple of pieces of cinnamon and a handful of raisins without seeds and pour everything into the bowl and place on ice to cool.

title: Zupa Normandzka (Normandy Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- one large calf's shank
- three quarts of cold water
- six white onions
- one quarter pound of stale white bread
- two tablespoons of flour
- one tablespoon of butter
- salt
- pepper
- a little grated nutmeg
- half a quart of boiled milk

## Directions

1. Cover one large calf's shank with three quarts of cold water and cook very slowly for three hours.
2. Slice six white onions thinly and crumble one quarter pound of stale white bread, add these to the soup and cook for another hour.
3. Remove the meat and press the soup through a sieve, trying to press through as much onion and bread as possible.
4. Pour back into the pot, blend two tablespoons of flour with one tablespoon of butter and add salt, pepper to taste, and also a little grated nutmeg.
5. Just before serving, add half a quart of boiled milk.

title: Zupa Ogórkowa (Cucumber Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- three large pickled cucumbers
- flour
- butter
- mixed vegetable broth
- one quarter quart of sour cream

## Directions

1. Peel three large pickled cucumbers and slice them
2. Dust with flour and fry in a pot with butter
3. Separately prepare a broth from mixed vegetables available on hand
4. When the cucumbers are well browned, pour this broth over them and boil together for about half an hour
5. Strain the soup, add one quarter quart of sour cream and serve

title: Zupa Ogórkowa (Pickled Cucumber Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- three large pickled cucumbers
- flour
- butter
- previously cooked ordinary broth
- one quarter of sour cream

## Directions

1. Peel three large pickled cucumbers, cut into slices, sprinkle with flour and fry in a small pot with butter.
2. When the cucumbers are well fried, cover them with previously cooked ordinary broth, put on the fire and cook for at least half an hour.
3. Then strain the soup, season with one quarter of sour cream and serve.

title: Zupa Ostrygowa (Oyster Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart of oysters
- milk
- flour
- butter

## Directions

1. Pour a quart of oysters into a colander and strain all the liquid into a bowl
2. When all the liquid has drained, rinse the oysters with cold water, being careful that no shell pieces remain
3. Pour the drained oyster liquid into a pot, bring to a boil, and strain after skimming
4. Bring a quart of milk to a boil and thicken slightly with flour mixed with butter
5. Add the oysters to the strained oyster liquid and cook until they puff up and their edges begin to curl
6. Then add the milk, mix together, bring to a boil and serve

title: Zupa Owocowa (Fruit Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of dried fruits
- water
- sugar
- cinnamon
- some fruit juice
- corn flour
- cold water
- small dumplings or croutons

## Directions

1. Rinse one pound of dried fruits properly, pour water over them and cook until soft
2. Press through a sieve, add sugar, cinnamon and some fruit juice
3. Add a sufficient amount of water
4. Thicken with corn flour mixed in cold water
5. Bring to a boil and serve with small dumplings or croutons

title: Zupa Pomidorowa "Bisque" (Tomato Bisque)
body:
## Ingredients

- half quart of pickled tomatoes
- a few slices of onion
- half cupful of water
- a pinch of baking soda
- half quart of milk
- salt and pepper to taste
- one teaspoon of butter
- two tablespoons of soft cooked rice
- white bread
- butter for frying

## Directions

1. Boil slowly for fifteen to twenty minutes: half quart of pickled tomatoes, a few slices of onion, and half cupful of water
2. Press through a colander and pour back into the pot
3. Add a pinch of baking soda
4. Bring half a quart of milk to a boil and pour gradually into the tomatoes, stirring constantly
5. Add salt and pepper to taste, one teaspoon of butter and two tablespoons of soft cooked rice
6. Boil for five minutes, then serve with croutons prepared as follows: chop a piece of white bread into half-inch cubes and fry in a pan with one teaspoon of hot foaming butter until all croutons are evenly browned

title: Zupa Pomidorowa (Tomato Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart of tomatoes
- half a quart of beef broth
- one onion
- one bay leaf
- one celery stalk
- a few sprigs of parsley
- one teaspoon of sugar
- one quarter teaspoon of baking soda
- one tablespoon of butter
- two tablespoons of flour
- salt
- pepper
- croutons

## Directions

1. Stew one quart of tomatoes—they may be fresh or preserved—in a pot, add half a quart of beef broth, one onion, one bay leaf, one celery stalk and a few sprigs of parsley, one teaspoon of sugar, one quarter teaspoon of baking soda, one tablespoon of butter, two tablespoons of flour, salt and pepper to taste.
2. Place the tomatoes in the pot with the celery, parsley and onion; cook about ten minutes, then press through a colander or sieve to remove the seeds.
3. Pour into a clean pot, place on the fire and bring to a boil.
4. Rub butter with flour and work into the soup, then stir until the soup is uniform.
5. Add salt, pepper, sugar and soda just before serving. May be served with croutons.

title: Zupa Pomidorowa Amerykańska (American Tomato Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- one can of marinated tomatoes
- half a quart of liquid from the tomatoes
- one teaspoon of salt
- some pepper
- half a teaspoon of baking soda
- half a quart of milk
- one tablespoon of butter
- one tablespoon of flour

## Directions

1. Pour the can of marinated tomatoes into a pot and cook until soft
2. Strain through a fine sieve so that seeds and skins remain on the sieve
3. To half a quart of this liquid add one teaspoon of salt, some pepper, and half a teaspoon of baking soda
4. Pour back into the pot and place on the stove
5. In another pot, bring half a quart of milk to a boil
6. Separately, melt one tablespoon of butter, add one tablespoon of flour, and fry for about three minutes
7. Pour this mixture slowly into the milk while constantly stirring
8. Bring to a boil and pour into the tomatoes
9. Strain the soup once more before serving, bring to a boil, and serve immediately
10. Can be served with croutons

title: Zupa Pomidorowa Amerykańska (American Tomato Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- one can of pickled tomatoes
- salt
- pepper
- half teaspoon of baking soda

## Directions

1. Pour a can of pickled tomatoes into a pot and boil until soft
2. Press through a fine sieve so that only seeds and skins remain on the sieve
3. For half a quart of this liquid, add one teaspoon of salt, some pepper, and half a teaspoon of baking soda
4. Pour back into the pot and place on the stove

title: Zupa Pomidorowa Postna (Lenten Tomato Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart of tomatoes
- one quart of water
- one tablespoon of flour
- one tablespoon of butter
- half quart of milk
- salt and pepper to taste
- white bread for croutons (optional)

## Directions

1. Press one quart of tomatoes through a colander so as to separate the skins and seeds, which should be discarded
2. Add one quart of water and boil
3. Mix one tablespoon of flour with one tablespoon of butter and half a quart of milk
4. Bring to a boil and pour into the soup
5. Add salt and pepper to taste and serve on the table
6. This soup can be served with croutons made from white bread

title: Zupa Pomidorowa z Ryżem (Tomato Soup with Rice)
body:
## Ingredients

- two large cupfuls of pickled or fresh tomatoes
- half cupful of rice
- two onions
- one carrot
- three tablespoons of butter
- two tablespoons of flour
- one quart of cold water
- salt and pepper to taste

## Directions

1. Chop the onion and carrot finely and fry in a pot over low heat for twenty minutes
2. Pour the tomatoes and one quart of cold water into the pot and bring to a boil
3. Remove the carrot and onion from the butter and add to the boiling tomatoes
4. To the remaining butter in the pot, add two tablespoons of flour and brown to a smooth paste
5. Pour the soup over this and place on the fire, cooking slowly for half an hour
6. When all vegetables are soft, press through a sieve so that only seeds and skin remain, then discard these
7. Rinse the rice, add to the soup along with salt and pepper to taste
8. Cook over low heat for one hour, stirring from time to time so the rice does not burn
9. Serve directly from the fire

title: Zupa Pomidorowa ze Śmietaną (Tomato Soup with Cream)
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart of fresh or preserved tomatoes
- one tablespoon of butter
- a few tablespoons of broth
- one tablespoon of flour
- one cup of sour cream
- salt
- pepper
- rice
- white bread
- croutons

## Directions

1. Take one quart of fresh or preserved tomatoes, place in a pot with one tablespoon of butter, add a few tablespoons of broth and stew for half an hour.
2. When the tomatoes are completely soft, press through a fine sieve or colander so that all the seeds and skins remain on it, and then dissolve with as much broth as there is liquid from the cooked tomatoes.
3. Beat one tablespoon of flour with one cup of sour cream and season the soup with this, pouring the cream slowly and constantly stirring.
4. This soup is usually served with rice cooked thick with butter, or small croutons made from white bread fried in butter.
5. Instead of cream, the soup may be seasoned with egg yolks: for four people, beat two egg yolks well in a bowl and only then pour the soup over it and mix well.

title: Zupa Rabarbarowa (Rhubarb Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- one and a half pounds of rhubarb stalks
- one and a half quarts of water
- half a pound of sugar
- zest of half a lemon
- cinnamon
- nutmeg
- one spoonful of cornstarch
- cold water
- dumplings or cooked rice

## Directions

1. Finely chop one and a half pounds of rhubarb stalks
2. Pour one and a half quarts of water over them and cook until the rhubarb completely falls apart
3. Strain through a sieve
4. Add half a pound of sugar, zest from half a lemon, some cinnamon and nutmeg, and one spoonful of cornstarch mixed in cold water
5. Bring to a boil and serve with dumplings or cooked rice
6. This soup can also be served cold like a fruit soup with bread cubes

title: Zupa Rakowa (Crawfish Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- thirty small crawfish
- salt
- dill
- good butter
- flour
- beef broth, cooked previously
- sour cream
- bread for farce
- dill
- nutmeg
- egg yolks
- meat from crawfish tails and shells

## Directions

1. For four persons, cook thirty small crawfish with salt and dill, being careful not to overcook or they will be difficult to shell.
2. Remove the tails and extract the meat from the tails and soft mass under the shell on the body, using this for stuffing the shells.
3. Leave a dozen whole shells and place all the rest, as is, in a mortar and crush to a paste.
4. Take a spoon of good butter and crush again in the mortar together with the crawfish.
5. Place this mass in a pot and braise over low heat for at least an hour, pouring in previously cooked ordinary broth, and red crawfish butter will begin to appear on the surface, which should be collected with a spoon, strained through a sieve and poured into a pot in which a spoon of butter has been browned with a spoon of flour, mixing well together.
6. When the crawfish butter stops appearing, press the crawfish through fine cloth and allow the obtained liquid to settle; pour off the clear liquid from the top and discard the sediment at the bottom.
7. Pour the above liquid into the browned butter, mix well and dilute with sufficient broth, add some chopped dill and stuffed crawfish shells, bring to a boil, lighten with two spoons of sour cream, then put the removed tails into a bowl and pour the soup over them.
8. For the stuffing of the shells, proceed as follows: chop very finely the previously selected meat from the tails and shells, add grated bread crumbs, chopped dill, butter, nutmeg, salt and one egg yolk, mix everything well into a thick paste and fill the empty crawfish shells, well cleaned and washed.

title: Zupa Rakowa (Crayfish Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- thirty small crayfish
- salted water
- small amount of dill
- butter
- various vegetables for stock
- flour
- salt
- chopped dill
- rice
- filling

## Directions

1. Cook thirty small crayfish in salted water with a small amount of dill
2. Peel the tails and set aside, and also clean a dozen shells for later stuffing; the rest together with the shells, pound into a mass in a mortar
3. Place this mass with a piece of butter into a pot and stew for about twenty minutes
4. Have separately prepared a stock made from various vegetables
5. As soon as the crayfish mass takes on a red color, add a couple of tablespoons of flour, salt, and chopped dill
6. Add the vegetable stock, bring to a boil for about five minutes, then strain through a sieve and pour into a bowl in which crayfish shells are found, stuffed with the following filling: Mix cooked rice with butter and chopped dill into a thick mass, fry well over low heat without browning, and use this to fill the crayfish shells

title: Zupa Rumiana Postna (Browned Meatless Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- carrots
- celery
- kohlrabi
- cauliflower
- cabbage
- parsley
- green peas
- asparagus
- butter
- sugar
- water
- onions
- bread cubes (grzanki)

## Directions

1. Finely chop a large quantity of various vegetables (carrots, celery, kohlrabi, cauliflower, cabbage, parsley, green peas, asparagus, etc.)
2. Place a spoonful of good butter in a pot and add a couple of teaspoonfuls of sugar
3. Add the vegetables and fry covered on low heat, frequently shaking the pot, so the vegetables only brown without burning
4. When everything is nicely browned, add as much water as desired for the amount of soup
5. Add two onions baked in the oven, place on heat and cook until the vegetables fall apart
6. Strain the soup and serve with bread cubes fried in butter
7. Alternatively, serve with stuffed crab shells or thick pasta cooked separately and placed in the bowl before pouring the soup

title: Zupa Rybna (Fish Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- fish (carp, pike, or perch)
- vegetables (carrots, onions)
- butter
- dumplings (kluski krajane)
- sour cream

## Directions

1. Cook a large amount of chopped vegetables and onions
2. Place the fish on this base, taking care that it does not fall apart
3. Cook the fish for 15 to 25 minutes depending on size
4. When the fish feels soft when touched with a fork, remove it
5. Strain the soup, add a spoonful of butter and cook cut dumplings in the same broth
6. Before serving, thicken with sour cream
7. If the fish is large, you can cut off the head and place it in the pot with the vegetables for a more flavorful soup

title: Zupa Rybna (Fish Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- assorted vegetables and onion
- fish

## Directions

1. Fish soup is best made from crucians or pike, though in America they most often use perch
2. Cook a lot of chopped vegetables and onion, and on this stock place the fish, making sure it does not fall apart; fish cooks for 15 to 25 minutes, depending on size
3. When you feel by touching with a fork that the fish is already soft, remove the fish, strain the soup, add one tablespoon of butter, and on this same stock cook small dumplings
4. When serving, thicken with sour cream
5. If the fish is large, you can cut off the head and put it in the pot along with the vegetables

title: Zupa Ryżowa Amerykańska (American Rice Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- rice, washed
- broth from veal or chicken, or beef broth
- carrot
- celery
- onion
- butter
- flour
- nutmeg
- cloves
- pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Make this soup best on veal or chicken broth, though beef broth may also be used.
2. Pour half a small cup of washed rice into one and a half quarts of broth, bring to a boil, then add the following vegetables: three spoons of carrot, three spoons of celery, and three spoons of onion chopped finely.
3. In a small pot, melt three spoons of butter, add the vegetables and braise covered over very low heat for twenty minutes, then remove and place in the soup.
4. After adding these vegetables, rub two spoons of flour into the remaining butter in the pot, add some nutmeg, a few cloves, half a teaspoon of pepper and two teaspoons of salt and also pour into the soup, then cook everything for two hours over very low heat, then strain.

title: Zupa Salcyfiowa (Oyster Plant Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 roots of salsify
- 1 teaspoon of vinegar
- 1 quart of water
- 1/2 quart of milk
- 1 heaping tablespoon of butter
- salt and pepper to taste
- 1/2 pod of red or green pepper (optional, instead of black pepper)
- a little onion (optional)

## Directions

1. Scrape 6 salsify roots and put them in cold water with 1 teaspoon of vinegar to prevent them from blackening
2. Wash them well and chop into small pieces
3. Cook in 1 quart of water
4. Add 1/2 quart of milk, 1 heaping tablespoon of butter, salt and pepper to taste
5. Boil and serve
6. If desired, use 1/2 pod of red or green pepper instead of black pepper
7. If desired, you can add a little onion for flavor

title: Zupa Salcyfiowa (Salsify Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- six roots of salsify (oyster plant)
- cold water
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Scrape six salsify roots and place in cold water to which you should add one teaspoonful of vinegar to prevent the salsify from darkening

title: Zupa Selerowa na Mleku (Celery Soup with Milk)
body:
## Ingredients

- one heaping cup of celery cut into small pieces
- one and a half quarts of good milk
- one heaping spoonful of flour
- half a cup of cold milk
- butter
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Take one heaping cup of celery cut into small pieces and cook in one and a half quarts of good milk until soft
2. Beat one heaping spoonful of flour in half a cup of cold milk and slowly pour into the soup while constantly stirring
3. Then add one spoonful of butter, salt and pepper to taste and serve

title: Zupa Szczawiowa (Sorrel Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- vegetables (onions, celery, leeks, parsley, carrots, and other vegetables)
- sorrel
- butter
- sour cream
- flour
- egg yolks
- hard-boiled eggs or stuffed eggs

## Directions

1. Cook a broth with various vegetables such as onions, celery, leeks, parsley, carrots and whatever vegetables are on hand
2. Separately, stew a sufficient amount of sorrel in butter, and when completely soft, thin it with the vegetable broth
3. Strain through a fine sieve
4. Take one cup of sour cream and one spoonful of flour to thicken the soup
5. In a bowl, beat four egg yolks with four spoonfuls of cooled vegetable broth, then pour the hot soup into this, constantly stirring with a spoon
6. The above proportion is for four people
7. Serve with hard-boiled eggs cut into quarters, stuffed eggs, or eggs cooked in their shells, which can be placed in the soup and cooked in it

title: Zupa Szczawiowa (Sorrel Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- broth made on beef
- sorrel, enough to make the soup sour
- butter
- sour cream or sour cream mixed with flour
- soft-boiled eggs in shells (optional)
- hard-boiled eggs (optional)
- stuffed eggs (optional)

## Directions

1. Cook broth on beef in ordinary manner
2. Chop enough sorrel to make the soup sour to taste
3. Stew the sorrel first in butter, then thin it with broth
4. Season with sour cream, either fried or mixed with flour
5. Bring to a boil and serve on the table
6. The best additions to this soup are soft-boiled eggs in shells, which can be put in the soup and stirred in it, hard-boiled eggs, or stuffed eggs

title: Zupa Szparagowa (Asparagus Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- asparagus
- salted boiling water
- broth made on beef bones
- butter and flour, or sour cream or sour milk
- asparagus for garnish

## Directions

1. Chop asparagus into pieces one inch long, place in a pot and cover with salted boiling water
2. Cook for a few minutes to remove the bitterness, then remove and place in previously cooked broth made on beef bones
3. Cook until the asparagus is soft
4. When the asparagus is well cooked, strain it through a colander
5. Season with butter mixed with flour, or with one quarter sour cream or sour milk
6. Separately cook some fine asparagus in salted water, chop it and place in a bowl, then pour the soup over it
7. If the soup is without sour cream, beat two egg yolks in the bowl and pour hot soup over them

title: Zupa Szparagowa Biała (White Asparagus Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- one quart of asparagus
- water
- milk
- butter
- flour
- bread cubes (grzanki)

## Directions

1. Chop a quart of asparagus into one-inch pieces
2. Pour one quart of water over them and cook until completely soft
3. Strain through a colander and return to the water in which they cooked
4. Heat half a quart of milk and whisk in a spoonful of butter mixed with a spoonful of flour
5. Bring to a boil and pour into the asparagus, then bring everything to a boil and pour into a serving bowl lined with bread cubes cut into small pieces

title: Zupa Szpinakowa (Spinach Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of spinach
- water
- one tablespoon of butter
- one tablespoon of flour
- salt and pepper to taste
- one quart of any broth
- two egg yolks
- a few drops of lemon juice
- small croutons of white bread fried in butter

## Directions

1. Separate and carefully wash one pound of spinach, pour a small amount of water in a pot and cook until soft, then strain through a sieve
2. Mix one tablespoon of butter with one tablespoon of flour in a pot, add salt and pepper to taste, pour one quart of any broth and bring to a boil
3. When it boils, remove from heat and add to the soup two well-beaten egg yolks mixed with a few drops of lemon juice
4. Serve on the table with small croutons of white bread fried in butter

title: Zupa Wexham (Wexham Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of chopped beef
- one small carrot
- one small turnip
- three small onions
- one cup of tomatoes
- a clean cloth
- one celery stalk
- a few sprigs of parsley
- a few cloves
- four peppercorns
- a little marjoram
- one half tablespoon of salt
- one half teaspoon of sugar
- a little ground pepper
- one and one quarter quarts of cold water

## Directions

1. Chop finely each of the vegetables: one small carrot, one small turnip, three small onions, and one cup of tomatoes
2. Tie together in a piece of clean cloth: one celery stalk, a few sprigs of parsley, a few cloves, four peppercorns and a little marjoram
3. Mix together on a saucer: one half tablespoon of salt, one half teaspoon of sugar and a little ground pepper
4. Take a clay pot with a lid, place meat at the bottom, then salt and pepper, then arrange the vegetables in layers on top, finally pour one and one quarter quarts of cold water
5. Cover tightly and place in a moderate oven to cook slowly for five hours
6. This soup is best prepared a couple of hours before dinner and reheated when serving

title: Zupa Wielkopostna (Lenten Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound of dried plums
- pieces of cinnamon
- 1/2 dozen dried mushrooms
- 1/2 quart of water
- a little flour
- cold water
- wheat groats for thickening
- sugar to taste

## Directions

1. Cook 1 pound of dried plums in a small amount of water with a few pieces of cinnamon
2. Separately cook 1/2 dozen dried mushrooms in 1/2 quart of water until completely soft
3. Strain the plums through a sieve, pouring the mushroom broth over them
4. Chop the mushrooms very finely and add to the soup
5. Thicken with a little flour mixed in cold water
6. Cook wheat groats separately in 1/2 quart of water and add to the soup
7. Sweeten to taste and serve

title: Zupa Wiosenna (Spring Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- one onion
- a handful of lettuce
- a bit of parsley
- one cup of pickled green peas
- one half quart of water
- one quart of previously cooked broth
- three egg yolks
- salt and pepper to taste

## Directions

1. Chop finely one onion, a handful of lettuce and a bit of parsley and place in a pot with one cup of pickled green peas, pour one half quart of water and cook until everything is soft, then salt and pepper to taste
2. Strain the water into another dish and pour one third of this liquid into one quart of previously cooked broth; in the remaining two thirds beat three egg yolks and quickly bring to a boil
3. Add together with the vegetables to the rest of the soup and serve on the table

title: Zupa Wiśniowa (Cherry Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart ripe cherries
- cinnamon stick
- a few cloves
- water
- 1/2 pound sugar
- 1/2 tablespoon flour
- cold water
- ice
- small fried bread cubes (grzaneczki)
- sour cream (optional)

## Directions

1. Pit 1 quart of ripe cherries, put them with the pits in a pot, add a piece of cinnamon and a few cloves. Pour enough water to barely cover the cherries and cook for several minutes.
2. Strain through a colander and add 1/2 pound of sugar and mix.
3. Next, mix 1/2 tablespoon of flour with cold water, bring everything together to a boil and place on ice to cool.
4. When completely cooled, serve on the table with small fried bread cubes.
5. Note: Some also thicken this soup with sour cream.

title: Zupa z Bułek (Bread Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- four bread rolls
- one quart of hot water
- two spoons of butter
- salt to taste
- half a quarter litre of sour cream or milk
- two egg yolks

## Directions

1. Chop four bread rolls, pour over with one quart of hot water, add two spoons of butter and salt to taste, bring all this to a boil together and then pass through a sieve
2. Thicken the soup with half a quarter litre of sour cream or milk in which two egg yolks have been beaten

title: Zupa z Czernic (Blueberry Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quart ripe blueberries
- water
- 1/4 pound sugar
- cinnamon stick
- a few tablespoons cold water
- 1 small teaspoon wheat flour
- handful of choice blueberries
- ice
- small fried bread cubes (grzaneczki)
- white bread
- small noodles (kluseczki) (optional)
- cold water

## Directions

1. Pour 1 quart of ripe blueberries with just enough water to soak them, then cook until they burst. Strain through a sieve and let the liquid settle.
2. When all the sediment settles to the bottom, pour the clear juice into a pot, add 1/4 pound of sugar and a piece of cinnamon.
3. Mix a few tablespoons of cold water with a small teaspoon of wheat flour, thicken the soup and place on heat.
4. As soon as it begins to boil, add a handful of choice blueberries, cook for a few minutes, then place on ice.
5. As a side serve fried small bread cubes from white bread, or separately cooked small noodles rinsed with cold water.
6. Note: This soup can also be served hot.

title: Zupa z Dyni (Pumpkin Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- half quart yellow pumpkin
- celery stalks
- butter
- hot water
- salt
- pepper
- flour
- white bread

## Directions

1. Peel and finely chop half quart yellow pumpkin and a couple of celery stalks
2. Melt a spoonful of butter in a pot, add the pumpkin and celery and fry until lightly browned
3. Pour a quart of hot water, salt and pepper, then cook until everything is soft enough to strain through a sieve
4. After straining, pour the soup back into the pot and place on fire
5. Separately make a roux in a small pot with a large half spoonful of flour and the same amount of butter and brown into a uniform mass, pour into the soup and mix well and cook for about ten minutes
6. Line a bowl with small croutons of white bread and pour the soup over them
7. These croutons should be made from stale bread, cut into small cubes and fried in butter in a pan

title: Zupa z Fasoli (Bean Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- White beans
- A piece of ham
- A piece of smoked bacon
- One onion
- A piece of cabbage head
- Celery
- Leeks
- Several fresh tomatoes
- Salt and pepper
- A handful of cooked rice

## Directions

1. Take white beans; if very dry, soak in water for several hours
2. Put a piece of ham and a piece of smoked bacon together with the beans in a pot and cook almost until tender
3. Add one sliced onion, a piece of sliced cabbage head, celery, leeks, and several fresh tomatoes, salt and pepper to taste
4. Cook further one hour
5. Before serving, add a handful of cooked rice
6. Meatballs made from beef fat are the best addition to this soup

title: Zupa z Jabłek (Apple Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 large tart apples
- 3 cups water
- about 1/4 pound sugar
- 1 glass white sour wine
- zest of 1/2 lemon
- 1 large cup sour cream
- 2 egg yolks
- ice
- small fried bread cubes (grzaneczki)
- butter

## Directions

1. Chop finely 6 large tart apples, put in a pot, pour 3 cups of water and cook until the apples completely fall apart.
2. Strain through a loose sieve, add about 1/4 pound of sugar, 1 glass of white sour wine and zest from 1/2 lemon rubbed on sugar.
3. Whip 1 large cup of sour cream with 2 egg yolks, then slowly pour the hot soup onto this cream, stirring continuously.
4. Then place on ice to cool.
5. As a side, serve small fried bread cubes dipped in cream and then fried in butter and sprinkled with sugar in the pan.
6. Note: Egg yolks can be omitted. This soup can also be made with pears in this way with the addition of a few sour apples, otherwise it will be bland.

title: Zupa z Jarzyn po Wiejsku (Country-Style Vegetable Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- carrots
- onions
- celery
- parsley
- tomatoes
- salt
- pepper
- bay leaf
- cold water
- potatoes
- flour
- butter
- fresh parsley

## Directions

1. Take carrots, onions, celery, parsley and tomatoes, chop these vegetables
2. Add salt and pepper to taste, and also a bay leaf
3. Pour cold water over them, place on fire and cook twenty minutes from the moment of boiling
4. After twenty minutes, add finely chopped potatoes, cover and cook another twenty minutes on low heat
5. Then bind with flour browned in butter, sprinkle with finely chopped fresh parsley and serve

title: Zupa z Karpia (Carp Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- one carrot
- a couple of onions
- a couple of celery stalks
- parsley
- bay leaf
- whole pepper grains
- salt
- flour
- butter
- dumplings (kluski krajane)
- fish head and scraps

## Directions

1. Finely chop one carrot, a couple of onions, a couple of celery stalks, and some parsley
2. Add one bay leaf, several peppercorns and salt to taste
3. Cover everything with cold water and cook until all vegetables are completely soft
4. Strain through a sieve and pour this broth over the fish head and other scraps
5. Cook for one hour
6. Before serving, make a roux of flour and butter and thicken the strained soup with it
7. Serve with cut dumplings
8. The fish may be cooked separately and added to the soup twenty minutes before serving

title: Zupa z Kasztanów (Chestnut Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound chestnuts
- one-quarter pound sugar
- two tablespoons water
- one-quarter pound fresh butter
- one glass light white wine
- light veal broth
- about ten whole cooked and peeled chestnuts
- a handful of finely chopped croutons fried in butter

## Directions

1. Cook one pound of chestnuts, drain the water, peel them while hot, covering with a napkin so they do not cool.
2. Immediately after peeling, crush them in a mortar, then pass through a fine sieve, pouring water at the same time so they pass through more easily.
3. Lightly brown one-quarter pound of sugar with two tablespoons of water over high heat.
4. Into this sugar place the mashed chestnuts, add one-quarter pound of fresh butter, mix well, then pour in one glass of light white wine.
5. Prepare separately a light veal broth, from which all the fat should first be carefully removed.
6. Take one-quarter of this broth and thin the chestnuts with wine, then pour into a dish into which first place about ten whole cooked and peeled chestnuts, as well as a handful of finely chopped croutons fried in butter.
7. If the soup is to be meatless, water can be used instead of broth.

title: Zupa z Kury z Szynką (Soup with Chicken and Ham)
body:
## Ingredients

- one chicken, quartered
- half pound ham
- two onions
- three quarts water
- half a cup rice
- two cups good milk
- a little flour

## Directions

1. Quarter the chicken and place in a pot with a half pound piece of ham, add two onions and cover with three quarts of water.
2. Cook on low heat until one-third of the water has evaporated and the meat pulls away from the bones.
3. Then add half a cup of rice.
4. Cook until the rice is soft and then remove the meat.
5. Enrich the soup with two cups of good milk mixed with a little flour, then serve at table.

title: Zupa z Marynowanego Łososia (Pickled Salmon Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- one small can of pickled salmon
- one quart of water
- a few chopped onions
- one quart of milk
- one tablespoon of butter
- one tablespoon of flour
- salt and pepper to taste
- a few stalks of celery (optional)

## Directions

1. Take a small can of pickled salmon, remove bones and skin so that one large cupful of meat remains
2. Pour one quart of water over the meat and add a few chopped onions
3. Boil slowly for fifteen to twenty minutes, then press everything through a sieve
4. Bring one quart of milk to a boil and thicken it with one tablespoon of butter rubbed with one tablespoon of flour
5. Pour into the soup, season with salt and pepper to taste and serve
6. If desired, add a few stalks of celery when cooking the soup

title: Zupa z Małych Rybek (Small Fish Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- small fish (preferably carp)
- vegetables (various)
- flour
- butter
- sour cream
- dumplings (kluski)
- cooked groats (kaszka)

## Directions

1. Cook a broth with various vegetables
2. Clean the fish, preferably carp, discarding the roe, and place in the broth
3. Cook until the fish completely falls apart
4. Strain everything through a colander
5. Make a roux of half a spoonful of flour with a spoonful of butter and thicken the soup
6. Sour cream may also be added
7. Place dumplings or cooked groats (chilled and cut into cubes) in the serving bowl before pouring the soup

title: Zupa z Podróbek (Offal Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- offal from two pieces of poultry
- various vegetables for broth
- semolina or fine Cracow barley
- egg
- egg yolks

## Directions

1. For four or five persons, take offal from two pieces of poultry, clean carefully, add plenty of various vegetables and cook like broth.
2. After skimming, sprinkle in semolina and cook until tender, then beat two egg yolks in a bowl and pour the soup over them.
3. Instead of semolina, fine Cracow barley mixed with egg may be used.

title: Zupa z Powideł (Fruit Preserve Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound of fruit preserves
- one quart of water
- a couple of pieces of cinnamon
- two tablespoons of sugar
- quarter cup of sour cream
- a little flour

## Directions

1. Pour half a pound of fruit preserves with one quart of water, add a couple of pieces of cinnamon and two tablespoons of sugar, and place on fire
2. When the preserves cook well, thicken with a quarter cup of sour cream mixed with a little flour
3. Strain through a sieve and serve to the table
4. As an addition to this soup, fried croutons from white bread or French dumplings are served

title: Zupa z Ryżu i Kapusty (Rice and Cabbage Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- ordinary broth made on beef
- one head of cabbage, not too finely chopped
- some finely chopped bacon
- a little garlic
- green parsley
- one quarter pound of half-cooked rice
- salt and pepper to taste

## Directions

1. Cook ordinary broth on beef
2. Add to it one head of cabbage, not too finely chopped, and cook further for fifteen minutes
3. Fry some finely chopped bacon, add a little garlic and green parsley to it, then add one quarter pound of half-cooked rice
4. Put everything into the broth, mix well and cook further for half an hour
5. Add salt and pepper to taste and serve on the table

title: Zupa z Soczewicy (Lentil Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- broth made on beef bones with celery, onion, parsley, carrot, leeks and turnips
- one quart of lentils
- fat from the broth
- croutons

## Directions

1. Cook broth on beef bones with celery, onion, parsley, carrot, leeks and turnips
2. Dry lentils well on a baking sheet and brown them
3. Crush the lentils in a mortar, mix with fat collected from the broth into a smooth paste
4. Strain through a sieve and mix with broth
5. Serve to the table with croutons

title: Zupa z Truskawek (Strawberry Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cup sour cream
- 1 cup sweet cream
- 3 egg yolks
- 1/2 pound fine sugar
- 1 quart ripe strawberries
- 1 glass light sour wine
- whole strawberries
- ice
- biscuits (biszkopciki), cut into cubes

## Directions

1. Mix together 1 cup of sour cream, 1 cup of sweet cream and 3 egg yolks. Add 1/2 pound of fine sugar and whip everything together into a foam.
2. Place in a pot or pan on hot heat and stir with a spoon until it thickens slightly, being careful that the yolks do not curdle.
3. Strain 1 quart of ripe strawberries through a sieve so that only the seeds remain on the sieve. Pour the obtained liquid into the hot cream, mix well.
4. Then add, while constantly stirring, 1 glass of light sour wine. Put a handful of whole nice strawberries into a bowl, pour the soup over and place on ice.
5. When completely cooled, serve on the table, providing separately biscuits cut into cubes.

title: Zupa z Wątróbki (Liver Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- a piece of calf's liver
- a piece of bacon
- one large onion
- previously cooked broth
- a little flour and butter

## Directions

1. Clean a piece of calf's liver of membrane and chop finely, chop bacon into cubes and one large onion, place everything in a pot and stew for about twenty minutes covered
2. Remove and chop everything very finely, pour with previously cooked broth and cook for half an hour
3. Just before serving, fry a little flour with butter and thicken the soup with it
4. Serve to the table with croutons of rolls or white bread

title: Zupa z Zielonego Grochu (Green Pea Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- Shelled green peas
- Water
- One tablespoon of butter
- One teaspoon of sugar
- Beef broth with vegetables
- Empty pea pods (young ones)
- Flour
- Egg

## Directions

1. Pour shelled peas in a pot with a small amount of water, add one tablespoon of butter and cook until soft
2. Add one teaspoon of sugar
3. Separately cook broth with beef bones and various vegetables, and toward the end of cooking add some empty pods (husks) from the shelled peas, selecting only young ones
4. When the broth is cooked, remove the bones and vegetables and pass the cooked husks through a sieve
5. To the thus cooked broth, mix in a little paste made from flour and egg
6. Add the peas cooked with butter and serve

title: Zupa z Zielonej Kukurydzy (Green Corn Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- kernels from several ears of green corn, one large cupful of the scraped kernels
- remaining corn cobs covered with water
- one tablespoon of flour
- one tablespoon of butter
- salt and pepper to taste
- half quart of good milk or cream

## Directions

1. Scrape the kernels from several ears of green corn with a knife and take one large cupful of this mass
2. Pour enough water over the remaining cobs to barely cover them and boil for half an hour, then strain carefully
3. The resulting liquid should yield three cupfuls and pour into a pot
4. Bring to a boil, then add the scraped corn mass and boil for fifteen to twenty minutes
5. Mix one tablespoon of flour with the liquid, add one tablespoon of butter, salt and pepper to taste
6. Add half a quart of good milk or cream
7. Boil for another five minutes and serve

title: Zupa ze świeżych Ogórków (Fresh Cucumber Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- three medium cucumbers
- two onions
- half a quart of water
- half a quart of veal or chicken broth
- half a quart of milk
- one tablespoon of flour
- one tablespoon of butter
- salt
- pepper
- small croutons

## Directions

1. Cut three medium cucumbers and two onions into thin slices and cover with half a quart of water and half a quart of veal or chicken broth.
2. Cook on a low fire for one hour, then press everything through a sieve, pour back into the pot and place on a hot stove.
3. Boil half a quart of milk and add to it one tablespoon of flour rubbed with one tablespoon of butter, mix well and pour into the soup, also add salt and pepper to taste.
4. Cook for about five minutes and serve with small croutons.

title: Zupa à la Reine (Queen's Soup)
body:
## Ingredients

- chicken
- vegetables for broth

## Directions

1. This soup is made from chicken
2. Cook broth from chicken in the usual manner, adding vegetables

title: Ćwikła (Pickled Beets)
body:
## Ingredients

- small red beets
- horseradish, grated
- sugar
- vinegar
- salt

## Directions

1. Take small red beets and cook them until tender
2. Cool and peel them
3. Slice into thin slices
4. Layer the slices in a jar, sprinkling grated horseradish between layers
5. Add a little sugar
6. Finally pour light vinegar that has been boiled with salt and cooled over everything
7. Place in a cool location
8. Can be used after three days

title: Łazanki z Kapustą (Noodle Squares with Cabbage)
body:
## Ingredients

- noodle squares made as above
- one medium head of cabbage
- one large white onion
- butter
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Cook ordinary noodle squares in salted water as described above
2. Peel cabbage of outer leaves, cut into quarters and parboil one medium head of cabbage and one large white onion in water
3. Chop both the cabbage and onion and stew in butter until tender
4. Add salt and pepper to taste
5. Mix while hot with the cooked noodle squares
6. Grease a baking dish or mold generously with butter
7. Fill with the mixture of noodles and cabbage
8. Place in a moderately hot oven until it bakes and browns nicely
9. Transfer to a serving platter or bowl and serve on the table

title: Łazanki z Twarogiem (Noodle Squares with Cottage Cheese)
body:
## Ingredients

- half quart of flour
- one egg
- half pound or more of fresh cottage cheese
- three egg yolks
- one cup of sour cream
- one tablespoon of sugar
- butter
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Knead half quart of flour and one egg into stiff dough
2. Roll out thinly
3. Cut first into strips, then into square noodle squares
4. Scatter on work surface to dry slightly
5. Cook in salted water
6. Rub half pound or more of fresh cottage cheese with three egg yolks and one cup of sour cream and add one tablespoon of sugar
7. Grease a baking dish with butter and sprinkle with breadcrumbs
8. Place a layer of noodle squares on the bottom
9. Add cottage cheese, then noodles and cottage cheese again until all ingredients are used
10. Place in a hot oven for one hour
11. When well baked and browned, transfer to a serving platter and serve

title: Łemieszka (Unbrowned Buckwheat and Wheat Flour Porridge)
body:
## Ingredients

- half quart of buckwheat flour
- half quart of wheat flour
- one quart of milk
- salt
- lard

## Directions

1. Make in the same way as Prażucha, with the difference that for Łemieszka use raw flour instead of browned flour
2. Pour fried bacon over it

title: Łódeczki z Ogórków (Cucumber Boats)
body:
## Ingredients

- two large cucumbers
- two medium onions
- one tablespoon celery seed
- half tablespoon mustard seed
- small teaspoon salt
- red pepper

## Directions

1. Cut two large cucumbers in half lengthwise
2. Carefully scoop out all the insides with a small spoon, leaving the skin intact so each half looks like a small boat
3. Place the boats in cold water
4. Finely chop the scooped-out cucumber flesh together with two medium onions
5. Spread the mixture on a colander to drain excess juice
6. After fifteen minutes, add one tablespoon of celery seed, half tablespoon of mustard seed, small teaspoon of salt, and some red pepper
7. Mix everything together and fill the hollowed cucumber boats
8. Serve on the table

title: Śledzie Marynowane (Marinated Herring) - Method 1
body:
## Ingredients

- herring
- water
- onion
- pepper
- dill
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Soak herring in cold water for one and a half days
2. Remove water, wipe clean, clean without removing skin and arrange in a jar
3. Intersperse with sliced onion, pepper and dill
4. Pour boiled vinegar over them

title: Śledzie Marynowane (Marinated Herring) - Method 2
body:
## Ingredients

- herring
- water
- onion
- lemon
- herring roe
- oil
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Soak herring as above, clean and wipe dry
2. Arrange in a jar interspersed with sliced onion and lemon slices, peeled of skin and pith
3. Chop herring roe very finely, mix with oil and then dilute with vinegar
4. Pour this sauce over the arranged herring

title: Śledzie Marynowane (Marinated Herring) - Method 3
body:
## Ingredients

- herring
- water
- onion
- lemon
- herring roe
- sour cream
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Prepare herring as in method two
2. Instead of oil, blend the roe with sweet sour cream, using half a quart for six herring
3. Dilute the roe blended with sour cream with strong vinegar, pass through a sieve
4. Pour this sauce over the herring
5. Herring prepared by any of these methods is ready to eat the next day

title: Śledzie po Hambursku (Hamburg-Style Herring)
body:
## Ingredients

- one good fat herring
- water
- mustard
- oil
- bay leaves
- pepper

## Directions

1. Soak one good fat herring in cold water for twenty-four hours
2. Remove from water, clean, remove skin, remove backbone, cut off head and tail
3. Flatten on a board and smear each half with mustard
4. Roll into a tube
5. Once all are rolled, arrange in a jar and cover with oil
6. Intersperse with bay leaves and coarsely ground pepper
7. When serving, cut each half in two on the plate, pour the same oil over and sprinkle with strong vinegar

title: Śledzie Smażone Nadziewane (Stuffed Fried Herring)
body:
## Ingredients

- four large soaked herring
- one large onion
- herring roe
- one tablespoon butter
- one egg
- pepper
- egg
- water
- grated bread

## Directions

1. Soak four large herring for a full day, then wipe dry with paper
2. Clean the herring inside without removing the skin
3. Chop one large onion finely, add finely chopped herring roe, one tablespoon butter, one egg and pepper
4. Mix all ingredients well
5. Stuff the herring with this filling
6. Dip in egg beaten with water and coat in grated bread
7. Fry in hot clarified butter

title: Śledzie Smażone z Jajkiem (Herring Fried with Egg)
body:
## Ingredients

- soaked herring
- egg
- water
- flour
- butter

## Directions

1. Clean the soaked herring inside, keeping the skin, split it open and remove the backbone
2. Wipe dry with paper, dip in egg beaten with water, coat with flour and fry in hot butter
3. Fry first skin-side down, then turn over to brown both sides
4. When the herring is almost fried, add two eggs per herring in the same butter
5. Serve as soon as the egg whites set, typically for breakfast

title: Śledzie z Kartoflami (Herring with Potatoes)
body:
## Ingredients

- three large herring
- small potatoes
- chives

## Directions

1. Soak, clean and slice three large herring into rings
2. Cook small potatoes, peel and slice them
3. On a wide dish, arrange alternating rows of herring and potatoes so that potatoes are at the edges
4. Arrange rows in this order: potatoes, herring, potatoes, herring, potatoes, herring, potatoes - four rows of potatoes and three rows of herring
5. Arrange potato slices so they overlap, arrange herring rings next to each other to form a complete row
6. Drizzle with oil and sprinkle potatoes with finely chopped chives

title: Śledzie Zapiekane (Baked Herring)
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 fine and fatty herring
- several cold cooked potatoes
- butter
- 2 eggs
- 2 cups milk
- 1 cup breadcrumbs
- 1 teaspoon melted butter

## Directions

1. Soak 4 fine and fatty herring in cold water overnight
2. The next day split lengthwise along the back, remove the skin, take out the bones and cut the flesh into inch-sized pieces
3. Cut several cold cooked potatoes into slices
4. Butter a metal dish or baking form
5. Place a layer of sliced potatoes on the bottom, then herring and so forth until the dish is filled, with potatoes on top
6. Beat 2 eggs, mix with 2 cups of milk and pour over the herring and potatoes
7. Sprinkle 1 cup of breadcrumbs mixed with 1 teaspoon of melted butter on top
8. Place in the oven for 40 or 45 minutes and serve hot at table

title: Żabie Udka (Frog Legs)
body:
## Ingredients

- frog legs
- beaten egg
- grated bread
- butter or fat

## Directions

1. Remove the feet from the frog legs
2. Dip in beaten egg, then coat with grated bread
3. Fry in butter or fat, using a large amount of fat
4. In first-class restaurants they are fried in deep pans filled with fat
5. Serve on a platter dressed with lettuce leaves and fresh parsley

title: Żeberka Pieczone (Roasted Ribs)
body:
## Ingredients

- a large piece of ribs
- salt
- pepper (for seasoning ribs)
- three tablespoons grated breadcrumbs or bread
- one onion finely minced
- one small apple minced
- one-half teaspoon salt (for stuffing)
- one-quarter teaspoon ground pepper
- one-quarter teaspoon powdered sage
- two tablespoons beef suet, preferably from the kidneys
- one-half quart water

## Directions

1. Take a large piece of ribs and wipe with salt and pepper
2. Break in the middle across the ribs
3. Prepare the stuffing: mix three tablespoons of grated breadcrumbs or bread, one finely minced onion, one small minced apple, one-half teaspoon salt, one-quarter teaspoon ground pepper, one-quarter teaspoon powdered sage, and two tablespoons of beef suet
4. Mix everything well
5. Place the stuffing on the inner side of the ribs
6. Roll the ribs and tie with twine or sew
7. Place in a baking pan, pour in one-half quart of water and roast in a moderately hot oven until well browned
8. Baste frequently during roasting
